# The Doctrine and Covenants of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints

Containing Revelations Given to
Joseph Smith, the Prophet
with Some Additions by His Successors
in the Presidency of the Church

Published by
The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints

Salt Lake City, Utah, USA

© 2013 by Intellectual Reserve, Inc. All rights reserved. Source: 2015/03/24

# Introduction

The Doctrine and Covenants is a collection of divine revelations and inspired declarations given for the establishment and regulation of the kingdom of God on the earth in the last days. Although most of the sections are directed to members of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, the messages, warnings, and exhortations are for the benefit of all mankind and contain an invitation to all people everywhere to hear the voice of the Lord Jesus Christ, speaking to them for their temporal well-being and their everlasting salvation.

Most of the revelations in this compilation were received through Joseph Smith Jr., the first prophet and president of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints. Others were issued through some of his successors in the Presidency (see [headings to D&C 135](../dc-testament/dc/135.xhtml), [136](../dc-testament/dc/136.xhtml), and [138](../dc-testament/dc/138.xhtml), and Official Declarations 1 and 2).

The book of Doctrine and Covenants is one of the standard works of the Church in company with the Holy Bible, the Book of Mormon, and the Pearl of Great Price. However, the Doctrine and Covenants is unique because it is not a translation of an ancient document, but is of modern origin and was given of God through His chosen prophets for the restoration of His holy work and the establishment of the kingdom of God on the earth in these days. In the revelations, one hears the tender but firm voice of the Lord Jesus Christ, speaking anew in the dispensation of the fulness of times; and the work that is initiated herein is preparatory to His Second Coming, in fulfillment of and in concert with the words of all the holy prophets since the world began.

Joseph Smith Jr. was born December 23, 1805, in Sharon, Windsor County, Vermont. During his early life, he moved with his family to present-day Manchester, in western New York. It was while he was living there in the spring of 1820, when he was fourteen years of age, that he experienced his first vision, in which he was visited in person by God, the Eternal Father, and His Son Jesus Christ. He was told in this vision that the true Church of Jesus Christ that had been established in New Testament times, and which had administered the fulness of the gospel, was no longer on the earth. Other divine manifestations followed in which he was taught by many angels; it was shown to him that God had a special work for him to do on the earth and that through him the Church of Jesus Christ would be restored to the earth.

In the course of time, Joseph Smith was enabled by divine assistance to translate and publish the Book of Mormon. In the meantime he and Oliver Cowdery were ordained to the Aaronic Priesthood by John the Baptist in May 1829 (see [D&C 13](../dc-testament/dc/13.xhtml)), and soon thereafter they were also ordained to the Melchizedek Priesthood by the ancient Apostles Peter, James, and John (see [D&C 27:12](../dc-testament/dc/27.xhtml#p12)). Other ordinations followed in which priesthood keys were conferred by Moses, Elijah, Elias, and many ancient prophets (see [D&C 110](../dc-testament/dc/110.xhtml); [128:18, 21](../dc-testament/dc/128.xhtml#p18)). These ordinations were, in fact, a restoration of divine authority to man on the earth. On April 6, 1830, under heavenly direction, the Prophet Joseph Smith organized the Church, and thus the true Church of Jesus Christ is once again operative as an institution among men, with authority to teach the gospel and administer the ordinances of salvation. (See [D&C 20](../dc-testament/dc/20.xhtml) and the Pearl of Great Price, [Joseph Smith—History 1](../pgp/js-h/1.xhtml).)

These sacred revelations were received in answer to prayer, in times of need, and came out of real-life situations involving real people. The Prophet and his associates sought for divine guidance, and these revelations certify that they received it. In the revelations, one sees the restoration and unfolding of the gospel of Jesus Christ and the ushering in of the dispensation of the fulness of times. The westward movement of the Church from New York and Pennsylvania to Ohio, to Missouri, to Illinois, and finally to the Great Basin of western America and the mighty struggles of the Saints in attempting to build Zion on the earth in modern times are also shown forth in these revelations.

Several of the earlier sections involve matters regarding the translation and publication of the Book of Mormon (see [sections 3](../dc-testament/dc/3.xhtml), [5](../dc-testament/dc/5.xhtml), [10](../dc-testament/dc/10.xhtml), [17](../dc-testament/dc/17.xhtml), and [19](../dc-testament/dc/19.xhtml)). Some later sections reflect the work of the Prophet Joseph Smith in making an inspired translation of the Bible, during which many of the great doctrinal sections were received (see, for example, [sections 37](../dc-testament/dc/37.xhtml), [45](../dc-testament/dc/45.xhtml), [73](../dc-testament/dc/73.xhtml), [76](../dc-testament/dc/76.xhtml), [77](../dc-testament/dc/77.xhtml), [86](../dc-testament/dc/86.xhtml), [91](../dc-testament/dc/91.xhtml), and [132](../dc-testament/dc/132.xhtml), each of which has some direct relationship to the Bible translation).

In the revelations, the doctrines of the gospel are set forth with explanations about such fundamental matters as the nature of the Godhead, the origin of man, the reality of Satan, the purpose of mortality, the necessity for obedience, the need for repentance, the workings of the Holy Spirit, the ordinances and performances that pertain to salvation, the destiny of the earth, the future conditions of man after the Resurrection and the Judgment, the eternity of the marriage relationship, and the eternal nature of the family. Likewise, the gradual unfolding of the administrative structure of the Church is shown with the calling of bishops, the First Presidency, the Council of the Twelve, and the Seventy and the establishment of other presiding offices and quorums. Finally, the testimony that is given of Jesus Christ—His divinity, His majesty, His perfection, His love, and His redeeming power—makes this book of great value to the human family and “worth to the Church the riches of the whole Earth” (see [heading to D&C 70](../dc-testament/dc/70.xhtml)).

The revelations were originally recorded by Joseph Smith’s scribes, and Church members enthusiastically shared handwritten copies with each other. To create a more permanent record, scribes soon copied these revelations into manuscript record books, which Church leaders used in preparing the revelations to be printed. Joseph and the early Saints viewed the revelations as they did the Church: living, dynamic, and subject to refinement with additional revelation. They also recognized that unintentional errors had likely occurred through the process of copying the revelations and preparing them for publication. Thus, a Church conference asked Joseph Smith in 1831 to “correct those errors or mistakes which he may discover by the Holy Spirit.”

After the revelations had been reviewed and corrected, Church members in Missouri began printing a book titled *A Book of Commandments for the Government of the Church of Christ,* which contained many of the Prophet’s early revelations. This first attempt to publish the revelations ended, however, when a mob destroyed the Saints’ printing office in Jackson County on July 20, 1833.

Upon hearing of the destruction of the Missouri printing office, Joseph Smith and other Church leaders began preparations to publish the revelations in Kirtland, Ohio. To again correct errors, clarify wording, and recognize developments in Church doctrine and organization, Joseph Smith oversaw the editing of the text of some revelations to prepare them for publication in 1835 as the *Doctrine and Covenants of the Church of the Latter Day Saints.* Joseph Smith authorized another edition of the Doctrine and Covenants, which was published only months after the Prophet’s martyrdom in 1844.

The early Latter-day Saints prized the revelations and viewed them as messages from God. On one occasion in late 1831, several elders of the Church gave solemn testimony that the Lord had borne record to their souls of the truth of the revelations. This testimony was published in the 1835 edition of the Doctrine and Covenants as the written testimony of the Twelve Apostles:

## Testimony of the Twelve Apostles to the Truth of the Book of Doctrine and Covenants

*The Testimony of the Witnesses to the Book of the Lord’s Commandments, which commandments He gave to His Church through Joseph Smith, Jun., who was appointed by the voice of the Church for this purpose:*

We, therefore, feel willing to bear testimony to all the world of mankind, to every creature upon the face of the earth, that the Lord has borne record to our souls, through the Holy Ghost shed forth upon us, that these commandments were given by inspiration of God, and are profitable for all men and are verily true.

We give this testimony unto the world, the Lord being our helper; and it is through the grace of God the Father, and His Son, Jesus Christ, that we are permitted to have this privilege of bearing this testimony unto the world, in the which we rejoice exceedingly, praying the Lord always that the children of men may be profited thereby.

The names of the Twelve were:

* Thomas B. Marsh
* David W. Patten
* Brigham Young
* Heber C. Kimball
* Orson Hyde
* William E. McLellin
* Parley P. Pratt
* Luke S. Johnson
* William Smith
* Orson Pratt
* John F. Boynton
* Lyman E. Johnson

In successive editions of the Doctrine and Covenants, additional revelations or other matters of record have been added, as received and as accepted by competent assemblies or conferences of the Church. The 1876 edition, prepared by Elder Orson Pratt under Brigham Young’s direction, arranged the revelations chronologically and supplied new headings with historical introductions.

Beginning with the 1835 edition, a series of seven theological lessons was also included; these were titled the *Lectures on Faith.* These had been prepared for use in the School of the Prophets in Kirtland, Ohio, from 1834 to 1835. Although profitable for doctrine and instruction, these lectures have been omitted from the Doctrine and Covenants since the 1921 edition because they were not given or presented as revelations to the whole Church.

In the 1981 edition of the Doctrine and Covenants, three documents were included for the first time. These are [sections 137](../dc-testament/dc/137.xhtml) and [138](../dc-testament/dc/138.xhtml), setting forth the fundamentals of salvation for the dead; and Official Declaration 2, announcing that all worthy male members of the Church may be ordained to the priesthood without regard for race or color.

Each new edition of the Doctrine and Covenants has corrected past errors and added new information, particularly in the historical portions of the section headings. The present edition further refines dates and place-names and makes other corrections. These changes have been made to bring the material into conformity with the most accurate historical information. Other special features of this latest edition include revised maps showing the major geographical locations in which the revelations were received, plus improved photographs of Church historical sites, cross-references, section headings, and subject-matter summaries, all of which are designed to help readers to understand and rejoice in the message of the Lord as given in the Doctrine and Covenants. Information for the section headings has been taken from the Manuscript History of the Church and the published *History of the Church* (collectively referred to in the headings as Joseph Smith’s history) and the *Joseph Smith Papers.*

Section 1

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, on November 1, 1831, during a special conference of elders of the Church, held at Hiram, Ohio. Many revelations had been received from the Lord prior to this time, and the compilation of these for publication in book form was one of the principal subjects passed upon at the conference. This section constitutes the Lord’s preface to the doctrines, covenants, and commandments given in this dispensation.

[1–7](1.xhtml#p1), The voice of warning is to all people; [8–16](1.xhtml#p8), Apostasy and wickedness precede the Second Coming; [17–23](1.xhtml#p17), Joseph Smith is called to restore to earth the Lord’s truths and powers; [24–33](1.xhtml#p24), The Book of Mormon is brought forth and the true Church is established; [34–36](1.xhtml#p34), Peace will be taken from the earth; [37–39](1.xhtml#p37), Search these commandments.

1 [aHearken](#note1a), O ye people of my [bchurch](#note1b), saith the voice of him who dwells on high, and whose [ceyes](#note1c) are upon all men; yea, verily I say: Hearken ye people from afar; and ye that are upon the islands of the sea, listen together.

2 For verily the [avoice](#note2a) of the Lord is unto all men, and there is none to [bescape](#note2b); and there is no eye that shall not see, neither [cear](#note2c) that shall not hear, neither [dheart](#note2d) that shall not be penetrated.

3 And the [arebellious](#note3a) shall be [bpierced](#note3b) with much [csorrow](#note3c); for their iniquities shall be [dspoken](#note3d) upon the housetops, and their secret acts shall be revealed.

4 And the [avoice](#note4a) of warning shall be unto all people, by the mouths of my disciples, whom I have [bchosen](#note4b) in these [clast days](#note4c).

5 And they shall [ago forth](#note5a) and none shall stay them, for I the Lord have commanded them.

6 Behold, this is mine [aauthority](#note6a), and the authority of my servants, and my preface unto the book of my [bcommandments](#note6b), which I have given them to [cpublish](#note6c) unto you, O [dinhabitants](#note6d) of the earth.

7 Wherefore, [afear](#note7a) and [btremble](#note7b), O ye people, for what I the Lord have [cdecreed](#note7c) in them shall be [dfulfilled](#note7d).

8 And verily I say unto you, that they who go forth, bearing these tidings unto the inhabitants of the earth, to them is power given to [aseal](#note8a) both on earth and in heaven, the unbelieving and [brebellious](#note8b);

9 Yea, verily, to seal them up unto the [aday](#note9a) when the [bwrath](#note9b) of God shall be poured out upon the [cwicked](#note9c) without measure—

10 Unto the [aday](#note10a) when the Lord shall come to [brecompense](#note10b) unto every man according to his [cwork](#note10c), and [dmeasure](#note10d) to every man according to the measure which he has measured to his fellow man.

11 Wherefore the voice of the Lord is unto the ends of the earth, that all that will hear may hear:

12 Prepare ye, [aprepare](#note12a) ye for that which is to come, for the Lord is [bnigh](#note12b);

13 And the [aanger](#note13a) of the Lord is kindled, and his [bsword](#note13b) is bathed in heaven, and it shall fall upon the inhabitants of the earth.

14 And the [aarm](#note14a) of the Lord shall be revealed; and the [bday](#note14b) cometh that they who will not [chear](#note14c) the voice of the Lord, neither the voice of his [dservants](#note14d), neither give [eheed](#note14e) to the words of the prophets and [fapostles](#note14f), shall be [gcut off](#note14g) from among the people;

15 For they have [astrayed](#note15a) from mine [bordinances](#note15b), and have [cbroken](#note15c) mine [deverlasting covenant](#note15d);

16 They [aseek](#note16a) not the Lord to establish his righteousness, but every man [bwalketh](#note16b) in his [cown](#note16c) [dway](#note16d), and after the [eimage](#note16e) of his own god, whose image is in the likeness of the world, and whose substance is that of an idol, which waxeth [fold](#note16f) and shall perish in [gBabylon](#note16g), even Babylon the great, which shall fall.

17 Wherefore, I the Lord, [aknowing](#note17a) the calamity which should come upon the [binhabitants](#note17b) of the earth, [ccalled upon](#note17c) my [dservant](#note17d) Joseph Smith, Jun., and [espake](#note17e) unto him from heaven, and gave him [fcommandments](#note17f);

18 And also gave commandments to others, that they should proclaim these things unto the world; and all this that it might be fulfilled, which was written by the prophets—

19 The [aweak](#note19a) things of the world shall come forth and break down the mighty and strong ones, that man [bshould not](#note19b) counsel his fellow man, neither [ctrust](#note19c) in the arm of flesh—

20 But that every man might [aspeak](#note20a) in the name of God the Lord, even the Savior of the world;

21 That faith also might increase in the earth;

22 That mine everlasting [acovenant](#note22a) might be established;

23 That the [afulness](#note23a) of my [bgospel](#note23b) might be [cproclaimed](#note23c) by the [dweak](#note23d) and the simple unto the ends of the world, and before [ekings](#note23e) and [frulers](#note23f).

24 Behold, I am God and have spoken it; these [acommandments](#note24a) are of me, and were given unto my servants in their weakness, after the manner of their [blanguage](#note24b), that they might come to [cunderstanding](#note24c).

25 And inasmuch as they [aerred](#note25a) it might be made known;

26 And inasmuch as they sought [awisdom](#note26a) they might be [binstructed](#note26b);

27 And inasmuch as they sinned they might be [achastened](#note27a), that they might [brepent](#note27b);

28 And inasmuch as they were [ahumble](#note28a) they might be made strong, and blessed from on high, and receive [bknowledge](#note28b) from time to time.

29 And after having received the record of the Nephites, yea, even my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., might have power to [atranslate](#note29a) through the [bmercy](#note29b) of God, by the power of God, the [cBook of Mormon](#note29c).

30 And also those to whom these commandments were given, might have [apower](#note30a) to lay the foundation of this [bchurch](#note30b), and to bring it forth out of obscurity and out of [cdarkness](#note30c), the only true and living [dchurch](#note30d) upon the face of the whole earth, with which I, the Lord, am well [epleased](#note30e), [fspeaking](#note30f) unto the church collectively and not individually—

31 For I the Lord cannot look upon [asin](#note31a) with the least degree of allowance;

32 Nevertheless, he that [arepents](#note32a) and does the [bcommandments](#note32b) of the Lord shall be [cforgiven](#note32c);

33 And he that [arepents](#note33a) not, from him shall be [btaken](#note33b) even the light which he has received; for my [cSpirit](#note33c) shall not always [dstrive](#note33d) with man, saith the Lord of Hosts.

34 And again, verily I say unto you, O inhabitants of the earth: I the Lord am willing to make these things [aknown](#note34a) unto [ball](#note34b) flesh;

35 For I am no [arespecter](#note35a) of persons, and will that all men shall know that the [bday](#note35b) speedily cometh; the hour is not yet, but is nigh at hand, when [cpeace](#note35c) shall be taken from the earth, and the [ddevil](#note35d) shall have power over his own dominion.

36 And also the Lord shall have [apower](#note36a) over his [bsaints](#note36b), and shall [creign](#note36c) in their [dmidst](#note36d), and shall come down in [ejudgment](#note36e) upon [fIdumea](#note36f), or the world.

37 [aSearch](#note37a) these [bcommandments](#note37b), for they are true and [cfaithful](#note37c), and the prophecies and [dpromises](#note37d) which are in them shall all be fulfilled.

38 What I the Lord have spoken, I have spoken, and I excuse not myself; and though the heavens and the earth pass away, my [aword](#note38a) shall not pass away, but shall all be [bfulfilled](#note38b), whether by mine own [cvoice](#note38c) or by the [dvoice](#note38d) of my [eservants](#note38e), it is the [fsame](#note38f).

39 For behold, and lo, the Lord is God, and the [aSpirit](#note39a) beareth record, and the record is true, and the [btruth](#note39b) abideth forever and ever. Amen.

Section 2

An extract from Joseph Smith’s history relating the words of the angel Moroni to Joseph Smith the Prophet, while in the house of the Prophet’s father at Manchester, New York, on the evening of September 21, 1823. Moroni was the last of a long line of historians who had made the record that is now before the world as the Book of Mormon. (Compare Malachi 4:5–6; also [sections 27:9](27.xhtml#p9); [110:13–16](110.xhtml#p13); and [128:18](128.xhtml#p18).)

[1](2.xhtml#p1), Elijah is to reveal the priesthood; [2–3](2.xhtml#p2), The promises of the fathers are planted in the hearts of the children.

1 Behold, I will reveal unto you the Priesthood, by the hand of [aElijah](#note1a) the prophet, before the coming of the great and [bdreadful](#note1b) day of the Lord.

2 And [ahe](#note2a) shall plant in the hearts of the children the [bpromises](#note2b) made to the fathers, and the hearts of the children shall turn to their fathers.

3 If it were not so, the whole [aearth](#note3a) would be utterly wasted at his coming.

Section 3

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Harmony, Pennsylvania, July 1828, relating to the loss of 116 pages of manuscript translated from the first part of the Book of Mormon, which was called the book of Lehi. The Prophet had reluctantly allowed these pages to pass from his custody to that of Martin Harris, who had served for a brief period as scribe in the translation of the Book of Mormon. The revelation was given through the Urim and Thummim. (See [section 10](10.xhtml).)

[1–4](3.xhtml#p1), The Lord’s course is one eternal round; [5–15](3.xhtml#p5), Joseph Smith must repent or lose the gift to translate; [16–20](3.xhtml#p16), The Book of Mormon comes forth to save the seed of Lehi.

1 The [aworks](#note1a), and the designs, and the purposes of God cannot be [bfrustrated](#note1b), neither can they come to naught.

2 For God doth not [awalk](#note2a) in crooked paths, neither doth he [bturn](#note2b) to the right hand nor to the left, neither doth he vary from that which he hath said, therefore his paths are straight, and his [ccourse](#note2c) is one eternal round.

3 Remember, remember that it is not the [awork](#note3a) of God that is frustrated, but the work of men;

4 For although a man may have many [arevelations](#note4a), and have [bpower](#note4b) to do many mighty works, yet if he [cboasts](#note4c) in his own [dstrength](#note4d), and sets at naught the [ecounsels](#note4e) of God, and follows after the dictates of his own will and [fcarnal](#note4f) desires, he must fall and incur the [gvengeance](#note4g) of a [hjust](#note4h) God upon him.

5 Behold, you have been [aentrusted](#note5a) with these things, but how strict were your commandments; and remember also the promises which were made to you, if you did not transgress them.

6 And behold, how [aoft](#note6a) you have [btransgressed](#note6b) the commandments and the laws of God, and have gone on in the [cpersuasions](#note6c) of men.

7 For, behold, you should not have [afeared](#note7a) man more than God. Although men set at naught the counsels of God, and [bdespise](#note7b) his words—

8 Yet you should have been faithful; and he would have extended his arm and [asupported](#note8a) you against all the fiery [bdarts](#note8b) of the [cadversary](#note8c); and he would have been with you in every time of [dtrouble](#note8d).

9 Behold, thou art Joseph, and thou wast chosen to do the work of the Lord, but because of transgression, if thou art not aware thou wilt [afall](#note9a).

10 But remember, God is merciful; therefore, repent of that which thou hast done which is contrary to the commandment which I gave you, and thou art still chosen, and art [aagain](#note10a) called to the work;

11 Except thou [ado](#note11a) this, thou shalt be delivered up and become as other men, and have no more gift.

12 And when thou deliveredst up that which God had given thee sight and power to [atranslate](#note12a), thou deliveredst up that which was [bsacred](#note12b) into the hands of a wicked [cman](#note12c),

13 Who has set at naught the counsels of God, and has broken the most sacred promises which were made before God, and has depended upon his own judgment and [aboasted](#note13a) in his own wisdom.

14 And this is the reason that thou hast lost thy privileges for a season—

15 For thou hast suffered the counsel of thy [adirector](#note15a) to be trampled upon from the beginning.

16 Nevertheless, my [awork](#note16a) shall go forth, for inasmuch as the knowledge of a Savior has come unto the world, through the [btestimony](#note16b) of the Jews, even so shall the [cknowledge](#note16c) of a [dSavior](#note16d) come unto my people—

17 And to the [aNephites](#note17a), and the Jacobites, and the Josephites, and the Zoramites, through the testimony of their fathers—

18 And this [atestimony](#note18a) shall come to the knowledge of the [bLamanites](#note18b), and the Lemuelites, and the Ishmaelites, who [cdwindled](#note18c) in unbelief because of the [diniquity](#note18d) of their fathers, whom the Lord has suffered to destroy their [ebrethren](#note18e) the Nephites, because of their [finiquities](#note18f) and their abominations.

19 And for this very [apurpose](#note19a) are these [bplates](#note19b) [cpreserved](#note19c), which contain these records—that the [dpromises](#note19d) of the Lord might be fulfilled, which he made to his [epeople](#note19e);

20 And that the [aLamanites](#note20a) might come to the knowledge of their fathers, and that they might know the [bpromises](#note20b) of the Lord, and that they may [cbelieve](#note20c) the gospel and [drely](#note20d) upon the merits of Jesus Christ, and be [eglorified](#note20e) through faith in his name, and that through their repentance they might be saved. Amen.

Section 4

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to his father, Joseph Smith Sr., at Harmony, Pennsylvania, February 1829.

[1–4](4.xhtml#p1), Valiant service saves the Lord’s ministers; [5–6](4.xhtml#p5), Godly attributes qualify them for the ministry; [7](4.xhtml#p7), The things of God must be sought after.

1 Now behold, a [amarvelous work](#note1a) is about to come forth among the children of men.

2 Therefore, O ye that embark in the [aservice](#note2a) of God, see that ye [bserve](#note2b) him with all your heart, might, mind and strength, that ye may stand [cblameless](#note2c) before God at the last day.

3 Therefore, if ye have desires to serve God ye are [acalled](#note3a) to the work;

4 For behold the [afield](#note4a) is white already to [bharvest](#note4b); and lo, he that thrusteth in his sickle with his might, the same layeth up in [cstore](#note4c) that he perisheth not, but bringeth salvation to his soul;

5 And [afaith](#note5a), [bhope](#note5b), [ccharity](#note5c) and [dlove](#note5d), with an [eeye](#note5e) single to the [fglory](#note5f) of God, [gqualify](#note5g) him for the work.

6 Remember faith, [avirtue](#note6a), knowledge, [btemperance](#note6b), [cpatience](#note6c), [dbrotherly](#note6d) [ekindness](#note6e), [fgodliness](#note6f), charity, [ghumility](#note6g), [hdiligence](#note6h).

7 [aAsk](#note7a), and ye shall receive; [bknock](#note7b), and it shall be opened unto you. Amen.

Section 5

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Harmony, Pennsylvania, March 1829, at the request of Martin Harris.

[1–10](5.xhtml#p1), This generation will receive the Lord’s word through Joseph Smith; [11–18](5.xhtml#p11), Three witnesses will testify of the Book of Mormon; [19–20](5.xhtml#p19), The word of the Lord will be verified as in previous times; [21–35](5.xhtml#p21), Martin Harris may repent and be one of the witnesses.

1 Behold, I say unto you, that as my servant [aMartin Harris](#note1a) has desired a witness at my hand, that you, my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., have got the plates of which you have testified and borne record that you have received of me;

2 And now, behold, this shall you say unto him—he who spake unto you, said unto you: I, the Lord, am God, and have given these things unto you, my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., and have commanded you that you should stand as a [awitness](#note2a) of these things;

3 And I have caused you that you should enter into a [acovenant](#note3a) with me, that you should not [bshow](#note3b) them except to those [cpersons](#note3c) to whom I commanded you; and you have no [dpower](#note3d) over them except I grant it unto you.

4 And you have a gift to [atranslate](#note4a) the plates; and this is the first gift that I bestowed upon you; and I have commanded that you should pretend to no other gift until my purpose is fulfilled in this; for I will grant unto you no other gift until it is finished.

5 Verily, I say unto you, that [awoe](#note5a) shall come unto the inhabitants of the earth if they will not [bhearken](#note5b) unto my words;

6 For hereafter you shall be [aordained](#note6a) and go forth and deliver my [bwords](#note6b) unto the children of men.

7 Behold, if they will not [abelieve](#note7a) my words, they would not believe you, my servant Joseph, if it were possible that you should show them all these things which I have committed unto you.

8 Oh, this [aunbelieving](#note8a) and [bstiffnecked](#note8b) generation—mine [canger](#note8c) is kindled against them.

9 Behold, verily I say unto you, I have [areserved](#note9a) those things which I have entrusted unto you, my servant Joseph, for a wise purpose in me, and it shall be made known unto future generations;

10 But this generation shall have my word [athrough](#note10a) you;

11 And in addition to your testimony, the [atestimony](#note11a) of three of my servants, whom I shall call and ordain, unto whom I will show these things, and they shall go forth with my words that are given through you.

12 Yea, they shall know of a [asurety](#note12a) that these things are true, for from heaven will I declare it unto them.

13 I will give them power that they may behold and view these things as they are;

14 And to [anone](#note14a) else will I grant this power, to receive this same testimony among this generation, in this the beginning of the rising up and the coming forth of my [bchurch](#note14b) out of the wilderness—clear as the [cmoon](#note14c), and fair as the sun, and terrible as an army with banners.

15 And the testimony of three [awitnesses](#note15a) will I send forth of my word.

16 And behold, whosoever [abelieveth](#note16a) on my words, them will I [bvisit](#note16b) with the [cmanifestation](#note16c) of my [dSpirit](#note16d); and they shall be [eborn](#note16e) of me, even of water and of the Spirit—

17 And you must wait yet a little while, for ye are not yet [aordained](#note17a)—

18 And their testimony shall also go forth unto the [acondemnation](#note18a) of this generation if they [bharden](#note18b) their hearts against them;

19 For a desolating [ascourge](#note19a) shall go forth among the inhabitants of the earth, and shall continue to be poured out from time to time, if they [brepent not](#note19b), until the earth is [cempty](#note19c), and the inhabitants thereof are [dconsumed](#note19d) away and utterly destroyed by the brightness of my [ecoming](#note19e).

20 Behold, I tell you these things, even as I also [atold](#note20a) the people of the destruction of Jerusalem; and my [bword](#note20b) shall be verified at this time as it hath hitherto been verified.

21 And now I command you, my servant Joseph, to [arepent](#note21a) and [bwalk](#note21b) more uprightly before me, and to yield to the [cpersuasions](#note21c) of men no more;

22 And that you be [afirm](#note22a) in [bkeeping](#note22b) the commandments wherewith I have commanded you; and if you do this, behold I grant unto you [ceternal life](#note22c), even if you should be [dslain](#note22d).

23 And now, again, I speak unto you, my servant Joseph, concerning the [aman](#note23a) that desires the witness—

24 Behold, I say unto him, he exalts himself and does not [ahumble](#note24a) himself sufficiently before me; but if he will [bbow](#note24b) down before me, and humble himself in mighty [cprayer](#note24c) and faith, in the [dsincerity](#note24d) of his heart, then will I grant unto him a [eview](#note24e) of the things which he desires to see.

25 And then he shall say unto the people of this generation: Behold, I have seen the things which the Lord hath shown unto Joseph Smith, Jun., and I [aknow](#note25a) of a surety that they are true, for I have seen them, for they have been shown unto me by the power of God and not of man.

26 And I the Lord command him, my servant Martin Harris, that he shall say no more unto them concerning these things, except he shall say: I have seen them, and they have been shown unto me by the power of God; and these are the words which he shall say.

27 But if he deny this he will break the [acovenant](#note27a) which he has before covenanted with me, and behold, he is condemned.

28 And now, except he humble himself and acknowledge unto me the things that he has done which are wrong, and covenant with me that he will keep my commandments, and exercise [afaith](#note28a) in me, behold, I say unto him, he shall have no such views, for I will grant unto him no views of the things of which I have spoken.

29 And if this be the case, I command you, my servant Joseph, that you shall say unto him, that he shall do no more, nor trouble me any more concerning this matter.

30 And if this be the case, behold, I say unto thee Joseph, when thou hast translated a few more pages thou shalt stop for a season, even until I command thee again; then thou mayest translate again.

31 And except thou do this, behold, thou shalt have no more gift, and I will take away the things which I have entrusted with thee.

32 And now, because I foresee the lying in wait to destroy thee, yea, I foresee that if my servant Martin Harris humbleth not himself and receive a witness from my hand, that he will fall into [atransgression](#note32a);

33 And there are many that lie in wait to [adestroy](#note33a) thee from off the face of the earth; and for this cause, that thy days may be [bprolonged](#note33b), I have given unto thee these commandments.

34 Yea, for this cause I have said: Stop, and [astand](#note34a) still until I command thee, and I will [bprovide](#note34b) means whereby thou mayest accomplish the thing which I have commanded thee.

35 And if thou art [afaithful](#note35a) in keeping my commandments, thou shalt be [blifted up](#note35b) at the last day. Amen.

Section 6

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet and Oliver Cowdery, at Harmony, Pennsylvania, April 1829. Oliver Cowdery began his labors as scribe in the translation of the Book of Mormon, April 7, 1829. He had already received a divine manifestation of the truth of the Prophet’s testimony respecting the plates on which was engraved the Book of Mormon record. The Prophet inquired of the Lord through the Urim and Thummim and received this response.

[1–6](6.xhtml#p1), Laborers in the Lord’s field gain salvation; [7–13](6.xhtml#p7), There is no gift greater than the gift of salvation; [14–27](6.xhtml#p14), A witness of the truth comes by the power of the Spirit; [28–37](6.xhtml#p28), Look unto Christ, and do good continually.

1 A great and [amarvelous work](#note1a) is about to come forth unto the children of men.

2 Behold, I am God; give heed unto my [aword](#note2a), which is quick and powerful, [bsharper](#note2b) than a two-edged sword, to the dividing asunder of both joints and marrow; therefore give heed unto my words.

3 Behold, the [afield](#note3a) is white already to harvest; therefore, whoso desireth to reap, let him thrust in his sickle with his might, and reap while the day [blasts](#note3b), that he may [ctreasure](#note3c) up for his soul everlasting salvation in the kingdom of God.

4 Yea, whosoever will thrust in his sickle and reap, the same is [acalled](#note4a) of God.

5 Therefore, if you will [aask](#note5a) of me you shall receive; if you will knock it shall be opened unto you.

6 Now, as you have asked, behold, I say unto you, keep my commandments, and [aseek](#note6a) to bring forth and establish the cause of [bZion](#note6b);

7 [aSeek not](#note7a) for [briches](#note7b) but for [cwisdom](#note7c), and behold, the [dmysteries](#note7d) of God shall be unfolded unto you, and then shall you be made [erich](#note7e). Behold, he that hath [feternal life](#note7f) is rich.

8 Verily, verily, I say unto you, even as you [adesire](#note8a) of me so it shall be unto you; and if you desire, you shall be the means of doing much [bgood](#note8b) in this generation.

9 Say nothing but [arepentance](#note9a) unto this generation; keep my commandments, and assist to bring forth my work, according to my commandments, and you shall be blessed.

10 Behold thou hast a gift, and blessed art thou because of thy [agift](#note10a). Remember it is [bsacred](#note10b) and cometh from above—

11 And if thou wilt [ainquire](#note11a), thou shalt know [bmysteries](#note11b) which are great and marvelous; therefore thou shalt exercise thy [cgift](#note11c), that thou mayest find out mysteries, that thou mayest bring [dmany](#note11d) to the knowledge of the truth, yea, [econvince](#note11e) them of the error of their ways.

12 Make not thy gift known unto any save it be those who are of thy faith. Trifle not with [asacred](#note12a) things.

13 If thou wilt do [agood](#note13a), yea, and [bhold](#note13b) out [cfaithful](#note13c) to the [dend](#note13d), thou shalt be saved in the [ekingdom](#note13e) of God, which is the greatest of all the [fgifts](#note13f) of God; for there is no gift greater than the gift of [gsalvation](#note13g).

14 Verily, verily, I say unto thee, blessed art thou for what thou hast done; for thou hast [ainquired](#note14a) of me, and behold, as often as thou hast inquired thou hast received instruction of my Spirit. If it had not been so, thou wouldst not have come to the place where thou art at this time.

15 Behold, thou knowest that thou hast inquired of me and I did enlighten thy [amind](#note15a); and now I tell thee these things that thou mayest know that thou hast been [benlightened](#note15b) by the [cSpirit of truth](#note15c);

16 Yea, I tell thee, that thou mayest know that there is none else save God that [aknowest](#note16a) thy thoughts and the [bintents](#note16b) of thy [cheart](#note16c).

17 I tell thee these things as a witness unto thee—that the words or the work which thou hast been writing are [atrue](#note17a).

18 Therefore be diligent; [astand](#note18a) by my [bservant](#note18b) Joseph, faithfully, in whatsoever difficult circumstances he may be for the word’s sake.

19 Admonish him in his faults, and also [areceive](#note19a) admonition of him. [bBe](#note19b) patient; be sober; be temperate; have patience, faith, hope and charity.

20 Behold, thou art Oliver, and I have spoken unto thee because of thy desires; therefore [atreasure](#note20a) up these words in thy heart. Be faithful and [bdiligent](#note20b) in keeping the commandments of God, and I will encircle thee in the arms of my [clove](#note20c).

21 Behold, I am Jesus Christ, the [aSon of God](#note21a). I am the same that came unto mine [bown](#note21b), and mine own received me not. I am the [clight](#note21c) which shineth in [ddarkness](#note21d), and the darkness comprehendeth it not.

22 Verily, verily, I say unto you, if you desire a further witness, cast your mind upon the night that you cried unto me in your heart, that you might [aknow](#note22a) concerning the truth of these things.

23 Did I not speak [apeace](#note23a) to your mind concerning the matter? What greater [bwitness](#note23b) can you have than from God?

24 And now, behold, you have received a [awitness](#note24a); for if I have [btold](#note24b) you things which no man knoweth have you not received a witness?

25 And, behold, I grant unto you a gift, if you desire of me, to [atranslate](#note25a), even as my servant Joseph.

26 Verily, verily, I say unto you, that there are [arecords](#note26a) which contain much of my gospel, which have been kept back because of the [bwickedness](#note26b) of the people;

27 And now I command you, that if you have good desires—a desire to lay up [atreasures](#note27a) for yourself in heaven—then shall you assist in bringing to light, with your gift, those parts of my [bscriptures](#note27b) which have been hidden because of iniquity.

28 And now, behold, I give unto you, and also unto my servant Joseph, the [akeys](#note28a) of this gift, which shall bring to light this ministry; and in the mouth of two or three [bwitnesses](#note28b) shall every word be established.

29 Verily, verily, I say unto you, if they [areject](#note29a) my words, and this part of my gospel and ministry, blessed are ye, for they can do no more unto you than unto me.

30 And even if they [ado](#note30a) unto you even as they have done unto me, blessed are ye, for you shall [bdwell](#note30b) with me in [cglory](#note30c).

31 But if they [areject](#note31a) not my words, which shall be established by the [btestimony](#note31b) which shall be given, blessed are they, and then shall ye have joy in the fruit of your labors.

32 Verily, verily, I say unto you, as I said unto my disciples, where two or three are gathered together in my name, as [atouching](#note32a) [bone](#note32b) thing, behold, there will I be in the [cmidst](#note32c) of them—even so am I in the [dmidst](#note32d) of you.

33 [aFear not](#note33a) to do [bgood](#note33b), my sons, for whatsoever ye [csow](#note33c), that shall ye also reap; therefore, if ye sow [dgood](#note33d) ye shall also reap good for your [ereward](#note33e).

34 Therefore, fear not, little [aflock](#note34a); do good; let earth and hell combine against you, for if ye are [bbuilt](#note34b) upon my rock, they cannot prevail.

35 Behold, I do not condemn you; go your ways and [asin](#note35a) no more; perform with soberness the work which I have commanded you.

36 [aLook](#note36a) unto me in every [bthought](#note36b); [cdoubt](#note36c) not, fear not.

37 [aBehold](#note37a) the wounds which pierced my side, and also the prints of the [bnails](#note37b) in my hands and feet; be faithful, keep my commandments, and ye shall [cinherit](#note37c) the [dkingdom](#note37d) of heaven. Amen.

Section 7

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet and Oliver Cowdery, at Harmony, Pennsylvania, April 1829, when they inquired through the Urim and Thummim as to whether John, the beloved disciple, tarried in the flesh or had died. The revelation is a translated version of the record made on parchment by John and hidden up by himself.

[1–3](7.xhtml#p1), John the Beloved will live until the Lord comes; [4–8](7.xhtml#p4), Peter, James, and John hold gospel keys.

1 And the Lord said unto me: [aJohn](#note1a), my beloved, what [bdesirest](#note1b) thou? For if you shall ask what you will, it shall be granted unto you.

2 And I said unto him: Lord, give unto me [apower](#note2a) over [bdeath](#note2b), that I may live and bring souls unto thee.

3 And the Lord said unto me: Verily, verily, I say unto thee, because thou desirest this thou shalt [atarry](#note3a) until I come in my [bglory](#note3b), and shalt [cprophesy](#note3c) before nations, kindreds, tongues and people.

4 And for this cause the Lord said unto Peter: If I will that he tarry till I come, what is that to thee? For he desired of me that he might bring [asouls](#note4a) unto me, but thou desiredst that thou mightest speedily come unto me in my [bkingdom](#note4b).

5 I say unto thee, Peter, this was a good desire; but my beloved has desired that he might do more, or a greater [awork](#note5a) yet among men than what he has before done.

6 Yea, he has undertaken a greater work; therefore I will make him as flaming fire and a [aministering](#note6a) angel; he shall minister for those who shall be [bheirs](#note6b) of salvation who dwell on the earth.

7 And I will make thee to minister for him and for thy brother James; and unto you three I will [agive](#note7a) this power and the [bkeys](#note7b) of this ministry until I come.

8 Verily I say unto you, ye shall both have according to your desires, for ye both [ajoy](#note8a) in that which ye have desired.

Section 8

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Oliver Cowdery, at Harmony, Pennsylvania, April 1829. In the course of the translation of the Book of Mormon, Oliver, who continued to serve as scribe, writing at the Prophet’s dictation, desired to be endowed with the gift of translation. The Lord responded to his supplication by granting this revelation.

[1–5](8.xhtml#p1), Revelation comes by the power of the Holy Ghost; [6–12](8.xhtml#p6), Knowledge of the mysteries of God and the power to translate ancient records come by faith.

1 [aOliver Cowdery](#note1a), verily, verily, I say unto you, that assuredly as the Lord liveth, who is your God and your Redeemer, even so surely shall you receive a [bknowledge](#note1b) of whatsoever things you shall [cask](#note1c) in faith, with an [dhonest](#note1d) heart, believing that you shall receive a [eknowledge](#note1e) concerning the engravings of old [frecords](#note1f), which are ancient, which contain those parts of my scripture of which has been spoken by the [gmanifestation](#note1g) of my Spirit.

2 Yea, behold, I will [atell](#note2a) you in your mind and in your [bheart](#note2b), by the [cHoly Ghost](#note2c), which shall come upon you and which shall dwell in your heart.

3 Now, behold, this is the spirit of revelation; behold, this is the spirit by which Moses [abrought](#note3a) the children of Israel through the Red Sea on dry ground.

4 Therefore this is thy [agift](#note4a); apply unto it, and blessed art thou, for it shall [bdeliver](#note4b) you out of the hands of your [cenemies](#note4c), when, if it were not so, they would slay you and bring your soul to destruction.

5 Oh, remember these [awords](#note5a), and keep my commandments. Remember, this is your gift.

6 Now this is not all thy [agift](#note6a); for you have another gift, which is the gift of Aaron; behold, it has told you many things;

7 Behold, there is no other power, save the power of God, that can cause this gift of Aaron to be with you.

8 Therefore, [adoubt](#note8a) not, for it is the gift of God; and you shall hold it in your hands, and do marvelous works; and no power shall be able to take it away out of your hands, for it is the [bwork](#note8b) of God.

9 And, therefore, whatsoever you shall ask me to tell you by that means, that will I grant unto you, and you shall have knowledge concerning it.

10 Remember that without [afaith](#note10a) you can do nothing; therefore ask in faith. Trifle not with these things; do not [bask](#note10b) for that which you ought not.

11 Ask that you may know the mysteries of God, and that you may [atranslate](#note11a) and receive knowledge from all those ancient records which have been hid up, that are [bsacred](#note11b); and according to your faith shall it be done unto you.

12 Behold, it is I that have spoken it; and I am the same that spake unto you from the beginning. Amen.

Section 9

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Oliver Cowdery, at Harmony, Pennsylvania, April 1829. Oliver is admonished to be patient and is urged to be content to write, for the time being, at the dictation of the translator, rather than to attempt to translate.

[1–6](9.xhtml#p1), Other ancient records are yet to be translated; [7–14](9.xhtml#p7), The Book of Mormon is translated by study and by spiritual confirmation.

1 Behold, I say unto you, my son, that because you did not [atranslate](#note1a) according to that which you desired of me, and did commence again to [bwrite](#note1b) for my servant, Joseph Smith, Jun., even so I would that ye should continue until you have finished this record, which I have entrusted unto him.

2 And then, behold, [aother](#note2a) [brecords](#note2b) have I, that I will give unto you power that you may assist to [ctranslate](#note2c).

3 Be patient, my son, for it is [awisdom](#note3a) in me, and it is not expedient that you should translate at this present time.

4 Behold, the work which you are called to do is to [awrite](#note4a) for my servant Joseph.

5 And, behold, it is because that you did not continue as you commenced, when you began to translate, that I have taken away this privilege from you.

6 Do not [amurmur](#note6a), my son, for it is wisdom in me that I have dealt with you after this manner.

7 Behold, you have not understood; you have supposed that I would give it unto you, when you took no thought save it was to ask me.

8 But, behold, I say unto you, that you must [astudy](#note8a) it out in your [bmind](#note8b); then you must [cask](#note8c) me if it be right, and if it is right I will cause that your [dbosom](#note8d) shall [eburn](#note8e) within you; therefore, you shall [ffeel](#note8f) that it is right.

9 But if it be not right you shall have no such feelings, but you shall have a [astupor](#note9a) of thought that shall cause you to forget the thing which is wrong; therefore, you cannot write that which is [bsacred](#note9b) save it be given you from me.

10 Now, if you had known this you could have [atranslated](#note10a); nevertheless, it is not expedient that you should translate now.

11 Behold, it was expedient when you commenced; but you [afeared](#note11a), and the time is past, and it is not expedient now;

12 For, do you not behold that I have [agiven](#note12a) unto my servant [bJoseph](#note12b) sufficient strength, whereby it is made up? And neither of you have I condemned.

13 Do this thing which I have commanded you, and you shall [aprosper](#note13a). Be faithful, and yield to no [btemptation](#note13b).

14 Stand fast in the [awork](#note14a) wherewith I have [bcalled](#note14b) you, and a hair of your head shall not be lost, and you shall be [clifted up](#note14c) at the last day. Amen.

Section 10

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Harmony, Pennsylvania, likely around April 1829, though portions may have been received as early as the summer of 1828. Herein the Lord informs Joseph of alterations made by wicked men in the 116 manuscript pages from the translation of the book of Lehi, in the Book of Mormon. These manuscript pages had been lost from the possession of Martin Harris, to whom the sheets had been temporarily entrusted. (See the [heading to section 3](3.xhtml).) The evil design was to await the expected retranslation of the matter covered by the stolen pages and then to discredit the translator by showing discrepancies created by the alterations. That this wicked purpose had been conceived by the evil one and was known to the Lord even while Mormon, the ancient Nephite historian, was making his abridgment of the accumulated plates, is shown in the Book of Mormon (see Words of Mormon 1:3–7).

[1–26](10.xhtml#p1), Satan stirs up wicked men to oppose the Lord’s work; [27–33](10.xhtml#p27), He seeks to destroy the souls of men; [34–52](10.xhtml#p34), The gospel is to go to the Lamanites and all nations through the Book of Mormon; [53–63](10.xhtml#p53), The Lord will establish His Church and His gospel among men; [64–70](10.xhtml#p64), He will gather the repentant into His Church and will save the obedient.

1 Now, behold, I say unto you, that because you [adelivered up](#note1a) those writings which you had power given unto you to translate by the means of the [bUrim and Thummim](#note1b), into the hands of a wicked man, you have lost them.

2 And you also lost your gift at the same time, and your [amind](#note2a) became [bdarkened](#note2b).

3 Nevertheless, it is now [arestored](#note3a) unto you again; therefore see that you are faithful and continue on unto the finishing of the remainder of the work of [btranslation](#note3b) as you have begun.

4 Do not run [afaster](#note4a) or labor more than you have [bstrength](#note4b) and means provided to enable you to translate; but be [cdiligent](#note4c) unto the end.

5 [aPray](#note5a) always, that you may come off [bconqueror](#note5b); yea, that you may conquer Satan, and that you may [cescape](#note5c) the hands of the servants of Satan that do uphold his work.

6 Behold, they have sought to [adestroy](#note6a) you; yea, even the [bman](#note6b) in whom you have trusted has sought to destroy you.

7 And for this cause I said that he is a wicked man, for he has sought to take away the things wherewith you have been entrusted; and he has also sought to destroy your gift.

8 And because you have delivered the writings into his hands, behold, wicked men have taken them from you.

9 Therefore, you have delivered them up, yea, that which was [asacred](#note9a), unto wickedness.

10 And, behold, [aSatan](#note10a) hath put it into their hearts to alter the words which you have caused to be [bwritten](#note10b), or which you have translated, which have gone out of your hands.

11 And behold, I say unto you, that because they have altered the words, they read contrary from that which you translated and caused to be written;

12 And, on this wise, the devil has sought to lay a cunning plan, that he may destroy this work;

13 For he hath put into their hearts to do this, that by lying they may say they have [acaught](#note13a) you in the words which you have pretended to translate.

14 Verily, I say unto you, that I will not suffer that Satan shall accomplish his [aevil](#note14a) design in this thing.

15 For behold, he has put it into their [ahearts](#note15a) to get thee to [btempt](#note15b) the Lord thy God, in asking to translate it over again.

16 And then, behold, they say and think in their hearts—We will see if God has given him power to translate; if so, he will also give him power again;

17 And if God giveth him power again, or if he translates again, or, in other words, if he bringeth forth the same words, behold, we have the same with us, and we have altered them;

18 Therefore they will not agree, and we will say that he has lied in his words, and that he has no [agift](#note18a), and that he has no power;

19 Therefore we will destroy him, and also the work; and we will do this that we may not be ashamed in the end, and that we may get [aglory](#note19a) of the world.

20 Verily, verily, I say unto you, that [aSatan](#note20a) has great hold upon their hearts; he stirreth them up to [biniquity](#note20b) against that which is good;

21 And their hearts are [acorrupt](#note21a), and [bfull](#note21b) of wickedness and abominations; and they [clove](#note21c) [ddarkness](#note21d) rather than light, because their [edeeds](#note21e) are evil; therefore they will not ask of me.

22 [aSatan](#note22a) stirreth them up, that he may [blead](#note22b) their souls to destruction.

23 And thus he has laid a cunning plan, thinking to [adestroy](#note23a) the work of God; but I will [brequire](#note23b) this at their hands, and it shall turn to their shame and condemnation in the day of [cjudgment](#note23c).

24 Yea, he stirreth up their hearts to [aanger](#note24a) against this work.

25 Yea, he saith unto them: [aDeceive](#note25a) and lie in wait to catch, that ye may destroy; behold, this is no harm. And thus he flattereth them, and telleth them that it is no sin to [blie](#note25b) that they may catch a man in a lie, that they may destroy him.

26 And thus he [aflattereth](#note26a) them, and leadeth them along until he draggeth their souls down to [bhell](#note26b); and thus he causeth them to catch themselves in their own [csnare](#note26c).

27 And thus he goeth up and down, [ato](#note27a) and fro in the earth, seeking to [bdestroy](#note27b) the souls of men.

28 Verily, verily, I say unto you, wo be unto him that [alieth](#note28a) to [bdeceive](#note28b) because he supposeth that another lieth to deceive, for such are not exempt from the [cjustice](#note28c) of God.

29 Now, behold, they have altered these words, because Satan saith unto them: He hath deceived you—and thus he [aflattereth](#note29a) them away to do iniquity, to get thee to [btempt](#note29b) the Lord thy God.

30 Behold, I say unto you, that you shall not translate again those words which have gone forth out of your hands;

31 For, behold, they shall not accomplish their evil designs in lying against those words. For, behold, if you should bring forth the same words they will say that you have lied and that you have pretended to translate, but that you have contradicted yourself.

32 And, behold, they will publish this, and Satan will [aharden](#note32a) the hearts of the people to stir them up to anger against you, that they will not believe my words.

33 Thus [aSatan](#note33a) thinketh to overpower your [btestimony](#note33b) in this generation, that the work may not come forth in this generation.

34 But behold, here is wisdom, and because I show unto you wisdom, and give you commandments concerning these things, what you shall do, show it not unto the world until you have accomplished the work of translation.

35 Marvel not that I said unto you: Here is [awisdom](#note35a), show it not unto the world—for I said, show it not unto the world, that you may be preserved.

36 Behold, I do not say that you shall not show it unto the righteous;

37 But as you cannot always judge the [arighteous](#note37a), or as you cannot always tell the wicked from the righteous, therefore I say unto you, hold your [bpeace](#note37b) until I shall see fit to make all things known unto the world concerning the matter.

38 And now, verily I say unto you, that an account of those things that you have written, which have gone out of your hands, is engraven upon the [aplates of Nephi](#note38a);

39 Yea, and you remember it was said in those writings that a more particular account was given of these things upon the plates of Nephi.

40 And now, because the account which is engraven upon the plates of Nephi is more particular concerning the things which, in my wisdom, I [awould](#note40a) bring to the knowledge of the people in this account—

41 Therefore, you shall translate the engravings which are on the plates of Nephi, down even till you come to the reign of king Benjamin, or until you come to that which you have translated, which you have retained;

42 And behold, you shall publish it as the record of Nephi; and thus I will [aconfound](#note42a) those who have altered my words.

43 I will not suffer that they shall destroy my [awork](#note43a); yea, I will show unto them that my [bwisdom](#note43b) is greater than the cunning of the devil.

44 Behold, they have only got a part, or an [aabridgment](#note44a) of the account of Nephi.

45 Behold, there are many things engraven upon the [aplates of Nephi](#note45a) which do throw greater views upon my gospel; therefore, it is wisdom in me that you should [btranslate](#note45b) this first part of the engravings of Nephi, and send forth in this work.

46 And, behold, all the remainder of this work does contain all those parts of my [agospel](#note46a) which my holy prophets, yea, and also my disciples, [bdesired](#note46b) in their prayers should come forth unto this people.

47 And I said unto them, that it should be [agranted](#note47a) unto them according to their [bfaith](#note47b) in their prayers;

48 Yea, and this was their faith—that my gospel, which I gave unto them that they might preach in their days, might come unto their brethren the [aLamanites](#note48a), and also all that had become Lamanites because of their dissensions.

49 Now, this is not all—their faith in their prayers was that this gospel should be made known also, if it were possible that other nations should possess this land;

50 And thus they did leave a blessing upon this land in their prayers, that whosoever should believe in this [agospel](#note50a) in this land might have eternal life;

51 Yea, that it might be [afree](#note51a) unto all of whatsoever nation, kindred, tongue, or people they may be.

52 And now, behold, according to their faith in their prayers will I bring this part of my gospel to the knowledge of my people. Behold, I do not bring it to [adestroy](#note52a) that which they have received, but to build it up.

53 And for this cause have I said: If this generation [aharden](#note53a) not their hearts, I will establish my [bchurch](#note53b) among them.

54 Now I do not say this to destroy my church, but I say this to build up my church;

55 Therefore, whosoever belongeth to my church need not [afear](#note55a), for such shall [binherit](#note55b) the [ckingdom](#note55c) of heaven.

56 But it is they who do not [afear](#note56a) me, neither keep my commandments but build up [bchurches](#note56b) unto themselves to get [cgain](#note56c), yea, and all those that do wickedly and build up the kingdom of the devil—yea, verily, verily, I say unto you, that it is they that I will disturb, and cause to tremble and shake to the center.

57 Behold, I am Jesus Christ, the [aSon of God](#note57a). I came unto mine own, and mine own [breceived me not](#note57b).

58 I am the [alight](#note58a) which shineth in darkness, and the darkness comprehendeth it not.

59 I am he who said—[aOther](#note59a) [bsheep](#note59b) have I which are not of this fold—unto my disciples, and many there were that [cunderstood](#note59c) me not.

60 And I will show unto this people that I had other [asheep](#note60a), and that they were a [bbranch](#note60b) of the house of [cJacob](#note60c);

61 And I will bring to light their marvelous works, which they did in my name;

62 Yea, and I will also bring to light my gospel which was ministered unto them, and, behold, they shall not deny that which you have received, but they shall build it up, and shall bring to light the true points of my [adoctrine](#note62a), yea, and the only doctrine which is in me.

63 And this I do that I may establish my gospel, that there may not be so much [acontention](#note63a); yea, [bSatan](#note63b) doth [cstir up](#note63c) the hearts of the people to [dcontention](#note63d) concerning the points of my doctrine; and in these things they do err, for they do [ewrest](#note63e) the scriptures and do not understand them.

64 Therefore, I will unfold unto them this great mystery;

65 For, behold, I will [agather](#note65a) them as a hen gathereth her chickens under her wings, if they will not harden their hearts;

66 Yea, if they will come, they may, and partake of the [awaters of life](#note66a) freely.

67 Behold, this is my doctrine—whosoever repenteth and [acometh](#note67a) unto me, the same is my [bchurch](#note67b).

68 Whosoever [adeclareth](#note68a) more or less than this, the same is not of me, but is [bagainst](#note68b) me; therefore he is not of my church.

69 And now, behold, whosoever is of my church, and [aendureth](#note69a) of my church to the end, him will I establish upon my [brock](#note69b), and the [cgates](#note69c) of hell shall not prevail against them.

70 And now, remember the words of him who is the life and [alight](#note70a) of the [bworld](#note70b), your Redeemer, your [cLord](#note70c) and your God. Amen.

Section 11

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to his brother Hyrum Smith, at Harmony, Pennsylvania, May 1829. This revelation was received through the Urim and Thummim in answer to Joseph’s supplication and inquiry. Joseph Smith’s history suggests that this revelation was received after the restoration of the Aaronic Priesthood.

[1–6](11.xhtml#p1), Laborers in the vineyard will gain salvation; [7–14](11.xhtml#p7), Seek wisdom, cry repentance, trust in the Spirit; [15–22](11.xhtml#p15), Keep the commandments, and study the Lord’s word; [23–27](11.xhtml#p23), Deny not the spirit of revelation and of prophecy; [28–30](11.xhtml#p28), Those who receive Christ become the sons of God.

1 [aA](#note1a) great and marvelous work is about to come forth among the children of men.

2 Behold, I am God; give [aheed](#note2a) to my [bword](#note2b), which is quick and [cpowerful](#note2c), [dsharper](#note2d) than a two-edged sword, to the dividing asunder of both joints and marrow; therefore give [eheed](#note2e) unto my word.

3 Behold, the field is [awhite](#note3a) already to harvest; therefore, whoso desireth to reap let him thrust in his sickle with his [bmight](#note3b), and reap while the day lasts, that he may [ctreasure](#note3c) up for his soul [deverlasting](#note3d) salvation in the kingdom of God.

4 Yea, whosoever will thrust in his sickle and [areap](#note4a), the same is [bcalled](#note4b) of God.

5 Therefore, if you will ask of me you shall receive; if you will [aknock](#note5a) it shall be opened unto you.

6 Now, as you have asked, behold, I say unto you, keep my commandments, and seek to bring forth and establish the cause of [aZion](#note6a).

7 Seek not for [ariches](#note7a) but for [bwisdom](#note7b); and, behold, the mysteries of God shall be unfolded unto you, and then shall you be made [crich](#note7c). Behold, he that hath eternal life is rich.

8 Verily, verily, I say unto you, even as you desire of me so it shall be done unto you; and, if you desire, you shall be the means of doing much good in this generation.

9 [aSay](#note9a) nothing but [brepentance](#note9b) unto this generation. Keep my commandments, and assist to bring forth my [cwork](#note9c), [daccording](#note9d) to my commandments, and you shall be blessed.

10 Behold, thou hast a [agift](#note10a), or thou shalt have a gift if thou wilt desire of me in faith, with an [bhonest](#note10b) heart, believing in the power of Jesus Christ, or in my power which speaketh unto thee;

11 For, behold, it is I that speak; behold, I am the [alight](#note11a) which shineth in darkness, and by my [bpower](#note11b) I give these words unto thee.

12 And now, verily, verily, I say unto thee, put your [atrust](#note12a) in that [bSpirit](#note12b) which [cleadeth](#note12c) to do [dgood](#note12d)—yea, to do [ejustly](#note12e), to walk [fhumbly](#note12f), to [gjudge](#note12g) righteously; and this is my Spirit.

13 Verily, verily, I say unto you, I will impart unto you of my Spirit, which shall [aenlighten](#note13a) your [bmind](#note13b), which shall fill your soul with [cjoy](#note13c);

14 And then shall ye know, or by this shall you know, all things whatsoever you desire of me, which are pertaining unto things of [arighteousness](#note14a), in faith believing in me that you shall receive.

15 Behold, I command you that you need not suppose that you are [acalled](#note15a) to [bpreach](#note15b) [cuntil](#note15c) you are called.

16 Wait a little longer, until you shall have my word, my [arock](#note16a), my [bchurch](#note16b), and my gospel, that you may know of a surety my doctrine.

17 And then, behold, according to your desires, yea, even according to your [afaith](#note17a) shall it be done unto you.

18 Keep my commandments; hold your peace; appeal unto my Spirit;

19 Yea, [acleave](#note19a) unto me with all your heart, that you may assist in bringing to light those things of which has been spoken—yea, the [btranslation](#note19b) of my work; be patient until you shall accomplish it.

20 Behold, this is your work, to [akeep](#note20a) my commandments, yea, with all your might, [bmind](#note20b) and strength.

21 Seek not to [adeclare](#note21a) my word, but first seek to [bobtain](#note21b) my [cword](#note21c), and then shall your tongue be loosed; then, if you desire, you shall have my [dSpirit](#note21d) and my word, yea, the power of God unto the [econvincing](#note21e) of men.

22 But now hold your [apeace](#note22a); study [bmy word](#note22b) which hath gone forth among the children of men, and also [cstudy](#note22c) my word which shall come forth among the children of men, or that which is [dnow translating](#note22d), yea, until you have obtained all which I shall [egrant](#note22e) unto the children of men in this generation, and then shall all things be added thereto.

23 Behold thou art [aHyrum](#note23a), my son; [bseek](#note23b) the kingdom of God, and all things shall be added according to that which is just.

24 [aBuild](#note24a) upon my [brock](#note24b), which is my [cgospel](#note24c);

25 Deny not the spirit of [arevelation](#note25a), nor the spirit of [bprophecy](#note25b), for wo unto him that [cdenieth](#note25c) these things;

26 Therefore, [atreasure](#note26a) up in your [bheart](#note26b) until the time which is in my wisdom that you shall go forth.

27 Behold, I speak unto [aall](#note27a) who have good desires, and have thrust in their sickle to reap.

28 Behold, I am Jesus Christ, the [aSon of God](#note28a). I am the life and the [blight](#note28b) of the world.

29 I am the same who came unto mine own and mine own [areceived me not](#note29a);

30 But verily, verily, I say unto you, that as many as receive me, to them will I give [apower](#note30a) to become the [bsons](#note30b) of God, even to them that [cbelieve](#note30c) on my name. Amen.

Section 12

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Joseph Knight Sr., at Harmony, Pennsylvania, May 1829. Joseph Knight believed the declarations of Joseph Smith concerning his possession of the Book of Mormon plates and the work of translation then in progress and several times had given material assistance to Joseph Smith and his scribe, which enabled them to continue translating. At Joseph Knight’s request, the Prophet inquired of the Lord and received the revelation.

[1–6](12.xhtml#p1), Laborers in the vineyard are to gain salvation; [7–9](12.xhtml#p7), All who desire and are qualified may assist in the Lord’s work.

1 [aA](#note1a) great and [bmarvelous work](#note1b) is about to come forth among the children of men.

2 Behold, I am God; give heed to my [aword](#note2a), which is quick and powerful, sharper than a two-edged sword, to the dividing asunder of both joints and marrow; therefore, give heed unto my word.

3 Behold, the field is [awhite](#note3a) already to harvest; therefore, whoso desireth to reap let him thrust in his sickle with his might, and reap while the day lasts, that he may treasure up for his soul everlasting salvation in the kingdom of God.

4 Yea, whosoever will thrust in his sickle and [areap](#note4a), the same is [bcalled](#note4b) of God.

5 Therefore, if you will ask of me you shall receive; if you will knock it shall be opened unto you.

6 Now, as you have asked, behold, I say unto you, keep my commandments, and seek to bring forth and establish the cause of [aZion](#note6a).

7 Behold, I speak unto you, and also to all those who have desires to bring forth and establish this work;

8 And no one can assist in this work except he shall be [ahumble](#note8a) and full of [blove](#note8b), having faith, hope, and charity, being temperate in all things, whatsoever shall be [centrusted](#note8c) to his care.

9 Behold, I am the light and the life of the world, that speak these words, therefore give heed with your might, and then you are called. Amen.

Section 13

An extract from Joseph Smith’s history recounting the ordination of the Prophet and Oliver Cowdery to the Aaronic Priesthood near Harmony, Pennsylvania, May 15, 1829. The ordination was done by the hands of an angel who announced himself as John, the same that is called John the Baptist in the New Testament. The angel explained that he was acting under the direction of Peter, James, and John, the ancient Apostles, who held the keys of the higher priesthood, which was called the Priesthood of Melchizedek. The promise was given to Joseph and Oliver that in due time this higher priesthood would be conferred upon them. (See [section 27:7–8, 12](27.xhtml#p7).)

The keys and powers of the Aaronic Priesthood are set forth.

1 Upon you my fellow servants, in the name of Messiah I [aconfer](#note1a) the [bPriesthood of Aaron](#note1b), which holds the [ckeys](#note1c) of the ministering of [dangels](#note1d), and of the gospel of [erepentance](#note1e), and of [fbaptism](#note1f) by immersion for the remission of sins; and this shall never be taken again from the earth, until the [gsons of Levi](#note1g) do offer again an offering unto the Lord in [hrighteousness](#note1h).

Section 14

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to David Whitmer, at Fayette, New York, June 1829. The Whitmer family had become greatly interested in the translating of the Book of Mormon. The Prophet established his residence at the home of Peter Whitmer Sr., where he dwelt until the work of translation was carried to completion and the copyright on the forthcoming book was secured. Three of the Whitmer sons, each having received a testimony as to the genuineness of the work, became deeply concerned over the matter of their individual duty. This revelation and the two following ([sections 15](15.xhtml) and [16](16.xhtml)) were given in answer to an inquiry through the Urim and Thummim. David Whitmer later became one of the Three Witnesses to the Book of Mormon.

[1–6](14.xhtml#p1), Laborers in the vineyard will gain salvation; [7–8](14.xhtml#p7), Eternal life is the greatest of God’s gifts; [9–11](14.xhtml#p9), Christ created the heavens and the earth.

1 [aA](#note1a) great and marvelous work is about to come forth unto the children of men.

2 Behold, I am God; give heed to my word, which is quick and powerful, sharper than a two-edged sword, to the dividing asunder of both joints and marrow; therefore give heed unto my word.

3 Behold, the field is white already to harvest; therefore, whoso desireth to reap let him thrust in his sickle with his might, and reap while the day lasts, that he may treasure up for his soul everlasting salvation in the kingdom of God.

4 Yea, whosoever will thrust in his sickle and reap, the same is called of God.

5 Therefore, if you will ask of me you shall receive; if you will [aknock](#note5a) it shall be opened unto you.

6 Seek to bring forth and establish my Zion. Keep my commandments in all things.

7 And, if you [akeep](#note7a) my commandments and [bendure](#note7b) to the end you shall have [ceternal life](#note7c), which gift is the greatest of all the gifts of God.

8 And it shall come to pass, that if you shall ask the Father in my name, in faith [abelieving](#note8a), you shall receive the [bHoly Ghost](#note8b), which [cgiveth](#note8c) utterance, that you may stand as a [dwitness](#note8d) of the things of which you shall both [ehear](#note8e) and see, and also that you may declare [frepentance](#note8f) unto this generation.

9 Behold, I am [aJesus Christ](#note9a), the [bSon](#note9b) of the [cliving God](#note9c), who [dcreated](#note9d) the heavens and the [eearth](#note9e), a [flight](#note9f) which cannot be hid in [gdarkness](#note9g);

10 Wherefore, I must bring forth the [afulness](#note10a) of my [bgospel](#note10b) from the [cGentiles](#note10c) unto the house of Israel.

11 And behold, thou art David, and thou art called to assist; which thing if ye do, and are faithful, ye shall be blessed both spiritually and temporally, and great shall be your reward. Amen.

Section 15

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to John Whitmer, at Fayette, New York, June 1829 (see the [heading to section 14](14.xhtml)). The message is intimately and impressively personal in that the Lord tells of what was known only to John Whitmer and Himself. John Whitmer later became one of the Eight Witnesses to the Book of Mormon.

[1–2](15.xhtml#p1), The Lord’s arm is over all the earth; [3–6](15.xhtml#p3), To preach the gospel and save souls is the thing of most worth.

1 Hearken, my servant John, and listen to the words of Jesus Christ, your Lord and your Redeemer.

2 For behold, I speak unto you with [asharpness](#note2a) and with power, for mine arm is over all the [bearth](#note2b).

3 And I will [atell](#note3a) you that which no man [bknoweth](#note3b) save me and thee alone—

4 For many times you have desired of me to know that which would be of the most worth unto you.

5 Behold, blessed are you for this thing, and for speaking my words which I have given you according to my commandments.

6 And now, behold, I say unto you, that the thing which will be of the most worth unto you will be to [adeclare](#note6a) [brepentance](#note6b) unto this people, that you may bring [csouls](#note6c) unto me, that you may [drest](#note6d) with them in the [ekingdom](#note6e) of my [fFather](#note6f). Amen.
