Section 16

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Peter Whitmer Jr., at Fayette, New York, June 1829 (see the [heading to section 14](14.xhtml)). Peter Whitmer Jr. later became one of the Eight Witnesses to the Book of Mormon.

[1–2](16.xhtml#p1), The Lord’s arm is over all the earth; [3–6](16.xhtml#p3), To preach the gospel and save souls is the thing of most worth.

1 [aHearken](#note1a), my servant Peter, and listen to the words of Jesus Christ, your Lord and your Redeemer.

2 For behold, I speak unto you with sharpness and with power, for mine arm is over all the earth.

3 And I will tell you that which no man knoweth save me and thee alone—

4 For many times you have desired of me to know that which would be of the most worth unto you.

5 Behold, blessed are you for this thing, and for speaking my words which I have given unto you according to my commandments.

6 And now, behold, I say unto you, that the thing which will be of the most worth unto you will be to declare repentance unto this people, that you may bring souls unto me, that you may rest with them in the kingdom of my Father. Amen.

* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1a)

  1a

  See [D&C 15](15.xhtml) for similar concepts and cross-references.

Section 17

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Oliver Cowdery, David Whitmer, and Martin Harris, at Fayette, New York, June 1829, prior to their viewing the engraved plates that contained the Book of Mormon record. Joseph and his scribe, Oliver Cowdery, had learned from the translation of the Book of Mormon plates that three special witnesses would be designated (see Ether 5:2–4; 2 Nephi 11:3; 27:12). Oliver Cowdery, David Whitmer, and Martin Harris were moved upon by an inspired desire to be the three special witnesses. The Prophet inquired of the Lord, and this revelation was given in answer through the Urim and Thummim.

[1–4](17.xhtml#p1), By faith the Three Witnesses will see the plates and other sacred items; [5–9](17.xhtml#p5), Christ bears testimony of the divinity of the Book of Mormon.

1 Behold, I say unto you, that you must rely upon my word, which if you do with full purpose of heart, you shall have a [aview](#note1a) of the [bplates](#note1b), and also of the [cbreastplate](#note1c), the [dsword](#note1d) of Laban, the [eUrim and Thummim](#note1e), which were given to the [fbrother of Jared](#note1f) upon the mount, when he talked with the Lord [gface to face](#note1g), and the [hmiraculous](#note1h) directors which were given to Lehi while in the wilderness, on the borders of the [iRed Sea](#note1i).

2 And it is by your faith that you shall obtain a view of them, even by that faith which was had by the prophets of old.

3 And after that you have obtained faith, and have seen them with your eyes, you shall [atestify](#note3a) of them, by the power of God;

4 And this you shall do that my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., may not be destroyed, that I may bring about my righteous purposes unto the children of men in this work.

5 And ye shall testify that you have seen them, even as my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., has seen them; for it is by my power that he has seen them, and it is because he had faith.

6 And he has translated the [abook](#note6a), even that [bpart](#note6b) which I have commanded him, and as your Lord and your God liveth it is true.

7 Wherefore, you have received the same power, and the same faith, and the same gift like unto him;

8 And if you do these last [acommandments](#note8a) of mine, which I have given you, the [bgates](#note8b) of hell shall not prevail against you; for my [cgrace](#note8c) is sufficient for you, and you shall be [dlifted up](#note8d) at the last day.

9 And I, Jesus Christ, your [aLord](#note9a) and your God, have spoken it unto you, that I might bring about my righteous purposes unto the children of men. Amen.

Section 18

Revelation to Joseph Smith the Prophet, Oliver Cowdery, and David Whitmer, given at Fayette, New York, June 1829. According to the Prophet, this revelation made known “the calling of twelve apostles in these last days, and also instructions relative to building up the Church.”

[1–5](18.xhtml#p1), Scriptures show how to build up the Church; [6–8](18.xhtml#p6), The world is ripening in iniquity; [9–16](18.xhtml#p9), The worth of souls is great; [17–25](18.xhtml#p17), To gain salvation, men must take upon themselves the name of Christ; [26–36](18.xhtml#p26), The calling and mission of the Twelve are revealed; [37–39](18.xhtml#p37), Oliver Cowdery and David Whitmer are to search out the Twelve; [40–47](18.xhtml#p40), To gain salvation, men must repent, be baptized, and keep the commandments.

1 Now, behold, because of the thing which you, my servant Oliver Cowdery, have desired to know of me, I give unto you these words:

2 Behold, I have [amanifested](#note2a) unto you, by my Spirit in many instances, that the [bthings](#note2b) which you have written are [ctrue](#note2c); wherefore you know that they are true.

3 And if you know that they are true, behold, I give unto you a commandment, that you [arely](#note3a) upon the things which are [bwritten](#note3b);

4 For in them are all things [awritten](#note4a) concerning the foundation of my church, my gospel, and my [brock](#note4b).

5 Wherefore, if you shall build up my [achurch](#note5a), upon the foundation of my gospel and my [brock](#note5b), the [cgates](#note5c) of hell shall not prevail against you.

6 Behold, the [aworld](#note6a) is [bripening](#note6b) in iniquity; and it must needs be that the children of men are stirred up unto repentance, both the [cGentiles](#note6c) and also the house of Israel.

7 Wherefore, as thou hast been [abaptized](#note7a) by the hands of my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., according to that which I have commanded him, he hath fulfilled the thing which I commanded him.

8 And now, marvel not that I have [acalled](#note8a) him unto mine own purpose, which purpose is known in me; wherefore, if he shall be [bdiligent](#note8b) in keeping my commandments he shall be [cblessed](#note8c) unto eternal life; and his name is [dJoseph](#note8d).

9 And now, Oliver Cowdery, I speak unto you, and also unto David Whitmer, by the way of commandment; for, behold, I [acommand](#note9a) all men everywhere to repent, and I speak unto you, even as unto Paul mine [bapostle](#note9b), for you are called even with that same calling with which he was called.

10 Remember the [aworth](#note10a) of [bsouls](#note10b) is great in the sight of God;

11 For, behold, the Lord your [aRedeemer](#note11a) suffered [bdeath](#note11b) in the flesh; wherefore he [csuffered](#note11c) the [dpain](#note11d) of all men, that all men might repent and [ecome](#note11e) unto him.

12 And he hath [arisen](#note12a) again from the dead, that he might bring all men unto him, on conditions of [brepentance](#note12b).

13 And how great is his [ajoy](#note13a) in the [bsoul](#note13b) that [crepenteth](#note13c)!

14 Wherefore, you are called to [acry](#note14a) repentance unto this people.

15 And if it so be that you should labor all your days in crying repentance unto this people, and bring, save it be one [asoul](#note15a) unto me, how great shall be your joy with him in the kingdom of my Father!

16 And now, if your joy will be great with one soul that you have brought unto me into the [akingdom](#note16a) of my Father, how great will be your [bjoy](#note16b) if you should bring many [csouls](#note16c) unto me!

17 Behold, you have my gospel before you, and my rock, and my [asalvation](#note17a).

18 [aAsk](#note18a) the Father in my [bname](#note18b) in faith, believing that you shall receive, and you shall have the Holy Ghost, which manifesteth all things which are [cexpedient](#note18c) unto the children of men.

19 And if you have not [afaith](#note19a), [bhope](#note19b), and [ccharity](#note19c), you can do nothing.

20 [aContend](#note20a) against no church, save it be the [bchurch of the devil](#note20b).

21 Take upon you the [aname](#note21a) of Christ, and [bspeak](#note21b) the truth in [csoberness](#note21c).

22 And as many as repent and are [abaptized](#note22a) in my name, which is Jesus Christ, and [bendure](#note22b) to the end, the same shall be saved.

23 Behold, Jesus Christ is the [aname](#note23a) which is given of the Father, and there is none other name given whereby man can be [bsaved](#note23b);

24 Wherefore, all men must take upon them the [aname](#note24a) which is given of the Father, for in that name shall they be called at the last day;

25 Wherefore, if they [aknow not](#note25a) the [bname](#note25b) by which they are called, they cannot have place in the [ckingdom](#note25c) of my Father.

26 And now, behold, there are others who are [acalled](#note26a) to declare my gospel, both unto [bGentile](#note26b) and unto Jew;

27 Yea, even twelve; and the [aTwelve](#note27a) shall be my disciples, and they shall take upon them my name; and the Twelve are they who shall desire to take upon them my [bname](#note27b) with full purpose of heart.

28 And if they desire to take upon them my name with full purpose of heart, they are called to go into all the [aworld](#note28a) to preach my [bgospel](#note28b) unto [cevery creature](#note28c).

29 And they are they who are ordained of me to [abaptize](#note29a) in my name, according to that which is written;

30 And you have that which is written before you; wherefore, you must perform it [aaccording](#note30a) to the words which are [bwritten](#note30b).

31 And now I speak unto you, the [aTwelve](#note31a)—Behold, my grace is sufficient for you; you must walk uprightly before me and sin not.

32 And, behold, you are they who are ordained of me to [aordain](#note32a) priests and teachers; to declare my gospel, [baccording](#note32b) to the power of the Holy Ghost which is in you, and according to the [ccallings](#note32c) and gifts of God unto men;

33 And I, Jesus Christ, your Lord and your God, have spoken it.

34 These [awords](#note34a) are not of men nor of man, but of me; wherefore, you shall testify they are of me and not of man;

35 For it is my [avoice](#note35a) which speaketh them unto you; for they are given by my Spirit unto you, and by my power you can read them one to another; and save it were by my power you could not have them;

36 Wherefore, you can [atestify](#note36a) that you have [bheard](#note36b) my voice, and know my words.

37 And now, behold, I give unto you, Oliver Cowdery, and also unto David Whitmer, that you shall search out the Twelve, who shall have the desires of which I have spoken;

38 And by their [adesires](#note38a) and their [bworks](#note38b) you shall know them.

39 And when you have found them you shall show these things unto them.

40 And you shall fall down and [aworship](#note40a) the Father in my [bname](#note40b).

41 And you must preach unto the world, saying: You must [arepent](#note41a) and be baptized, in the name of Jesus Christ;

42 For all men must repent and be baptized, and not only men, but women, and [achildren](#note42a) who have arrived at the years of [baccountability](#note42b).

43 And now, after that you have received this, you must keep my [acommandments](#note43a) in all things;

44 And by your hands I will work a [amarvelous work](#note44a) among the children of men, unto the [bconvincing](#note44b) of many of their sins, that they may come unto repentance, and that they may come unto the kingdom of my Father.

45 Wherefore, the blessings which I give unto you are [aabove](#note45a) all things.

46 And after that you have received this, if you [akeep](#note46a) not my commandments you cannot be saved in the kingdom of my Father.

47 Behold, I, Jesus Christ, your Lord and your God, and your [aRedeemer](#note47a), by the [bpower](#note47b) of my Spirit have spoken it. Amen.

  TG Jesus Christ, Power of.

Section 19

Revelation given through Joseph Smith, at Manchester, New York, likely in the summer of 1829. In his history, the Prophet introduces it as “a commandment of God and not of man, to Martin Harris, given by him who is Eternal.”

[1–3](19.xhtml#p1), Christ has all power; [4–5](19.xhtml#p4), All men must repent or suffer; [6–12](19.xhtml#p6), Eternal punishment is God’s punishment; [13–20](19.xhtml#p13), Christ suffered for all, that they might not suffer if they would repent; [21–28](19.xhtml#p21), Preach the gospel of repentance; [29–41](19.xhtml#p29), Declare glad tidings.

1 I am [aAlpha and Omega](#note1a), [bChrist](#note1b) the Lord; yea, even I am he, the beginning and the end, the Redeemer of the [cworld](#note1c).

2 I, having accomplished and [afinished](#note2a) the will of him whose I am, even the Father, concerning me—having done this that I might [bsubdue](#note2b) all things unto myself—

3 Retaining all [apower](#note3a), even to the [bdestroying](#note3b) of Satan and his works at the [cend](#note3c) of the world, and the last great day of judgment, which I shall pass upon the inhabitants thereof, [djudging](#note3d) every man according to his [eworks](#note3e) and the deeds which he hath done.

4 And surely every man must [arepent](#note4a) or [bsuffer](#note4b), for I, God, am [cendless](#note4c).

5 Wherefore, I [arevoke](#note5a) not the judgments which I shall pass, but woes shall go forth, weeping, [bwailing](#note5b) and gnashing of teeth, yea, to those who are found on my [cleft](#note5c) hand.

6 Nevertheless, it is [anot](#note6a) written that there shall be no end to this torment, but it is written *[bendless](#note6b) [ctorment](#note6c).*

7 Again, it is written *[aeternal damnation](#note7a);* wherefore it is more express than other scriptures, that it might work upon the hearts of the children of men, altogether for my name’s glory.

8 Wherefore, I will explain unto you this [amystery](#note8a), for it is meet unto you to know even as mine apostles.

9 I speak unto you that are chosen in this thing, even as one, that you may enter into my [arest](#note9a).

10 For, behold, the [amystery](#note10a) of godliness, how great is it! For, behold, I am [bendless](#note10b), and the punishment which is given from my hand is endless [cpunishment](#note10c), for [dEndless](#note10d) is my name. Wherefore—

11 [aEternal](#note11a) punishment is God’s punishment.

12 Endless punishment is God’s punishment.

13 Wherefore, I command you to repent, and keep the [acommandments](#note13a) which you have received by the hand of my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., in my name;

14 And it is by my almighty power that you have received them;

15 Therefore I command you to repent—repent, lest I [asmite](#note15a) you by the rod of my mouth, and by my wrath, and by my anger, and your [bsufferings](#note15b) be sore—how sore you know not, how exquisite you know not, yea, how hard to bear you know not.

16 For behold, I, God, have [asuffered](#note16a) these things for all, that they [bmight](#note16b) not suffer if they would [crepent](#note16c);

17 But if they would not repent they must [asuffer](#note17a) even as I;

18 Which [asuffering](#note18a) caused myself, even God, the greatest of all, to tremble because of pain, and to bleed at every pore, and to suffer both body and spirit—and would that I might [bnot](#note18b) drink the bitter cup, and shrink—

19 Nevertheless, glory be to the Father, and I partook and [afinished](#note19a) my preparations unto the children of men.

20 Wherefore, I command you again to repent, lest I [ahumble](#note20a) you with my almighty power; and that you [bconfess](#note20b) your sins, lest you suffer these [cpunishments](#note20c) of which I have spoken, of which in the smallest, yea, even in the least degree you have [dtasted](#note20d) at the time I withdrew my Spirit.

21 And I command you that you [apreach](#note21a) naught but repentance, and show [bnot](#note21b) these things unto the world until it is wisdom in me.

22 For they cannot [abear](#note22a) meat now, but [bmilk](#note22b) they must receive; wherefore, they must not know these things, lest they perish.

23 [aLearn](#note23a) of me, and listen to my words; [bwalk](#note23b) in the [cmeekness](#note23c) of my Spirit, and you shall have [dpeace](#note23d) in me.

24 I am Jesus Christ; I [acame](#note24a) by the [bwill](#note24b) of the Father, and I do his will.

25 And again, I command thee that thou shalt not [acovet](#note25a) thy [bneighbor’s](#note25b) [cwife](#note25c); nor seek thy neighbor’s life.

26 And again, I command thee that thou shalt not [acovet](#note26a) thine own property, but impart it freely to the printing of the Book of Mormon, which contains the [btruth](#note26b) and the word of God—

27 Which is my word to the [aGentile](#note27a), that soon it may go to the [bJew](#note27b), of whom the Lamanites are a [cremnant](#note27c), that they may believe the gospel, and look not for a [dMessiah](#note27d) to come who has already come.

28 And again, I command thee that thou shalt [apray](#note28a) [bvocally](#note28b) as well as in thy heart; yea, before the world as well as in secret, in public as well as in private.

29 And thou shalt [adeclare](#note29a) glad tidings, yea, [bpublish](#note29b) it upon the mountains, and upon every high place, and among every people that thou shalt be permitted to see.

30 And thou shalt do it with all humility, [atrusting](#note30a) in me, [breviling](#note30b) not against revilers.

31 And of [atenets](#note31a) thou shalt not talk, but thou shalt declare repentance and [bfaith](#note31b) on the Savior, and [cremission](#note31c) of sins by [dbaptism](#note31d), and by [efire](#note31e), yea, even the [fHoly Ghost](#note31f).

32 Behold, this is a great and the last [acommandment](#note32a) which I shall give unto you concerning this matter; for this shall suffice for thy daily walk, even unto the end of thy life.

33 And misery thou shalt receive if thou wilt slight these [acounsels](#note33a), yea, even the destruction of thyself and property.

34 [aImpart](#note34a) a portion of thy property, yea, even part of thy lands, and all save the support of thy [bfamily](#note34b).

35 Pay the [adebt](#note35a) thou hast [bcontracted](#note35b) with the printer. Release thyself from [cbondage](#note35c).

36 [aLeave](#note36a) thy house and home, except when thou shalt desire to see thy family;

37 And [aspeak](#note37a) freely to all; yea, preach, exhort, declare the [btruth](#note37b), even with a loud voice, with a sound of rejoicing, crying—Hosanna, hosanna, blessed be the name of the Lord God!

38 [aPray](#note38a) always, and I will [bpour out](#note38b) my Spirit upon you, and great shall be your blessing—yea, even more than if you should obtain [ctreasures](#note38c) of earth and corruptibleness to the extent thereof.

39 Behold, canst thou read this without [arejoicing](#note39a) and lifting up thy heart for [bgladness](#note39b)?

40 Or canst thou run about longer as a [ablind](#note40a) guide?

41 Or canst thou be [ahumble](#note41a) and meek, and conduct thyself wisely before me? Yea, [bcome](#note41b) unto me thy Savior. Amen.

Section 20

Revelation on Church organization and government, given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at or near Fayette, New York. Portions of this revelation may have been given as early as summer 1829. The complete revelation, known at the time as the Articles and Covenants, was likely recorded soon after April 6, 1830 (the day the Church was organized). The Prophet wrote, “We obtained of Him [Jesus Christ] the following, by the spirit of prophecy and revelation; which not only gave us much information, but also pointed out to us the precise day upon which, according to His will and commandment, we should proceed to organize His Church once more here upon the earth.”

[1–16](20.xhtml#p1), The Book of Mormon proves the divinity of the latter-day work; [17–28](20.xhtml#p17), The doctrines of creation, fall, atonement, and baptism are affirmed; [29–37](20.xhtml#p29), Laws governing repentance, justification, sanctification, and baptism are set forth; [38–67](20.xhtml#p38), Duties of elders, priests, teachers, and deacons are summarized; [68–74](20.xhtml#p68), Duties of members, blessing of children, and the mode of baptism are revealed; [75–84](20.xhtml#p75), Sacramental prayers and regulations governing Church membership are given.

1 The [arise](#note1a) of the [bChurch](#note1b) of Christ in these last days, being one thousand eight hundred and thirty years since the [ccoming](#note1c) of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ in the flesh, it being regularly [dorganized](#note1d) and established agreeable to the [elaws](#note1e) of our country, by the will and commandments of God, in the fourth month, and on the sixth day of the month which is called April—

2 Which commandments were given to Joseph Smith, Jun., who was [acalled](#note2a) of God, and [bordained](#note2b) an [capostle](#note2c) of Jesus Christ, to be the [dfirst](#note2d) [eelder](#note2e) of this church;

3 And to Oliver Cowdery, who was also called of God, an apostle of Jesus Christ, to be the [asecond](#note3a) elder of this church, and ordained under his hand;

4 And this according to the [agrace](#note4a) of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ, to whom be all glory, both now and forever. Amen.

5 After it was truly manifested unto this first elder that he had received a [aremission](#note5a) of his sins, he was [bentangled](#note5b) again in the [cvanities](#note5c) of the world;

6 But after [arepenting](#note6a), and humbling himself sincerely, through faith, God ministered unto him by an holy [bangel](#note6b), whose [ccountenance](#note6c) was as lightning, and whose garments were pure and white above all other whiteness;

7 And gave unto him [acommandments](#note7a) which inspired him;

8 And [agave](#note8a) him power from on high, by the [bmeans](#note8b) which were before prepared, to translate the Book of Mormon;

9 Which contains a [arecord](#note9a) of a fallen people, and the [bfulness](#note9b) of the [cgospel](#note9c) of Jesus Christ to the Gentiles and to the Jews also;

10 Which was given by inspiration, and is confirmed to [aothers](#note10a) by the ministering of angels, and is [bdeclared](#note10b) unto the world by them—

11 Proving to the world that the holy scriptures are [atrue](#note11a), and that God does [binspire](#note11b) men and call them to his [choly](#note11c) work in this age and generation, as well as in generations of old;

12 Thereby showing that he is the [asame](#note12a) God yesterday, today, and [bforever](#note12b). Amen.

13 Therefore, having so great witnesses, by [athem](#note13a) shall the world be judged, even as many as shall hereafter come to a knowledge of this work.

14 And those who receive it in faith, and work [arighteousness](#note14a), shall receive a [bcrown](#note14b) of eternal life;

15 But those who [aharden](#note15a) their hearts in [bunbelief](#note15b), and [creject](#note15c) it, it shall turn to their own [dcondemnation](#note15d)—

16 For the Lord God has spoken it; and we, the elders of the church, have heard and bear [awitness](#note16a) to the words of the glorious Majesty on high, to whom be glory forever and ever. Amen.

17 By these things we [aknow](#note17a) that there is a [bGod](#note17b) in heaven, who is infinite and [ceternal](#note17c), from everlasting to everlasting the same [dunchangeable](#note17d) God, the framer of heaven and earth, and all things which are in them;

18 And that he [acreated](#note18a) man, male and female, after his own [bimage](#note18b) and in his own likeness, created he them;

19 And gave unto them commandments that they should [alove](#note19a) and [bserve](#note19b) him, the only [cliving](#note19c) and true [dGod](#note19d), and that he should be the only being whom they should worship.

20 But by the [atransgression](#note20a) of these holy laws man became [bsensual](#note20b) and [cdevilish](#note20c), and became [dfallen](#note20d) man.

21 Wherefore, the Almighty God gave his [aOnly Begotten Son](#note21a), as it is written in those scriptures which have been given of him.

22 He [asuffered](#note22a) [btemptations](#note22b) but gave no heed unto them.

23 He was [acrucified](#note23a), [bdied](#note23b), and [crose](#note23c) again the third day;

24 And [aascended](#note24a) into heaven, to sit down on the right hand of the [bFather](#note24b), to [creign](#note24c) with almighty power according to the will of the Father;

25 That as many as would [abelieve](#note25a) and be baptized in his holy name, and [bendure](#note25b) in faith to the end, should be saved—

26 Not only those who believed after he came in the [ameridian of time](#note26a), in the [bflesh](#note26b), but all those from the beginning, even as many as were before he came, who believed in the words of the holy prophets, who [cspake](#note26c) as they were inspired by the [dgift](#note26d) of the Holy Ghost, who truly [etestified](#note26e) of him in all things, should have eternal life,

27 As well as those who should come after, who should believe in the [agifts](#note27a) and callings of God by the Holy Ghost, which [bbeareth](#note27b) record of the Father and of the Son;

28 Which Father, Son, and Holy Ghost are [aone God](#note28a), infinite and eternal, without end. Amen.

29 And we know that all men must [arepent](#note29a) and [bbelieve](#note29b) on the name of Jesus Christ, and worship the Father in his name, and [cendure](#note29c) in [dfaith](#note29d) on his name to the end, or they cannot be [esaved](#note29e) in the kingdom of God.

30 And we know that [ajustification](#note30a) through the [bgrace](#note30b) of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ is just and true;

31 And we know also, that [asanctification](#note31a) through the grace of our Lord and Savior Jesus Christ is just and true, to all those who [blove](#note31b) and serve God with all their [cmights](#note31c), minds, and strength.

32 But there is a possibility that man may [afall](#note32a) from [bgrace](#note32b) and depart from the living God;

33 Therefore let the church take heed and pray always, lest they fall into [atemptation](#note33a);

34 Yea, and even let those who are [asanctified](#note34a) take heed also.

35 And we know that these things are true and according to the revelations of John, neither [aadding](#note35a) to, nor diminishing from the prophecy of his book, the holy scriptures, or the revelations of God which shall come hereafter by the gift and power of the Holy Ghost, the [bvoice](#note35b) of God, or the ministering of angels.

36 And the Lord God has [aspoken](#note36a) it; and honor, power and glory be rendered to his holy [bname](#note36b), both now and ever. Amen.

37 *And again, by way of commandment to the church concerning the manner of baptism*—All those who [ahumble](#note37a) themselves before God, and desire to be baptized, and come forth with broken hearts and [bcontrite spirits](#note37b), and witness before the church that they have truly repented of all their sins, and are willing to take upon them the [cname](#note37c) of Jesus Christ, having a [ddetermination](#note37d) to serve him to the end, and truly manifest by their [eworks](#note37e) that they have received of the [fSpirit of Christ](#note37f) unto the [gremission](#note37g) of their sins, shall be received by baptism into his church.

38 *The [aduty](#note38a) of the elders, priests, teachers, deacons, and members of the church of Christ*—An [bapostle](#note38b) is an [celder](#note38c), and it is his calling to [dbaptize](#note38d);

39 And to [aordain](#note39a) other elders, priests, teachers, and deacons;

40 And to administer [abread and wine](#note40a)—the [bemblems](#note40b) of the flesh and blood of Christ—

41 And to [aconfirm](#note41a) those who are baptized into the church, by the laying on of [bhands](#note41b) for the baptism of fire and the Holy Ghost, according to the scriptures;

42 And to teach, expound, exhort, baptize, and watch over the church;

43 And to confirm the church by the laying on of the hands, and the giving of the Holy Ghost;

44 And to take the [alead](#note44a) of all meetings.

45 The elders are to [aconduct](#note45a) the [bmeetings](#note45b) as they are [cled](#note45c) by the Holy Ghost, according to the commandments and revelations of God.

46 The [apriest’s](#note46a) duty is to preach, [bteach](#note46b), expound, exhort, and baptize, and administer the sacrament,

47 And visit the house of each member, and exhort them to [apray](#note47a) [bvocally](#note47b) and in secret and attend to all [cfamily](#note47c) duties.

48 And he may also [aordain](#note48a) other priests, teachers, and deacons.

49 And he is to take the [alead](#note49a) of meetings when there is no elder present;

50 But when there is an elder present, he is only to preach, teach, expound, exhort, and baptize,

51 And visit the house of each member, exhorting them to pray vocally and in secret and attend to all family duties.

52 In all these duties the priest is to [aassist](#note52a) the elder if occasion requires.

53 The [ateacher’s](#note53a) duty is to [bwatch](#note53b) over the [cchurch](#note53c) always, and be with and strengthen them;

54 And see that there is no iniquity in the church, neither [ahardness](#note54a) with each other, neither lying, [bbackbiting](#note54b), nor [cevil](#note54c) [dspeaking](#note54d);

55 And see that the church meet together often, and also see that all the members do their duty.

56 And he is to take the lead of meetings in the absence of the elder or priest—

57 And is to be assisted always, in all his duties in the church, by the [adeacons](#note57a), if occasion requires.

58 But neither teachers nor deacons have authority to baptize, administer the [asacrament](#note58a), or lay on [bhands](#note58b);

59 They are, however, to warn, expound, exhort, and teach, and invite all to come unto Christ.

60 Every [aelder](#note60a), [bpriest](#note60b), teacher, or deacon is to be [cordained](#note60c) [daccording](#note60d) to the gifts and callings of God unto him; and he is to be [eordained](#note60e) by the power of the Holy Ghost, which is in the one who ordains him.

61 The several elders composing this church of Christ are to [ameet](#note61a) in conference once in three months, or from time to time as said conferences shall direct or appoint;

62 And said conferences are to do whatever church business is necessary to be done at the time.

63 The elders are to receive their licenses from other elders, by [avote](#note63a) of the church to which they belong, or from the conferences.

64 Each priest, teacher, or deacon, who is ordained by a priest, may take a certificate from him at the time, which [acertificate](#note64a), when presented to an elder, shall entitle him to a license, which shall authorize him to perform the duties of his calling, or he may receive it from a conference.

65 No person is to be [aordained](#note65a) to any office in this church, where there is a regularly organized branch of the same, without the [bvote](#note65b) of that church;

66 But the presiding elders, traveling bishops, high councilors, high priests, and elders, may have the privilege of ordaining, where there is no branch of the church that a vote may be called.

67 Every president of the high priesthood (or presiding elder), [abishop](#note67a), high councilor, and [bhigh priest](#note67b), is to be ordained by the direction of a [chigh council](#note67c) or general conference.

68 *The [aduty](#note68a) of the members after they are received by [bbaptism](#note68b)*—The elders or priests are to have a sufficient time to expound all things concerning the church of Christ to their [cunderstanding](#note68c), previous to their partaking of the [dsacrament](#note68d) and being confirmed by the laying on of the [ehands](#note68e) of the elders, so that all things may be done in [forder](#note68f).

69 And the members shall manifest before the church, and also before the elders, by a [agodly](#note69a) walk and conversation, that they are worthy of it, that there may be works and [bfaith](#note69b) agreeable to the holy scriptures—walking in [choliness](#note69c) before the Lord.

70 Every member of the church of Christ having [achildren](#note70a) is to bring them unto the elders before the church, who are to lay their [bhands](#note70b) upon them in the name of Jesus Christ, and bless them in his name.

71 No one can be received into the church of Christ unless he has arrived unto the years of [aaccountability](#note71a) before God, and is capable of [brepentance](#note71b).

72 [aBaptism](#note72a) is to be administered in the following manner unto all those who repent—

73 The person who is called of God and has authority from Jesus Christ to baptize, shall go down into the water with the person who has presented himself or herself for baptism, and shall say, calling him or her by name: Having been commissioned of Jesus Christ, I baptize you in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost. Amen.

74 Then shall he [aimmerse](#note74a) him or her in the water, and come forth again out of the water.

75 It is expedient that the church [ameet](#note75a) together often to [bpartake](#note75b) of [cbread and wine](#note75c) in the [dremembrance](#note75d) of the Lord Jesus;

76 And the elder or priest shall administer it; and after this [amanner](#note76a) shall he administer it—he shall kneel with the church and call upon the Father in solemn prayer, saying:

77 O God, the Eternal Father, we ask thee in the name of thy Son, Jesus Christ, to bless and sanctify this [abread](#note77a) to the souls of all those who partake of it, that they may eat in remembrance of the body of thy Son, and [bwitness](#note77b) unto thee, O God, the Eternal Father, that they are willing to take upon them the name of thy Son, and always remember him and keep his [ccommandments](#note77c) which he has given them; that they may always have his [dSpirit](#note77d) to be with them. Amen.

78 The [amanner](#note78a) of administering the wine—he shall take the [bcup](#note78b) also, and say:

79 O God, the Eternal Father, we ask thee in the name of thy Son, Jesus Christ, to bless and sanctify this [awine](#note79a) to the souls of all those who drink of it, that they may do it in remembrance of the blood of thy Son, which was shed for them; that they may witness unto thee, O God, the Eternal Father, that they do always remember him, that they may have his Spirit to be with them. Amen.

80 Any member of the church of Christ [atransgressing](#note80a), or being [bovertaken](#note80b) in a fault, shall be dealt with as the scriptures direct.

81 It shall be the duty of the several churches, composing the church of Christ, to send one or more of their teachers to attend the several conferences held by the elders of the church,

82 With a list of the [anames](#note82a) of the several members uniting themselves with the church since the last conference; or send by the hand of some priest; so that a regular list of all the names of the whole church may be kept in a book by one of the elders, whomsoever the other elders shall appoint from time to time;

83 And also, if any have been [aexpelled](#note83a) from the church, so that their names may be blotted out of the general church [brecord](#note83b) of names.

84 All members removing from the church where they reside, if going to a church where they are not known, may take a letter [acertifying](#note84a) that they are regular members and in good standing, which certificate may be signed by any elder or priest if the member receiving the letter is personally acquainted with the elder or priest, or it may be signed by the teachers or deacons of the church.

* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1a)

  1a

  [JS—H 1:2](../../pgp/js-h/1.xhtml#p2).
* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1b)

  b

  3 Ne. 27:3 (1–8); [D&C 1:1](1.xhtml#p1). TG Church.
* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1c)

Section 21

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Fayette, New York, April 6, 1830. This revelation was given at the organization of the Church, on the date named, in the home of Peter Whitmer Sr. Six men, who had previously been baptized, participated. By unanimous vote these persons expressed their desire and determination to organize, according to the commandment of God (see [section 20](20.xhtml)). They also voted to accept and sustain Joseph Smith Jr. and Oliver Cowdery as the presiding officers of the Church. With the laying on of hands, Joseph then ordained Oliver an elder of the Church, and Oliver similarly ordained Joseph. After administration of the sacrament, Joseph and Oliver laid hands upon the participants individually for the bestowal of the Holy Ghost and for the confirmation of each as a member of the Church.

[1–3](21.xhtml#p1), Joseph Smith is called to be a seer, translator, prophet, apostle, and elder; [4–8](21.xhtml#p4), His word will guide the cause of Zion; [9–12](21.xhtml#p9), The Saints will believe his words as he speaks by the Comforter.

1 Behold, there shall be a [arecord](#note1a) kept among you; and in it thou shalt be called a [bseer](#note1b), a translator, a prophet, an [capostle](#note1c) of Jesus Christ, an elder of the church through the will of God the Father, and the grace of your Lord Jesus Christ,

2 Being [ainspired](#note2a) of the Holy Ghost to lay the foundation thereof, and to [bbuild](#note2b) it up unto the most holy faith.

3 Which [achurch](#note3a) was [borganized](#note3b) and established in the year of your Lord eighteen hundred and thirty, in the fourth month, and on the sixth day of the month which is called April.

4 Wherefore, meaning the church, thou shalt give [aheed](#note4a) unto all his words and [bcommandments](#note4b) which he shall give unto you as he receiveth them, walking in all [choliness](#note4c) before me;

5 For his [aword](#note5a) ye shall receive, as if from mine own mouth, in all patience and faith.

6 For by doing these things the [agates](#note6a) of hell shall not prevail against you; yea, and the Lord God will disperse the powers of [bdarkness](#note6b) from before you, and cause the heavens to [cshake](#note6c) for your [dgood](#note6d), and his name’s [eglory](#note6e).

7 For thus saith the Lord God: Him have I inspired to move the cause of [aZion](#note7a) in mighty power for good, and his diligence I know, and his prayers I have heard.

8 Yea, his weeping for Zion I have seen, and I will cause that he shall mourn for her no longer; for his days of rejoicing are come unto the [aremission](#note8a) of his sins, and the manifestations of my blessings upon his works.

9 For, behold, I will [abless](#note9a) all those who labor in my vineyard with a mighty blessing, and they shall believe on his words, which are given him through me by the [bComforter](#note9b), which [cmanifesteth](#note9c) that Jesus was [dcrucified](#note9d) by [esinful](#note9e) men for the sins of the [fworld](#note9f), yea, for the remission of sins unto the [gcontrite heart](#note9g).

10 Wherefore it behooveth me that he should be [aordained](#note10a) by you, Oliver Cowdery mine apostle;

11 This being an ordinance unto you, that you are an elder under his hand, he being the [afirst](#note11a) unto you, that you might be an elder unto this church of Christ, bearing my name—

12 And the first [apreacher](#note12a) of this church unto the church, and before the world, yea, before the Gentiles; yea, and thus saith the Lord God, lo, lo! to the [bJews](#note12b) also. Amen.

Section 22

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Manchester, New York, April 16, 1830. This revelation was given to the Church in consequence of some who had previously been baptized desiring to unite with the Church without rebaptism.

[1](22.xhtml#p1), Baptism is a new and everlasting covenant; [2–4](22.xhtml#p2), Authoritative baptism is required.

1 Behold, I say unto you that all [aold](#note1a) covenants have I caused to be done away in this thing; and this is a [bnew](#note1b) and an everlasting [ccovenant](#note1c), even that which was from the beginning.

2 Wherefore, although a man should be baptized an hundred times it availeth him nothing, for you cannot enter in at the strait gate by the [alaw](#note2a) of Moses, neither by your [bdead works](#note2b).

3 For it is because of your dead works that I have caused this last covenant and this church to be built up unto me, even as in days of old.

4 Wherefore, enter ye in at the [agate](#note4a), as I have commanded, and [bseek](#note4b) not to counsel your God. Amen.

* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1c)

  c

  2a

Section 23

A series of five revelations given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Manchester, New York, April 1830, to Oliver Cowdery, Hyrum Smith, Samuel H. Smith, Joseph Smith Sr., and Joseph Knight Sr. As the result of earnest desire on the part of the five persons named to know of their respective duties, the Prophet inquired of the Lord and received a revelation for each person.

[1–7](23.xhtml#p1), These early disciples are called to preach, exhort, and strengthen the Church.

1 Behold, I speak unto you, Oliver, a few words. Behold, thou art blessed, and art under no condemnation. But beware of [apride](#note1a), lest thou shouldst enter into [btemptation](#note1b).

2 Make known thy calling unto the church, and also before the [aworld](#note2a), and thy heart shall be opened to preach the truth from henceforth and forever. Amen.

3 Behold, I speak unto you, Hyrum, a few words; for thou also art under no condemnation, and thy heart is opened, and thy tongue [aloosed](#note3a); and thy calling is to exhortation, and to [bstrengthen](#note3b) the church continually. Wherefore thy duty is unto the church forever, and this because of thy family. Amen.

4 Behold, I speak a few words unto you, [aSamuel](#note4a); for thou also art under no condemnation, and thy calling is to exhortation, and to strengthen the church; and thou art not as yet called to preach before the world. Amen.

5 Behold, I speak a few words unto you, Joseph; for thou also art under no [acondemnation](#note5a), and thy calling also is to exhortation, and to strengthen the church; and this is thy duty from henceforth and forever. Amen.

6 Behold, I manifest unto you, Joseph Knight, by these words, that you must take up your [across](#note6a), in the which you must [bpray](#note6b) [cvocally](#note6c) before the world as well as in secret, and in your family, and among your friends, and in all places.

7 And, behold, it is your duty to unite with the true [achurch](#note7a), and give your language to exhortation continually, that you may receive the reward of the [blaborer](#note7b). Amen.

Section 24

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet and Oliver Cowdery, at Harmony, Pennsylvania, July 1830. Though less than four months had elapsed since the Church was organized, persecution had become intense, and the leaders had to seek safety in partial seclusion. The following three revelations were given at this time to strengthen, encourage, and instruct them.

[1–9](24.xhtml#p1), Joseph Smith is called to translate, preach, and expound scriptures; [10–12](24.xhtml#p10), Oliver Cowdery is called to preach the gospel; [13–19](24.xhtml#p13), The law is revealed relative to miracles, cursings, casting off the dust of one’s feet, and going without purse or scrip.

1 Behold, thou wast called and chosen to [awrite](#note1a) the Book of Mormon, and to my ministry; and I have [blifted](#note1b) thee up out of thine afflictions, and have counseled thee, that thou hast been delivered from all thine enemies, and thou hast been [cdelivered](#note1c) from the powers of Satan and from [ddarkness](#note1d)!

2 Nevertheless, thou art not excusable in thy [atransgressions](#note2a); nevertheless, go thy way and sin no more.

3 [aMagnify](#note3a) thine office; and after thou hast [bsowed](#note3b) thy fields and secured them, go speedily unto the church which is in [cColesville](#note3c), Fayette, and Manchester, and they shall [dsupport](#note3d) thee; and I will bless them both spiritually and [etemporally](#note3e);

4 But if they receive thee not, I will send upon them a [acursing](#note4a) instead of a blessing.

5 And thou shalt continue in calling upon God in my name, and writing the things which shall be given thee by the [aComforter](#note5a), and expounding all scriptures unto the church.

6 And it shall be given thee in the very moment what thou shalt [aspeak](#note6a) and [bwrite](#note6b), and they shall hear it, or I will send unto them a cursing instead of a blessing.

7 For thou shalt devote all thy [aservice](#note7a) in Zion; and in this thou shalt have strength.

8 Be [apatient](#note8a) in [bafflictions](#note8b), for thou shalt have many; but [cendure](#note8c) them, for, lo, I am with thee, even unto the [dend](#note8d) of thy days.

9 And in temporal labors thou shalt not have strength, for this is not thy calling. Attend to thy [acalling](#note9a) and thou shalt have wherewith to magnify thine office, and to expound all scriptures, and continue in laying on of the hands and [bconfirming](#note9b) the churches.

10 And thy brother Oliver shall continue in bearing my name before the [aworld](#note10a), and also to the church. And he shall not suppose that he can say enough in my cause; and lo, I am with him to the end.

11 In me he shall have glory, and not of himself, whether in weakness or in strength, whether in [abonds](#note11a) or free;

12 And at all times, and in all places, he shall open his mouth and [adeclare](#note12a) my gospel as with the voice of a [btrump](#note12b), both day and night. And I will give unto him strength such as is not known among men.

13 Require not [amiracles](#note13a), except I shall [bcommand](#note13b) you, except [ccasting](#note13c) out [ddevils](#note13d), [ehealing](#note13e) the sick, and against [fpoisonous](#note13f) serpents, and against deadly poisons;

14 And these things ye shall not do, except it be required of you by them who [adesire](#note14a) it, that the scriptures might be [bfulfilled](#note14b); for ye shall do according to that which is written.

15 And in whatsoever place ye shall [aenter](#note15a), and they receive you not in my name, ye shall leave a cursing instead of a blessing, by casting off the [bdust](#note15b) of your feet against them as a testimony, and cleansing your feet by the wayside.

16 And it shall come to pass that whosoever shall lay their hands upon you by violence, ye shall command to be smitten in my name; and, behold, I will [asmite](#note16a) them according to your words, in mine own due time.

17 And whosoever shall go to law with thee shall be cursed by the law.

18 And thou shalt take no [apurse](#note18a) nor scrip, neither staves, neither two coats, for the church shall give unto thee in the very hour what thou needest for food and for raiment, and for shoes and for money, and for scrip.

19 For thou art called to [aprune](#note19a) my vineyard with a mighty pruning, yea, even for the last time; yea, and also all those whom thou hast [bordained](#note19b), and they shall do even according to this pattern. Amen.

Section 25

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Harmony, Pennsylvania, July 1830 (see the [heading to section 24](24.xhtml)). This revelation manifests the will of the Lord to Emma Smith, the Prophet’s wife.

[1–6](25.xhtml#p1), Emma Smith, an elect lady, is called to aid and comfort her husband; [7–11](25.xhtml#p7), She is also called to write, to expound scriptures, and to select hymns; [12–14](25.xhtml#p12), The song of the righteous is a prayer unto the Lord; [15–16](25.xhtml#p15), Principles of obedience in this revelation are applicable to all.

1 Hearken unto the voice of the Lord your God, while I speak unto you, Emma Smith, my daughter; for verily I say unto you, all those who [areceive](#note1a) my gospel are sons and daughters in my [bkingdom](#note1b).

2 A revelation I give unto you concerning my will; and if thou art faithful and [awalk](#note2a) in the paths of [bvirtue](#note2b) before me, I will preserve thy life, and thou shalt receive an [cinheritance](#note2c) in Zion.

3 Behold, thy [asins](#note3a) are forgiven thee, and thou art an [belect](#note3b) [clady](#note3c), whom I have [dcalled](#note3d).

4 [aMurmur](#note4a) not because of the [bthings](#note4b) which thou hast not seen, for they are [cwithheld](#note4c) from thee and from the world, which is wisdom in me in a time to come.

5 And the office of thy calling shall be for a [acomfort](#note5a) unto my servant, Joseph Smith, Jun., thy [bhusband](#note5b), in his [cafflictions](#note5c), with consoling words, in the spirit of [dmeekness](#note5d).

6 And thou shalt go with him at the time of his going, and be unto him for a scribe, while there is no one to be a scribe for him, that I may send my servant, Oliver Cowdery, whithersoever I will.

7 And thou shalt be [aordained](#note7a) under his hand to expound scriptures, and to exhort the church, according as it shall be given thee by my [bSpirit](#note7b).

8 For he shall lay his [ahands](#note8a) upon thee, and thou shalt receive the Holy Ghost, and thy time shall be given to writing, and to learning much.

9 And thou needest not fear, for thy [ahusband](#note9a) shall support thee in the church; for unto them is his [bcalling](#note9b), that all things might be [crevealed](#note9c) unto them, whatsoever I will, according to their faith.

10 And verily I say unto thee that thou shalt lay aside the [athings](#note10a) of this [bworld](#note10b), and [cseek](#note10c) for the things of a [dbetter](#note10d).

11 And it shall be given thee, also, to make a selection of [asacred](#note11a) [bhymns](#note11b), as it shall be given thee, which is pleasing unto me, to be had in my church.

12 For my soul [adelighteth](#note12a) in the [bsong](#note12b) of the [cheart](#note12c); yea, the [dsong](#note12d) of the righteous is a prayer unto me, and it shall be answered with a blessing upon their heads.

13 Wherefore, [alift up](#note13a) thy heart and [brejoice](#note13b), and cleave unto the covenants which thou hast made.

14 Continue in the spirit of meekness, and beware of [apride](#note14a). Let thy soul delight in thy [bhusband](#note14b), and the [cglory](#note14c) which shall come upon him.

15 Keep my commandments continually, and a [acrown](#note15a) of [brighteousness](#note15b) thou shalt receive. And except thou do this, where I am you [ccannot](#note15c) come.

16 And verily, verily, I say unto you, that this is my [avoice](#note16a) unto all. Amen.

Section 26

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, Oliver Cowdery, and John Whitmer, at Harmony, Pennsylvania, July 1830 (see the [heading to section 24](24.xhtml)).

[1](26.xhtml#p1), They are instructed to study the scriptures and to preach; [2](26.xhtml#p2), The law of common consent is affirmed.

1 Behold, I say unto you that you shall let your [atime](#note1a) be devoted to the [bstudying](#note1b) of the scriptures, and to preaching, and to confirming the church at [cColesville](#note1c), and to performing your [dlabors](#note1d) on the land, such as is required, until after you shall go to the west to hold the next conference; and then it shall be made [eknown](#note1e) what you shall do.

2 And all things shall be done by [acommon consent](#note2a) in the [bchurch](#note2b), by much prayer and faith, for all things you shall receive by faith. Amen.

* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1a)

  1a

  TG Time.
* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1b)

  b

  TG Scriptures, Study of; Study.

Section 27

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Harmony, Pennsylvania, August 1830. In preparation for a religious service at which the sacrament of bread and wine was to be administered, Joseph set out to procure wine. He was met by a heavenly messenger and received this revelation, a portion of which was written at the time and the remainder in the September following. Water is now used instead of wine in the sacramental services of the Church.

[1–4](27.xhtml#p1), The emblems to be used in partaking of the sacrament are set forth; [5–14](27.xhtml#p5), Christ and His servants from all dispensations are to partake of the sacrament; [15–18](27.xhtml#p15), Put on the whole armor of God.

1 Listen to the [avoice](#note1a) of Jesus Christ, your Lord, your God, and your Redeemer, whose word is [bquick](#note1b) and powerful.

2 For, behold, I say unto you, that it mattereth not what ye shall [aeat](#note2a) or what ye shall drink when ye partake of the sacrament, if it so be that ye do it with an eye single to my [bglory](#note2b)—[cremembering](#note2c) unto the Father my [dbody](#note2d) which was laid down for you, and my [eblood](#note2e) which was shed for the [fremission](#note2f) of your sins.

3 Wherefore, a commandment I give unto you, that you shall not purchase [awine](#note3a) neither strong drink of your enemies;

4 Wherefore, you shall partake of none except it is made [anew](#note4a) among you; yea, in this my Father’s kingdom which shall be built up on the earth.

5 Behold, this is wisdom in me; wherefore, marvel not, for the hour cometh that I will [adrink](#note5a) of the fruit of the [bvine](#note5b) with you on the earth, and with [cMoroni](#note5c), whom I have sent unto you to reveal the Book of Mormon, containing the fulness of my everlasting gospel, to whom I have committed the keys of the [drecord](#note5d) of the [estick](#note5e) of [fEphraim](#note5f);

6 And also with [aElias](#note6a), to whom I have committed the keys of bringing to pass the restoration of all things spoken by the mouth of all the holy prophets since the world began, concerning the last days;

7 And also John the son of Zacharias, which Zacharias he [a(Elias)](#note7a) visited and gave promise that he should have a son, and his name should be [bJohn](#note7b), and he should be filled with the spirit of Elias;

8 Which John I have sent unto you, my servants, Joseph Smith, Jun., and Oliver Cowdery, to ordain you unto the first [apriesthood](#note8a) which you have received, that you might be called and [bordained](#note8b) even as [cAaron](#note8c);

9 And also [aElijah](#note9a), unto whom I have committed the keys of the power of turning the hearts of the fathers to the children, and the hearts of the children to the [bfathers](#note9b), that the whole earth may not be smitten with a [ccurse](#note9c);

10 And also with Joseph and [aJacob](#note10a), and [bIsaac](#note10b), and Abraham, your [cfathers](#note10c), by whom the [dpromises](#note10d) remain;

11 And also with Michael, or [aAdam](#note11a), the father of all, the prince of all, the [bancient](#note11b) of days;

12 And also with Peter, and James, and John, whom I have sent unto you, by whom I have [aordained](#note12a) you and confirmed you to be [bapostles](#note12b), and especial [cwitnesses](#note12c) of my [dname](#note12d), and bear the keys of your ministry and of the same things which I revealed unto them;

13 Unto whom I have [acommitted](#note13a) the [bkeys](#note13b) of my kingdom, and a [cdispensation](#note13c) of the [dgospel](#note13d) for the [elast times](#note13e); and for the [ffulness](#note13f) of times, in the which I will gather together in [gone](#note13g) all things, both which are in heaven, and which are on earth;

14 And also with all those whom my Father hath [agiven](#note14a) me out of the world.

15 Wherefore, [alift up](#note15a) your hearts and [brejoice](#note15b), and [cgird](#note15c) up your loins, and take upon you my whole [darmor](#note15d), that ye may be able to withstand the evil day, having done all, that ye may be able to [estand](#note15e).

16 Stand, therefore, having your loins [agirt](#note16a) about with [btruth](#note16b), having on the [cbreastplate](#note16c) of [drighteousness](#note16d), and your feet shod with the preparation of the [egospel](#note16e) of [fpeace](#note16f), which I have sent mine [gangels](#note16g) to commit unto you;

17 Taking the shield of faith wherewith ye shall be able to quench all the [afiery](#note17a) darts of the wicked;

18 And take the helmet of salvation, and the sword of my [aSpirit](#note18a), which I will pour out upon you, and my word which I reveal unto you, and be agreed as touching all things whatsoever ye ask of me, and be faithful until I come, and ye shall be [bcaught up](#note18b), that where I am ye shall be [calso](#note18c). Amen.

Section 28

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Oliver Cowdery, at Fayette, New York, September 1830. Hiram Page, a member of the Church, had a certain stone and professed to be receiving revelations by its aid concerning the upbuilding of Zion and the order of the Church. Several members had been deceived by these claims, and even Oliver Cowdery was wrongly influenced thereby. Just prior to an appointed conference, the Prophet inquired earnestly of the Lord concerning the matter, and this revelation followed.

[1–7](28.xhtml#p1), Joseph Smith holds the keys of the mysteries, and only he receives revelations for the Church; [8–10](28.xhtml#p8), Oliver Cowdery is to preach to the Lamanites; [11–16](28.xhtml#p11), Satan deceived Hiram Page and gave him false revelations.

1 Behold, I say unto thee, [aOliver](#note1a), that it shall be given unto thee that thou shalt be heard by the church in all things whatsoever thou shalt teach them by the [bComforter](#note1b), concerning the revelations and commandments which I have given.

2 But, behold, verily, verily, I say unto thee, [ano one](#note2a) shall be appointed to receive commandments and [brevelations](#note2b) in this church excepting my servant [cJoseph Smith, Jun.](#note2c), for he receiveth them even as [dMoses](#note2d).

3 And thou shalt be obedient unto the things which I shall give unto him, even as [aAaron](#note3a), to [bdeclare](#note3b) faithfully the commandments and the revelations, with power and [cauthority](#note3c) unto the church.

4 And if thou art [aled](#note4a) at any time by the Comforter to [bspeak](#note4b) or teach, or at all times by the way of commandment unto the church, thou mayest do it.

5 But thou shalt not write by way of [acommandment](#note5a), but by wisdom;

6 And thou shalt not command him who is at thy head, and at the head of the church;

7 For I have given him the [akeys](#note7a) of the [bmysteries](#note7b), and the revelations which are sealed, until I shall appoint unto them another in his stead.

8 And now, behold, I say unto you that you shall go unto the [aLamanites](#note8a) and preach my [bgospel](#note8b) unto them; and inasmuch as they [creceive](#note8c) thy teachings thou shalt cause my [dchurch](#note8d) to be established among them; and thou shalt have revelations, but write them not by way of commandment.

9 And now, behold, I say unto you that it is not revealed, and no man knoweth where the [acity](#note9a) [bZion](#note9b) shall be built, but it shall be given hereafter. Behold, I say unto you that it shall be on the borders by the Lamanites.

10 Thou shalt not leave this place until after the conference; and my servant Joseph shall be appointed to preside over the conference by the voice of it, and what he saith to thee thou shalt tell.

11 And again, thou shalt take thy brother, Hiram Page, [abetween](#note11a) him and thee alone, and tell him that those things which he hath written from that [bstone](#note11b) are not of me and that [cSatan](#note11c) [ddeceiveth](#note11d) him;

12 For, behold, these things have not been appointed unto him, neither shall anything be appointed unto any of this church contrary to the church covenants.

13 For all things must be done in [aorder](#note13a), and by common [bconsent](#note13b) in the church, by the prayer of faith.

14 And thou shalt assist to settle all these things, according to the covenants of the church, before thou shalt take thy journey among the Lamanites.

15 And it shall be [agiven](#note15a) thee from the time thou shalt go, until the time thou shalt return, what thou shalt do.

16 And thou must open thy mouth at all times, declaring my gospel with the sound of rejoicing. Amen.

Section 29

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, in the presence of six elders, at Fayette, New York, September 1830. This revelation was given some days prior to the conference, beginning September 26, 1830.

[1–8](29.xhtml#p1), Christ gathers His elect; [9–11](29.xhtml#p9), His coming ushers in the Millennium; [12–13](29.xhtml#p12), The Twelve will judge all Israel; [14–21](29.xhtml#p14), Signs, plagues, and desolations will precede the Second Coming; [22–28](29.xhtml#p22), The last resurrection and final judgment follow the Millennium; [29–35](29.xhtml#p29), All things are spiritual unto the Lord; [36–39](29.xhtml#p36), The devil and his hosts were cast out of heaven to tempt man; [40–45](29.xhtml#p40), The Fall and Atonement bring salvation; [46–50](29.xhtml#p46), Little children are redeemed through the Atonement.

1 Listen to the voice of Jesus Christ, your Redeemer, the Great [aI Am](#note1a), whose arm of [bmercy](#note1b) hath [catoned](#note1c) for your sins;

2 Who will [agather](#note2a) his people even as a hen gathereth her chickens under her wings, even as many as will hearken to my voice and [bhumble](#note2b) themselves before me, and call upon me in mighty prayer.

3 Behold, verily, verily, I say unto you, that at this time your [asins](#note3a) are [bforgiven](#note3b) you, therefore ye receive these things; but remember to sin no more, lest perils shall come upon you.

4 Verily, I say unto you that ye are chosen out of the world to declare my gospel with the sound of rejoicing, as with the [avoice](#note4a) of a trump.

5 Lift up your hearts and be [aglad](#note5a), for I am in your [bmidst](#note5b), and am your [cadvocate](#note5c) with the Father; and it is his good will to give you the [dkingdom](#note5d).

6 And, as it is written—Whatsoever ye shall [aask](#note6a) in [bfaith](#note6b), being [cunited](#note6c) in prayer according to my command, ye shall receive.

7 And ye are called to bring to pass the [agathering](#note7a) of mine [belect](#note7b); for mine elect [chear](#note7c) my voice and [dharden](#note7d) not their [ehearts](#note7e);

8 Wherefore the decree hath gone forth from the Father that they shall be [agathered](#note8a) in unto one place upon the face of this land, to [bprepare](#note8b) their hearts and be prepared in all things against the day when [ctribulation](#note8c) and desolation are sent forth upon the wicked.

9 For the hour is nigh and the [aday](#note9a) soon at hand when the earth is ripe; and all the [bproud](#note9b) and they that do wickedly shall be as [cstubble](#note9c); and I will [dburn](#note9d) them up, saith the Lord of Hosts, that wickedness shall not be upon the earth;

10 For the hour is nigh, and that which was [aspoken](#note10a) by mine [bapostles](#note10b) must be fulfilled; for as they spoke so shall it come to pass;

11 For I will reveal [amyself](#note11a) from heaven with power and great glory, with all the [bhosts](#note11b) thereof, and [cdwell](#note11c) in [drighteousness](#note11d) with men on earth a [ethousand](#note11e) years, and the wicked shall not stand.

12 And again, verily, verily, I say unto you, and it hath gone forth in a firm decree, by the will of the Father, that mine [aapostles](#note12a), the Twelve which were with me in my ministry at Jerusalem, shall stand at my right hand at the day of my coming in a pillar of [bfire](#note12b), being clothed with robes of righteousness, with crowns upon their heads, in [cglory](#note12c) even as I am, to [djudge](#note12d) the whole house of Israel, even as many as have loved me and kept my commandments, and none else.

13 For a [atrump](#note13a) shall sound both long and loud, even as upon Mount Sinai, and all the [bearth](#note13b) shall quake, and they shall [ccome](#note13c) forth—yea, even the [ddead](#note13d) which died in me, to receive a [ecrown](#note13e) of righteousness, and to be clothed upon, [feven](#note13f) as I am, to be with me, that we may be one.

14 But, behold, I say unto you that before this great [aday](#note14a) shall come the [bsun](#note14b) shall be [cdarkened](#note14c), and the moon shall be turned into blood, and the stars shall fall from heaven, and there shall be greater [dsigns](#note14d) in heaven above and in the earth beneath;

15 And there shall be weeping and [awailing](#note15a) among the hosts of men;

16 And there shall be a great [ahailstorm](#note16a) sent forth to destroy the [bcrops](#note16b) of the earth.

17 And it shall come to pass, because of the wickedness of the world, that I will take [avengeance](#note17a) upon the [bwicked](#note17b), for they will not repent; for the [ccup](#note17c) of mine [dindignation](#note17d) is full; for behold, my [eblood](#note17e) shall not [fcleanse](#note17f) them if they hear me not.

18 Wherefore, I the Lord God will send forth [aflies](#note18a) upon the face of the earth, which shall take hold of the inhabitants thereof, and shall eat their flesh, and shall cause maggots to come in upon them;

19 And their tongues shall be stayed that they shall not [autter](#note19a) against me; and their flesh shall fall from off their bones, and their eyes from their sockets;

20 And it shall come to pass that the [abeasts](#note20a) of the forest and the fowls of the air shall devour them up.

21 And the great and [aabominable](#note21a) church, which is the [bwhore](#note21b) of all the earth, shall be cast down by [cdevouring fire](#note21c), according as it is spoken by the mouth of Ezekiel the prophet, who spoke of these things, which have not come to pass but surely [dmust](#note21d), as I live, for [eabominations](#note21e) shall not reign.

22 And again, verily, verily, I say unto you that when the [athousand](#note22a) years are ended, and men again begin to deny their God, then will I spare the earth but for a [blittle season](#note22b);

23 And the [aend](#note23a) shall come, and the heaven and the earth shall be consumed and [bpass away](#note23b), and there shall be a new heaven and a [cnew earth](#note23c).

24 For all [aold](#note24a) things shall [bpass away](#note24b), and all things shall become new, even the heaven and the earth, and all the fulness thereof, both men and [cbeasts](#note24c), the fowls of the air, and the fishes of the sea;

25 And not one [ahair](#note25a), neither mote, shall be lost, for it is the [bworkmanship](#note25b) of mine hand.

26 But, behold, verily I say unto you, before the earth shall pass away, [aMichael](#note26a), mine archangel, shall sound his [btrump](#note26b), and then shall all the dead [cawake](#note26c), for their graves shall be opened, and they shall [dcome](#note26d) forth—yea, even all.

27 And the [arighteous](#note27a) shall be gathered on my [bright hand](#note27b) unto eternal life; and the wicked on my left hand will I be ashamed to own before the Father;

28 Wherefore I will say unto them—[aDepart](#note28a) from me, ye cursed, into everlasting [bfire](#note28b), prepared for the [cdevil](#note28c) and his angels.

29 And now, behold, I say unto you, never at any time have I declared from mine own mouth that they should return, for [awhere](#note29a) I am they cannot come, for they have no power.

30 But remember that all my judgments are not given unto men; and as the words have gone forth out of my mouth even so shall they be fulfilled, that the [afirst](#note30a) shall be last, and that the last shall be first in all things whatsoever I have created by the word of my power, which is the power of my [bSpirit](#note30b).

31 For by the power of my Spirit [acreated](#note31a) I them; yea, all things both [bspiritual](#note31b) and temporal—

32 [aFirst](#note32a) [bspiritual](#note32b), secondly temporal, which is the beginning of my work; and again, first temporal, and secondly spiritual, which is the last of my work—

33 [aSpeaking](#note33a) unto you that you may naturally understand; but unto myself my works have no [bend](#note33b), neither beginning; but it is given unto you that ye may understand, because ye have asked it of me and are agreed.

34 Wherefore, verily I say unto you that all things unto me are spiritual, and not at any time have I given unto you a [alaw](#note34a) which was [btemporal](#note34b); neither any man, nor the children of men; neither Adam, your father, whom I created.

35 Behold, I gave unto him that he should be an [aagent](#note35a) unto himself; and I gave unto him commandment, but no temporal commandment gave I unto him, for my [bcommandments](#note35b) are [cspiritual](#note35c); they are not natural nor temporal, neither carnal nor sensual.

36 And it came to pass that Adam, being tempted of the [adevil](#note36a)—for, behold, the [bdevil](#note36b) was before Adam, for he [crebelled](#note36c) against me, saying, Give me thine [dhonor](#note36d), which is my [epower](#note36e); and also a [fthird](#note36f) part of the [ghosts](#note36g) of heaven turned he away from me because of their [hagency](#note36h);

37 And they were thrust down, and thus came the [adevil](#note37a) and his [bangels](#note37b);

38 And, behold, there is a place [aprepared](#note38a) for them from the beginning, which place is [bhell](#note38b).

39 And it must needs be that the [adevil](#note39a) should [btempt](#note39b) the children of men, or they could not be [cagents](#note39c) unto themselves; for if they never should have [dbitter](#note39d) they could not know the sweet—

40 Wherefore, it came to pass that the devil tempted Adam, and he partook of the forbidden [afruit](#note40a) and [btransgressed](#note40b) the commandment, wherein he became [csubject](#note40c) to the will of the devil, because he yielded unto temptation.

41 Wherefore, I, the Lord God, caused that he should be [acast out](#note41a) from the Garden of [bEden](#note41b), from my presence, because of his transgression, wherein he became [cspiritually](#note41c) [ddead](#note41d), which is the first death, even that same death which is the last [edeath](#note41e), which is spiritual, which shall be pronounced upon the wicked when I shall say: Depart, ye [fcursed](#note41f).

42 But, behold, I say unto you that I, the Lord God, gave unto Adam and unto his seed, that they should not [adie](#note42a) as to the temporal death, until I, the Lord God, should send forth [bangels](#note42b) to declare unto them [crepentance](#note42c) and [dredemption](#note42d), through faith on the name of mine [eOnly Begotten Son](#note42e).

43 And thus did I, the Lord God, appoint unto man the days of his [aprobation](#note43a)—that by his [bnatural](#note43b) death he might be [craised](#note43c) in [dimmortality](#note43d) unto eternal life, even as many as would believe;

44 And they that believe not unto eternal [adamnation](#note44a); for they cannot be redeemed from their spiritual [bfall](#note44b), because they repent not;

45 For they love darkness rather than light, and their [adeeds](#note45a) are evil, and they receive their [bwages](#note45b) of [cwhom](#note45c) they list to obey.

46 But behold, I say unto you, that little [achildren](#note46a) are [bredeemed](#note46b) from the foundation of the world through mine Only Begotten;

47 Wherefore, they cannot [asin](#note47a), for power is not given unto Satan to [btempt](#note47b) little children, until they [cbegin](#note47c) to become [daccountable](#note47d) before me;

48 For it is given unto them even as I will, according to mine own [apleasure](#note48a), that great things may be required at the hand of their [bfathers](#note48b).

49 And, again, I say unto you, that whoso having knowledge, have I not commanded to [arepent](#note49a)?

50 And he that hath no [aunderstanding](#note50a), it remaineth in me to do according as it is written. And now I declare no more unto you at this time. Amen.

Section 30

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to David Whitmer, Peter Whitmer Jr., and John Whitmer, at Fayette, New York, September 1830, following the three-day conference at Fayette, but before the elders of the Church had separated. Originally this material was published as three revelations; it was combined into one section by the Prophet for the 1835 edition of the Doctrine and Covenants.

[1–4](30.xhtml#p1), David Whitmer is chastened for failure to serve diligently; [5–8](30.xhtml#p5), Peter Whitmer Jr. is to accompany Oliver Cowdery on a mission to the Lamanites; [9–11](30.xhtml#p9), John Whitmer is called to preach the gospel.

1 Behold, I say unto you, David, that you have [afeared](#note1a) man and have not [brelied](#note1b) on me for strength as you ought.

2 But your mind has been on the things of the [aearth](#note2a) more than on the things of me, your Maker, and the ministry whereunto you have been called; and you have not given heed unto my [bSpirit](#note2b), and to those who were set over you, but have been persuaded by those whom I have not commanded.

3 Wherefore, you are left to inquire for yourself at my hand, and [aponder](#note3a) upon the things which you have received.

4 And your home shall be at your [afather’s](#note4a) house, until I give unto you further commandments. And you shall attend to the [bministry](#note4b) in the church, and before the world, and in the regions round about. Amen.

5 Behold, I say unto you, Peter, that you shall take your [ajourney](#note5a) with your brother Oliver; for the [btime](#note5b) has come that it is expedient in me that you shall open your mouth to declare my gospel; therefore, fear not, but give [cheed](#note5c) unto the words and advice of your brother, which he shall give you.

6 And be you afflicted in all his [aafflictions](#note6a), ever [blifting](#note6b) up your heart unto me in prayer and faith, for his and your [cdeliverance](#note6c); for I have given unto him power to [dbuild](#note6d) up my [echurch](#note6e) among the [fLamanites](#note6f);

7 And none have I appointed to be his counselor [aover](#note7a) him in the church, concerning church matters, except it is his brother, Joseph Smith, Jun.

8 Wherefore, give heed unto these things and be diligent in keeping my commandments, and you shall be blessed unto eternal life. Amen.

9 Behold, I say unto you, my servant John, that thou shalt commence from this time forth to [aproclaim](#note9a) my gospel, as with the [bvoice](#note9b) of a trump.

10 And your labor shall be at your brother Philip Burroughs’, and in that region round about, yea, wherever you can be heard, until I command you to go from hence.

11 And your whole labor shall be in Zion, with all your soul, from henceforth; yea, you shall ever open your mouth in my cause, not [afearing](#note11a) what [bman](#note11b) can do, for I am [cwith](#note11c) you. Amen.

Section 31

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Thomas B. Marsh, September 1830. The occasion was immediately following a conference of the Church (see the [heading to section 30](30.xhtml)). Thomas B. Marsh had been baptized earlier in the month and had been ordained an elder in the Church before this revelation was given.

[1–6](31.xhtml#p1), Thomas B. Marsh is called to preach the gospel and is assured of his family’s well-being; [7–13](31.xhtml#p7), He is counseled to be patient, pray always, and follow the Comforter.

1 [aThomas](#note1a), my son, blessed are you because of your faith in my work.

2 Behold, you have had many afflictions because of your family; nevertheless, I will bless you and your [afamily](#note2a), yea, your little ones; and the day cometh that they will believe and know the truth and be one with you in my church.

3 Lift up your heart and rejoice, for the hour of your mission is come; and your tongue shall be loosed, and you shall declare [aglad](#note3a) tidings of great joy unto this generation.

4 You shall [adeclare](#note4a) the things which have been revealed to my servant, Joseph Smith, Jun. You shall begin to preach from this time forth, yea, to reap in the field which is [bwhite](#note4b) already to be burned.

5 Therefore, [athrust](#note5a) in your sickle with all your soul, and your sins are [bforgiven](#note5b) you, and you shall be laden with [csheaves](#note5c) upon your back, for the [dlaborer](#note5d) is worthy of his hire. Wherefore, your family shall live.

6 Behold, verily I say unto you, go from them only for a little [atime](#note6a), and declare my word, and I will prepare a place for them.

7 Yea, I will [aopen](#note7a) the hearts of the people, and they will receive you. And I will establish a church by your hand;

8 And you shall [astrengthen](#note8a) them and prepare them against the time when they shall be gathered.

9 Be [apatient](#note9a) in [bafflictions](#note9b), [crevile](#note9c) not against those that revile. Govern your [dhouse](#note9d) in meekness, and be [esteadfast](#note9e).

10 Behold, I say unto you that you shall be a physician unto the church, but not unto the world, for they will not receive you.

11 Go your way whithersoever I will, and it shall be given you by the [aComforter](#note11a) what you shall do and whither you shall go.

12 [aPray](#note12a) always, lest you enter into [btemptation](#note12b) and lose your [creward](#note12c).

13 Be [afaithful](#note13a) unto the [bend](#note13b), and lo, I am [cwith](#note13c) you. These words are not of man nor of men, but of me, even Jesus Christ, your Redeemer, by the [dwill](#note13d) of the Father. Amen.

Section 32

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Parley P. Pratt and Ziba Peterson, in Manchester, New York, early October 1830. Great interest and desires were felt by the elders respecting the Lamanites, of whose predicted blessings the Church had learned from the Book of Mormon. In consequence, supplication was made that the Lord would indicate His will as to whether elders should be sent at that time to the Indian tribes in the West. The revelation followed.

[1–3](32.xhtml#p1), Parley P. Pratt and Ziba Peterson are called to preach to the Lamanites and to accompany Oliver Cowdery and Peter Whitmer Jr.; [4–5](32.xhtml#p4), They are to pray for an understanding of the scriptures.

1 And now concerning my servant [aParley P. Pratt](#note1a), behold, I say unto him that as I live I will that he shall declare my gospel and [blearn](#note1b) of me, and be meek and lowly of heart.

2 And that which I have appointed unto him is that he shall [ago](#note2a) with my servants, Oliver Cowdery and Peter Whitmer, Jun., into the wilderness among the [bLamanites](#note2b).

3 And [aZiba Peterson](#note3a) also shall go with them; and I myself will go with them and be in their [bmidst](#note3b); and I am their [cadvocate](#note3c) with the Father, and nothing shall [dprevail](#note3d) against them.

4 And they shall give [aheed](#note4a) to that which is written, and pretend to no other [brevelation](#note4b); and they shall pray always that I may [cunfold](#note4c) the same to their [dunderstanding](#note4d).

5 And they shall give heed unto these words and trifle not, and I will bless them. Amen.
