Section 33

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Ezra Thayre and Northrop Sweet, at Fayette, New York, October 1830. In introducing this revelation, Joseph Smith’s history affirms that “the Lord … is ever ready to instruct such as diligently seek in faith.”

[1–4](33.xhtml#p1), Laborers are called to declare the gospel in the eleventh hour; [5–6](33.xhtml#p5), The Church is established, and the elect are to be gathered; [7–10](33.xhtml#p7), Repent, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand; [11–15](33.xhtml#p11), The Church is built upon the gospel rock; [16–18](33.xhtml#p16), Prepare for the coming of the Bridegroom.

1 Behold, I say unto you, my servants [aEzra](#note1a) and Northrop, open ye your ears and hearken to the voice of the Lord your God, whose [bword](#note1b) is quick and powerful, sharper than a two-edged sword, to the dividing asunder of the joints and marrow, soul and spirit; and is a discerner of the thoughts and [cintents](#note1c) of the heart.

2 For verily, verily, I say unto you that ye are called to lift up your voices as with the [asound](#note2a) of a trump, to declare my gospel unto a crooked and [bperverse](#note2b) generation.

3 For behold, the [afield](#note3a) is [bwhite](#note3b) already to harvest; and it is the [celeventh](#note3c) hour, and the last time that I shall call [dlaborers](#note3d) into my vineyard.

4 And my [avineyard](#note4a) has become [bcorrupted](#note4b) every whit; and there is none which doeth [cgood](#note4c) save it be a few; and they [derr](#note4d) in many instances because of [epriestcrafts](#note4e), all having corrupt minds.

5 And verily, verily, I say unto you, that this [achurch](#note5a) have I [bestablished](#note5b) and called forth out of the [cwilderness](#note5c).

6 And even so will I [agather](#note6a) mine elect from the [bfour](#note6b) quarters of the earth, even as many as will believe in me, and hearken unto my voice.

7 Yea, verily, verily, I say unto you, that the field is [awhite](#note7a) already to harvest; wherefore, thrust in your sickles, and reap with all your might, mind, and strength.

8 [aOpen](#note8a) your mouths and they shall be filled, and you shall become even as [bNephi](#note8b) of old, who journeyed from Jerusalem in the wilderness.

9 Yea, open your mouths and spare not, and you shall be laden with [asheaves](#note9a) upon your backs, for lo, I am with you.

10 Yea, open your mouths and they shall be filled, saying: [aRepent](#note10a), repent, and prepare ye the way of the Lord, and make his paths straight; for the [bkingdom](#note10b) of heaven is at hand;

11 Yea, [arepent](#note11a) and be baptized, every one of you, for a [bremission](#note11b) of your sins; yea, be baptized even by water, and then cometh the baptism of fire and of the Holy Ghost.

12 Behold, verily, verily, I say unto you, this is my [agospel](#note12a); and remember that they shall have faith in me or they can in nowise be saved;

13 And upon this [arock](#note13a) I will build my church; yea, upon this rock ye are built, and if ye continue, the [bgates](#note13b) of hell shall not prevail against you.

14 And ye shall remember the church [aarticles and covenants](#note14a) to keep them.

15 And whoso having faith you shall [aconfirm](#note15a) in my church, by the laying on of the [bhands](#note15b), and I will bestow the [cgift](#note15c) of the Holy Ghost upon them.

16 And the Book of Mormon and the holy scriptures are given of me for your [ainstruction](#note16a); and the power of my [bSpirit](#note16b) [cquickeneth](#note16c) all things.

17 Wherefore, be faithful, praying always, having your [alamps](#note17a) [btrimmed](#note17b) and burning, and oil with you, that you may be [cready](#note17c) at the coming of the [dBridegroom](#note17d)—

18 For behold, verily, verily, I say unto you, that I [acome](#note18a) quickly. Even so. Amen.

Section 34

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Orson Pratt, at Fayette, New York, November 4, 1830. Brother Pratt was nineteen years old at the time. He had been converted and baptized when he first heard the preaching of the restored gospel by his older brother, Parley P. Pratt, six weeks before. This revelation was received in the Peter Whitmer Sr. home.

[1–4](34.xhtml#p1), The faithful become the sons of God through the Atonement; [5–9](34.xhtml#p5), The preaching of the gospel prepares the way for the Second Coming; [10–12](34.xhtml#p10), Prophecy comes by the power of the Holy Ghost.

1 My son [aOrson](#note1a), hearken and hear and behold what I, the Lord God, shall say unto you, even Jesus Christ your Redeemer;

2 The [alight](#note2a) and the life of the world, a light which shineth in darkness and the darkness comprehendeth it not;

3 Who so [aloved](#note3a) the world that he [bgave](#note3b) his own life, that as many as would believe might become the [csons](#note3c) of God. Wherefore you are my son;

4 And [ablessed](#note4a) are you because you have believed;

5 And more blessed are you because you are [acalled](#note5a) of me to preach my gospel—

6 To lift up your voice as with the sound of a [atrump](#note6a), both long and loud, and [bcry](#note6b) repentance unto a crooked and perverse generation, [cpreparing](#note6c) the way of the Lord for his [dsecond coming](#note6d).

7 For behold, verily, verily, I say unto you, the [atime](#note7a) is soon at hand that I shall [bcome](#note7b) in a [ccloud](#note7c) with power and great glory.

8 And it shall be a [agreat day](#note8a) at the time of my coming, for all nations shall [btremble](#note8b).

9 But before that great day shall come, the [asun](#note9a) shall be darkened, and the moon be turned into blood; and the stars shall refuse their shining, and some shall fall, and great destructions await the wicked.

10 Wherefore, lift up your voice and [aspare](#note10a) not, for the Lord God hath spoken; therefore [bprophesy](#note10b), and it shall be given by the [cpower](#note10c) of the Holy Ghost.

11 And if you are faithful, behold, I am with you until I come—

12 And verily, verily, I say unto you, I come [aquickly](#note12a). I am your [bLord](#note12b) and your Redeemer. Even so. Amen.

* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1a)

  1a

  [D&C 52:26](52.xhtml#p26); [103:40](103.xhtml#p40); [124:129](124.xhtml#p129); [136:13](136.xhtml#p13).
* [◀](#ldsBACK_note2a)

  2a

  John 1:5.

Section 35

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet and Sidney Rigdon, at or near Fayette, New York, December 7, 1830. At this time, the Prophet was engaged almost daily in making a translation of the Bible. The translation was begun as early as June 1830, and both Oliver Cowdery and John Whitmer had served as scribes. Since they had now been called to other duties, Sidney Rigdon was called by divine appointment to serve as the Prophet’s scribe in this work (see [verse 20](35.xhtml#p20)). As a preface to the record of this revelation, Joseph Smith’s history states: “In December Sidney Rigdon came [from Ohio] to inquire of the Lord, and with him came Edward Partridge. … Shortly after the arrival of these two brethren, thus spake the Lord.”

[1–2](35.xhtml#p1), How men may become the sons of God; [3–7](35.xhtml#p3), Sidney Rigdon is called to baptize and to confer the Holy Ghost; [8–12](35.xhtml#p8), Signs and miracles are wrought by faith; [13–16](35.xhtml#p13), The Lord’s servants will thresh the nations by the power of the Spirit; [17–19](35.xhtml#p17), Joseph Smith holds the keys of the mysteries; [20–21](35.xhtml#p20), The elect will abide the day of the Lord’s coming; [22–27](35.xhtml#p22), Israel will be saved.

1 Listen to the voice of the [aLord](#note1a) your God, even [bAlpha and Omega](#note1b), the beginning and the end, whose [ccourse](#note1c) is one [deternal](#note1d) round, the [esame](#note1e) today as yesterday, and forever.

2 I am Jesus Christ, the Son of God, who was [acrucified](#note2a) for the sins of the world, even as many as will [bbelieve](#note2b) on my name, that they may become the [csons](#note2c) of God, even [done](#note2d) in [eme](#note2e) as I am [fone](#note2f) in the Father, as the Father is one in me, that we may be one.

3 Behold, verily, verily, I say unto my servant Sidney, I have looked upon thee and thy works. I have [aheard](#note3a) thy prayers, and prepared thee for a greater work.

4 Thou art blessed, for thou shalt do great things. Behold thou wast sent forth, even as [aJohn](#note4a), to prepare the way before me, and before [bElijah](#note4b) which should come, and thou knewest it not.

5 Thou didst baptize by water unto repentance, but they [areceived not](#note5a) the Holy Ghost;

6 But now I give unto thee a commandment, that thou shalt [abaptize](#note6a) by water, and they shall receive the [bHoly Ghost](#note6b) by the laying on of the [chands](#note6c), even as the apostles of old.

7 And it shall come to pass that there shall be a great work in the land, even among the [aGentiles](#note7a), for their [bfolly](#note7b) and their abominations shall be made manifest in the eyes of all people.

8 For I am God, and mine arm is not [ashortened](#note8a); and I will show [bmiracles](#note8b), [csigns](#note8c), and wonders, unto all those who [dbelieve](#note8d) on my name.

9 And whoso shall ask it in my name in [afaith](#note9a), they shall [bcast out](#note9b) [cdevils](#note9c); they shall heal the [dsick](#note9d); they shall cause the blind to receive their [esight](#note9e), and the deaf to hear, and the dumb to speak, and the lame to walk.

10 And the time [aspeedily](#note10a) cometh that great things are to be shown forth unto the children of men;

11 But [awithout faith](#note11a) shall not anything be shown forth except [bdesolations](#note11b) upon [cBabylon](#note11c), the same which has made [dall](#note11d) nations drink of the wine of the wrath of her [efornication](#note11e).

12 And there are [anone](#note12a) that doeth good except those who are ready to [breceive](#note12b) the fulness of my gospel, which I have sent forth unto this generation.

13 Wherefore, I call upon the [aweak](#note13a) things of the world, those who are [bunlearned](#note13b) and despised, to thresh the [cnations](#note13c) by the power of my [dSpirit](#note13d);

14 And their arm shall be my arm, and I will be their [ashield](#note14a) and their buckler; and I will gird up their loins, and they shall fight manfully for me; and their [benemies](#note14b) shall be under their feet; and I will let [cfall](#note14c) the [dsword](#note14d) in their behalf, and by the [efire](#note14e) of mine indignation will I preserve them.

15 And the [apoor](#note15a) and the [bmeek](#note15b) shall have the gospel preached unto them, and they shall be [clooking](#note15c) forth for the time of my coming, for it is [dnigh](#note15d) at hand—

16 And they shall learn the parable of the [afig tree](#note16a), for even now already summer is nigh.

17 And I have sent forth the [afulness](#note17a) of my gospel by the hand of my servant Joseph; and in weakness have I blessed him;

18 And I have given unto him the [akeys](#note18a) of the mystery of those things which have been [bsealed](#note18b), even things which were from the [cfoundation](#note18c) of the world, and the things which shall come from this time until the time of my coming, if he [dabide](#note18d) in me, and if not, [eanother](#note18e) will I plant in his stead.

19 Wherefore, watch over him that his faith fail not, and it shall be given by the [aComforter](#note19a), the [bHoly Ghost](#note19b), that knoweth all things.

20 And a commandment I give unto thee—that thou shalt [awrite](#note20a) for him; and the [bscriptures](#note20b) shall be given, even as they are in mine own bosom, to the salvation of mine own [celect](#note20c);

21 For they will hear my [avoice](#note21a), and shall [bsee](#note21b) me, and shall not be [casleep](#note21c), and shall [dabide](#note21d) the day of my [ecoming](#note21e); for they shall be [fpurified](#note21f), even as I am pure.

22 And now I say unto you, [atarry](#note22a) with him, and he shall journey with you; forsake him not, and surely these things shall be fulfilled.

23 And [ainasmuch](#note23a) as ye do not write, behold, it shall be [bgiven](#note23b) unto him to prophesy; and thou shalt preach my gospel and call on [cthe holy prophets](#note23c) to prove his words, as they shall be given him.

24 [aKeep](#note24a) all the commandments and covenants by which ye are bound; and I will cause the heavens to [bshake](#note24b) for your [cgood](#note24c), and [dSatan](#note24d) shall tremble and [eZion](#note24e) shall [frejoice](#note24f) upon the hills and [gflourish](#note24g);

25 And [aIsrael](#note25a) shall be [bsaved](#note25b) in mine own due time; and by the [ckeys](#note25c) which I have given shall they be led, and no more be confounded at all.

26 [aLift](#note26a) up your hearts and be glad, your [bredemption](#note26b) draweth nigh.

27 Fear not, little [aflock](#note27a), the [bkingdom](#note27b) is yours until I come. Behold, I [ccome](#note27c) quickly. Even so. Amen.

Section 36

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Edward Partridge, near Fayette, New York, December 9, 1830 (see the [heading to section 35](35.xhtml)). Joseph Smith’s history states that Edward Partridge “was a pattern of piety, and one of the Lord’s great men.”

[1–3](36.xhtml#p1), The Lord lays His hand upon Edward Partridge by the hand of Sidney Rigdon; [4–8](36.xhtml#p4), Every man who receives the gospel and the priesthood is to be called to go forth and preach.

1 Thus saith the Lord God, the [aMighty One](#note1a) of Israel: Behold, I say unto you, my servant [bEdward](#note1b), that you are blessed, and your sins are forgiven you, and you are called to preach my gospel as with the voice of a trump;

2 And I will lay my [ahand](#note2a) upon you by the hand of my servant Sidney Rigdon, and you shall receive my Spirit, the Holy Ghost, even the [bComforter](#note2b), which shall [cteach](#note2c) you the peaceable things of the kingdom;

3 And you shall declare it with a loud voice, saying: Hosanna, [ablessed](#note3a) be the name of the most high God.

4 And now this calling and commandment give I unto you concerning all men—

5 That as many as shall come before my servants Sidney Rigdon and Joseph Smith, Jun., embracing this calling and commandment, shall be [aordained](#note5a) and sent forth to [bpreach](#note5b) the everlasting gospel among the nations—

6 Crying repentance, saying: [aSave](#note6a) yourselves from this untoward generation, and come forth out of the fire, hating even the [bgarments](#note6b) spotted with the flesh.

7 And this commandment shall be given unto the elders of my church, that every man which will [aembrace](#note7a) it with [bsingleness](#note7b) of heart may be ordained and sent forth, even as I have spoken.

8 I am Jesus Christ, the Son of God; wherefore, gird up your loins and I will [asuddenly](#note8a) [bcome](#note8b) to my [ctemple](#note8c). Even so. Amen.

Section 37

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet and Sidney Rigdon, near Fayette, New York, December 1830. Herein is given the first commandment concerning a gathering in this dispensation.

[1–4](37.xhtml#p1), The Saints are called to gather at the Ohio.

1 Behold, I say unto you that it is not expedient in me that ye should [atranslate](#note1a) any more until ye shall go to the Ohio, and this because of the enemy and for your sakes.

2 And again, I say unto you that ye shall not go until ye have preached my gospel in those parts, and have [astrengthened](#note2a) up the church whithersoever it is found, and more especially in [bColesville](#note2b); for, behold, they pray unto me in much faith.

3 And again, a commandment I give unto the church, that it is expedient in me that they should assemble together at [athe Ohio](#note3a), against the time that my servant Oliver Cowdery shall return unto them.

4 Behold, here is wisdom, and let every man [achoose](#note4a) for himself until I come. Even so. Amen.

* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1a)

  1a

  IE the translation of the Bible already in process. [D&C 1:29](1.xhtml#p29); [35:20](35.xhtml#p20).
* [◀](#ldsBACK_note2a)

  2a

  Zech. 10:12.
* [◀](#ldsBACK_note2b)

  b

  [D&C 24:3](24.xhtml#p3); [26:1](26.xhtml#p1).
* [◀](#ldsBACK_note3a)

  3a

  IE the state of Ohio. [D&C 38:32](38.xhtml#p32).
* [◀](#ldsBACK_note4a)

  4a

  TG Agency.

Section 38

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Fayette, New York, January 2, 1831. The occasion was a conference of the Church.

[1–6](38.xhtml#p1), Christ created all things; [7–8](38.xhtml#p7), He is in the midst of His Saints, who will soon see Him; [9–12](38.xhtml#p9), All flesh is corrupted before Him; [13–22](38.xhtml#p13), He has reserved a land of promise for His Saints in time and in eternity; [23–27](38.xhtml#p23), The Saints are commanded to be one and esteem each other as brethren; [28–29](38.xhtml#p28), Wars are predicted; [30–33](38.xhtml#p30), The Saints are to be given power from on high and to go forth among all nations; [34–42](38.xhtml#p34), The Church is commanded to care for the poor and needy and to seek the riches of eternity.

1 Thus saith the Lord your God, even Jesus Christ, the Great [aI Am](#note1a), Alpha and Omega, the [bbeginning](#note1b) and the end, the [csame](#note1c) which looked upon the [dwide](#note1d) expanse of eternity, and all the seraphic [ehosts](#note1e) of heaven, [fbefore](#note1f) the world was [gmade](#note1g);

2 The same which [aknoweth](#note2a) all things, for [ball](#note2b) things are [cpresent](#note2c) before mine eyes;

3 I am the same which [aspake](#note3a), and the world was made, and all things came by me.

4 I am the same which have taken the [aZion](#note4a) of [bEnoch](#note4b) into mine own bosom; and verily, I say, even as many as have [cbelieved](#note4c) in my name, for I am Christ, and in mine own name, by the virtue of the [dblood](#note4d) which I have spilt, have I pleaded before the Father for them.

5 But behold, the residue of the [awicked](#note5a) have I kept in [bchains](#note5b) of darkness until the [cjudgment](#note5c) of the great day, which shall come at the end of the earth;

6 And even so will I cause the wicked to be kept, that will not hear my voice but [aharden](#note6a) their hearts, and wo, wo, wo, is their doom.

7 But behold, verily, verily, I say unto you that mine [aeyes](#note7a) are upon you. I am in your [bmidst](#note7b) and ye cannot [csee](#note7c) me;

8 But the day soon cometh that ye shall [asee](#note8a) me, and know that I am; for the [bveil](#note8b) of darkness shall soon be rent, and he that is not [cpurified](#note8c) shall not [dabide](#note8d) the day.

9 Wherefore, gird up your loins and be prepared. Behold, the [akingdom](#note9a) is yours, and the enemy shall not overcome.

10 Verily I say unto you, ye are [aclean](#note10a), but not all; and there is none else with whom I am well [bpleased](#note10b);

11 For all [aflesh](#note11a) is corrupted before me; and the powers of [bdarkness](#note11b) prevail upon the earth, among the children of men, in the presence of all the hosts of heaven—

12 Which causeth [asilence](#note12a) to reign, and all eternity is [bpained](#note12b), and the [cangels](#note12c) are waiting the great command to [dreap](#note12d) down the earth, to [egather](#note12e) the [ftares](#note12f) that they may be [gburned](#note12g); and, behold, the enemy is combined.

13 And now I show unto you a mystery, a thing which is had in secret chambers, to bring to pass even your [adestruction](#note13a) in process of time, and ye knew it not;

14 But now I tell it unto you, and ye are blessed, not because of your iniquity, neither your hearts of unbelief; for verily some of you are [aguilty](#note14a) before me, but I will be merciful unto your [bweakness](#note14b).

15 Therefore, be ye [astrong](#note15a) from henceforth; [bfear not](#note15b), for the kingdom is yours.

16 And for your salvation I give unto you a commandment, for I have heard your [aprayers](#note16a), and the [bpoor](#note16b) have complained before me, and the [crich](#note16c) have I made, and all flesh is mine, and I am no [drespecter](#note16d) of persons.

17 And I have made the earth rich, and behold it is my [afootstool](#note17a), wherefore, again I will stand upon it.

18 And I hold forth and deign to give unto you greater riches, even a land of [apromise](#note18a), a land [bflowing](#note18b) with milk and honey, upon which there shall be no [ccurse](#note18c) when the Lord cometh;

19 And I will give it unto you for the land of your inheritance, if you seek it with all your hearts.

20 And this shall be my covenant with you, ye shall have it for the land of your inheritance, and for the [ainheritance](#note20a) of your children forever, while the earth shall stand, and ye shall possess it again in eternity, no more to pass away.

21 But, verily I say unto you that in time ye shall have no [aking](#note21a) nor ruler, for I will be your king and watch over you.

22 Wherefore, hear my voice and [afollow](#note22a) me, and you shall be a [bfree](#note22b) people, and ye shall have no laws but my laws when I come, for I am your [clawgiver](#note22c), and what can stay my hand?

23 But, verily I say unto you, [ateach](#note23a) one another according to the office wherewith I have appointed you;

24 And let every man [aesteem](#note24a) his brother as himself, and practice [bvirtue](#note24b) and holiness before me.

25 And again I say unto you, let every man esteem his [abrother](#note25a) as himself.

26 For what man among you having twelve sons, and is no respecter of them, and they serve him obediently, and he saith unto the one: Be thou clothed in robes and sit thou here; and to the other: Be thou clothed in rags and sit thou there—and looketh upon his sons and saith I am [ajust](#note26a)?

27 Behold, this I have given unto you as a parable, and it is even as I am. I say unto you, be [aone](#note27a); and if ye are not one ye are not mine.

28 And again, I say unto you that the enemy in the secret chambers seeketh your [alives](#note28a).

29 Ye hear of [awars](#note29a) in far countries, and you say that there will soon be great wars in far countries, but ye know not the hearts of men in your own land.

30 I tell you these things because of your prayers; wherefore, [atreasure](#note30a) up [bwisdom](#note30b) in your bosoms, lest the wickedness of men reveal these things unto you by their wickedness, in a manner which shall speak in your ears with a voice louder than that which shall shake the earth; but if ye are prepared ye shall not fear.

31 And that ye might escape the power of the [aenemy](#note31a), and be gathered unto me a righteous people, without [bspot](#note31b) and blameless—

32 Wherefore, for this cause I gave unto you the [acommandment](#note32a) that ye should go to the [bOhio](#note32b); and there I will give unto you my [claw](#note32c); and there you shall be [dendowed](#note32d) with power from on high;

33 And from thence, whosoever I will shall [ago forth](#note33a) among all nations, and it shall be told them what they shall do; for I have a great work laid up in store, for Israel shall be [bsaved](#note33b), and I will [clead](#note33c) them whithersoever I will, and no power shall [dstay](#note33d) my hand.

34 And now, I give unto the church in these parts a commandment, that certain men among them shall be appointed, and they shall be [aappointed](#note34a) by the [bvoice](#note34b) of the church;

35 And they shall look to the poor and the needy, and administer to their [arelief](#note35a) that they shall not suffer; and send them forth to the place which I have commanded them;

36 And this shall be their work, to govern the affairs of the property of this [achurch](#note36a).

37 And they that have farms that cannot be sold, let them be left or rented as seemeth them good.

38 See that all things are preserved; and when men are [aendowed](#note38a) with power from on high and sent forth, all these things shall be gathered unto the bosom of the church.

39 And if ye seek the [ariches](#note39a) which it is the will of the Father to give unto you, ye shall be the richest of all people, for ye shall have the riches of eternity; and it must needs be that the [briches](#note39b) of the earth are mine to give; but beware of [cpride](#note39c), lest ye become as the [dNephites](#note39d) of old.

40 And again, I say unto you, I give unto you a commandment, that every man, both elder, priest, teacher, and also member, go to with his might, with the [alabor](#note40a) of his [bhands](#note40b), to prepare and accomplish the things which I have commanded.

41 And let your [apreaching](#note41a) be the [bwarning](#note41b) voice, every man to his neighbor, in mildness and in [cmeekness](#note41c).

42 And go ye [aout](#note42a) from among the [bwicked](#note42b). Save yourselves. Be ye [cclean](#note42c) that bear the vessels of the Lord. Even so. Amen.

Section 39

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to James Covel, at Fayette, New York, January 5, 1831. James Covel, who had been a Methodist minister for about forty years, covenanted with the Lord that he would obey any command that the Lord would give to him through Joseph the Prophet.

[1–4](39.xhtml#p1), The Saints have power to become the sons of God; [5–6](39.xhtml#p5), To receive the gospel is to receive Christ; [7–14](39.xhtml#p7), James Covel is commanded to be baptized and labor in the Lord’s vineyard; [15–21](39.xhtml#p15), The Lord’s servants are to preach the gospel before the Second Coming; [22–24](39.xhtml#p22), Those who receive the gospel will be gathered in time and in eternity.

1 Hearken and listen to the voice of him who is from all [aeternity](#note1a) to all eternity, the Great [bI Am](#note1b), even Jesus Christ—

2 The [alight](#note2a) and the life of the world; a light which shineth in darkness and the darkness comprehendeth it not;

3 The same which came in the [ameridian of time](#note3a) unto mine own, and mine own [breceived me not](#note3b);

4 But to as many as received me, gave I power to become my [asons](#note4a); and even so will I give unto as many as will receive me, power to become my sons.

5 And verily, verily, I say unto you, he that receiveth my gospel [areceiveth](#note5a) me; and he that [breceiveth](#note5b) not my gospel receiveth not me.

6 And this is my [agospel](#note6a)—repentance and baptism by water, and then cometh the [bbaptism of fire](#note6b) and the Holy Ghost, even the Comforter, which showeth all things, and [cteacheth](#note6c) the peaceable things of the kingdom.

7 And now, behold, I say unto you, my servant [aJames](#note7a), I have looked upon thy works and I [bknow](#note7b) thee.

8 And verily I say unto thee, thine heart is now right before me at this time; and, behold, I have bestowed great [ablessings](#note8a) upon thy head;

9 Nevertheless, thou hast seen great [asorrow](#note9a), for thou hast [brejected](#note9b) me many times because of pride and the cares of the [cworld](#note9c).

10 But, behold, the days of thy [adeliverance](#note10a) are come, if thou wilt [bhearken](#note10b) to my voice, which saith unto thee: Arise and be baptized, and [cwash](#note10c) away your sins, calling on my name, and you shall receive my Spirit, and a blessing so great as you never have known.

11 And if thou do this, I have prepared thee for a greater work. Thou shalt preach the [afulness](#note11a) of my gospel, which I have sent forth in these last days, the [bcovenant](#note11b) which I have sent forth to [crecover](#note11c) my people, which are of the house of Israel.

12 And it shall come to pass that power shall [arest](#note12a) upon thee; thou shalt have great faith, and I will be with thee and go before thy face.

13 Thou art called to [alabor](#note13a) in my vineyard, and to [bbuild](#note13b) up my [cchurch](#note13c), and to bring forth [dZion](#note13d), that it may rejoice upon the hills and [eflourish](#note13e).

14 Behold, verily, verily, I say unto thee, thou art not called to go into the eastern countries, but thou art called to go to the Ohio.

15 And inasmuch as my people shall assemble themselves at the Ohio, I have kept in store a [ablessing](#note15a) such as is not known among the children of men, and it shall be poured forth upon their heads. And from thence men shall go forth into [ball](#note15b) [cnations](#note15c).

16 Behold, verily, verily, I say unto you, that the people in Ohio call upon me in much faith, thinking I will [astay](#note16a) my hand in judgment upon the nations, but I cannot [bdeny](#note16b) my word.

17 Wherefore lay to with your might and call faithful laborers into my vineyard, that it may be [apruned](#note17a) for the last time.

18 And inasmuch as they do repent and receive the fulness of my gospel, and become sanctified, I will stay mine hand in [ajudgment](#note18a).

19 Wherefore, go forth, crying with a loud voice, saying: The kingdom of heaven is at [ahand](#note19a); crying: Hosanna! blessed be the name of the Most High God.

20 Go forth [abaptizing](#note20a) with water, preparing the way before my face for the time of my [bcoming](#note20b);

21 For the time is at hand; the [aday](#note21a) or the hour no man [bknoweth](#note21b); but it surely shall [ccome](#note21c).

22 And he that receiveth these things receiveth me; and they shall be [agathered](#note22a) unto me in time and in eternity.

23 And again, it shall come to pass that on as many as ye shall baptize with water, ye shall lay your [ahands](#note23a), and they shall receive the [bgift](#note23b) of the Holy Ghost, and shall be [clooking](#note23c) forth for the signs of my [dcoming](#note23d), and shall know me.

24 Behold, I come quickly. Even so. Amen.

Section 40

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet and Sidney Rigdon, at Fayette, New York, January 6, 1831. Preceding the record of this revelation, the Prophet’s history states, “As James [Covel] rejected the word of the Lord, and returned to his former principles and people, the Lord gave unto me and Sidney Rigdon the following revelation” (see [section 39](39.xhtml)).

[1–3](40.xhtml#p1), Fear of persecution and cares of the world cause rejection of the gospel.

1 Behold, verily I say unto you, that the heart of my servant [aJames Covel](#note1a) was right before me, for he covenanted with me that he would obey my word.

2 And he [areceived](#note2a) the word with gladness, but straightway Satan [btempted](#note2b) him; and the fear of [cpersecution](#note2c) and the cares of the world caused him to [dreject](#note2d) the word.

3 Wherefore he [abroke](#note3a) my covenant, and it remaineth with me to do with him as seemeth me good. Amen.

Section 41

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to the Church, at Kirtland, Ohio, February 4, 1831. This revelation instructs the Prophet and Church elders to pray to receive God’s “law” (see [section 42](42.xhtml)). Joseph Smith had just arrived in Kirtland from New York, and Leman Copley, a Church member in nearby Thompson, Ohio, “requested Brother Joseph and Sidney [Rigdon] … live with him and he would furnish them houses and provisions.” The following revelation clarifies where Joseph and Sidney should live and also calls Edward Partridge to be the Church’s first bishop.

[1–3](41.xhtml#p1), The elders will govern the Church by the spirit of revelation; [4–6](41.xhtml#p4), True disciples will receive and keep the Lord’s law; [7–12](41.xhtml#p7), Edward Partridge is named as a bishop unto the Church.

1 Hearken and [ahear](#note1a), O ye my people, saith the Lord and your God, ye whom I delight to bless with the greatest of all [bblessings](#note1b), ye that hear me; and ye that hear me not will I [ccurse](#note1c), that have [dprofessed](#note1d) my [ename](#note1e), with the heaviest of all cursings.

2 Hearken, O ye elders of my church whom I have called, behold I give unto you a commandment, that ye shall assemble yourselves together to [aagree](#note2a) upon my word;

3 And by the prayer of your faith ye shall receive my [alaw](#note3a), that ye may know how to govern my [bchurch](#note3b) and have all things right before me.

4 And I will be your [aruler](#note4a) when I [bcome](#note4b); and behold, I come quickly, and ye shall see that my law is kept.

5 He that [areceiveth](#note5a) my [blaw](#note5b) and [cdoeth](#note5c) it, the same is my disciple; and he that saith he receiveth it and [ddoeth](#note5d) it not, the same is not my disciple, and shall be [ecast out](#note5e) from among you;

6 For it is not meet that the things which belong to the children of the kingdom should be given to them that are not worthy, or to [adogs](#note6a), or the [bpearls](#note6b) to be cast before swine.

7 And again, it is meet that my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., should have a [ahouse](#note7a) built, in which to live and [btranslate](#note7b).

8 And again, it is meet that my servant Sidney Rigdon should live as seemeth him good, inasmuch as he keepeth my commandments.

9 And again, I have called my servant [aEdward Partridge](#note9a); and I give a commandment, that he should be appointed by the voice of the church, and ordained a [bbishop](#note9b) unto the church, to leave his merchandise and to [cspend](#note9c) all his time in the labors of the church;

10 To see to all things as it shall be appointed unto him in my laws in the day that I shall give them.

11 And this because his heart is pure before me, for he is like unto [aNathanael](#note11a) of old, in whom there is no [bguile](#note11b).

12 These words are given unto you, and they are pure before me; wherefore, beware how you hold them, for they are to be answered upon your [asouls](#note12a) in the day of judgment. Even so. Amen.

Section 42

Revelation given in two parts through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, February 9 and 23, 1831. The first part, consisting of [verses 1 through 72](42.xhtml#p1), was received in the presence of twelve elders and in fulfillment of the Lord’s promise previously made that the “law” would be given in Ohio (see [section 38:32](38.xhtml#p32)). The second portion consists of [verses 73 through 93](42.xhtml#p73). The Prophet specifies this revelation as “embracing the law of the Church.”

[1–10](42.xhtml#p1), The elders are called to preach the gospel, baptize converts, and build up the Church; [11–12](42.xhtml#p11), They must be called and ordained and are to teach the principles of the gospel found in the scriptures; [13–17](42.xhtml#p13), They are to teach and prophesy by the power of the Spirit; [18–29](42.xhtml#p18), The Saints are commanded not to kill, steal, lie, lust, commit adultery, or speak evil against others; [30–39](42.xhtml#p30), Laws governing the consecration of properties are set forth; [40–42](42.xhtml#p40), Pride and idleness are condemned; [43–52](42.xhtml#p43), The sick are to be healed through administrations and by faith; [53–60](42.xhtml#p53), The scriptures govern the Church and are to be proclaimed to the world; [61–69](42.xhtml#p61), The site of the New Jerusalem and the mysteries of the kingdom will be revealed; [70–73](42.xhtml#p70), Consecrated properties are to be used to support Church officers; [74–93](42.xhtml#p74), Laws governing fornication, adultery, killing, stealing, and confession of sins are set forth.

1 Hearken, O ye elders of my [achurch](#note1a), who have assembled yourselves together in my name, even Jesus Christ the Son of the living God, the Savior of the world; inasmuch as ye believe on my name and keep my commandments.

2 Again I say unto you, hearken and hear and obey the [alaw](#note2a) which I shall give unto you.

3 For verily I say, as ye have assembled yourselves together according to the [acommandment](#note3a) wherewith I commanded you, and are agreed as [btouching](#note3b) this one thing, and have asked the Father in my name, even so ye shall receive.

4 Behold, verily I say unto you, I give unto you this first commandment, that ye shall [ago forth](#note4a) in my name, every one of you, excepting my servants Joseph Smith, Jun., and Sidney Rigdon.

5 And I give unto them a commandment that they shall go forth for a little season, and it shall be [agiven](#note5a) by the power of the Spirit when they shall return.

6 And ye shall go forth in the power of my Spirit, preaching my gospel, [atwo by two](#note6a), in my name, lifting up your voices as with the sound of a [btrump](#note6b), declaring my word like unto angels of God.

7 And ye shall go forth baptizing with water, saying: Repent ye, repent ye, for the kingdom of heaven is at hand.

8 And from this place ye shall go forth into the regions westward; and inasmuch as ye shall find them that will receive you ye shall [abuild](#note8a) up my church in every region—

9 Until the time shall come when it shall be revealed unto you from on high, when the [acity](#note9a) of the [bNew Jerusalem](#note9b) shall be prepared, that ye may be [cgathered](#note9c) in one, that ye may be my [dpeople](#note9d) and I will be your God.

10 And again, I say unto you, that my servant [aEdward Partridge](#note10a) shall stand in the office whereunto I have [bappointed](#note10b) him. And it shall come to pass, that if he transgress [canother](#note10c) shall be appointed in his stead. Even so. Amen.

11 Again I say unto you, that it shall not be given to any one to go forth to [apreach](#note11a) my gospel, or to build up my church, except he be [bordained](#note11b) by some one who has [cauthority](#note11c), and it is known to the church that he has authority and has been regularly ordained by the heads of the church.

12 And again, the [aelders](#note12a), priests and teachers of this church shall [bteach](#note12b) the principles of my gospel, which are in the Bible and the [cBook of Mormon](#note12c), in the which is the [dfulness](#note12d) of the [egospel](#note12e).

13 And they shall [aobserve](#note13a) the [bcovenants and church articles](#note13b) to [cdo](#note13c) them, and these shall be their teachings, as they shall be [ddirected](#note13d) by the Spirit.

14 And the Spirit shall be given unto you [aby](#note14a) the prayer of faith; and if ye receive not the [bSpirit](#note14b) ye shall not teach.

15 And all this ye shall observe to do as I have [acommanded](#note15a) concerning your teaching, until the fulness of my [bscriptures](#note15b) is given.

16 And as ye shall lift up your voices by the [aComforter](#note16a), ye shall speak and prophesy as seemeth me good;

17 For, behold, the [aComforter](#note17a) knoweth all things, and [bbeareth](#note17b) record of the Father and of the Son.

18 And now, behold, I speak unto the church. Thou shalt not [akill](#note18a); and he that [bkills](#note18b) shall [cnot](#note18c) have forgiveness in this world, nor in the world to come.

19 And again, I say, thou shalt not kill; but he that [akilleth](#note19a) shall [bdie](#note19b).

20 Thou shalt not steal; and he that [astealeth](#note20a) and will not repent shall be [bcast out](#note20b).

21 Thou shalt not [alie](#note21a); he that lieth and will not repent shall be cast out.

22 Thou shalt [alove](#note22a) thy wife with all thy heart, and shalt [bcleave](#note22b) unto her and none else.

23 And he that [alooketh](#note23a) upon a woman to lust after her shall deny the faith, and shall not have the [bSpirit](#note23b); and if he repents not he shall be cast out.

24 Thou shalt not commit [aadultery](#note24a); and he that committeth [badultery](#note24b), and repenteth not, shall be [ccast out](#note24c).

25 But he that has committed adultery and repents with all his heart, and forsaketh it, and doeth it [ano more](#note25a), thou shalt forgive;

26 But if he doeth it [aagain](#note26a), he shall not be forgiven, but shall be cast out.

27 Thou shalt not [aspeak evil](#note27a) of thy neighbor, nor do him any harm.

28 Thou knowest my laws concerning these things are given in my scriptures; he that sinneth and [arepenteth](#note28a) not shall be [bcast out](#note28b).

29 If thou [alovest](#note29a) me thou shalt [bserve](#note29b) me and [ckeep](#note29c) all my commandments.

30 And behold, thou wilt remember the [apoor](#note30a), and [bconsecrate](#note30b) of thy properties for their [csupport](#note30c) that which thou hast to impart unto them, with a covenant and a deed which cannot be broken.

31 And inasmuch as ye [aimpart](#note31a) of your [bsubstance](#note31b) unto the [cpoor](#note31c), ye will do it unto me; and they shall be [dlaid](#note31d) before the [ebishop](#note31e) of my church and his [fcounselors](#note31f), two of the elders, or high priests, such as he shall appoint or has appointed and [gset apart](#note31g) for that purpose.

32 And it shall come to pass, that after they are laid before the bishop of my church, and after that he has received these testimonies concerning the [aconsecration](#note32a) of the properties of my church, that they cannot be taken from the church, agreeable to my commandments, every man shall be made [baccountable](#note32b) unto me, a [csteward](#note32c) over his own property, or that which he has received by consecration, as much as is sufficient for himself and [dfamily](#note32d).

33 And again, if there shall be properties in the hands of the church, or any individuals of it, more than is necessary for their support after this first consecration, which is a [aresidue](#note33a) to be consecrated unto the bishop, it shall be kept to administer to those who have not, from time to time, that every man who has need may be amply supplied and receive according to his wants.

34 Therefore, the residue shall be kept in my [astorehouse](#note34a), to administer to the poor and the needy, as shall be appointed by the [bhigh council](#note34b) of the church, and the bishop and his council;

35 And for the purpose of [apurchasing](#note35a) lands for the public benefit of the church, and building houses of [bworship](#note35b), and building up of the [cNew Jerusalem](#note35c) which is hereafter to be revealed—

36 That my covenant people may be gathered in one in that day when I shall [acome](#note36a) to my [btemple](#note36b). And this I do for the salvation of my people.

37 And it shall come to pass, that he that sinneth and repenteth not shall be [acast out](#note37a) of the church, and shall not receive again that which he has [bconsecrated](#note37b) unto the poor and the needy of my church, or in other words, unto me—

38 For inasmuch as ye [ado](#note38a) it unto the least of these, ye do it unto me.

39 For it shall come to pass, that which I spake by the mouths of my prophets shall be fulfilled; for I will consecrate of the [ariches](#note39a) of those who embrace my gospel among the Gentiles unto the [bpoor](#note39b) of my people who are of the house of Israel.

40 And again, thou shalt not be [aproud](#note40a) in thy [bheart](#note40b); let all thy [cgarments](#note40c) be plain, and their [dbeauty](#note40d) the beauty of the [ework](#note40e) of thine own hands;

41 And let all things be done in [acleanliness](#note41a) before me.

42 Thou shalt not be [aidle](#note42a); for he that is idle shall not eat the [bbread](#note42b) nor wear the garments of the [claborer](#note42c).

43 And whosoever among you are [asick](#note43a), and have not faith to be healed, but believe, shall be [bnourished](#note43b) with all tenderness, with herbs and mild [cfood](#note43c), and that not by the hand of an enemy.

44 And the elders of the church, two or more, shall be called, and shall pray for and [alay](#note44a) their [bhands](#note44b) upon them in my name; and if they [cdie](#note44c) they shall [ddie](#note44d) unto me, and if they live they shall live unto me.

45 Thou shalt [alive](#note45a) together in [blove](#note45b), insomuch that thou shalt [cweep](#note45c) for the loss of them that die, and more especially for those that have not [dhope](#note45d) of a glorious resurrection.

46 And it shall come to pass that those that die in me shall not [ataste](#note46a) of [bdeath](#note46b), for it shall be [csweet](#note46c) unto them;

47 And they that die not in me, wo unto them, for their death is bitter.

48 And again, it shall come to pass that he that hath [afaith](#note48a) in me to be [bhealed](#note48b), and is not [cappointed](#note48c) unto death, shall be [dhealed](#note48d).

49 He who hath faith to see shall see.

50 He who hath faith to hear shall hear.

51 The lame who hath faith to leap shall leap.

52 And they who have not faith to do these things, but believe in me, have [apower](#note52a) to become my [bsons](#note52b); and inasmuch as they break not my laws thou shalt [cbear](#note52c) their infirmities.

53 Thou shalt [astand](#note53a) in the place of thy stewardship.

54 Thou shalt not take thy brother’s [agarment](#note54a); thou shalt [bpay](#note54b) for that which thou shalt receive of thy brother.

55 And if thou [aobtainest](#note55a) more than that which would be for thy support, thou shalt give it into my [bstorehouse](#note55b), that all things may be done according to that which I have said.

56 Thou shalt ask, and my [ascriptures](#note56a) shall be given as I have appointed, and they shall be [bpreserved](#note56b) in safety;

57 And it is expedient that thou shouldst hold thy peace concerning them, and [anot](#note57a) teach them until ye have received them in full.

58 And I give unto you a commandment that then ye shall teach them unto all men; for they shall be [ataught](#note58a) unto [ball](#note58b) [cnations](#note58c), kindreds, tongues and people.

59 Thou shalt take the things which thou hast received, which have been given unto thee in my scriptures for a law, to be my [alaw](#note59a) to govern my [bchurch](#note59b);

60 And he that [adoeth](#note60a) according to these things shall be saved, and he that doeth them not shall be [bdamned](#note60b) if he so continue.

61 If thou shalt ask, thou shalt receive [arevelation](#note61a) upon revelation, [bknowledge](#note61b) upon knowledge, that thou mayest know the [cmysteries](#note61c) and [dpeaceable](#note61d) things—that which bringeth [ejoy](#note61e), that which bringeth life eternal.

62 Thou shalt ask, and it shall be revealed unto you in mine own due time where the [aNew Jerusalem](#note62a) shall be built.

63 And behold, it shall come to pass that my servants shall be sent forth to the east and to the west, to the north and to the south.

64 And even now, let him that goeth to the east teach them that shall be converted to flee to the [awest](#note64a), and this in consequence of [bthat](#note64b) which is coming on the earth, and of [csecret combinations](#note64c).

65 Behold, thou shalt observe all these things, and great shall be thy [areward](#note65a); for unto you it is given to know the [bmysteries](#note65b) of the kingdom, but unto the world it is not given to know them.

66 Ye shall observe the laws which ye have received and be faithful.

67 And ye shall hereafter receive church [acovenants](#note67a), such as shall be sufficient to establish you, both here and in the New Jerusalem.

68 Therefore, he that lacketh [awisdom](#note68a), let him ask of me, and I will give him liberally and upbraid him not.

69 Lift up your hearts and rejoice, for unto you the [akingdom](#note69a), or in other words, the [bkeys](#note69b) of the church have been given. Even so. Amen.

70 The priests and [ateachers](#note70a) shall have their [bstewardships](#note70b), even as the members.

71 And the elders or high priests who are appointed to assist the bishop as counselors in all things, are to have their families [asupported](#note71a) out of the property which is [bconsecrated](#note71b) to the bishop, for the good of the poor, and for other purposes, as before mentioned;

72 Or they are to receive a just remuneration for all their services, either a stewardship or otherwise, as may be thought best or decided by the counselors and bishop.

73 And the bishop, also, shall receive his support, or a just remuneration for all his services in the church.

74 Behold, verily I say unto you, that whatever persons among you, having put away their [acompanions](#note74a) for the cause of [bfornication](#note74b), or in other words, if they shall testify before you in all lowliness of heart that this is the case, ye shall not cast them out from among you;

75 But if ye shall find that any persons have left their companions for the sake of [aadultery](#note75a), and they themselves are the offenders, and their companions are living, they shall be [bcast out](#note75b) from among you.

76 And again, I say unto you, that ye shall be [awatchful](#note76a) and careful, with all inquiry, that ye receive none such among you if they are married;

77 And if they are not married, they shall repent of all their sins or ye shall not receive them.

78 And again, every person who belongeth to this church of Christ, shall observe to keep all the commandments and covenants of the church.

79 And it shall come to pass, that if any persons among you shall [akill](#note79a) they shall be delivered up and dealt with according to the laws of the land; for remember that he hath no forgiveness; and it shall be proved according to the laws of the land.

80 And if any man or woman shall commit [aadultery](#note80a), he or she shall be tried before two elders of the church, or more, and every word shall be established against him or her by two [bwitnesses](#note80b) of the church, and not of the enemy; but if there are more than two witnesses it is better.

81 But he or she shall be condemned by the mouth of two witnesses; and the elders shall lay the case before the [achurch](#note81a), and the church shall lift up their hands against him or her, that they may be dealt with according to the [blaw](#note81b) of God.

82 And if it can be, it is necessary that the [abishop](#note82a) be present also.

83 And thus ye shall do in all cases which shall come before you.

84 And if a man or woman shall rob, he or she shall be delivered up unto the law of the land.

85 And if he or she shall [asteal](#note85a), he or she shall be delivered up unto the law of the land.

86 And if he or she shall [alie](#note86a), he or she shall be delivered up unto the law of the land.

87 And if he or she do any manner of [ainiquity](#note87a), he or she shall be delivered up unto the law, even that of God.

88 And if thy [abrother](#note88a) or sister [boffend](#note88b) thee, thou shalt take him or her between him or her and thee alone; and if he or she [cconfess](#note88c) thou shalt be [dreconciled](#note88d).

89 And if he or she confess not thou shalt deliver him or her up unto the church, not to the members, but to the elders. And it shall be done in a [ameeting](#note89a), and that not before the world.

90 And if thy brother or sister offend many, he or she shall be [achastened](#note90a) before many.

91 And if any one offend [aopenly](#note91a), he or she shall be rebuked openly, that he or she may be [bashamed](#note91b). And if he or she confess not, he or she shall be delivered up unto the law of God.

92 If any shall offend in secret, he or she shall be rebuked in secret, that he or she may have opportunity to confess in secret to him or her whom he or she has offended, and to God, that the church may not speak reproachfully of him or her.

93 And thus shall ye conduct in all things.

Section 43

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, in February 1831. At this time some members of the Church were disturbed by people making false claims as revelators. The Prophet inquired of the Lord and received this communication addressed to the elders of the Church. The first part deals with matters of Church polity; the latter part contains a warning that the elders are to give to the nations of the earth.

[1–7](43.xhtml#p1), Revelations and commandments come only through the one appointed; [8–14](43.xhtml#p8), The Saints are sanctified by acting in all holiness before the Lord; [15–22](43.xhtml#p15), Elders are sent forth to cry repentance and prepare men for the great day of the Lord; [23–28](43.xhtml#p23), The Lord calls upon men by His own voice and through the forces of nature; [29–35](43.xhtml#p29), The Millennium and the binding of Satan will come.

1 O hearken, ye elders of my church, and give ear to the words which I shall speak unto you.

2 For behold, verily, verily, I say unto you, that ye have received a commandment for a [alaw](#note2a) unto my church, through him whom I have appointed unto you to receive commandments and [brevelations](#note2b) from my hand.

3 And this ye shall know assuredly—that there is [anone](#note3a) other appointed unto you to receive [bcommandments](#note3b) and revelations until he be taken, if he [cabide](#note3c) in me.

4 But verily, verily, I say unto you, that [anone](#note4a) else shall be appointed unto this gift except it be through him; for if it be taken from him he shall not have power except to appoint another in his stead.

5 And this shall be a law unto you, that ye [areceive](#note5a) not the [bteachings](#note5b) of any that shall come before you as revelations or commandments;

6 And this I give unto you that you may not be [adeceived](#note6a), that you may know they are not of me.

7 For verily I say unto you, that he that is [aordained](#note7a) of me shall come in at the [bgate](#note7b) and be ordained as I have told you before, to teach those [crevelations](#note7c) which you have received and shall receive through him whom I have appointed.

8 And now, behold, I give unto you a [acommandment](#note8a), that when ye are [bassembled](#note8b) together ye shall [cinstruct](#note8c) and [dedify](#note8d) each other, that ye may know [ehow](#note8e) to act and direct my church, how to act upon the points of my law and commandments, which I have given.

9 And thus ye shall become instructed in the law of my church, and be [asanctified](#note9a) by that which ye have received, and ye shall bind yourselves to act in all holiness before me—

10 That inasmuch as ye do this, glory shall be [aadded](#note10a) to the kingdom which ye have received. Inasmuch as ye do it not, it shall be [btaken](#note10b), even that which ye have received.

11 [aPurge](#note11a) ye out the [biniquity](#note11b) which is among you; sanctify yourselves before me;

12 And if ye desire the glories of the kingdom, [aappoint](#note12a) ye my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., and uphold him before me by the prayer of faith.

13 And again, I say unto you, that if ye desire the [amysteries](#note13a) of the kingdom, provide for him food and raiment, and whatsoever thing he needeth to accomplish the work wherewith I have commanded him;

14 And if ye do it not he shall remain unto them that have received him, that I may reserve unto myself a [apure](#note14a) [bpeople](#note14b) before me.

15 Again I say, hearken ye elders of my [achurch](#note15a), whom I have appointed: Ye are not sent forth to be [btaught](#note15b), but to [cteach](#note15c) the children of men the things which I have put into your hands by the power of my [dSpirit](#note15d);

16 And ye are to be [ataught](#note16a) from on high. [bSanctify](#note16b) yourselves and ye shall be [cendowed](#note16c) with power, that ye may give even as I have spoken.

17 Hearken ye, for, behold, the [agreat](#note17a) [bday](#note17b) of the Lord is nigh at hand.

18 For the day cometh that the Lord shall utter his [avoice](#note18a) out of heaven; the heavens shall [bshake](#note18b) and the earth shall [ctremble](#note18c), and the [dtrump](#note18d) of God shall sound both long and loud, and shall say to the sleeping nations: Ye saints [earise](#note18e) and live; ye sinners [fstay](#note18f) and [gsleep](#note18g) until I shall call again.

19 Wherefore gird up your loins lest ye be found among the wicked.

20 Lift up your voices and spare not. Call upon the nations to repent, both old and young, both [abond](#note20a) and free, saying: Prepare yourselves for the great day of the Lord;

21 For if I, who am a man, do lift up my voice and call upon you to repent, and ye [ahate](#note21a) me, what will ye say when the [bday](#note21b) cometh when the [cthunders](#note21c) shall utter their voices from the ends of the earth, speaking to the ears of all that live, saying—Repent, and prepare for the great day of the Lord?

22 Yea, and again, when the [alightnings](#note22a) shall streak forth from the east unto the west, and shall utter forth their voices unto all that live, and make the ears of all tingle that hear, saying these words—Repent ye, for the great day of the Lord is come?

23 And again, the Lord shall utter his voice out of heaven, saying: Hearken, O ye nations of the earth, and hear the words of that God who [amade](#note23a) you.

24 O, ye nations of the earth, how often would I have gathered you together as a [ahen](#note24a) gathereth her chickens under her wings, but ye [bwould not](#note24b)!

25 How oft have I [acalled upon](#note25a) you by the mouth of my [bservants](#note25b), and by the [cministering](#note25c) of angels, and by mine own voice, and by the voice of [dthunderings](#note25d), and by the voice of lightnings, and by the voice of tempests, and by the voice of earthquakes, and great hailstorms, and by the voice of [efamines](#note25e) and pestilences of every kind, and by the great sound of a [ftrump](#note25f), and by the voice of judgment, and by the voice of [gmercy](#note25g) all the day long, and by the voice of glory and honor and the [hriches](#note25h) of eternal life, and would have saved you with an [ieverlasting](#note25i) salvation, but ye would not!

26 Behold, the day has come, when the [acup](#note26a) of the [bwrath](#note26b) of mine indignation is full.

27 Behold, verily I say unto you, that these are the words of the Lord your God.

28 Wherefore, labor ye, [alabor](#note28a) ye in my vineyard for the last time—for the last time call upon the inhabitants of the earth.

29 For in mine own due time will I [acome](#note29a) upon the earth in [bjudgment](#note29b), and my people shall be [credeemed](#note29c) and shall [dreign](#note29d) with me on earth.

30 For the great [aMillennium](#note30a), of which I have spoken by the mouth of my servants, shall come.

31 For [aSatan](#note31a) shall be [bbound](#note31b), and when he is loosed again he shall only reign for a [clittle season](#note31c), and then cometh the [dend](#note31d) of the earth.

32 And he that liveth in [arighteousness](#note32a) shall be [bchanged](#note32b) in the twinkling of an eye, and the earth shall pass away so as by [cfire](#note32c).

33 And the wicked shall go away into unquenchable [afire](#note33a), and their end no man knoweth on earth, nor ever shall know, until they come before me in [bjudgment](#note33b).

34 Hearken ye to these words. Behold, I am Jesus Christ, the [aSavior](#note34a) of the world. [bTreasure](#note34b) these things up in your hearts, and let the [csolemnities](#note34c) of [deternity](#note34d) [erest](#note34e) upon your [fminds](#note34f).

35 Be [asober](#note35a). Keep all my commandments. Even so. Amen.

Section 44

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet and Sidney Rigdon, at Kirtland, Ohio, in the latter part of February 1831. In compliance with the requirement herein set forth, the Church appointed a conference to be held early in the month of June following.

[1–3](44.xhtml#p1), Elders are to assemble in conference; [4–6](44.xhtml#p4), They are to organize according to the laws of the land and to care for the poor.

1 Behold, thus saith the Lord unto you my servants, it is expedient in me that the elders of my church should be [acalled](#note1a) together, from the east and from the west, and from the north and from the south, by letter or some other way.

2 And it shall come to pass, that inasmuch as they are faithful, and exercise faith in me, I will pour out my [aSpirit](#note2a) upon them in the day that they assemble themselves together.

3 And it shall come to pass that they shall go forth into the regions round about, and [apreach](#note3a) repentance unto the people.

4 And many shall be [aconverted](#note4a), insomuch that ye shall obtain [bpower](#note4b) to organize yourselves [caccording](#note4c) to the laws of man;

5 That your [aenemies](#note5a) may not have power over you; that you may be preserved in all things; that you may be enabled to keep my laws; that every bond may be broken wherewith the enemy seeketh to destroy my people.

6 Behold, I say unto you, that ye must [avisit](#note6a) the poor and the needy and administer to their relief, that they may be kept until all things may be done according to my law which ye have received. Amen.

Section 45

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to the Church, at Kirtland, Ohio, March 7, 1831. Prefacing the record of this revelation, Joseph Smith’s history states that “at this age of the Church … many false reports … and foolish stories, were published … and circulated, … to prevent people from investigating the work, or embracing the faith. … But to the joy of the Saints, … I received the following.”

[1–5](45.xhtml#p1), Christ is our advocate with the Father; [6–10](45.xhtml#p6), The gospel is a messenger to prepare the way before the Lord; [11–15](45.xhtml#p11), Enoch and his brethren were received by the Lord unto Himself; [16–23](45.xhtml#p16), Christ revealed signs of His coming as given on the Mount of Olives; [24–38](45.xhtml#p24), The gospel will be restored, the times of the Gentiles will be fulfilled, and a desolating sickness will cover the land; [39–47](45.xhtml#p39), Signs, wonders, and the Resurrection are to attend the Second Coming; [48–53](45.xhtml#p48), Christ will stand on the Mount of Olives, and the Jews will see the wounds in His hands and feet; [54–59](45.xhtml#p54), The Lord will reign during the Millennium; [60–62](45.xhtml#p60), The Prophet is instructed to begin the translation of the New Testament, through which important information will be made known; [63–75](45.xhtml#p63), The Saints are commanded to gather and build the New Jerusalem, to which people from all nations will come.

1 Hearken, O ye people of my [achurch](#note1a), to whom the [bkingdom](#note1b) has been given; hearken ye and give ear to him who laid the foundation of the earth, who [cmade](#note1c) the heavens and all the [dhosts](#note1d) thereof, and by whom all things were made which [elive](#note1e), and move, and have a being.

2 And again I say, hearken unto my voice, lest [adeath](#note2a) shall overtake you; in an [bhour](#note2b) when ye think not the summer shall be past, and the [charvest](#note2c) ended, and your souls not saved.

3 Listen to him who is the [aadvocate](#note3a) with the Father, who is pleading your cause before him—

4 Saying: Father, behold the [asufferings](#note4a) and [bdeath](#note4b) of him who did no [csin](#note4c), in whom thou wast well pleased; behold the blood of thy Son which was shed, the blood of him whom thou gavest that thyself might be [dglorified](#note4d);

5 Wherefore, Father, spare these my [abrethren](#note5a) that [bbelieve](#note5b) on my name, that they may come unto me and have [ceverlasting life](#note5c).

6 Hearken, O ye people of my church, and ye elders listen together, and hear my voice while it is called [atoday](#note6a), and harden not your hearts;

7 For verily I say unto you that I am [aAlpha and Omega](#note7a), the beginning and the end, the light and the life of the world—a [blight](#note7b) that shineth in darkness and the darkness comprehendeth it not.

8 I came unto mine own, and mine own [areceived me not](#note8a); but unto as many as received me gave I [bpower](#note8b) to do many [cmiracles](#note8c), and to become the [dsons](#note8d) of God; and even unto them that [ebelieved](#note8e) on my name gave I power to obtain eternal [flife](#note8f).

9 And even so I have sent mine [aeverlasting](#note9a) [bcovenant](#note9b) into the world, to be a [clight](#note9c) to the world, and to be a [dstandard](#note9d) for my people, and for the [eGentiles](#note9e) to seek to it, and to be a [fmessenger](#note9f) before my face to prepare the way before me.

10 Wherefore, come ye unto it, and with him that cometh I will [areason](#note10a) as with men in days of old, and I will show unto you my strong reasoning.

11 Wherefore, hearken ye together and let me show unto you even my [awisdom](#note11a)—the wisdom of him whom ye say is the God of [bEnoch](#note11b), and his brethren,

12 Who were [aseparated](#note12a) from the earth, and were received unto myself—a [bcity](#note12b) reserved until a [cday](#note12c) of righteousness shall come—a day which was sought for by all holy men, and they found it not because of wickedness and abominations;

13 And confessed they were [astrangers](#note13a) and pilgrims on the earth;

14 But obtained a [apromise](#note14a) that they should find it and see it in their flesh.

15 Wherefore, hearken and I will reason with you, and I will [aspeak](#note15a) unto you and prophesy, as unto men in days of old.

16 And I will show it plainly as I showed it unto my [adisciples](#note16a) as I stood before them in the flesh, and spake unto them, saying: As ye have asked of me concerning the [bsigns](#note16b) of my coming, in the day when I shall come in my [cglory](#note16c) in the clouds of heaven, to fulfil the promises that I have made unto your fathers,

17 For as ye have looked upon the long [aabsence](#note17a) of your [bspirits](#note17b) from your bodies to be a bondage, I will show unto you how the day of redemption shall come, and also the [crestoration](#note17c) of the [dscattered](#note17d) Israel.

18 And now ye [abehold](#note18a) this temple which is in Jerusalem, which ye call the house of God, and your enemies say that this house shall never fall.

19 But, verily I say unto you, that [adesolation](#note19a) shall come upon this generation as a thief in the night, and this people shall be destroyed and [bscattered](#note19b) among all nations.

20 And this [atemple](#note20a) which ye now see shall be thrown down that there shall not be left one stone upon another.

21 And it shall come to pass, that this [ageneration](#note21a) of Jews shall not pass away until every desolation which I have told you concerning them shall come [bto pass](#note21b).

22 Ye say that ye know that the [aend](#note22a) of the world cometh; ye say also that ye know that the heavens and the earth shall pass away;

23 And in this ye say truly, for so it is; but these things which I have told you shall not [apass away](#note23a) until all shall be fulfilled.

24 And this I have told you concerning Jerusalem; and when that day shall come, shall a remnant be [ascattered](#note24a) among all [bnations](#note24b);

25 But they shall be [agathered](#note25a) again; but they shall remain until the times of the [bGentiles](#note25b) be fulfilled.

26 And in [athat day](#note26a) shall be heard of [bwars](#note26b) and rumors of wars, and the whole earth shall be in commotion, and men’s hearts shall [cfail](#note26c) them, and they shall say that Christ [ddelayeth](#note26d) his coming until the end of the earth.

27 And the [alove](#note27a) of men shall wax cold, and [biniquity](#note27b) shall abound.

28 And when the times of the [aGentiles](#note28a) is come in, a [blight](#note28b) shall break forth among them that sit in darkness, and it shall be the fulness of my [cgospel](#note28c);

29 But they [areceive](#note29a) it not; for they perceive not the light, and they turn their [bhearts](#note29b) from me because of the [cprecepts](#note29c) of men.

30 And in that generation shall the [atimes](#note30a) of the Gentiles be fulfilled.

31 And there shall be men standing in that [ageneration](#note31a), that shall not pass until they shall see an overflowing [bscourge](#note31b); for a desolating [csickness](#note31c) shall cover the land.

32 But my disciples shall [astand](#note32a) in holy places, and shall not be moved; but among the wicked, men shall lift up their voices and [bcurse](#note32b) God and die.

33 And there shall be [aearthquakes](#note33a) also in divers places, and many desolations; yet men will harden their hearts against me, and they will take up the [bsword](#note33b), one against another, and they will kill one another.

34 And now, when I the Lord had spoken these words unto my disciples, they were troubled.

35 And I said unto them: Be not [atroubled](#note35a), for, when all these things shall come to pass, ye may know that the promises which have been made unto you shall be fulfilled.

36 And when the [alight](#note36a) shall begin to break forth, it shall be with them like unto a parable which I will show you—

37 Ye look and behold the [afig trees](#note37a), and ye see them with your eyes, and ye say when they begin to shoot forth, and their leaves are yet tender, that summer is now nigh at hand;

38 Even so it shall be in that day when they shall see all these things, then shall they know that the hour is nigh.

39 And it shall come to pass that he that [afeareth](#note39a) me shall be [blooking](#note39b) forth for the great [cday](#note39c) of the Lord to [dcome](#note39d), even for the [esigns](#note39e) of the coming of the [fSon of Man](#note39f).

40 And they shall see signs and [awonders](#note40a), for they shall be shown forth in the heavens above, and in the earth beneath.

41 And they shall behold blood, and [afire](#note41a), and vapors of [bsmoke](#note41b).

42 And before the day of the Lord shall come, the [asun](#note42a) shall be darkened, and the moon be turned into blood, and the stars fall from heaven.

43 And the [aremnant](#note43a) shall be gathered unto this place;

44 And then they shall look for me, and, behold, I will come; and they shall see me in the [aclouds](#note44a) of heaven, clothed with power and great [bglory](#note44b); with all the holy angels; and he that [cwatches](#note44c) not for me shall be cut off.

45 But before the arm of the Lord shall fall, an angel shall sound his [atrump](#note45a), and the [bsaints](#note45b) that have slept shall [ccome](#note45c) forth to meet me in the [dcloud](#note45d).

46 Wherefore, if ye have slept in [apeace](#note46a) blessed are you; for as you now behold me and know that I am, even so shall ye [bcome](#note46b) unto me and your souls shall [clive](#note46c), and your redemption shall be perfected; and the saints shall come forth from the [dfour](#note46d) quarters of the earth.

47 Then shall the [aarm](#note47a) of the Lord fall upon the nations.

48 And then shall the Lord set his foot upon this [amount](#note48a), and it shall cleave in twain, and the earth shall [btremble](#note48b), and reel to and fro, and the [cheavens](#note48c) also [dshall shake](#note48d).

49 And the Lord shall utter his voice, and all the ends of the earth shall hear it; and the nations of the earth shall [amourn](#note49a), and they that have [blaughed](#note49b) shall see their [cfolly](#note49c).

50 And calamity shall cover the [amocker](#note50a), and the scorner shall be consumed; and they that have watched for iniquity shall be hewn down and [bcast](#note50b) into the [cfire](#note50c).

51 And then shall the [aJews](#note51a) [blook](#note51b) upon me and say: What are these [cwounds](#note51c) in thine hands and in thy feet?

52 Then shall they know that I am the Lord; for I will say unto them: These wounds are the wounds with which I was [awounded](#note52a) in the house of my friends. I am he who was lifted up. I am Jesus that was [bcrucified](#note52b). I am the [cSon of God](#note52c).

53 And then shall they [aweep](#note53a) because of their iniquities; then shall they [blament](#note53b) because they [cpersecuted](#note53c) their [dking](#note53d).

54 And then shall the [aheathen](#note54a) nations be [bredeemed](#note54b), and they that [cknew no](#note54c) [dlaw](#note54d) shall have part in the [efirst](#note54e) [fresurrection](#note54f); and it shall be [gtolerable](#note54g) for them.

55 And [aSatan](#note55a) shall be [bbound](#note55b), that he shall have no place in the hearts of the children of men.

56 And at that day, when I shall come in my [aglory](#note56a), shall the parable be fulfilled which I spake concerning the ten [bvirgins](#note56b).

57 For they that are wise and have received the [atruth](#note57a), and have taken the Holy Spirit for their [bguide](#note57b), and have not been deceived—verily I say unto you, they shall not be hewn down and cast into the [cfire](#note57c), but shall abide the day.

58 And the [aearth](#note58a) shall be given unto them for an [binheritance](#note58b); and they shall [cmultiply](#note58c) and wax strong, and their [dchildren](#note58d) shall [egrow up](#note58e) without [fsin](#note58f) unto [gsalvation](#note58g).

59 For the Lord shall be in their [amidst](#note59a), and his [bglory](#note59b) shall be upon them, and he will be their [cking](#note59c) and their [dlawgiver](#note59d).

60 And now, behold, I say unto you, it shall not be given unto you to know any further concerning this chapter, until the [aNew Testament](#note60a) be translated, and in it all these things shall be made known;

61 Wherefore I give unto you that ye may now translate it, that ye may be prepared for the things to come.

62 For verily I say unto you, that great things await you;

63 Ye hear of [awars](#note63a) in foreign lands; but, behold, I say unto you, they are nigh, even at your [bdoors](#note63b), and not many years hence ye shall hear of wars in your own lands.

64 Wherefore I, the Lord, have said, gather ye out from the [aeastern](#note64a) lands, assemble ye yourselves together ye elders of my church; go ye forth into the western countries, call upon the inhabitants to repent, and inasmuch as they do repent, build up churches unto me.

65 And with one heart and with one mind, gather up your riches that ye may [apurchase](#note65a) an inheritance which shall hereafter be appointed unto you.

66 And it shall be called the [aNew Jerusalem](#note66a), a [bland](#note66b) of [cpeace](#note66c), a city of [drefuge](#note66d), a place of [esafety](#note66e) for the saints of the Most High God;

67 And the [aglory](#note67a) of the Lord shall be there, and the [bterror](#note67b) of the Lord also shall be there, insomuch that the wicked will not come unto it, and it shall be called Zion.

68 And it shall come to pass among the wicked, that every man that will not take his sword against his [aneighbor](#note68a) must needs flee unto [bZion](#note68b) for safety.

69 And there shall be [agathered](#note69a) unto it out of every [bnation](#note69b) under heaven; and it shall be the only people that shall not be at [cwar](#note69c) one with another.

70 And it shall be said among the wicked: Let us not go up to battle against Zion, for the inhabitants of Zion are [aterrible](#note70a); wherefore we cannot stand.

71 And it shall come to pass that the righteous shall be gathered out from among all nations, and shall come to Zion, singing with [asongs](#note71a) of everlasting [bjoy](#note71b).

72 And now I say unto you, keep these things from going abroad unto the world until it is expedient in me, that ye may accomplish this work in the eyes of the people, and in the eyes of your enemies, that they may not know your works until ye have accomplished the thing which I have commanded you;

73 That when they shall know it, that they may consider these things.

74 For when the Lord shall appear he shall be [aterrible](#note74a) unto them, that fear may seize upon them, and they shall stand afar off and tremble.

75 And all nations shall be afraid because of the terror of the Lord, and the power of his might. Even so. Amen.
