Section 46

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to the Church, at Kirtland, Ohio, March 8, 1831. In this early time of the Church, a unified pattern for the conducting of Church services had not yet developed. However, a custom of admitting only members and earnest investigators to the sacrament meetings and other assemblies of the Church had become somewhat general. This revelation expresses the will of the Lord relative to governing and conducting meetings and His direction on seeking and discerning the gifts of the Spirit.

[1–2](46.xhtml#p1), Elders are to conduct meetings as guided by the Holy Spirit; [3–6](46.xhtml#p3), Truth seekers should not be excluded from sacramental services; [7–12](46.xhtml#p7), Ask of God and seek the gifts of the Spirit; [13–26](46.xhtml#p13), An enumeration of some of these gifts is given; [27–33](46.xhtml#p27), Church leaders are given power to discern the gifts of the Spirit.

1 Hearken, O ye people of my church; for verily I say unto you that these things were spoken unto you for your [aprofit](#note1a) and learning.

2 But notwithstanding those things which are written, it always has been given to the [aelders](#note2a) of my church from the beginning, and ever shall be, to [bconduct](#note2b) all meetings as they are directed and guided by the Holy Spirit.

3 Nevertheless ye are commanded never to [acast](#note3a) any one out from your public [bmeetings](#note3b), which are held before the world.

4 Ye are also commanded not to cast any one who belongeth to the church out of your sacrament meetings; nevertheless, if any have trespassed, let him [anot](#note4a) [bpartake](#note4b) until he makes reconciliation.

5 And again I say unto you, ye shall not cast any out of your sacrament meetings who are earnestly [aseeking](#note5a) the kingdom—I speak this concerning those who are not of the church.

6 And again I say unto you, concerning your [aconfirmation meetings](#note6a), that if there be any that are not of the church, that are earnestly seeking after the kingdom, ye shall not cast them out.

7 But ye are commanded in all things to [aask](#note7a) of God, who giveth liberally; and that which the Spirit testifies unto you even so I would that ye should do in all [bholiness](#note7b) of heart, walking uprightly before me, [cconsidering](#note7c) the end of your salvation, doing all things with prayer and [dthanksgiving](#note7d), that ye may not be [eseduced](#note7e) by evil [fspirits](#note7f), or doctrines of devils, or the [gcommandments](#note7g) of men; for some are of men, and others of devils.

8 Wherefore, beware lest ye are deceived; and that ye may not be deceived [aseek](#note8a) ye earnestly the best gifts, always remembering for what they are given;

9 For verily I say unto you, they are given for the benefit of those who love me and keep all my commandments, and him that seeketh so to do; that all may be benefited that seek or that ask of me, that ask and not for a [asign](#note9a) that they may [bconsume](#note9b) it upon their lusts.

10 And again, verily I say unto you, I would that ye should always remember, and always retain in your [aminds](#note10a) what those [bgifts](#note10b) are, that are given unto the church.

11 For all have not every [agift](#note11a) given unto them; for there are many gifts, and to every man is given a gift by the Spirit of God.

12 To some is given one, and to some is given another, that all may be profited thereby.

13 To some it is given by the [aHoly Ghost](#note13a) to know that Jesus Christ is the Son of God, and that he was crucified for the sins of the world.

14 To others it is given to [abelieve](#note14a) on their words, that they also might have eternal life if they continue faithful.

15 And again, to some it is given by the Holy Ghost to know the [adifferences](#note15a) of administration, as it will be pleasing unto the same Lord, according as the Lord will, suiting his [bmercies](#note15b) according to the conditions of the children of men.

16 And again, it is given by the Holy Ghost to some to know the diversities of operations, whether they be of God, that the manifestations of the [aSpirit](#note16a) may be given to every man to profit withal.

17 And again, verily I say unto you, to some is given, by the Spirit of God, the word of [awisdom](#note17a).

18 To another is given the word of [aknowledge](#note18a), that all may be taught to be wise and to have knowledge.

19 And again, to some it is given to have [afaith](#note19a) to be healed;

20 And to others it is given to have faith to [aheal](#note20a).

21 And again, to some is given the working of [amiracles](#note21a);

22 And to others it is given to [aprophesy](#note22a);

23 And to others the [adiscerning](#note23a) of spirits.

24 And again, it is given to some to speak with [atongues](#note24a);

25 And to another is given the interpretation of tongues.

26 And all these [agifts](#note26a) come from God, for the benefit of the [bchildren](#note26b) of God.

27 And unto the [abishop](#note27a) of the church, and unto such as God shall appoint and ordain to watch over the church and to be elders unto the church, are to have it given unto them to [bdiscern](#note27b) all those gifts lest there shall be any among you professing and yet be not of God.

28 And it shall come to pass that he that asketh in [aSpirit](#note28a) shall receive in Spirit;

29 That unto some it may be given to have all those gifts, that there may be a head, in order that every member may be profited thereby.

30 He that [aasketh](#note30a) in the [bSpirit](#note30b) asketh according to the [cwill](#note30c) of God; wherefore it is done even as he asketh.

31 And again, I say unto you, all things must be done in the name of Christ, whatsoever you do in the Spirit;

32 And ye must give [athanks](#note32a) unto God in the Spirit for whatsoever blessing ye are blessed with.

33 And ye must practice [avirtue](#note33a) and holiness before me continually. Even so. Amen.

Section 47

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, March 8, 1831. John Whitmer, who had already served as a clerk to the Prophet, initially hesitated when he was asked to serve as the Church historian and recorder, replacing Oliver Cowdery. He wrote, “I would rather not do it but observed that the will of the Lord be done, and if he desires it, I desire that he would manifest it through Joseph the Seer.” After Joseph Smith received this revelation, John Whitmer accepted and served in his appointed office.

[1–4](47.xhtml#p1), John Whitmer is designated to keep the history of the Church and to write for the Prophet.

1 Behold, it is expedient in me that my servant John should write and keep a regular [ahistory](#note1a), and assist you, my servant Joseph, in transcribing all things which shall be given you, until he is called to further duties.

2 Again, verily I say unto you that he can also [alift up](#note2a) his voice in meetings, whenever it shall be expedient.

3 And again, I say unto you that it shall be appointed unto him to keep the church [arecord](#note3a) and history continually; for Oliver Cowdery I have appointed to another office.

4 Wherefore, it shall be given him, inasmuch as he is faithful, by the [aComforter](#note4a), to write these things. Even so. Amen.

* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1a)

  1a

  [D&C 21:1](21.xhtml#p1); [69:3 (3–8)](69.xhtml#p3); [85:1](85.xhtml#p1).

  TG Preaching.

Section 48

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, March 10, 1831. The Prophet had inquired of the Lord as to the mode of procedure in procuring lands for the settlement of the Saints. This was an important matter in view of the migration of members of the Church from the eastern United States, in obedience to the Lord’s command that they should assemble in Ohio (see [sections 37:1–3](37.xhtml#p1); [45:64](45.xhtml#p64)).

[1–3](48.xhtml#p1), The Saints in Ohio are to share their lands with their brethren; [4–6](48.xhtml#p4), The Saints are to purchase lands, build a city, and follow the counsel of their presiding officers.

1 It is necessary that ye should remain for the present time in your places of abode, as it shall be suitable to your circumstances.

2 And inasmuch as ye have lands, ye shall [aimpart](#note2a) to the [beastern](#note2b) brethren;

3 And inasmuch as ye have not lands, let them buy for the present time in those regions round about, as seemeth them good, for it must needs be necessary that they have places to live for the present time.

4 It must needs be necessary that ye [asave](#note4a) all the money that ye can, and that ye obtain all that ye can in righteousness, that in time ye may be enabled to [bpurchase](#note4b) [cland](#note4c) for an [dinheritance](#note4d), even the city.

5 The [aplace](#note5a) is not yet to be [brevealed](#note5b); but after your brethren come from the east there are to be certain men appointed, and to them it shall be given to know the place, or to them it shall be revealed.

6 And they shall be appointed to [apurchase](#note6a) the lands, and to make a commencement to lay the foundation of the city; and then shall ye begin to be gathered with your families, every man according to his [bfamily](#note6b), according to his circumstances, and as is appointed to him by the presidency and the bishop of the church, according to the laws and commandments which ye have received, and which ye shall hereafter receive. Even so. Amen.

Section 49

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Sidney Rigdon, Parley P. Pratt, and Leman Copley, at Kirtland, Ohio, May 7, 1831. Leman Copley had embraced the gospel but still held to some of the teachings of the Shakers (United Society of Believers in Christ’s Second Appearing), to which he had formerly belonged. Some of the beliefs of the Shakers were that Christ’s Second Coming had already occurred and that He had appeared in the form of a woman, Ann Lee. They did not consider baptism by water essential. They rejected marriage and believed in a life of total celibacy. Some Shakers also forbade the eating of meat. In prefacing this revelation, Joseph Smith’s history states, “In order to have [a] more perfect understanding on the subject, I inquired of the Lord, and received the following.” The revelation refutes some of the basic concepts of the Shaker group. The aforementioned brethren took a copy of the revelation to the Shaker community (near Cleveland, Ohio) and read it to them in its entirety, but it was rejected.

[1–7](49.xhtml#p1), The day and hour of Christ’s coming will remain unknown until He comes; [8–14](49.xhtml#p8), Men must repent, believe the gospel, and obey the ordinances to gain salvation; [15–16](49.xhtml#p15), Marriage is ordained of God; [17–21](49.xhtml#p17), The eating of meat is approved; [22–28](49.xhtml#p22), Zion will flourish and the Lamanites blossom as the rose before the Second Coming.

1 Hearken unto my word, my servants Sidney, and Parley, and Leman; for behold, verily I say unto you, that I give unto you a commandment that you shall go and [apreach](#note1a) my gospel which ye have received, even as ye have received it, unto the Shakers.

2 Behold, I say unto you, that they desire to know the truth in part, but not all, for they are not [aright](#note2a) before me and must needs repent.

3 Wherefore, I send you, my servants Sidney and Parley, to preach the gospel unto them.

4 And my servant Leman shall be ordained unto this work, that he may reason with them, not according to that which he has received of them, but according to that which shall be [ataught](#note4a) him by you my servants; and by so doing I will bless him, otherwise he shall not prosper.

5 Thus saith the Lord; for I am God, and have [asent](#note5a) mine [bOnly Begotten Son](#note5b) into the world for the [credemption](#note5c) of the world, and have decreed that he that receiveth him shall be saved, and he that receiveth him not shall be [ddamned](#note5d)—

6 And they have done unto the [aSon of Man](#note6a) even as they listed; and he has taken his power on the [bright hand](#note6b) of his [cglory](#note6c), and now reigneth in the heavens, and will reign till he descends on the earth to put all enemies [dunder](#note6d) his feet, which time is nigh at hand—

7 I, the Lord God, have spoken it; but the hour and the [aday](#note7a) no man knoweth, neither the angels in heaven, nor shall they know until he comes.

8 Wherefore, I will that all men shall repent, for all are under [asin](#note8a), except those which I have [breserved](#note8b) unto myself, [choly](#note8c) men that ye know not of.

9 Wherefore, I say unto you that I have sent unto you mine everlasting [acovenant](#note9a), even that which was from the beginning.

10 And that which I have promised I have so fulfilled, and the [anations](#note10a) of the earth shall [bbow](#note10b) to it; and, if not of themselves, they shall come down, for that which is now exalted of itself shall be laid [clow](#note10c) of power.

11 Wherefore, I give unto you a commandment that ye [ago](#note11a) among this people, and say unto them, like unto mine apostle of old, whose name was [bPeter](#note11b):

12 [aBelieve](#note12a) on the name of the Lord Jesus, who was on the earth, and is to come, the beginning and the end;

13 [aRepent](#note13a) and be baptized in the name of Jesus Christ, according to the holy commandment, for the remission of sins;

14 And whoso doeth this shall receive the [agift](#note14a) of the Holy Ghost, by the laying on of the [bhands](#note14b) of the elders of the church.

15 And again, verily I say unto you, that whoso [aforbiddeth](#note15a) to marry is not ordained of God, for [bmarriage](#note15b) is ordained of God unto man.

16 Wherefore, it is lawful that he should have one [awife](#note16a), and they twain shall be [bone](#note16b) flesh, and all this that the [cearth](#note16c) might answer the end of its creation;

17 And that it might be filled with the measure of man, according to his [acreation](#note17a) [bbefore](#note17b) the world was made.

18 And whoso [aforbiddeth](#note18a) to [babstain](#note18b) from [cmeats](#note18c), that man should not eat the same, is not ordained of God;

19 For, behold, the [abeasts](#note19a) of the field and the fowls of the air, and that which cometh of the earth, is [bordained](#note19b) for the use of man for food and for [craiment](#note19c), and that he might have in abundance.

20 But it is not given that one man should [apossess](#note20a) that which is above another, wherefore the [bworld](#note20b) lieth in [csin](#note20c).

21 And wo be unto man that [asheddeth](#note21a) blood or that [bwasteth](#note21b) [cflesh](#note21c) and hath no need.

22 And again, verily I say unto you, that the Son of Man [acometh](#note22a) not in the form of a woman, neither of a man traveling on the earth.

23 Wherefore, be not [adeceived](#note23a), but continue in steadfastness, [blooking](#note23b) forth for the heavens to be [cshaken](#note23c), and the earth to tremble and to reel to and fro as a drunken man, and for the [dvalleys](#note23d) to be exalted, and for the [emountains](#note23e) to be made low, and for the rough places to become smooth—and all this when the angel shall sound his [ftrumpet](#note23f).

24 But before the great day of the Lord shall come, [aJacob](#note24a) shall flourish in the wilderness, and the Lamanites shall [bblossom](#note24b) as the rose.

25 Zion shall [aflourish](#note25a) upon the [bhills](#note25b) and rejoice upon the mountains, and shall be assembled together unto the place which I have appointed.

26 Behold, I say unto you, go forth as I have commanded you; repent of all your sins; [aask](#note26a) and ye shall receive; knock and it shall be opened unto you.

27 Behold, I will go before you and be your [arearward](#note27a); and I will be in your [bmidst](#note27b), and you shall not be [cconfounded](#note27c).

28 Behold, I am Jesus Christ, and I come [aquickly](#note28a). Even so. Amen.

Section 50

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, May 9, 1831. Joseph Smith’s history states that some of the elders did not understand the manifestations of different spirits abroad in the earth and that this revelation was given in response to his special inquiry on the matter. So-called spiritual phenomena were not uncommon among the members, some of whom claimed to be receiving visions and revelations.

[1–5](50.xhtml#p1), Many false spirits are abroad in the earth; [6–9](50.xhtml#p6), Wo unto the hypocrites and those who are cut off from the Church; [10–14](50.xhtml#p10), Elders are to preach the gospel by the Spirit; [15–22](50.xhtml#p15), Both preachers and hearers need to be enlightened by the Spirit; [23–25](50.xhtml#p23), That which doth not edify is not of God; [26–28](50.xhtml#p26), The faithful are possessors of all things; [29–36](50.xhtml#p29), The prayers of the purified are answered; [37–46](50.xhtml#p37), Christ is the Good Shepherd and the Stone of Israel.

1 Hearken, O ye elders of my church, and give ear to the [avoice](#note1a) of the living God; and attend to the words of wisdom which shall be given unto you, according as ye have asked and are agreed as touching the church, and the [bspirits](#note1b) which have gone abroad in the earth.

2 Behold, verily I say unto you, that there are many spirits which are false [aspirits](#note2a), which have gone forth in the earth, deceiving the world.

3 And also [aSatan](#note3a) hath sought to deceive you, that he might overthrow you.

4 Behold, I, the Lord, have looked upon you, and have seen [aabominations](#note4a) in the church that [bprofess](#note4b) my name.

5 But blessed are they who are faithful and [aendure](#note5a), whether in life or in death, for they shall inherit eternal life.

6 But wo unto them that are [adeceivers](#note6a) and hypocrites, for, thus saith the Lord, I will bring them to judgment.

7 Behold, verily I say unto you, there are [ahypocrites](#note7a) among you, who have deceived some, which has given the [badversary](#note7b) [cpower](#note7c); but behold [dsuch](#note7d) shall be reclaimed;

8 But the [ahypocrites](#note8a) shall be detected and shall be [bcut off](#note8b), either in life or in death, even as I will; and wo unto them who are cut off from my church, for the same are overcome of the world.

9 Wherefore, let every man beware lest he do that which is not in truth and righteousness before me.

10 And now come, saith the Lord, by the Spirit, unto the elders of his church, and let us [areason](#note10a) together, that ye may understand;

11 Let us reason even as a man reasoneth one with another face to face.

12 Now, when a man reasoneth he is understood of man, because he reasoneth as a man; even so will I, the Lord, reason with you that you may [aunderstand](#note12a).

13 Wherefore, I the Lord ask you this question—unto what were ye [aordained](#note13a)?

14 To preach my gospel by the [aSpirit](#note14a), even the [bComforter](#note14b) which was sent forth to teach the truth.

15 And then received ye [aspirits](#note15a) which ye could not understand, and received them to be of God; and in this are ye justified?

16 Behold ye shall answer this question yourselves; nevertheless, I will be [amerciful](#note16a) unto you; he that is weak among you hereafter shall be made [bstrong](#note16b).

17 Verily I say unto you, he that is ordained of me and sent forth to [apreach](#note17a) the word of truth by the Comforter, in the Spirit of truth, doth he [bpreach](#note17b) it by the Spirit of truth or some other way?

18 And if it be by some other way it is not of God.

19 And again, he that receiveth the word of truth, doth he receive it by the Spirit of truth or some other way?

20 If it be some other way it is not of God.

21 Therefore, why is it that ye cannot understand and know, that he that receiveth the word by the [aSpirit of truth](#note21a) receiveth it as it is preached by the Spirit of truth?

22 Wherefore, he that preacheth and he that receiveth, understand one another, and both are [aedified](#note22a) and [brejoice](#note22b) together.

23 And that which doth not [aedify](#note23a) is not of God, and is [bdarkness](#note23b).

24 That which is of God is [alight](#note24a); and he that [breceiveth](#note24b) [clight](#note24c), and [dcontinueth](#note24d) in God, receiveth more [elight](#note24e); and that light groweth brighter and brighter until the perfect day.

25 And again, verily I say unto you, and I say it that you may know the [atruth](#note25a), that you may chase darkness from among you;

26 He that is [aordained](#note26a) of God and sent forth, the same is appointed to be the [bgreatest](#note26b), notwithstanding he is the [cleast](#note26c) and the [dservant](#note26d) of all.

27 Wherefore, he is possessor of all things; for all things are [asubject](#note27a) unto him, both in heaven and on the earth, the life and the light, the Spirit and the [bpower](#note27b), sent forth by the will of the Father through Jesus Christ, his Son.

28 But no man is possessor of [aall](#note28a) things except he be [bpurified](#note28b) and [ccleansed](#note28c) from all sin.

29 And if ye are [apurified](#note29a) and cleansed from all [bsin](#note29b), ye shall [cask](#note29c) whatsoever you will in the name of Jesus and it shall be done.

30 But know this, it shall be given you what you shall [aask](#note30a); and as ye are appointed to the [bhead](#note30b), the spirits shall be subject unto you.

31 Wherefore, it shall come to pass, that if you behold a [aspirit](#note31a) manifested that you cannot understand, and you receive not that spirit, ye shall ask of the Father in the name of Jesus; and if he give not unto you that spirit, then you may know that it is not of God.

32 And it shall be given unto you, [apower](#note32a) over that spirit; and you shall proclaim against that spirit with a loud voice that it is [bnot of God](#note32b)—

33 Not with [arailing](#note33a) accusation, that ye be not overcome, neither with [bboasting](#note33b) nor rejoicing, lest you be seized therewith.

34 He that receiveth of God, let him [aaccount](#note34a) it of God; and let him rejoice that he is accounted of God worthy to receive.

35 And by giving heed and doing these things which ye have received, and which ye shall hereafter receive—and the [akingdom](#note35a) is given [byou](#note35b) of the Father, and [cpower](#note35c) to [dovercome](#note35d) all things which are not ordained of him—

36 And behold, verily I say unto you, blessed are you who are now hearing these words of mine from the mouth of my servant, for your sins are [aforgiven](#note36a) you.

37 Let my servant Joseph Wakefield, in whom I am well pleased, and my servant [aParley P. Pratt](#note37a) go forth among the churches and strengthen them by the word of [bexhortation](#note37b);

38 And also my servant John Corrill, or as many of my servants as are ordained unto this office, and let them labor in the [avineyard](#note38a); and let no man hinder them doing that which I have appointed unto them—

39 Wherefore, in this thing my servant [aEdward Partridge](#note39a) is not justified; nevertheless let him repent and he shall be forgiven.

40 Behold, ye are little children and ye cannot [abear](#note40a) all things now; ye must [bgrow](#note40b) in [cgrace](#note40c) and in the knowledge of the truth.

41 [aFear not](#note41a), little [bchildren](#note41b), for you are mine, and I have [covercome](#note41c) the world, and you are of them that my Father hath [dgiven](#note41d) me;

42 And none of them that my Father hath given me shall be [alost](#note42a).

43 And the Father and I are [aone](#note43a). I am [bin](#note43b) the Father and the Father in me; and inasmuch as ye have received me, ye are in me and I in you.

44 Wherefore, I am in your midst, and I am the [agood](#note44a) [bshepherd](#note44b), and the [cstone](#note44c) of Israel. He that buildeth upon this [drock](#note44d) shall never [efall](#note44e).

45 And the [aday](#note45a) cometh that you shall hear my voice and [bsee](#note45b) me, and [cknow](#note45c) that I am.

46 [aWatch](#note46a), therefore, that ye may be [bready](#note46b). Even so. Amen.

Section 51

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Thompson, Ohio, May 20, 1831. At this time the Saints migrating from the eastern states began to arrive in Ohio, and it became necessary to make definite arrangements for their settlement. As this undertaking belonged particularly to the bishop’s office, Bishop Edward Partridge sought instruction on the matter, and the Prophet inquired of the Lord.

[1–8](51.xhtml#p1), Edward Partridge is appointed to regulate stewardships and properties; [9–12](51.xhtml#p9), The Saints are to deal honestly and receive alike; [13–15](51.xhtml#p13), They are to have a bishop’s storehouse and to organize properties according to the Lord’s law; [16–20](51.xhtml#p16), Ohio is to be a temporary gathering place.

1 Hearken unto me, saith the Lord your God, and I will speak unto my servant [aEdward Partridge](#note1a), and give unto him directions; for it must needs be that he receive directions how to organize this people.

2 For it must needs be that they be [aorganized](#note2a) according to my [blaws](#note2b); if otherwise, they will be cut off.

3 Wherefore, let my servant Edward Partridge, and those whom he has chosen, in whom I am well pleased, appoint unto this people their [aportions](#note3a), every man [bequal](#note3b) according to his family, according to his circumstances and his wants and [cneeds](#note3c).

4 And let my servant Edward Partridge, when he shall appoint a man his [aportion](#note4a), give unto him a writing that shall secure unto him his portion, that he shall hold it, even this right and this inheritance in the church, until he transgresses and is not accounted worthy by the voice of the church, according to the [blaws](#note4b) and [ccovenants](#note4c) of the church, to belong to the church.

5 And if he shall transgress and is not accounted worthy to belong to the church, he shall not have power to [aclaim](#note5a) that portion which he has consecrated unto the bishop for the poor and needy of my church; therefore, he shall not retain the gift, but shall only have [bclaim](#note5b) on that portion that is deeded unto him.

6 And thus all things shall be made sure, [aaccording](#note6a) to the [blaws](#note6b) of the land.

7 And let that which belongs to this people be appointed unto this people.

8 And the [amoney](#note8a) which is left unto this people—let there be an [bagent](#note8b) appointed unto this people, to take the [cmoney](#note8c) to provide food and raiment, according to the wants of this people.

9 And let every man deal [ahonestly](#note9a), and be alike among this people, and receive alike, that ye may be [bone](#note9b), even as I have commanded you.

10 And let that which belongeth to this people not be taken and given unto that of [aanother church](#note10a).

11 Wherefore, if another church would receive money of this church, let them [apay](#note11a) unto this church again according as they shall agree;

12 And this shall be done through the bishop or the agent, which shall be appointed by the [avoice](#note12a) of the church.

13 And again, let the bishop appoint a [astorehouse](#note13a) unto this church; and let all things both in money and in meat, which are more than is [bneedful](#note13b) for the wants of this people, be kept in the hands of the bishop.

14 And let him also reserve unto [ahimself](#note14a) for his own wants, and for the wants of his family, as he shall be employed in doing this business.

15 And thus I grant unto this people a privilege of organizing themselves according to my [alaws](#note15a).

16 And I consecrate unto them this land for a [alittle season](#note16a), until I, the Lord, shall provide for them otherwise, and command them to go hence;

17 And the hour and the day is not given unto them, wherefore let them act upon this land as for years, and this shall turn unto them for their good.

18 Behold, this shall be [aan example](#note18a) unto my servant Edward Partridge, in other places, in all churches.

19 And whoso is found a [afaithful](#note19a), a [bjust](#note19b), and a wise [csteward](#note19c) shall enter into the [djoy](#note19d) of his Lord, and shall inherit eternal life.

20 Verily, I say unto you, I am Jesus Christ, who [acometh](#note20a) quickly, in an [bhour](#note20b) you think not. Even so. Amen.

Section 52

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to the elders of the Church, at Kirtland, Ohio, June 6, 1831. A conference had been held at Kirtland, beginning on the 3rd and closing on the 6th of June. At this conference the first distinctive ordinations to the office of high priest were made, and certain manifestations of false and deceiving spirits were discerned and rebuked.

[1–2](52.xhtml#p1), The next conference is designated to be held in Missouri; [3–8](52.xhtml#p3), Appointments of certain elders to travel together are made; [9–11](52.xhtml#p9), The elders are to teach what the apostles and prophets have written; [12–21](52.xhtml#p12), Those enlightened by the Spirit bring forth fruits of praise and wisdom; [22–44](52.xhtml#p22), Various elders are appointed to go forth preaching the gospel while traveling to Missouri for the conference.

1 Behold, thus saith the Lord unto the elders whom he hath called and chosen in these last days, by the [avoice](#note1a) of his Spirit—

2 Saying: I, the Lord, will make known unto you what I will that ye shall do from this time until the next conference, which shall be held in Missouri, upon the [aland](#note2a) which I will [bconsecrate](#note2b) unto my people, which are a [cremnant](#note2c) of Jacob, and those who are heirs according to the [dcovenant](#note2d).

3 Wherefore, verily I say unto you, let my servants Joseph Smith, Jun., and Sidney Rigdon take their journey as soon as preparations can be made to leave their homes, and journey to the land of [aMissouri](#note3a).

4 And inasmuch as they are faithful unto me, it shall be made known unto them what they shall do;

5 And it shall also, inasmuch as they are faithful, be made [aknown](#note5a) unto them the [bland](#note5b) of your inheritance.

6 And inasmuch as they are not faithful, they shall be cut off, even as I will, as seemeth me good.

7 And again, verily I say unto you, let my servant Lyman Wight and my servant John Corrill take their journey speedily;

8 And also my servant John Murdock, and my servant Hyrum Smith, take their journey unto the same place by the way of Detroit.

9 And let them journey from thence preaching the word by the way, saying [anone](#note9a) other things than that which the [bprophets](#note9b) and apostles have written, and that which is taught them by the [cComforter](#note9c) through the prayer of faith.

10 Let them go [atwo by two](#note10a), and thus let them preach by the way in every congregation, baptizing by [bwater](#note10b), and the laying on of the [chands](#note10c) by the water’s side.

11 For thus saith the Lord, I will cut my work short in [arighteousness](#note11a), for the days come that I will send forth [bjudgment](#note11b) unto victory.

12 And let my servant Lyman Wight beware, for Satan desireth to [asift](#note12a) him as chaff.

13 And behold, he that is [afaithful](#note13a) shall be made ruler over many things.

14 And again, I will give unto you a pattern in all things, that ye may not be deceived; for Satan is abroad in the land, and he goeth forth [adeceiving](#note14a) the nations—

15 Wherefore he that prayeth, whose spirit is [acontrite](#note15a), the same is [baccepted](#note15b) of me if he obey mine [cordinances](#note15c).

16 He that [aspeaketh](#note16a), whose spirit is contrite, whose language is meek and [bedifieth](#note16b), the same is of God if he obey mine ordinances.

17 And again, he that trembleth under my power shall be made [astrong](#note17a), and shall bring forth fruits of praise and [bwisdom](#note17b), according to the revelations and truths which I have given you.

18 And again, he that is overcome and [abringeth](#note18a) not forth fruits, even according to this pattern, is not of me.

19 Wherefore, by this pattern ye shall [aknow](#note19a) the spirits in all cases under the whole heavens.

20 And the days have come; according to men’s faith it shall be [adone](#note20a) unto them.

21 Behold, this commandment is given unto all the elders whom I have chosen.

22 And again, verily I say unto you, let my servant [aThomas B. Marsh](#note22a) and my servant [bEzra Thayre](#note22b) take their journey also, preaching the word by the way unto this same land.

23 And again, let my servant Isaac Morley and my servant Ezra Booth take their journey, also preaching the word by the way unto this same land.

24 And again, let my servants [aEdward Partridge](#note24a) and Martin Harris take their journey with my servants Sidney Rigdon and Joseph Smith, Jun.

25 Let my servants David Whitmer and Harvey Whitlock also take their journey, and preach by the way unto this same land.

26 And let my servants [aParley P. Pratt](#note26a) and [bOrson Pratt](#note26b) take their journey, and preach by the way, even unto this same land.

27 And let my servants Solomon Hancock and Simeon Carter also take their journey unto this same land, and preach by the way.

28 Let my servants Edson Fuller and Jacob Scott also take their journey.

29 Let my servants Levi W. Hancock and Zebedee Coltrin also take their journey.

30 Let my servants Reynolds Cahoon and Samuel H. Smith also take their journey.

31 Let my servants Wheeler Baldwin and William Carter also take their journey.

32 And let my servants [aNewel Knight](#note32a) and [bSelah J. Griffin](#note32b) both be ordained, and also take their journey.

33 Yea, verily I say, let all these take their journey unto one place, in their several courses, and one man shall not build upon another’s [afoundation](#note33a), neither journey in another’s track.

34 He that is faithful, the same shall be kept and blessed with much [afruit](#note34a).

35 And again, I say unto you, let my servants Joseph Wakefield and Solomon Humphrey take their journey into the eastern lands;

36 Let them labor with their families, [adeclaring](#note36a) none other things than the prophets and apostles, that which they have [bseen](#note36b) and heard and most assuredly [cbelieve](#note36c), that the prophecies may be fulfilled.

37 In consequence of transgression, let that which was bestowed upon Heman Basset be [ataken](#note37a) from him, and placed upon the head of Simonds Ryder.

38 And again, verily I say unto you, let Jared Carter be [aordained](#note38a) a priest, and also George James be ordained a [bpriest](#note38b).

39 Let the residue of the elders [awatch](#note39a) over the churches, and declare the word in the regions round about them; and let them [blabor](#note39b) with their own hands that there be no [cidolatry](#note39c) nor wickedness practiced.

40 And remember in all things the [apoor](#note40a) and the [bneedy](#note40b), the [csick](#note40c) and the afflicted, for he that doeth not these things, the same is not my disciple.

41 And again, let my servants Joseph Smith, Jun., and Sidney Rigdon and Edward Partridge take with them a [arecommend](#note41a) from the church. And let there be one obtained for my servant Oliver Cowdery also.

42 And thus, even as I have said, if ye are faithful ye shall assemble yourselves together to rejoice upon the land of [aMissouri](#note42a), which is the land of your [binheritance](#note42b), which is now the land of your enemies.

43 But, behold, I, the Lord, will hasten the city in its time, and will crown the faithful with [ajoy](#note43a) and with rejoicing.

44 Behold, I am Jesus Christ, the Son of God, and I will [alift](#note44a) them up at the last day. Even so. Amen.

Section 53

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Algernon Sidney Gilbert, at Kirtland, Ohio, June 8, 1831. At Sidney Gilbert’s request, the Prophet inquired of the Lord as to Brother Gilbert’s work and appointment in the Church.

[1–3](53.xhtml#p1), Sidney Gilbert’s calling and election in the Church is to be ordained an elder; [4–7](53.xhtml#p4), He is also to serve as a bishop’s agent.

1 Behold, I say unto you, my servant Sidney Gilbert, that I have heard your prayers; and you have called upon me that it should be made known unto you, of the Lord your God, concerning your [acalling](#note1a) and [belection](#note1b) in the church, which I, the Lord, have raised up in these last days.

2 Behold, I, the Lord, who was [acrucified](#note2a) for the sins of the world, give unto you a commandment that you shall [bforsake](#note2b) the world.

3 Take upon you mine ordination, even that of an elder, to preach faith and repentance and [aremission](#note3a) of sins, according to my word, and the reception of the Holy Spirit by the laying on of [bhands](#note3b);

4 And also to be an [aagent](#note4a) unto this church in the place which shall be appointed by the bishop, according to commandments which shall be given hereafter.

5 And again, verily I say unto you, you shall take your journey with my servants Joseph Smith, Jun., and Sidney Rigdon.

6 Behold, these are the first ordinances which you shall receive; and the residue shall be made known in a time to come, according to your labor in my vineyard.

7 And again, I would that ye should learn that he only is saved who [aendureth](#note7a) unto the end. Even so. Amen.

Section 54

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Newel Knight, at Kirtland, Ohio, June 10, 1831. Members of the Church living in Thompson, Ohio, were divided on questions having to do with the consecration of properties. Selfishness and greed were manifest. Following his mission to the Shakers (see the [heading to section 49](49.xhtml)), Leman Copley had broken his covenant to consecrate his large farm as a place of inheritance for the Saints arriving from Colesville, New York. As a consequence, Newel Knight (leader of the members living in Thompson) and other elders had come to the Prophet asking how to proceed. The Prophet inquired of the Lord and received this revelation, which commands the members in Thompson to leave Leman Copley’s farm and journey to Missouri.

[1–6](54.xhtml#p1), The Saints must keep the gospel covenant to gain mercy; [7–10](54.xhtml#p7), They must be patient in tribulation.

1 Behold, thus saith the Lord, even [aAlpha and Omega](#note1a), the beginning and the end, even he who was [bcrucified](#note1b) for the sins of the world—

2 Behold, verily, verily, I say unto you, my servant Newel Knight, you shall stand fast in the office whereunto I have appointed you.

3 And if your brethren desire to escape their enemies, let them repent of all their sins, and become truly [ahumble](#note3a) before me and contrite.

4 And as the covenant which they made unto me has been [abroken](#note4a), even so it has become [bvoid](#note4b) and of none effect.

5 And wo to him by whom this [aoffense](#note5a) cometh, for it had been better for him that he had been drowned in the depth of the sea.

6 But blessed are they who have kept the [acovenant](#note6a) and observed the [bcommandment](#note6b), for they shall obtain [cmercy](#note6c).

7 Wherefore, go to now and flee the land, lest your enemies come upon you; and take your journey, and appoint whom you will to be your leader, and to pay moneys for you.

8 And thus you shall take your journey into the regions westward, unto the land of [aMissouri](#note8a), unto the borders of the Lamanites.

9 And after you have done journeying, behold, I say unto you, seek ye a [aliving](#note9a) like unto men, until I prepare a place for you.

10 And again, be [apatient](#note10a) in tribulation until I [bcome](#note10b); and, behold, I come quickly, and my [creward](#note10c) is with me, and they who have [dsought](#note10d) me early shall find [erest](#note10e) to their souls. Even so. Amen.

Section 55

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to William W. Phelps, at Kirtland, Ohio, June 14, 1831. William W. Phelps, a printer, and his family had just arrived at Kirtland, and the Prophet sought the Lord for information concerning him.

[1–3](55.xhtml#p1), William W. Phelps is called and chosen to be baptized, to be ordained an elder, and to preach the gospel; [4](55.xhtml#p4), He is also to write books for children in Church schools; [5–6](55.xhtml#p5), He is to travel to Missouri, which will be the area of his labors.

1 Behold, thus saith the Lord unto you, my servant William, yea, even the Lord of the whole [aearth](#note1a), thou art called and chosen; and after thou hast been [bbaptized](#note1b) by water, which if you do with an eye single to my glory, you shall have a remission of your sins and a reception of the Holy Spirit by the laying on of [chands](#note1c);

2 And then thou shalt be ordained by the hand of my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., to be an elder unto this church, to preach repentance and [aremission](#note2a) of sins by way of baptism in the name of Jesus Christ, the Son of the living God.

3 And on whomsoever you shall [alay](#note3a) your hands, if they are contrite before me, you shall have power to give the Holy Spirit.

4 And again, you shall be ordained to assist my servant Oliver Cowdery to do the work of printing, and of selecting and writing [abooks](#note4a) for [bschools](#note4b) in this church, that little [cchildren](#note4c) also may receive [dinstruction](#note4d) before me as is pleasing unto me.

5 And again, verily I say unto you, for this cause you shall take your journey with my servants Joseph Smith, Jun., and Sidney Rigdon, that you may be [aplanted](#note5a) in the land of your inheritance to do this work.

6 And again, let my servant [aJoseph Coe](#note6a) also take his journey with them. The residue shall be made known hereafter, even as I will. Amen.

Section 56

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, June 15, 1831. This revelation chastises Ezra Thayre for not obeying a former revelation (the “commandment” referred to in [verse 8](56.xhtml#p8)), which Joseph Smith had received for him, instructing Thayre concerning his duties on Frederick G. Williams’ farm, where he lived. The following revelation also revokes Thayre’s call to travel to Missouri with Thomas B. Marsh (see [section 52:22](52.xhtml#p22)).

[1–2](56.xhtml#p1), The Saints must take up their cross and follow the Lord to gain salvation; [3–13](56.xhtml#p3), The Lord commands and revokes, and the disobedient are cast off; [14–17](56.xhtml#p14), Wo unto the rich who will not help the poor, and wo unto the poor whose hearts are not broken; [18–20](56.xhtml#p18), Blessed are the poor who are pure in heart, for they will inherit the earth.

1 Hearken, O ye people who [aprofess](#note1a) my name, saith the Lord your God; for behold, mine anger is [bkindled](#note1b) against the rebellious, and they shall know mine arm and mine indignation, in the day of [cvisitation](#note1c) and of wrath upon the nations.

2 And he that will not take up his [across](#note2a) and [bfollow](#note2b) me, and keep my commandments, the same shall not be saved.

3 Behold, I, the Lord, command; and he that will not [aobey](#note3a) shall be [bcut off](#note3b) in mine own due time, after I have commanded and the commandment is broken.

4 Wherefore I, the Lord, command and [arevoke](#note4a), as it seemeth me good; and all this to be answered upon the heads of the [brebellious](#note4b), saith the Lord.

5 Wherefore, I revoke the commandment which was given unto my servants [aThomas B. Marsh](#note5a) and Ezra Thayre, and give a new commandment unto my servant Thomas, that he shall take up his journey speedily to the land of Missouri, and my servant Selah J. Griffin shall also go with him.

6 For behold, I revoke the commandment which was given unto my servants [aSelah J. Griffin](#note6a) and Newel Knight, in consequence of the [bstiffneckedness](#note6b) of my people which are in Thompson, and their rebellions.

7 Wherefore, let my servant Newel Knight remain with them; and as many as will go may go, that are contrite before me, and be led by him to the land which I have appointed.

8 And again, verily I say unto you, that my servant Ezra Thayre must repent of his [apride](#note8a), and of his [bselfishness](#note8b), and obey the former commandment which I have given him concerning the place upon which he lives.

9 And if he will do this, as there shall be no divisions made upon the land, he shall be appointed still to go to the land of Missouri;

10 Otherwise he shall receive the [amoney](#note10a) which he has paid, and shall leave the place, and shall be [bcut off](#note10b) out of my church, saith the Lord God of hosts;

11 And though the heaven and the earth pass away, these words shall not [apass away](#note11a), but shall be fulfilled.

12 And if my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., must needs pay the money, behold, I, the Lord, will pay it unto him again in the land of Missouri, that those of whom he shall receive may be rewarded again according to that which they do;

13 For according to that which they do they shall receive, even in lands for their inheritance.

14 Behold, thus saith the Lord unto my people—you have many things to do and to repent of; for behold, your [asins](#note14a) have come up unto me, and are not [bpardoned](#note14b), because you seek to [ccounsel](#note14c) in your own ways.

15 And your hearts are not satisfied. And ye obey not the truth, but have [apleasure](#note15a) in unrighteousness.

16 Wo unto you [arich](#note16a) men, that will not [bgive](#note16b) your substance to the [cpoor](#note16c), for your [driches](#note16d) will canker your souls; and this shall be your lamentation in the day of visitation, and of judgment, and of indignation: The [eharvest](#note16e) is past, the summer is ended, and my soul is not saved!

17 Wo unto you [apoor](#note17a) men, whose hearts are not broken, whose spirits are not contrite, and whose bellies are not satisfied, and whose hands are not stayed from laying hold upon other men’s goods, whose eyes are full of [bgreediness](#note17b), and who will not [clabor](#note17c) with your own hands!

18 But blessed are the [apoor](#note18a) who are pure in heart, whose hearts are broken, and whose spirits are [bcontrite](#note18b), for they shall see the [ckingdom](#note18c) of God coming in power and great glory unto their deliverance; for the fatness of the [dearth](#note18d) shall be theirs.

19 For behold, the Lord shall come, and his [arecompense](#note19a) shall be with him, and he shall [breward](#note19b) every man, and the poor shall rejoice;

20 And their generations shall [ainherit](#note20a) the earth from generation to generation, forever and ever. And now I make an end of speaking unto you. Even so. Amen.

Section 57

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, in Zion, Jackson County, Missouri, July 20, 1831. In compliance with the Lord’s command to travel to Missouri, where He would reveal “the land of your inheritance” ([section 52](52.xhtml)), the elders had journeyed from Ohio to Missouri’s western border. Joseph Smith contemplated the state of the Lamanites and wondered: “When will the wilderness blossom as the rose? When will Zion be built up in her glory, and where will Thy temple stand, unto which all nations shall come in the last days?” Subsequently he received this revelation.

[1–3](57.xhtml#p1), Independence, Missouri, is the place for the City of Zion and the temple; [4–7](57.xhtml#p4), The Saints are to purchase lands and receive inheritances in that area; [8–16](57.xhtml#p8), Sidney Gilbert is to establish a store, William W. Phelps is to be a printer, and Oliver Cowdery is to edit material for publication.

1 Hearken, O ye elders of my church, saith the Lord your God, who have assembled yourselves together, according to my commandments, in this land, which is the land of [aMissouri](#note1a), which is the [bland](#note1b) which I have appointed and [cconsecrated](#note1c) for the [dgathering](#note1d) of the saints.

2 Wherefore, this is the [aland of promise](#note2a), and the [bplace](#note2b) for the city of [cZion](#note2c).

3 And thus saith the Lord your God, if you will receive wisdom here is wisdom. Behold, the place which is now called Independence is the [acenter](#note3a) place; and a spot for the [btemple](#note3b) is lying westward, upon a lot which is not far from the courthouse.

4 Wherefore, it is wisdom that the land should be [apurchased](#note4a) by the saints, and also every tract lying westward, even unto the line running directly [bbetween Jew and Gentile](#note4b);

5 And also every tract bordering by the prairies, inasmuch as my disciples are enabled to [abuy](#note5a) lands. Behold, this is wisdom, that they may [bobtain](#note5b) it for an everlasting inheritance.

6 And let my servant Sidney Gilbert stand in the office to which I have appointed him, to receive moneys, to be an [aagent](#note6a) unto the church, to buy land in all the regions round about, inasmuch as can be done in righteousness, and as wisdom shall direct.

7 And let my servant [aEdward Partridge](#note7a) stand in the [boffice](#note7b) to which I have appointed him, and [cdivide](#note7c) unto the saints their inheritance, even as I have commanded; and also those whom he has appointed to assist him.

8 And again, verily I say unto you, let my servant Sidney Gilbert plant himself in this place, and establish a [astore](#note8a), that he may sell goods [bwithout fraud](#note8b), that he may obtain money to buy [clands](#note8c) for the good of the saints, and that he may obtain whatsoever things the disciples may need to plant them in their inheritance.

9 And also let my servant Sidney Gilbert obtain a license—behold here is [awisdom](#note9a), and whoso readeth let him [bunderstand](#note9b)—that he may send goods also unto the people, even by whom he will as clerks employed in his service;

10 And thus provide for my saints, that my gospel may be preached unto those who sit in [adarkness](#note10a) and in the region and [bshadow of death](#note10b).

11 And again, verily I say unto you, let my servant [aWilliam W. Phelps](#note11a) be planted in this place, and be established as a [bprinter](#note11b) unto the church.

12 And lo, if the world receive his writings—behold here is wisdom—let him obtain whatsoever he can obtain in righteousness, for the good of the saints.

13 And let my servant [aOliver Cowdery](#note13a) assist him, even as I have commanded, in whatsoever place I shall appoint unto him, to copy, and to correct, and select, that all things may be right before me, as it shall be proved by the Spirit through him.

14 And thus let those of whom I have spoken be planted in the land of Zion, as speedily as can be, with their [afamilies](#note14a), to do those things even as I have spoken.

15 And now concerning the gathering—Let the bishop and the agent make preparations for those families which have been commanded to come to this land, as soon as possible, and plant them in their inheritance.

16 And unto the residue of both elders and members further directions shall be given hereafter. Even so. Amen.

Section 58

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, in Zion, Jackson County, Missouri, August 1, 1831. Earlier, on the first Sabbath after the arrival of the Prophet and his party in Jackson County, Missouri, a religious service had been held, and two members had been received by baptism. During that week, some of the Colesville Saints from the Thompson Branch and others arrived (see [section 54](54.xhtml)). Many were eager to learn the will of the Lord concerning them in the new place of gathering.

[1–5](58.xhtml#p1), Those who endure tribulation will be crowned with glory; [6–12](58.xhtml#p6), The Saints are to prepare for the marriage of the Lamb and the supper of the Lord; [13–18](58.xhtml#p13), Bishops are judges in Israel; [19–23](58.xhtml#p19), The Saints are to obey the laws of the land; [24–29](58.xhtml#p24), Men should use their agency to do good; [30–33](58.xhtml#p30), The Lord commands and revokes; [34–43](58.xhtml#p34), To repent, men must confess and forsake their sins; [44–58](58.xhtml#p44), The Saints are to purchase their inheritance and gather in Missouri; [59–65](58.xhtml#p59), The gospel must be preached unto every creature.

1 Hearken, O ye elders of my church, and give [aear](#note1a) to my word, and learn of me what I will concerning you, and also concerning [bthis land](#note1b) unto which I have sent you.

2 For verily I say unto you, blessed is he that [akeepeth](#note2a) my commandments, whether in life or in [bdeath](#note2b); and he that is [cfaithful](#note2c) in [dtribulation](#note2d), the [ereward](#note2e) of the same is greater in the kingdom of heaven.

3 Ye cannot behold with your natural [aeyes](#note3a), for the present time, the design of your God concerning those things which shall come hereafter, and the [bglory](#note3b) which shall follow after much tribulation.

4 For after much [atribulation](#note4a) come the [bblessings](#note4b). Wherefore the day cometh that ye shall be [ccrowned](#note4c) with much [dglory](#note4d); the hour is not yet, but is nigh at hand.

5 Remember this, which I tell you before, that you may [alay](#note5a) it to heart, and receive that which is to follow.

6 Behold, verily I say unto you, for this cause I have sent you—that you might be obedient, and that your hearts might be [aprepared](#note6a) to [bbear](#note6b) [ctestimony](#note6c) of the things which are to come;

7 And also that you might be honored in laying the foundation, and in bearing record of the land upon which the [aZion](#note7a) of God shall stand;

8 And also that a feast of fat things might be prepared for the [apoor](#note8a); yea, a feast of fat things, of wine on the [blees](#note8b) well refined, that the earth may know that the mouths of the prophets shall not fail;

9 Yea, a supper of the house of the Lord, well prepared, unto which all [anations](#note9a) shall be invited.

10 First, the rich and the learned, the wise and the noble;

11 And after that cometh the day of my power; then shall the [apoor](#note11a), the lame, and the blind, and the deaf, come in unto the [bmarriage](#note11b) of the Lamb, and partake of the [csupper](#note11c) of the Lord, prepared for the great day to come.

12 Behold, I, the Lord, have spoken it.

13 And that the [atestimony](#note13a) might go forth from Zion, yea, from the mouth of the city of the heritage of God—

14 Yea, for this cause I have sent you hither, and have selected my servant [aEdward Partridge](#note14a), and have appointed unto him his mission in this land.

15 But if he repent not of his sins, which are [aunbelief](#note15a) and blindness of [bheart](#note15b), let him take heed lest he [cfall](#note15c).

16 Behold his mission is given unto him, and it shall not be given again.

17 And whoso standeth in this mission is appointed to be a [ajudge](#note17a) in Israel, like as it was in ancient days, to [bdivide](#note17b) the lands of the heritage of God unto his [cchildren](#note17c);

18 And to judge his people by the testimony of the just, and by the assistance of his [acounselors](#note18a), according to the laws of the kingdom which are given by the [bprophets](#note18b) of God.

19 For verily I say unto you, my law shall be kept on this land.

20 Let no man think he is [aruler](#note20a); but let God rule him that judgeth, according to the counsel of his own [bwill](#note20b), or, in other words, him that counseleth or sitteth upon the judgment seat.

21 Let no man break the [alaws](#note21a) of the land, for he that keepeth the laws of God hath no need to break the laws of the land.

22 Wherefore, be [asubject](#note22a) to the powers that be, [buntil](#note22b) he reigns whose right it is to reign, and subdues all enemies under his feet.

23 Behold, the [alaws](#note23a) which ye have received from my hand are the laws of the church, and in this light ye shall hold them forth. Behold, here is wisdom.

24 And now, as I spake concerning my servant Edward Partridge, this land is the land of his [aresidence](#note24a), and those whom he has appointed for his counselors; and also the land of the residence of him whom I have appointed to keep my [bstorehouse](#note24b);

25 Wherefore, let them bring their families to this land, as they shall [acounsel](#note25a) between themselves and me.

26 For behold, it is not meet that I should command in all things; for he that is [acompelled](#note26a) in all things, the same is a [bslothful](#note26b) and not a wise servant; wherefore he receiveth no reward.

27 Verily I say, men should be [aanxiously engaged](#note27a) in a good cause, and do many things of their own free will, and bring to pass much righteousness;

28 For the power is in them, wherein they are [aagents](#note28a) unto themselves. And inasmuch as men do good they shall in nowise lose their [breward](#note28b).

29 But he that [adoeth](#note29a) not anything until he is commanded, and receiveth a commandment with [bdoubtful](#note29b) heart, and keepeth it with slothfulness, the same is [cdamned](#note29c).

30 Who am I that [amade](#note30a) man, saith the Lord, that will hold him [bguiltless](#note30b) that obeys not my commandments?

31 Who am I, saith the Lord, that have [apromised](#note31a) and have not fulfilled?

32 I command and men [aobey not](#note32a); I [brevoke](#note32b) and they receive not the blessing.

33 Then they [asay](#note33a) in their hearts: This is not the work of the Lord, for his promises are not fulfilled. But wo unto such, for their [breward](#note33b) lurketh [cbeneath](#note33c), and not from above.

34 And now I give unto you further directions concerning this land.

35 It is wisdom in me that my servant Martin Harris should be an example unto the church, in [alaying](#note35a) his moneys before the bishop of the church.

36 And also, this is a law unto every man that cometh unto this land to receive an inheritance; and he shall do with his moneys according as the law directs.

37 And it is wisdom also that there should be lands purchased in Independence, for the place of the storehouse, and also for the house of the [aprinting](#note37a).

38 And other directions concerning my servant Martin Harris shall be given him of the Spirit, that he may receive his inheritance as seemeth him good;

39 And let him repent of his sins, for he seeketh the [apraise](#note39a) of the world.

40 And also let my servant [aWilliam W. Phelps](#note40a) stand in the office to which I have appointed him, and receive his inheritance in the land;

41 And also he hath need to repent, for I, the Lord, am not well pleased with him, for he seeketh to excel, and he is not sufficiently meek before me.

42 Behold, he who has [arepented](#note42a) of his [bsins](#note42b), the same is [cforgiven](#note42c), and I, the Lord, remember them no more.

43 By this ye may know if a man repenteth of his sins—behold, he will [aconfess](#note43a) them and [bforsake](#note43b) them.

44 And now, verily, I say concerning the residue of the elders of my [achurch](#note44a), the time has not yet come, for many years, for them to receive their [binheritance](#note44b) in this land, except they desire it through the prayer of faith, only as it shall be appointed unto them of the Lord.

45 For, behold, they shall [apush](#note45a) the people together from the [bends](#note45b) of the earth.

46 Wherefore, assemble yourselves together; and they who are not appointed to stay in this land, let them preach the gospel in the regions round about; and after that let them return to their homes.

47 Let them preach by the way, and [abear](#note47a) testimony of the truth in all places, and call upon the rich, the high and the low, and the poor to repent.

48 And let them build up [achurches](#note48a), inasmuch as the inhabitants of the earth will repent.

49 And let there be an agent appointed by the voice of the church, unto the church in Ohio, to receive moneys to [apurchase](#note49a) lands in [bZion](#note49b).

50 And I give unto my servant Sidney Rigdon a commandment, that he shall [awrite](#note50a) a description of the land of Zion, and a statement of the will of God, as it shall be made known by the Spirit unto him;

51 And an epistle and subscription, to be presented unto all the churches to obtain moneys, to be put into the hands of the bishop, of himself or the agent, as seemeth him good or as he shall direct, to [apurchase](#note51a) lands for an [binheritance](#note51b) for the children of God.

52 For, behold, verily I say unto you, the Lord willeth that the disciples and the children of men should open their hearts, even to purchase this whole region of country, as soon as time will permit.

53 Behold, here is wisdom. Let them do this lest they [areceive](#note53a) none inheritance, save it be by the shedding of blood.

54 And again, inasmuch as there is land obtained, let there be workmen sent forth of all kinds unto this land, to labor for the saints of God.

55 Let all these things be done in [aorder](#note55a); and let the privileges of the lands be made known from time to time, by the bishop or the agent of the church.

56 And let the work of the [agathering](#note56a) be not in [bhaste](#note56b), nor by flight; but let it be done as it shall be [ccounseled](#note56c) by the elders of the church at the conferences, according to the knowledge which they receive from time to time.

57 And let my servant Sidney Rigdon [aconsecrate](#note57a) and [bdedicate](#note57b) this land, and the spot for the [ctemple](#note57c), unto the Lord.

58 And let a conference meeting be called; and after that let my servants Sidney Rigdon and Joseph Smith, Jun., return, and also Oliver Cowdery with them, to accomplish the residue of the work which I have appointed unto them in their own land, and the residue as shall be [aruled](#note58a) by the conferences.

59 And let no man return from this land except he bear [arecord](#note59a) by the way, of that which he knows and most assuredly believes.

60 Let that which has been bestowed upon [aZiba Peterson](#note60a) be taken from him; and let him stand as a member in the church, and labor with his own hands, with the brethren, until he is sufficiently [bchastened](#note60b) for all his sins; for he confesseth them not, and he thinketh to hide them.

61 Let the residue of the elders of this church, who are coming to this land, some of whom are exceedingly blessed even above measure, also hold a [aconference](#note61a) upon this land.

62 And let my servant Edward Partridge direct the conference which shall be held by them.

63 And let them also return, preaching the gospel by the way, bearing record of the things which are revealed unto them.

64 For, verily, the sound must go forth from this place into all the world, and unto the uttermost parts of the earth—the gospel must be [apreached](#note64a) unto every creature, with [bsigns](#note64b) following them that believe.

65 And behold the Son of Man [acometh](#note65a). Amen.

Section 59

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, in Zion, Jackson County, Missouri, August 7, 1831. Preceding this revelation, the land was consecrated, as the Lord had directed, and the site for the future temple was dedicated. On the day this revelation was received, Polly Knight, the wife of Joseph Knight Sr., died, the first Church member to die in Zion. Early members characterized this revelation as “instructing the Saints how to keep the sabbath and how to fast and pray.”

[1–4](59.xhtml#p1), The faithful Saints in Zion will be blessed; [5–8](59.xhtml#p5), They are to love and serve the Lord and keep His commandments; [9–19](59.xhtml#p9), By keeping the Lord’s day holy, the Saints are blessed temporally and spiritually; [20–24](59.xhtml#p20), The righteous are promised peace in this world and eternal life in the world to come.

1 Behold, blessed, saith the Lord, are they who have come up unto this land with an [aeye](#note1a) single to my glory, according to my commandments.

2 For those that live shall [ainherit](#note2a) the earth, and those that [bdie](#note2b) shall rest from all their labors, and their works shall follow them; and they shall receive a [ccrown](#note2c) in the [dmansions](#note2d) of my Father, which I have prepared for them.

3 Yea, blessed are they whose feet stand upon the land of Zion, who have obeyed my gospel; for they shall receive for their reward the good things of the earth, and it shall bring forth in its [astrength](#note3a).

4 And they shall also be crowned with blessings from above, yea, and with [acommandments](#note4a) not a few, and with [brevelations](#note4b) in their time—they that are [cfaithful](#note4c) and [ddiligent](#note4d) before me.

5 Wherefore, I give unto them a commandment, saying thus: Thou shalt [alove](#note5a) the Lord thy God with all thy [bheart](#note5b), with all thy might, mind, and strength; and in the name of Jesus Christ thou shalt [cserve](#note5c) him.

6 Thou shalt [alove](#note6a) thy [bneighbor](#note6b) as thyself. Thou shalt not [csteal](#note6c); neither commit [dadultery](#note6d), nor [ekill](#note6e), nor do anything [flike](#note6f) unto it.

7 Thou shalt [athank](#note7a) the Lord thy God in all things.

8 Thou shalt offer a [asacrifice](#note8a) unto the Lord thy God in [brighteousness](#note8b), even that of a broken heart and a [ccontrite spirit](#note8c).

9 And that thou mayest more fully keep thyself [aunspotted](#note9a) from the world, thou shalt go to the house of [bprayer](#note9b) and offer up thy [csacraments](#note9c) upon my [dholy day](#note9d);

10 For verily this is a [aday](#note10a) appointed unto you to rest from your labors, and to pay thy devotions unto the Most High;

11 Nevertheless thy [avows](#note11a) shall be offered up in righteousness on all days and at all times;

12 But remember that on this, the [aLord’s day](#note12a), thou shalt offer thine [boblations](#note12b) and thy sacraments unto the Most High, [cconfessing](#note12c) thy sins unto thy brethren, and before the Lord.

13 And on this day thou shalt do none other thing, only let thy food be prepared with singleness of heart that thy [afasting](#note13a) may be perfect, or, in other words, that thy [bjoy](#note13b) may be full.

14 Verily, this is fasting and prayer, or in other words, rejoicing and prayer.

15 And inasmuch as ye do these things with [athanksgiving](#note15a), with [bcheerful](#note15b) [chearts](#note15c) and countenances, not with [dmuch](#note15d) [elaughter](#note15e), for this is sin, but with a glad heart and a cheerful countenance—

16 Verily I say, that inasmuch as ye do this, the [afulness](#note16a) of the earth is yours, the beasts of the field and the fowls of the air, and that which climbeth upon the trees and walketh upon the earth;

17 Yea, and the herb, and the [agood](#note17a) things which come of the earth, whether for food or for [braiment](#note17b), or for houses, or for barns, or for orchards, or for gardens, or for vineyards;

18 Yea, all things which come of the earth, in the season thereof, are made for the [abenefit](#note18a) and the [buse](#note18b) of man, both to please the eye and to [cgladden](#note18c) the heart;

19 Yea, for [afood](#note19a) and for raiment, for taste and for smell, to [bstrengthen](#note19b) the body and to enliven the soul.

20 And it pleaseth God that he hath given all these things unto man; for unto this end were they made to be used, with judgment, not to [aexcess](#note20a), neither by extortion.

21 And in nothing doth man [aoffend](#note21a) God, or against none is his [bwrath](#note21b) [ckindled](#note21c), save those who [dconfess not](#note21d) his hand in all things, and [eobey not](#note21e) his commandments.

22 Behold, this is according to the law and the prophets; wherefore, trouble me no more concerning this matter.

23 But learn that he who doeth the works of [arighteousness](#note23a) shall receive his [breward](#note23b), even [cpeace](#note23c) in this world, and [deternal life](#note23d) in the world to come.

24 I, the Lord, have spoken it, and the Spirit beareth record. Amen.

Section 60

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, in Independence, Jackson County, Missouri, August 8, 1831. On this occasion the elders who had traveled to Jackson County and participated in the dedication of the land and the temple site desired to know what they were to do.

[1–9](60.xhtml#p1), The elders are to preach the gospel in the congregations of the wicked; [10–14](60.xhtml#p10), They should not idle away their time, nor bury their talents; [15–17](60.xhtml#p15), They may wash their feet as a testimony against those who reject the gospel.

1 Behold, thus saith the Lord unto the elders of his church, who are to return speedily to the land from whence they came: Behold, it pleaseth me, that you have come up hither;

2 But with some I am not well pleased, for they will not open their [amouths](#note2a), but they hide the [btalent](#note2b) which I have given unto them, because of the [cfear](#note2c) of man. Wo unto such, for mine [danger](#note2d) is [ekindled](#note2e) against them.

3 And it shall come to pass, if they are not more faithful unto me, it shall be [ataken](#note3a) away, even that which they have.

4 For I, the Lord, [arule](#note4a) in the heavens above, and among the [barmies](#note4b) of the earth; and in the day when I shall make up my [cjewels](#note4c), all men shall know what it is that bespeaketh the power of God.

5 But, verily, I will speak unto you concerning your journey unto the land from whence you came. Let there be a craft made, or bought, as [aseemeth](#note5a) you good, it mattereth not unto me, and take your journey speedily for the place which is called St. Louis.

6 And from thence let my servants, Sidney Rigdon, Joseph Smith, Jun., and Oliver Cowdery, take their journey for Cincinnati;

7 And in this place let them lift up their voice and declare my word with loud voices, without wrath or [adoubting](#note7a), lifting up holy hands upon them. For I am able to make you [bholy](#note7b), and your sins are [cforgiven](#note7c) you.

8 And let the residue take their journey from St. Louis, [atwo by two](#note8a), and preach the word, not in haste, among the congregations of the wicked, until they return to the churches from whence they came.

9 And all this for the good of the [achurches](#note9a); for this intent have I sent them.

10 And let my servant [aEdward Partridge](#note10a) impart of the [bmoney](#note10b) which I have given him, a portion unto mine elders who are commanded to return;

11 And he that is able, let him return it by the way of the agent; and he that is not, of him it is not required.

12 And now I speak of the residue who are to come unto this land.

13 Behold, they have been sent to preach my gospel among the congregations of the wicked; wherefore, I give unto them a commandment, thus: Thou shalt not [aidle](#note13a) away thy time, neither shalt thou bury thy [btalent](#note13b) that it may not be known.

14 And after thou hast come up unto the land of Zion, and hast proclaimed my word, thou shalt speedily return, proclaiming my word among the congregations of the wicked, not in [ahaste](#note14a), neither in [bwrath](#note14b) nor with [cstrife](#note14c).

15 And shake off the [adust](#note15a) of thy feet against those who receive thee not, not in their presence, lest thou [bprovoke](#note15b) them, but in secret; and [cwash](#note15c) thy feet, as a testimony against them in the day of judgment.

16 Behold, this is sufficient for you, and the will of him who hath sent you.

17 And by the mouth of my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., it shall be made known concerning Sidney Rigdon and Oliver Cowdery. The residue hereafter. Even so. Amen.

Section 61

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, on the bank of the Missouri River, McIlwaine’s Bend, August 12, 1831. On their return trip to Kirtland, the Prophet and ten elders had traveled down the Missouri River in canoes. On the third day of the journey, many dangers were experienced. Elder William W. Phelps, in a daylight vision, saw the destroyer riding in power upon the face of the waters.

[1–12](61.xhtml#p1), The Lord has decreed many destructions upon the waters; [13–22](61.xhtml#p13), The waters were cursed by John, and the destroyer rides upon their face; [23–29](61.xhtml#p23), Some have power to command the waters; [30–35](61.xhtml#p30), Elders are to journey two by two and preach the gospel; [36–39](61.xhtml#p36), They are to prepare for the coming of the Son of Man.

1 Behold, and hearken unto the voice of him who has all [apower](#note1a), who is from everlasting to everlasting, even [bAlpha and Omega](#note1b), the beginning and the end.

2 Behold, verily thus saith the Lord unto you, O ye elders of my church, who are assembled upon this spot, whose sins are now forgiven you, for I, the Lord, [aforgive](#note2a) sins, and am [bmerciful](#note2b) unto those who [cconfess](#note2c) their sins with humble hearts;

3 But verily I say unto you, that it is not needful for this whole company of mine elders to be moving swiftly upon the waters, whilst the inhabitants on either side are perishing in unbelief.

4 Nevertheless, I suffered it that ye might bear record; behold, there are many dangers upon the waters, and more especially hereafter;

5 For I, the Lord, have decreed in mine anger many destructions upon the waters; yea, and especially upon these waters.

6 Nevertheless, all flesh is in mine hand, and he that is faithful among you shall not [aperish](#note6a) by the waters.

7 Wherefore, it is expedient that my servant Sidney Gilbert and my servant [aWilliam W. Phelps](#note7a) be in haste upon their errand and mission.

8 Nevertheless, I would not suffer that ye should part until you were [achastened](#note8a) for all your sins, that you might be one, that you might not perish in [bwickedness](#note8b);

9 But now, verily I say, it behooveth me that ye should part. Wherefore let my servants Sidney Gilbert and William W. Phelps take their former company, and let them take their journey in haste that they may fill their mission, and through faith they shall overcome;

10 And inasmuch as they are [afaithful](#note10a) they shall be preserved, and I, the Lord, will be [bwith them](#note10b).

11 And let the residue take that which is needful for clothing.

12 Let my servant Sidney Gilbert take that which is not needful with him, as you shall agree.

13 And now, behold, for your [agood](#note13a) I gave unto you a [bcommandment](#note13b) concerning these things; and I, the Lord, will reason with you as with men in days of old.

14 Behold, I, the Lord, in the beginning blessed the [awaters](#note14a); but in the last days, by the mouth of my servant John, I [bcursed](#note14b) the waters.

15 Wherefore, the days will come that no flesh shall be safe upon the waters.

16 And it shall be said in days to come that none is able to go up to the land of Zion upon the waters, but he that is upright in heart.

17 And, as I, the Lord, in the beginning [acursed](#note17a) the land, even so in the last days have I [bblessed](#note17b) it, in its time, for the use of my saints, that they may partake the fatness thereof.

18 And now I give unto you a commandment that what I say unto one I say unto all, that you shall forewarn your brethren concerning these waters, that they come not in journeying upon them, lest their faith fail and they are caught in snares;

19 I, the Lord, have decreed, and the destroyer rideth upon the face thereof, and I revoke not the decree.

20 I, the Lord, was [aangry](#note20a) with you yesterday, but today mine anger is turned away.

21 Wherefore, let those concerning whom I have spoken, that should take their journey in haste—again I say unto you, let them take their journey in haste.

22 And it mattereth not unto me, after a little, if it so be that they fill their mission, whether they go by water or by land; let this be as it is made known unto them [aaccording](#note22a) to their judgments hereafter.

23 And now, concerning my servants, Sidney Rigdon, Joseph Smith, Jun., and Oliver Cowdery, let them come not again upon the waters, save it be upon the canal, while journeying unto their homes; or in other words they shall not come upon the waters to journey, save upon the canal.

24 Behold, I, the Lord, have appointed a way for the journeying of my saints; and behold, this is the way—that after they leave the canal they shall journey by land, inasmuch as they are commanded to journey and go up unto the land of Zion;

25 And they shall do [alike](#note25a) unto the children of Israel, [bpitching](#note25b) their tents by the way.

26 And, behold, this commandment you shall give unto all your brethren.

27 Nevertheless, unto whom is given [apower](#note27a) to command the waters, unto him it is given by the Spirit to know all his ways;

28 Wherefore, let him do as the Spirit of the living God commandeth him, whether upon the land or upon the waters, as it remaineth with me to do hereafter.

29 And unto you is given the course for the saints, or the way for the saints of the camp of the Lord, to journey.

30 And again, verily I say unto you, my servants, Sidney Rigdon, Joseph Smith, Jun., and Oliver Cowdery, shall not open their mouths in the [acongregations](#note30a) of the wicked until they arrive at Cincinnati;

31 And in that place they shall lift up their voices unto God against that people, yea, unto him whose anger is [akindled](#note31a) against their wickedness, a people who are well-nigh [bripened](#note31b) for destruction.

32 And from thence let them journey for the congregations of their brethren, for their labors even now are wanted more abundantly among them than among the congregations of the wicked.

33 And now, concerning the residue, let them journey and [adeclare](#note33a) the word among the congregations of the wicked, inasmuch as it is given;

34 And inasmuch as they do this they shall [arid](#note34a) their garments, and they shall be spotless before me.

35 And let them journey together, or [atwo by two](#note35a), as seemeth them good, only let my servant Reynolds Cahoon, and my servant Samuel H. Smith, with whom I am well pleased, be not separated until they return to their homes, and this for a wise purpose in me.

36 And now, verily I say unto you, and what I say unto one I say unto all, be of good [acheer](#note36a), [blittle children](#note36b); for I am in your [cmidst](#note36c), and I have not [dforsaken](#note36d) you;

37 And inasmuch as you have humbled yourselves before me, the blessings of the [akingdom](#note37a) are yours.

38 Gird up your loins and be [awatchful](#note38a) and be sober, looking forth for the coming of the Son of Man, for he cometh in an hour you think not.

39 Pray always that you enter not into [atemptation](#note39a), that you may abide the day of his coming, whether in life or in death. Even so. Amen.
