Section 62

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, on the bank of the Missouri River at Chariton, Missouri, August 13, 1831. On this day the Prophet and his group, who were on their way from Independence to Kirtland, met several elders who were on their way to the land of Zion, and, after joyful salutations, received this revelation.

[1–3](62.xhtml#p1), Testimonies are recorded in heaven; [4–9](62.xhtml#p4), The elders are to travel and preach according to judgment and as directed by the Spirit.

1 Behold, and hearken, O ye elders of my church, saith the Lord your God, even Jesus Christ, your [aadvocate](#note1a), who knoweth the weakness of man and how to [bsuccor](#note1b) them who are [ctempted](#note1c).

2 And verily mine eyes are upon those who have not as yet gone up unto the land of Zion; wherefore your mission is not yet full.

3 Nevertheless, ye are [ablessed](#note3a), for the [btestimony](#note3b) which ye have borne is [crecorded](#note3c) in heaven for the angels to look upon; and they rejoice over you, and your [dsins](#note3d) are forgiven you.

4 And now continue your journey. Assemble yourselves upon the land of [aZion](#note4a); and hold a meeting and rejoice together, and offer a [bsacrament](#note4b) unto the Most High.

5 And then you may return to bear record, yea, even altogether, or [atwo by two](#note5a), as seemeth you good, it mattereth not unto me; only be faithful, and [bdeclare](#note5b) glad tidings unto the inhabitants of the earth, or among the [ccongregations](#note5c) of the wicked.

6 Behold, I, the Lord, have brought you together that the promise might be fulfilled, that the faithful among you should be preserved and rejoice together in the land of Missouri. I, the Lord, [apromise](#note6a) the faithful and cannot [blie](#note6b).

7 I, the Lord, am willing, if any among you [adesire](#note7a) to ride upon horses, or upon mules, or in chariots, he shall receive this blessing, if he receive it from the hand of the Lord, with a [bthankful](#note7b) heart in all things.

8 These things remain with you to do according to judgment and the directions of the Spirit.

9 Behold, the [akingdom](#note9a) is yours. And behold, and lo, I am [bwith](#note9b) the faithful always. Even so. Amen.

Section 63

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, August 30, 1831. The Prophet, Sidney Rigdon, and Oliver Cowdery had arrived in Kirtland on August 27 from their visit to Missouri. Joseph Smith’s history describes this revelation: “In these infant days of the Church, there was a great anxiety to obtain the word of the Lord upon every subject that in any way concerned our salvation; and as the land of Zion was now the most important temporal object in view, I enquired of the Lord for further information upon the gathering of the Saints, and the purchase of the land, and other matters.”

[1–6](63.xhtml#p1), A day of wrath will come upon the wicked; [7–12](63.xhtml#p7), Signs come by faith; [13–19](63.xhtml#p13), The adulterous in heart will deny the faith and be cast into the lake of fire; [20](63.xhtml#p20), The faithful will receive an inheritance upon the transfigured earth; [21](63.xhtml#p21), A full account of the events on the Mount of Transfiguration has not yet been revealed; [22–23](63.xhtml#p22), The obedient receive the mysteries of the kingdom; [24–31](63.xhtml#p24), Inheritances in Zion are to be purchased; [32–35](63.xhtml#p32), The Lord decrees wars, and the wicked slay the wicked; [36–48](63.xhtml#p36), The Saints are to gather to Zion and provide moneys to build it up; [49–54](63.xhtml#p49), Blessings are assured the faithful at the Second Coming, in the Resurrection, and during the Millennium; [55–58](63.xhtml#p55), This is a day of warning; [59–66](63.xhtml#p59), The Lord’s name is taken in vain by those who use it without authority.

1 Hearken, O ye people, and open your hearts and give ear from afar; and listen, you that call yourselves the [apeople](#note1a) of the Lord, and hear the word of the Lord and his will concerning you.

2 Yea, verily, I say, hear the word of him whose anger is [akindled](#note2a) against the wicked and [brebellious](#note2b);

3 Who willeth to take even them whom he will [atake](#note3a), and [bpreserveth](#note3b) in life them whom he will preserve;

4 Who buildeth up at his own will and [apleasure](#note4a); and destroyeth when he pleases, and is able to [bcast](#note4b) the soul down to hell.

5 Behold, I, the Lord, utter my voice, and it shall be [aobeyed](#note5a).

6 Wherefore, verily I say, let the wicked take heed, and let the [arebellious](#note6a) [bfear](#note6b) and tremble; and let the unbelieving hold their lips, for the [cday](#note6c) of wrath shall come upon them as a [dwhirlwind](#note6d), and all flesh shall [eknow](#note6e) that I am God.

7 And he that seeketh [asigns](#note7a) shall see [bsigns](#note7b), but not unto salvation.

8 Verily, I say unto you, there are those among you who seek signs, and there have been such even from the beginning;

9 But, behold, faith cometh not by signs, but [asigns](#note9a) follow those that believe.

10 Yea, [asigns](#note10a) come by [bfaith](#note10b), not by the will of men, nor as they please, but by the will of God.

11 Yea, signs come by faith, unto mighty works, for without [afaith](#note11a) no man pleaseth God; and with whom God is [bangry](#note11b) he is not well pleased; wherefore, unto such he showeth no signs, only in [cwrath](#note11c) unto their [dcondemnation](#note11d).

12 Wherefore, I, the Lord, am not pleased with those among you who have sought after signs and wonders for faith, and not for the good of men unto my glory.

13 Nevertheless, I give commandments, and many have turned away from my commandments and have [anot kept](#note13a) them.

14 There were among you [aadulterers](#note14a) and adulteresses; some of whom have turned away from you, and others remain with you that hereafter shall be revealed.

15 Let such beware and repent speedily, lest judgment shall come upon them as a [asnare](#note15a), and their [bfolly](#note15b) shall be made manifest, and their works shall follow them in the eyes of the people.

16 And verily I say unto you, as I have said before, he that [alooketh](#note16a) on a woman to [blust after](#note16b) her, or if any shall commit [cadultery](#note16c) in their hearts, they shall not have the [dSpirit](#note16d), but shall deny the faith and shall fear.

17 Wherefore, I, the Lord, have said that the [afearful](#note17a), and the [bunbelieving](#note17b), and all [cliars](#note17c), and whosoever loveth and maketh a lie, and the [dwhoremonger](#note17d), and the [esorcerer](#note17e), shall have their part in that [flake](#note17f) which burneth with fire and brimstone, which is the [gsecond death](#note17g).

18 Verily I say, that they shall not have part in the [afirst resurrection](#note18a).

19 And now behold, I, the Lord, say unto you that ye are not [ajustified](#note19a), because these things are among you.

20 Nevertheless, he that [aendureth](#note20a) in faith and doeth my [bwill](#note20b), the same shall overcome, and shall receive an [cinheritance](#note20c) upon the earth when the day of transfiguration shall come;

21 When the [aearth](#note21a) shall be [btransfigured](#note21b), even according to the pattern which was shown unto mine apostles upon the [cmount](#note21c); of which account the fulness ye have not yet received.

22 And now, verily I say unto you, that as I said that I would make known my will unto you, behold I will make it known unto you, [anot](#note22a) by the way of commandment, for there are many who observe not to keep my commandments.

23 But unto him that keepeth my commandments I will give the [amysteries](#note23a) of my kingdom, and the same shall be in him a well of living [bwater](#note23b), [cspringing up](#note23c) unto everlasting life.

24 And now, behold, this is the will of the Lord your God concerning his saints, that they should [aassemble](#note24a) themselves together unto the land of Zion, not in haste, lest there should be confusion, which bringeth pestilence.

25 Behold, the land of [aZion](#note25a)—I, the Lord, hold it in mine own hands;

26 Nevertheless, I, the Lord, render unto [aCæsar](#note26a) the things which are Cæsar’s.

27 Wherefore, I the Lord will that you should [apurchase](#note27a) the lands, that you may have advantage of the world, that you may have claim on the world, that they may not be [bstirred up](#note27b) unto anger.

28 For [aSatan](#note28a) [bputteth](#note28b) it into their hearts to anger against you, and to the shedding of blood.

29 Wherefore, the land of Zion shall not be obtained but by [apurchase](#note29a) or by blood, otherwise there is none inheritance for you.

30 And if by purchase, behold you are blessed;

31 And if by [ablood](#note31a), as you are forbidden to shed blood, lo, your enemies are upon you, and ye shall be scourged from city to city, and from synagogue to synagogue, and but [bfew](#note31b) shall stand to receive an inheritance.

32 I, the Lord, am [aangry](#note32a) with the [bwicked](#note32b); I am holding my [cSpirit](#note32c) from the inhabitants of the earth.

33 I have sworn in my wrath, and [adecreed wars](#note33a) upon the face of the earth, and the wicked shall [bslay](#note33b) the wicked, and fear shall come upon every man;

34 And the [asaints](#note34a) also shall [bhardly](#note34b) [cescape](#note34c); nevertheless, I, the Lord, am with them, and will [dcome](#note34d) down in heaven from the presence of my Father and [econsume](#note34e) the wicked with unquenchable fire.

35 And behold, this is not yet, but [aby and by](#note35a).

36 Wherefore, seeing that I, the Lord, have decreed all these things upon the face of the earth, I will that my saints should be assembled upon the land of Zion;

37 [aAnd](#note37a) that every man should take [brighteousness](#note37b) in his hands and [cfaithfulness](#note37c) upon his loins, and lift a warning [dvoice](#note37d) unto the inhabitants of the earth; and declare both by word and by flight that [edesolation](#note37e) shall come upon the wicked.

38 Wherefore, let my disciples in Kirtland arrange their temporal concerns, who dwell upon this farm.

39 Let my servant Titus Billings, who has the care thereof, dispose of the land, that he may be prepared in the coming spring to take his journey up unto the land of Zion, with those that dwell upon the face thereof, excepting those whom I shall reserve unto myself, that shall [anot go](#note39a) until I shall command them.

40 And let all the moneys which can be spared, it mattereth not unto me whether it be little or much, be sent up unto the land of Zion, unto them whom I have appointed to [areceive](#note40a).

41 Behold, I, the Lord, will give unto my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., power that he shall be enabled to [adiscern](#note41a) by the Spirit those who shall go up unto the land of Zion, and those of my disciples who shall tarry.

42 Let my servant [aNewel K. Whitney](#note42a) retain his [bstore](#note42b), or in other words, the store, yet for a little season.

43 Nevertheless, let him impart all the money which he can impart, to be sent up unto the land of Zion.

44 Behold, these things are in his own hands, let him do [aaccording](#note44a) to wisdom.

45 Verily I say, let him be ordained as an agent unto the disciples that shall tarry, and let him be [aordained](#note45a) unto this power;

46 And now speedily visit the churches, expounding these things unto them, with my servant Oliver Cowdery. Behold, this is my will, obtaining moneys even as I have directed.

47 He that is [afaithful](#note47a) and [bendureth](#note47b) shall overcome the world.

48 He that sendeth up treasures unto the land of Zion shall receive an [ainheritance](#note48a) in this world, and his works shall follow him, and also a [breward](#note48b) in the world to come.

49 Yea, and blessed are the dead that [adie](#note49a) in the Lord, from henceforth, when the Lord shall come, and old things shall [bpass away](#note49b), and all things become new, they shall [crise](#note49c) from the dead and shall not [ddie](#note49d) after, and shall receive an inheritance before the Lord, in the [eholy city](#note49e).

50 And he that liveth when the Lord shall come, and hath kept the faith, [ablessed](#note50a) is he; nevertheless, it is appointed to him to [bdie](#note50b) at the age of man.

51 Wherefore, [achildren](#note51a) shall [bgrow up](#note51b) until they become old; old men shall die; but they shall not sleep in the dust, but they shall be [cchanged](#note51c) in the twinkling of an eye.

52 Wherefore, for this cause preached the apostles unto the world the resurrection of the dead.

53 These things are the things that ye must look for; and, speaking after the manner of the Lord, they are now [anigh](#note53a) at hand, and in a time to come, even in the day of the coming of the Son of Man.

54 And until that hour there will be foolish [avirgins](#note54a) among the wise; and at that hour cometh an entire [bseparation](#note54b) of the righteous and the wicked; and in that day will I send mine angels to [cpluck](#note54c) out the wicked and cast them into unquenchable fire.

55 And now behold, verily I say unto you, I, the Lord, am not pleased with my servant [aSidney Rigdon](#note55a); he [bexalted](#note55b) himself in his heart, and received not counsel, but [cgrieved](#note55c) the Spirit;

56 Wherefore his [awriting](#note56a) is not acceptable unto the Lord, and he shall make another; and if the Lord receive it not, behold he standeth no longer in the office to which I have appointed him.

57 And again, verily I say unto you, [athose](#note57a) who desire in their hearts, in meekness, to [bwarn](#note57b) sinners to repentance, let them be ordained unto this power.

58 For this is a day of [awarning](#note58a), and not a day of many words. For I, the Lord, am not to be [bmocked](#note58b) in the last days.

59 Behold, I am from above, and my power lieth beneath. I am over all, and in all, and through all, and [asearch](#note59a) all things, and the day cometh that all things shall be [bsubject](#note59b) unto me.

60 Behold, I am [aAlpha and Omega](#note60a), even Jesus Christ.

61 Wherefore, let all men [abeware](#note61a) how they take my [bname](#note61b) in their lips—

62 For behold, verily I say, that many there be who are under this condemnation, who use the name of the Lord, and use it in vain, having not [aauthority](#note62a).

63 Wherefore, let the church repent of their sins, and I, the Lord, will [aown](#note63a) them; otherwise they shall be cut off.

64 Remember that that which cometh from above is [asacred](#note64a), and must be [bspoken](#note64b) with care, and by constraint of the Spirit; and in this there is no condemnation, and ye receive the Spirit [cthrough](#note64c) prayer; wherefore, without this there remaineth condemnation.

65 Let my servants, Joseph Smith, Jun., and Sidney Rigdon, seek them a home, as they are [ataught](#note65a) through prayer by the Spirit.

66 These things remain to overcome through patience, that such may receive a more exceeding and eternal [aweight](#note66a) of [bglory](#note66b), otherwise, a greater condemnation. Amen.

Section 64

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to the elders of the Church, at Kirtland, Ohio, September 11, 1831. The Prophet was preparing to move to Hiram, Ohio, to renew his work on the translation of the Bible, which had been laid aside while he had been in Missouri. A company of brethren who had been commanded to journey to Zion (Missouri) was earnestly engaged in making preparations to leave in October. At this busy time, the revelation was received.

[1–11](64.xhtml#p1), The Saints are commanded to forgive one another, lest there remain in them the greater sin; [12–22](64.xhtml#p12), The unrepentant are to be brought before the Church; [23–25](64.xhtml#p23), He that is tithed will not be burned at the Lord’s coming; [26–32](64.xhtml#p26), The Saints are warned against debt; [33–36](64.xhtml#p33), The rebellious will be cut off out of Zion; [37–40](64.xhtml#p37), The Church will judge the nations; [41–43](64.xhtml#p41), Zion will flourish.

1 Behold, thus saith the Lord your God unto you, O ye elders of my [achurch](#note1a), hearken ye and hear, and receive my will concerning you.

2 For verily I say unto you, I will that ye should [aovercome](#note2a) the world; wherefore I will have [bcompassion](#note2b) upon you.

3 There are those among you who have sinned; but verily I say, for this once, for mine own [aglory](#note3a), and for the salvation of souls, I have [bforgiven](#note3b) you your sins.

4 I will be merciful unto you, for I have given unto you the [akingdom](#note4a).

5 And the [akeys](#note5a) of the mysteries of the kingdom shall not be taken from my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., through the means I have appointed, while he liveth, inasmuch as he obeyeth mine [bordinances](#note5b).

6 There are those who have sought occasion against him without cause;

7 Nevertheless, he has sinned; but verily I say unto you, I, the Lord, [aforgive](#note7a) sins unto those who [bconfess](#note7b) their sins before me and ask forgiveness, who have not [csinned](#note7c) unto [ddeath](#note7d).

8 My disciples, in days of old, sought [aoccasion](#note8a) against one another and forgave not one another in their hearts; and for this [bevil](#note8b) they were [cafflicted](#note8c) and sorely [dchastened](#note8d).

9 Wherefore, I say unto you, that ye ought to [aforgive](#note9a) one another; for he that [bforgiveth](#note9b) not his brother his trespasses standeth condemned before the Lord; for there remaineth in him the greater sin.

10 I, the Lord, will [aforgive](#note10a) whom I will forgive, but of you it is required to [bforgive](#note10b) all men.

11 And ye ought to say in your hearts—let God [ajudge](#note11a) between me and thee, and [breward](#note11b) thee according to thy [cdeeds](#note11c).

12 And him that [arepenteth](#note12a) not of his sins, and [bconfesseth](#note12b) them not, ye shall bring before the [cchurch](#note12c), and do with him as the [dscripture](#note12d) saith unto you, either by commandment or by revelation.

13 And this ye shall do that God may be glorified—not because ye forgive not, having not compassion, but that ye may be justified in the eyes of the law, that ye may not [aoffend](#note13a) him who is your lawgiver—

14 Verily I say, for this cause ye shall do these things.

15 Behold, I, the Lord, was angry with him who was my servant Ezra Booth, and also my servant Isaac Morley, for they [akept](#note15a) not the law, neither the commandment;

16 They sought [aevil](#note16a) in their hearts, and I, the Lord, [bwithheld](#note16b) my Spirit. They [ccondemned](#note16c) for evil that thing in which there was no evil; nevertheless I have forgiven my servant Isaac Morley.

17 And also my servant [aEdward Partridge](#note17a), behold, he hath sinned, and [bSatan](#note17b) seeketh to destroy his soul; but when these things are made known unto them, and they repent of the evil, they shall be forgiven.

18 And now, verily I say that it is expedient in me that my servant Sidney Gilbert, after a few weeks, shall return upon his business, and to his [aagency](#note18a) in the land of Zion;

19 And that which he hath seen and heard may be made known unto my disciples, that they perish not. And for this cause have I spoken these things.

20 And again, I say unto you, that my servant Isaac Morley may not be [atempted](#note20a) above that which he is able to bear, and counsel wrongfully to your hurt, I gave commandment that his farm should be sold.

21 I will not that my servant Frederick G. Williams should sell his farm, for I, the Lord, will to retain a strong hold in the land of Kirtland, for the space of five years, in the which I will not overthrow the wicked, that thereby I may save some.

22 And after that day, I, the Lord, will not hold any [aguilty](#note22a) that shall go with an open heart up to the land of Zion; for I, the Lord, require the [bhearts](#note22b) of the children of men.

23 Behold, now it is called [atoday](#note23a) until the [bcoming](#note23b) of the Son of Man, and verily it is a day of [csacrifice](#note23c), and a day for the tithing of my people; for he that is [dtithed](#note23d) shall not be [eburned](#note23e) at his coming.

24 For after today cometh the [aburning](#note24a)—this is speaking after the manner of the Lord—for verily I say, tomorrow all the [bproud](#note24b) and they that do wickedly shall be as [cstubble](#note24c); and I will burn them up, for I am the Lord of Hosts; and I will not [dspare](#note24d) any that remain in [eBabylon](#note24e).

25 Wherefore, if ye believe me, ye will labor while it is called [atoday](#note25a).

26 And it is not meet that my servants, [aNewel K. Whitney](#note26a) and Sidney Gilbert, should sell their [bstore](#note26b) and their possessions here; for this is not wisdom until the residue of the church, which remaineth in this place, shall go up unto the land of Zion.

27 Behold, it is said in my laws, or forbidden, to get in [adebt](#note27a) to thine enemies;

28 But behold, it is not said at any time that the Lord should not take when he please, and pay as seemeth him good.

29 Wherefore, as ye are agents, ye are on the Lord’s errand; and whatever ye do according to the will of the Lord is the Lord’s business.

30 And he hath set you to provide for his saints in these last days, that they may obtain an [ainheritance](#note30a) in the land of Zion.

31 And behold, I, the Lord, declare unto you, and my [awords](#note31a) are sure and shall not [bfail](#note31b), that they shall obtain it.

32 But all things must come to pass in their time.

33 Wherefore, be not [aweary](#note33a) in [bwell-doing](#note33b), for ye are laying the foundation of a great work. And out of [csmall things](#note33c) proceedeth that which is great.

34 Behold, the Lord [arequireth](#note34a) the [bheart](#note34b) and a [cwilling](#note34c) mind; and the willing and [dobedient](#note34d) shall [eeat](#note34e) the good of the land of Zion in these last days.

35 And the [arebellious](#note35a) shall be [bcut off](#note35b) out of the land of Zion, and shall be sent away, and shall not inherit the land.

36 For, verily I say that the rebellious are not of the blood of [aEphraim](#note36a), wherefore they shall be plucked out.

37 Behold, I, the Lord, have made my church in these last days like unto a [ajudge](#note37a) sitting on a hill, or in a high place, to [bjudge](#note37b) the nations.

38 For it shall come to pass that the inhabitants of Zion shall [ajudge](#note38a) all things pertaining to Zion.

39 And [aliars](#note39a) and hypocrites shall be proved by them, and they who are [bnot](#note39b) [capostles](#note39c) and prophets shall be [dknown](#note39d).

40 And even the [abishop](#note40a), who is a [bjudge](#note40b), and his counselors, if they are not faithful in their [cstewardships](#note40c) shall be condemned, and [dothers](#note40d) shall be planted in their [estead](#note40e).

41 For, behold, I say unto you that [aZion](#note41a) shall flourish, and the [bglory](#note41b) of the Lord shall be upon her;

42 And she shall be an [aensign](#note42a) unto the people, and there shall come unto her out of every [bnation](#note42b) under heaven.

43 And the day shall come when the nations of the earth shall [atremble](#note43a) because of her, and shall fear because of her terrible ones. The Lord hath spoken it. Amen.

Section 65

Revelation on prayer given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, October 30, 1831.

[1–2](65.xhtml#p1), The keys of the kingdom of God are committed to man on earth, and the gospel cause will triumph; [3–6](65.xhtml#p3), The millennial kingdom of heaven will come and join the kingdom of God on earth.

1 Hearken, and lo, a voice as of one sent down from on high, who is mighty and powerful, whose going forth is unto the ends of the earth, yea, whose voice is unto men—[aPrepare](#note1a) ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight.

2 The [akeys](#note2a) of the [bkingdom](#note2b) of God are committed unto man on the earth, and from thence shall the [cgospel](#note2c) roll forth unto the ends of the earth, as the [dstone](#note2d) which is cut out of the mountain without hands shall roll forth, until it has [efilled](#note2e) the whole earth.

3 Yea, a voice crying—[aPrepare](#note3a) ye the way of the Lord, prepare ye the [bsupper](#note3b) of the Lamb, make ready for the [cBridegroom](#note3c).

4 Pray unto the Lord, [acall](#note4a) upon his holy name, make known his wonderful [bworks](#note4b) among the people.

5 Call upon the Lord, that his kingdom may go forth upon the earth, that the inhabitants thereof may receive it, and be prepared for the days to come, in the which the Son of Man shall [acome](#note5a) down in heaven, [bclothed](#note5b) in the brightness of his [cglory](#note5c), to meet the [dkingdom](#note5d) of God which is set up on the earth.

6 Wherefore, may the [akingdom](#note6a) of God go forth, that the [bkingdom](#note6b) of heaven may come, that thou, O God, mayest be [cglorified](#note6c) in heaven so on earth, that thine [denemies](#note6d) may be subdued; for [ethine](#note6e) is the honor, power and glory, forever and ever. Amen.

Section 66

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, October 29, 1831. William E. McLellin had petitioned the Lord in secret to make known through the Prophet the answer to five questions, which were unknown to Joseph Smith. At McLellin’s request, the Prophet inquired of the Lord and received this revelation.

[1–4](66.xhtml#p1), The everlasting covenant is the fulness of the gospel; [5–8](66.xhtml#p5), Elders are to preach, testify, and reason with the people; [9–13](66.xhtml#p9), Faithful ministerial service ensures an inheritance of eternal life.

1 Behold, thus saith the Lord unto my servant [aWilliam E. McLellin](#note1a)—Blessed are you, inasmuch as you have [bturned away](#note1b) from your iniquities, and have received my truths, saith the Lord your Redeemer, the [cSavior](#note1c) of the world, even of as many as believe on my name.

2 Verily I say unto you, blessed are you for receiving mine [aeverlasting covenant](#note2a), even the fulness of my gospel, sent forth unto the children of men, that they might have [blife](#note2b) and be made [cpartakers](#note2c) of the [dglories](#note2d) which are to be revealed in the last days, as it was written by the prophets and apostles in days of old.

3 Verily I say unto you, my servant William, that you are clean, but not [aall](#note3a); repent, therefore, of those things which are not pleasing in my sight, saith the Lord, for the Lord will [bshow](#note3b) them unto you.

4 And now, verily, I, the Lord, will show unto you what I [awill](#note4a) concerning you, or what is my will concerning you.

5 Behold, verily I say unto you, that it is my will that you should [aproclaim](#note5a) my gospel from land to land, and from [bcity to city](#note5b), yea, in those regions round about where it has not been proclaimed.

6 Tarry not many days in this place; go [anot](#note6a) up unto the land of Zion as yet; but inasmuch as you can [bsend](#note6b), send; otherwise, think not of thy property.

7 [aGo](#note7a) unto the eastern lands, bear [btestimony](#note7b) in every place, unto every people and in their [csynagogues](#note7c), reasoning with the people.

8 Let my servant Samuel H. Smith go with you, and forsake him not, and give him thine instructions; and he that is [afaithful](#note8a) shall be made [bstrong](#note8b) in every place; and I, the Lord, will go with you.

9 Lay your [ahands](#note9a) upon the [bsick](#note9b), and they shall [crecover](#note9c). Return not till I, the Lord, shall send you. Be patient in affliction. [dAsk](#note9d), and ye shall receive; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.

10 Seek not to be [acumbered](#note10a). Forsake all [bunrighteousness](#note10b). Commit not [cadultery](#note10c)—a temptation with which thou hast been troubled.

11 [aKeep](#note11a) these sayings, for they are true and [bfaithful](#note11b); and thou shalt [cmagnify](#note11c) thine office, and push many people to [dZion](#note11d) with [esongs](#note11e) of everlasting joy upon their heads.

12 [aContinue](#note12a) in these things even unto the end, and you shall have a [bcrown](#note12b) of eternal life at the right hand of my Father, who is full of [cgrace](#note12c) and truth.

13 Verily, thus saith the Lord your [aGod](#note13a), your Redeemer, even Jesus Christ. Amen.

Section 67

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, early November 1831. The occasion was that of a special conference, and the publication of the revelations already received from the Lord through the Prophet was considered and acted upon (see the [heading to section 1](1.xhtml)). William W. Phelps had recently established the Church printing press in Independence, Missouri. The conference decided to publish the revelations in the Book of Commandments and to print 10,000 copies (which because of unforeseen difficulties was later reduced to 3,000 copies). Many of the brethren bore solemn testimony that the revelations then compiled for publication were verily true, as was witnessed by the Holy Ghost shed forth upon them. Joseph Smith’s history records that after the revelation known as [section 1](1.xhtml) had been received, some conversation was had concerning the language used in the revelations. The present revelation followed.

[1–3](67.xhtml#p1), The Lord hears the prayers of and watches over His elders; [4–9](67.xhtml#p4), He challenges the wisest person to duplicate the least of His revelations; [10–14](67.xhtml#p10), Faithful elders will be quickened by the Spirit and see the face of God.

1 Behold and hearken, O ye [aelders](#note1a) of my church, who have assembled yourselves together, whose [bprayers](#note1b) I have heard, and whose [chearts](#note1c) I know, and whose desires have come up before me.

2 Behold and lo, mine [aeyes](#note2a) are upon you, and the heavens and the earth are in mine [bhands](#note2b), and the riches of eternity are mine to give.

3 Ye endeavored to [abelieve](#note3a) that ye should receive the blessing which was offered unto you; but behold, verily I say unto you there were [bfears](#note3b) in your hearts, and verily this is the reason that ye did not receive.

4 And now I, the Lord, give unto you a [atestimony](#note4a) of the truth of these commandments which are lying before you.

5 Your eyes have been upon my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., and his [alanguage](#note5a) you have known, and his imperfections you have known; and you have sought in your hearts knowledge that you might express beyond his language; this you also know.

6 Now, seek ye out of the Book of Commandments, even the least that is among them, and appoint him that is the most [awise](#note6a) among you;

7 Or, if there be any among you that shall make one [alike](#note7a) unto it, then ye are justified in saying that ye do not know that they are true;

8 But if ye cannot make one like unto it, ye are under condemnation if ye do not [abear record](#note8a) that they are true.

9 For ye know that there is no unrighteousness in them, and that which is [arighteous](#note9a) cometh down from above, from the Father of [blights](#note9b).

10 And again, verily I say unto you that it is your privilege, and a [apromise](#note10a) I give unto you that have been ordained unto this ministry, that inasmuch as you [bstrip](#note10b) yourselves from [cjealousies](#note10c) and [dfears](#note10d), and [ehumble](#note10e) yourselves before me, for ye are not sufficiently humble, the [fveil](#note10f) shall be rent and you shall [gsee](#note10g) me and know that I am—not with the carnal neither natural mind, but with the spiritual.

11 For no [aman](#note11a) has seen God at any time in the flesh, except quickened by the Spirit of God.

12 Neither can any [anatural man](#note12a) abide the presence of God, neither after the carnal mind.

13 Ye are not able to abide the presence of God now, neither the ministering of angels; wherefore, [acontinue](#note13a) in patience until ye are [bperfected](#note13b).

14 Let not your minds [aturn](#note14a) back; and when ye are [bworthy](#note14b), in mine own due time, ye shall see and know that which was conferred upon you by the hands of my servant Joseph Smith, Jun. Amen.

Section 68

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, November 1, 1831, in response to prayer that the mind of the Lord be made known concerning Orson Hyde, Luke S. Johnson, Lyman E. Johnson, and William E. McLellin. Although part of this revelation was directed toward these four men, much of the content pertains to the whole Church. This revelation was expanded under Joseph Smith’s direction when it was published in the 1835 edition of the Doctrine and Covenants.

[1–5](68.xhtml#p1), The words of the elders when moved upon by the Holy Ghost are scripture; [6–12](68.xhtml#p6), Elders are to preach and baptize, and signs will follow true believers; [13–24](68.xhtml#p13), The firstborn among the sons of Aaron may serve as the Presiding Bishop (that is, hold the keys of presidency as a bishop) under the direction of the First Presidency; [25–28](68.xhtml#p25), Parents are commanded to teach the gospel to their children; [29–35](68.xhtml#p29), The Saints are to observe the Sabbath, labor diligently, and pray.

1 My servant, Orson Hyde, was called by his ordination to proclaim the [aeverlasting gospel](#note1a), by the [bSpirit](#note1b) of the living God, from people to people, and from land to land, in the [ccongregations](#note1c) of the wicked, in their [dsynagogues](#note1d), reasoning with and [eexpounding](#note1e) all scriptures unto them.

2 And, behold, and lo, this is an ensample unto all those who were ordained unto this priesthood, whose mission is appointed unto them to go forth—

3 And this is the [aensample](#note3a) unto them, that they shall [bspeak](#note3b) as they are moved upon by the Holy Ghost.

4 And whatsoever they shall speak when moved upon by the [aHoly Ghost](#note4a) shall be scripture, shall be the will of the Lord, shall be the mind of the Lord, shall be the word of the Lord, shall be the voice of the Lord, and the [bpower](#note4b) of God unto salvation.

5 Behold, this is the promise of the Lord unto you, O ye my servants.

6 Wherefore, be of good [acheer](#note6a), and do not [bfear](#note6b), for I the Lord am with you, and will stand by you; and ye shall bear record of me, even Jesus Christ, that I am the Son of the living God, that I [cwas](#note6c), that I am, and that I am to come.

7 This is the word of the Lord unto you, my servant Orson Hyde, and also unto my servant Luke Johnson, and unto my servant Lyman Johnson, and unto my servant [aWilliam E. McLellin](#note7a), and unto all the faithful elders of my church—

8 [aGo](#note8a) ye into all the world, [bpreach](#note8b) the gospel to every [ccreature](#note8c), acting in the [dauthority](#note8d) which I have given you, [ebaptizing](#note8e) in the name of the Father, and of the Son, and of the Holy Ghost.

9 And [ahe](#note9a) that believeth and is baptized shall be saved, and he that believeth not shall be [bdamned](#note9b).

10 And he that believeth shall be blest with [asigns](#note10a) following, even as it is written.

11 And unto you it shall be given to know the signs of the [atimes](#note11a), and the [bsigns](#note11b) of the coming of the Son of Man;

12 And of as many as the Father shall bear record, to you shall be given power to [aseal](#note12a) them up unto eternal life. Amen.

13 And now, concerning the items in addition to the [acovenants](#note13a) and commandments, they are these—

14 There remain hereafter, in the due time of the Lord, other [abishops](#note14a) to be set apart unto the [bchurch](#note14b), to minister even according to the first;

15 Wherefore they shall be [ahigh priests](#note15a) who are worthy, and they shall be appointed by the [bFirst Presidency](#note15b) of the Melchizedek Priesthood, except they be literal descendants of [cAaron](#note15c).

16 And if they be literal descendants of [aAaron](#note16a) they have a legal right to the bishopric, if they are the [bfirstborn](#note16b) among the sons of Aaron;

17 For the firstborn holds the right of the presidency over this priesthood, and the [akeys](#note17a) or authority of the same.

18 No man has a legal right to this office, to hold the keys of this priesthood, except he be a [aliteral descendant](#note18a) and the firstborn of Aaron.

19 But, as a [ahigh priest](#note19a) of the Melchizedek Priesthood has authority to officiate in all the lesser offices he may officiate in the office of [bbishop](#note19b) when no literal descendant of Aaron can be found, provided he is called and set apart and ordained unto this power, under the hands of the First Presidency of the Melchizedek Priesthood.

20 And a literal descendant of Aaron, also, must be designated by this Presidency, and found worthy, and [aanointed](#note20a), and ordained under the hands of this Presidency, otherwise they are not legally authorized to officiate in their priesthood.

21 But, by virtue of the decree concerning their right of the priesthood descending from father to son, they may claim their [aanointing](#note21a) if at any time they can prove their lineage, or do ascertain it by revelation from the Lord under the [bhands](#note21b) of the above named Presidency.

22 And again, no bishop or high priest who shall be set apart for this ministry shall be tried or [acondemned](#note22a) for any crime, save it be before the [bFirst Presidency](#note22b) of the church;

23 And inasmuch as he is found [aguilty](#note23a) before this Presidency, by testimony that cannot be impeached, he shall be condemned;

24 And if he repent he shall be [aforgiven](#note24a), according to the covenants and [bcommandments](#note24b) of the church.

25 And again, inasmuch as [aparents](#note25a) have children in Zion, or in any of her [bstakes](#note25b) which are organized, that [cteach](#note25c) them not to understand the [ddoctrine](#note25d) of repentance, faith in Christ the Son of the living God, and of baptism and the gift of the Holy Ghost by the laying on of the hands, when [eeight](#note25e) years old, the [fsin](#note25f) be upon the heads of the parents.

26 For this shall be a law unto the [ainhabitants](#note26a) of Zion, or in any of her stakes which are organized.

27 And their children shall be [abaptized](#note27a) for the [bremission](#note27b) of their sins when [ceight](#note27c) years old, and receive the laying on of the hands.

28 And they shall also [ateach](#note28a) their children to pray, and to walk uprightly before the Lord.

29 And the inhabitants of Zion shall also observe the [aSabbath](#note29a) day to keep it holy.

30 And the inhabitants of Zion also shall remember their [alabors](#note30a), inasmuch as they are appointed to labor, in all faithfulness; for the [bidler](#note30b) shall be had in remembrance before the Lord.

31 Now, I, the Lord, am not well [apleased](#note31a) with the inhabitants of Zion, for there are [bidlers](#note31b) among them; and their [cchildren](#note31c) are also growing up in [dwickedness](#note31d); they also [eseek](#note31e) not earnestly the riches of eternity, but their eyes are full of [fgreediness](#note31f).

32 These things ought not to be, and must be done away from among them; wherefore, let my servant Oliver Cowdery [acarry](#note32a) these sayings unto the land of Zion.

33 And a commandment I give unto them—that he that observeth not his [aprayers](#note33a) before the Lord in the season thereof, let him be had in [bremembrance](#note33b) before the judge of my people.

34 These sayings are [atrue](#note34a) and faithful; wherefore, transgress them not, neither [btake](#note34b) therefrom.

35 Behold, I am [aAlpha and Omega](#note35a), and I [bcome](#note35b) quickly. Amen.

Section 69

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, November 11, 1831. The compilation of revelations intended for early publication had been passed upon at the special conference of November 1–2. On November 3, the revelation herein appearing as [section 133](133.xhtml), later called the Appendix, was added. Oliver Cowdery had previously been appointed to carry the manuscript of the compiled revelations and commandments to Independence, Missouri, for printing. He was also to take with him money that had been contributed for the building up of the Church in Missouri. This revelation instructs John Whitmer to accompany Oliver Cowdery and also directs Whitmer to travel and collect historical material in his calling as Church historian and recorder.

[1–2](69.xhtml#p1), John Whitmer is to accompany Oliver Cowdery to Missouri; [3–8](69.xhtml#p3), He is also to preach and to collect, record, and write historical data.

1 Hearken unto me, saith the Lord your God, for my servant Oliver Cowdery’s sake. It is not wisdom in me that he should be entrusted with the commandments and the moneys which he shall [acarry](#note1a) unto the land of Zion, except one go with him who will be [btrue](#note1b) and faithful.

2 Wherefore, I, the Lord, will that my servant, John Whitmer, should go with my servant Oliver Cowdery;

3 And also that he shall continue in [awriting](#note3a) and making a [bhistory](#note3b) of all the important things which he shall observe and know concerning my church;

4 And also that he receive [acounsel](#note4a) and assistance from my servant Oliver Cowdery and others.

5 And also, my servants who are abroad in the earth should send forth the accounts of their [astewardships](#note5a) to the land of Zion;

6 For the land of Zion shall be a [aseat](#note6a) and a place to receive and do all these things.

7 Nevertheless, let my servant John Whitmer travel many times from place to place, and from church to church, that he may the more easily obtain knowledge—

8 Preaching and expounding, writing, copying, selecting, and obtaining all things which shall be for the good of the church, and for the rising generations that shall grow up on the land of Zion, to [apossess](#note8a) it from generation to generation, forever and ever. Amen.

Section 70

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, November 12, 1831. The Prophet’s history states that four special conferences were held from the 1st to the 12th of November, inclusive. In the last of these assemblies, the great importance of the revelations that would later be published as the Book of Commandments and then the Doctrine and Covenants was considered. This revelation was given after the conference voted that the revelations were “worth to the Church the riches of the whole Earth.” Joseph Smith’s history refers to the revelations as “the foundation of the Church in these last days, and a benefit to the world, showing that the keys of the mysteries of the kingdom of our Savior are again entrusted to man.”

[1–5](70.xhtml#p1), Stewards are appointed to publish the revelations; [6–13](70.xhtml#p6), Those who labor in spiritual things are worthy of their hire; [14–18](70.xhtml#p14), The Saints should be equal in temporal things.

1 Behold, and hearken, O ye inhabitants of Zion, and all ye people of my church who are afar off, and [ahear](#note1a) the word of the Lord which I give unto my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., and also unto my servant Martin Harris, and also unto my servant Oliver Cowdery, and also unto my servant John Whitmer, and also unto my servant Sidney Rigdon, and also unto my servant [bWilliam W. Phelps](#note1b), by the way of commandment unto them.

2 For I give unto them a commandment; wherefore hearken and hear, for thus saith the Lord unto them—

3 I, the Lord, have appointed them, and ordained them to be [astewards](#note3a) over the revelations and commandments which I have given unto them, and which I shall hereafter give unto them;

4 And an account of this [astewardship](#note4a) will I require of them in the day of judgment.

5 Wherefore, I have appointed unto them, and this is their business in the church of God, to [amanage](#note5a) them and the concerns thereof, yea, the benefits thereof.

6 Wherefore, a commandment I give unto them, that they shall not give these things unto the church, neither unto the [aworld](#note6a);

7 Nevertheless, inasmuch as they [areceive](#note7a) more than is needful for their necessities and their wants, it shall be given into my [bstorehouse](#note7b);

8 And the [abenefits](#note8a) shall be consecrated unto the inhabitants of Zion, and unto their generations, inasmuch as they become [bheirs](#note8b) according to the laws of the kingdom.

9 Behold, this is what the Lord requires of every man in his [astewardship](#note9a), even as I, the Lord, have appointed or shall hereafter appoint unto any man.

10 And behold, none are exempt from this [alaw](#note10a) who belong to the church of the living God;

11 Yea, neither the bishop, neither the [aagent](#note11a) who keepeth the Lord’s storehouse, neither he who is appointed in a stewardship over [btemporal](#note11b) things.

12 He who is appointed to administer spiritual things, the same is [aworthy](#note12a) of his hire, even as those who are appointed to a stewardship to administer in temporal things;

13 Yea, even more abundantly, which abundance is multiplied unto them through the [amanifestations](#note13a) of the Spirit.

14 Nevertheless, in your temporal things you shall be [aequal](#note14a), and this not grudgingly, otherwise the abundance of the manifestations of the Spirit shall be [bwithheld](#note14b).

15 Now, this commandment I give unto my servants for their [abenefit](#note15a) while they remain, for a manifestation of my blessings upon their heads, and for a [breward](#note15b) of their [cdiligence](#note15c) and for their security;

16 For food and for [araiment](#note16a); for an inheritance; for houses and for lands, in whatsoever circumstances I, the Lord, shall place them, and whithersoever I, the Lord, shall send them.

17 For they have been faithful over [amany things](#note17a), and have done well inasmuch as they have not sinned.

18 Behold, I, the Lord, am [amerciful](#note18a) and will bless them, and they shall enter into the joy of these things. Even so. Amen.

Section 71

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet and Sidney Rigdon, at Hiram, Ohio, December 1, 1831. The Prophet had continued to translate the Bible with Sidney Rigdon as his scribe until this revelation was received, at which time it was temporarily laid aside so as to enable them to fulfill the instruction given herein. The brethren were to go forth to preach in order to allay the unfriendly feelings that had developed against the Church as a result of the publication of letters written by Ezra Booth, who had apostatized.

[1–4](71.xhtml#p1), Joseph Smith and Sidney Rigdon are sent forth to proclaim the gospel; [5–11](71.xhtml#p5), Enemies of the Saints will be confounded.

1 Behold, thus saith the Lord unto you my servants Joseph Smith, Jun., and Sidney Rigdon, that the time has verily come that it is necessary and expedient in me that you should open your mouths in [aproclaiming](#note1a) my gospel, the things of the kingdom, expounding the [bmysteries](#note1b) thereof out of the scriptures, according to that portion of Spirit and power which shall be given unto you, even as I will.

2 Verily I say unto you, proclaim unto the world in the regions round about, and in the church also, for the space of a season, even until it shall be [amade](#note2a) known unto you.

3 Verily this is a mission for a season, which I give unto you.

4 Wherefore, [alabor](#note4a) ye in my vineyard. Call upon the inhabitants of the earth, and bear record, and prepare the way for the commandments and revelations which are to come.

5 Now, behold this is wisdom; whoso readeth, let him [aunderstand](#note5a) and [breceive](#note5b) also;

6 For unto him that receiveth it shall be given more [aabundantly](#note6a), even power.

7 Wherefore, [aconfound](#note7a) your [benemies](#note7b); call upon them to [cmeet](#note7c) you both in public and in private; and inasmuch as ye are faithful their [dshame](#note7d) shall be made manifest.

8 Wherefore, let them bring forth their [astrong reasons](#note8a) against the Lord.

9 Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you—there is no [aweapon](#note9a) that is formed against you shall prosper;

10 And if any man lift his voice against you he shall be [aconfounded](#note10a) in mine own due time.

11 Wherefore, [akeep](#note11a) my commandments; they are true and faithful. Even so. Amen.

Section 72

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, December 4, 1831. Several elders and members had assembled to learn their duty and to be further edified in the teachings of the Church. This section is a compilation of three revelations received on the same day. [Verses 1 through 8](72.xhtml#p1) make known the calling of Newel K. Whitney as a bishop. He was then called and ordained, after which [verses 9 through 23](72.xhtml#p9) were received, giving additional information as to a bishop’s duties. Thereafter, [verses 24 through 26](72.xhtml#p24) were given, providing instructions concerning the gathering to Zion.

[1–8](72.xhtml#p1), Elders are to render an account of their stewardship unto the bishop; [9–15](72.xhtml#p9), The bishop keeps the storehouse and cares for the poor and needy; [16–26](72.xhtml#p16), Bishops are to certify the worthiness of elders.

1 Hearken, and listen to the voice of the Lord, O ye who have assembled yourselves together, who are the [ahigh priests](#note1a) of my church, to whom the [bkingdom](#note1b) and power have been given.

2 For verily thus saith the Lord, it is expedient in me for a [abishop](#note2a) to be appointed unto you, or of you, unto the church in this part of the Lord’s vineyard.

3 And verily in this thing ye have done wisely, for it is required of the Lord, at the hand of every [asteward](#note3a), to render an [baccount](#note3b) of his [cstewardship](#note3c), both in time and in eternity.

4 For he who is faithful and [awise](#note4a) in time is accounted worthy to inherit the [bmansions](#note4b) prepared for him of my Father.

5 Verily I say unto you, the elders of the church in this part of my vineyard shall render an account of their stewardship unto the [abishop](#note5a), who shall be appointed of me in this part of my vineyard.

6 These things shall be had on [arecord](#note6a), to be handed over unto the [bbishop](#note6b) in Zion.

7 And the duty of the [abishop](#note7a) shall be made known by the commandments which have been given, and the voice of the conference.

8 And now, verily I say unto you, my servant Newel K. Whitney is the man who shall be appointed and ordained unto this power. This is the will of the Lord your God, your Redeemer. Even so. Amen.

9 The word of the Lord, in addition to the [alaw](#note9a) which has been given, making known the [bduty](#note9b) of the [cbishop](#note9c) who has been ordained unto the church in this part of the vineyard, which is verily this—

10 To keep the Lord’s [astorehouse](#note10a); to receive the funds of the church in this part of the vineyard;

11 To take an account of the elders as before has been commanded; and to [aadminister](#note11a) to their wants, who shall pay for that which they receive, inasmuch as they have wherewith to pay;

12 That this also may be consecrated to the good of the church, to the poor and needy.

13 And he who [ahath](#note13a) not wherewith to pay, an account shall be taken and handed over to the bishop of Zion, who shall pay the debt out of that which the Lord shall put into his hands.

14 And the labors of the faithful who labor in spiritual things, in administering the gospel and the [athings](#note14a) of the kingdom unto the church, and unto the world, shall answer the debt unto the bishop in Zion;

15 Thus it cometh out of the church, for according to the [alaw](#note15a) every man that cometh up to Zion must lay all things before the bishop in Zion.

16 And now, verily I say unto you, that as every elder in this part of the vineyard must give an account of his stewardship unto the bishop in this part of the vineyard—

17 A [acertificate](#note17a) from the judge or bishop in this part of the vineyard, unto the bishop in Zion, rendereth every man acceptable, and answereth all things, for an inheritance, and to be received as a wise [bsteward](#note17b) and as a faithful [claborer](#note17c);

18 Otherwise he shall not be [aaccepted](#note18a) of the bishop of Zion.

19 And now, verily I say unto you, let every elder who shall give an account unto the bishop of the church in this part of the vineyard be [arecommended](#note19a) by the church or churches, in which he labors, that he may render himself and his accounts approved in all things.

20 And again, let my servants who are appointed as stewards over the [aliterary](#note20a) concerns of my church have claim for assistance upon the bishop or bishops in all things—

21 That the revelations may be [apublished](#note21a), and go forth unto the ends of the earth; that they also may obtain [bfunds](#note21b) which shall benefit the church in all things;

22 That they also may render themselves approved in all things, and be accounted as [awise](#note22a) stewards.

23 And now, behold, this shall be an [aensample](#note23a) for all the extensive branches of my church, in whatsoever land they shall be established. And now I make an end of my sayings. Amen.

24 A few words in addition to the laws of the kingdom, respecting the members of the church—they that are [aappointed](#note24a) by the Holy Spirit to go up unto Zion, and they who are [bprivileged](#note24b) to go up unto Zion—

25 Let them carry up unto the bishop a [acertificate](#note25a) from three elders of the church, or a certificate from the bishop;

26 Otherwise he who shall go up unto the land of Zion shall not be accounted as a wise steward. This is also an ensample. Amen.

Section 73

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet and Sidney Rigdon, at Hiram, Ohio, January 10, 1832. Since the early part of the preceding December, the Prophet and Sidney had been engaged in preaching, and by this means much was accomplished in diminishing the unfavorable feelings that had arisen against the Church (see the [heading to section 71](71.xhtml)).

[1–2](73.xhtml#p1), Elders are to continue to preach; [3–6](73.xhtml#p3), Joseph Smith and Sidney Rigdon are to continue to translate the Bible until it is finished.

1 For verily, thus saith the Lord, it is expedient in me that [athey](#note1a) should continue preaching the gospel, and in exhortation to the churches in the regions round about, until conference;

2 And then, behold, it shall be made known unto them, by the [avoice](#note2a) of the conference, their several missions.

3 Now, verily I say unto you my servants, Joseph Smith, Jun., and Sidney Rigdon, saith the Lord, it is [aexpedient](#note3a) to [btranslate](#note3b) again;

4 And, inasmuch as it is practicable, to preach in the regions round about until conference; and after that it is expedient to continue the work of [atranslation](#note4a) until it be finished.

5 And let this be a pattern unto the elders until further knowledge, even as it is written.

6 Now I give no more unto you at this time. [aGird](#note6a) up your loins and be sober. Even so. Amen.

Section 74

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Wayne County, New York, in 1830. Even before the organization of the Church, questions had arisen about the proper mode of baptism, leading the Prophet to seek answers on the subject. Joseph Smith’s history states that this revelation is an explanation of 1 Corinthians 7:14, a scripture that had often been used to justify infant baptism.

[1–5](74.xhtml#p1), Paul counsels the Church of his day not to keep the law of Moses; [6–7](74.xhtml#p6), Little children are holy and are sanctified through the Atonement.

1 For the [aunbelieving](#note1a) [bhusband](#note1b) is [csanctified](#note1c) by the wife, and the unbelieving wife is sanctified by the husband; else were your children unclean, but now are they holy.

2 Now, in the days of the apostles the law of circumcision was had among all the Jews who believed not the gospel of Jesus Christ.

3 And it came to pass that there arose a great [acontention](#note3a) among the people concerning the law of [bcircumcision](#note3b), for the unbelieving husband was desirous that his children should be circumcised and become subject to the [claw](#note3c) of Moses, which law was fulfilled.

4 And it came to pass that the children, being brought up in subjection to the law of Moses, gave heed to the [atraditions](#note4a) of their fathers and believed not the gospel of Christ, wherein they became unholy.

5 Wherefore, for this cause the apostle wrote unto the church, giving unto them a commandment, not of the Lord, but of himself, that a believer should not be [aunited](#note5a) to an [bunbeliever](#note5b); except the [claw](#note5c) of Moses should be done away among them,

6 That their children might remain without circumcision; and that the [atradition](#note6a) might be done away, which saith that little children are unholy; for it was had among the Jews;

7 But little [achildren](#note7a) are [bholy](#note7b), being [csanctified](#note7c) through the [datonement](#note7d) of Jesus Christ; and this is what the scriptures mean.

Section 75

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Amherst, Ohio, January 25, 1832. This section comprises two separate revelations (the first in [verses 1 through 22](75.xhtml#p1) and the second in [verses 23 through 36](75.xhtml#p23)) given on the same day. The occasion was a conference at which Joseph Smith was sustained and ordained President of the High Priesthood. Certain elders desired to learn more about their immediate duties. These revelations followed.

[1–5](75.xhtml#p1), Faithful elders who preach the gospel will gain eternal life; [6–12](75.xhtml#p6), Pray to receive the Comforter, who teaches all things; [13–22](75.xhtml#p13), Elders will sit in judgment on those who reject their message; [23–36](75.xhtml#p23), Families of missionaries are to receive help from the Church.

1 Verily, verily, I say unto you, I who speak even by the [avoice](#note1a) of my Spirit, even [bAlpha and Omega](#note1b), your Lord and your God—

2 Hearken, O ye who have [agiven](#note2a) your names to go forth to proclaim my gospel, and to [bprune](#note2b) my vineyard.

3 Behold, I say unto you that it is my will that you should go forth and not tarry, neither be [aidle](#note3a) but [blabor](#note3b) with your might—

4 Lifting up your voices as with the sound of a trump, [aproclaiming](#note4a) the [btruth](#note4b) according to the revelations and commandments which I have given you.

5 And thus, if ye are faithful ye shall be laden with many [asheaves](#note5a), and [bcrowned](#note5b) with honor, and glory, and immortality, and eternal life.

6 Therefore, verily I say unto my servant [aWilliam E. McLellin](#note6a), I [brevoke](#note6b) the commission which I gave unto him to go unto the eastern countries;

7 And I give unto him a new commission and a new commandment, in the which I, the Lord, [achasten](#note7a) him for the [bmurmurings](#note7b) of his heart;

8 And he sinned; nevertheless, I forgive him and say unto him again, Go ye into the south countries.

9 And let my servant Luke Johnson go with him, and proclaim the things which I have commanded them—

10 Calling on the name of the Lord for the [aComforter](#note10a), which shall teach them all things that are expedient for them—

11 [aPraying](#note11a) always that they [bfaint not](#note11b); and inasmuch as they do this, I will be with them even unto the end.

12 Behold, this is the will of the Lord your God concerning you. Even so. Amen.

13 And again, verily thus saith the Lord, let my servant Orson Hyde and my servant Samuel H. Smith take their journey into the eastern countries, and proclaim the things which I have commanded them; and inasmuch as they are faithful, lo, I will be [awith them](#note13a) even unto the end.

14 And again, verily I say unto my servant Lyman Johnson, and unto my servant Orson Pratt, they shall also take their journey into the eastern countries; and behold, and lo, I am with them also, even unto the end.

15 And again, I say unto my servant Asa Dodds, and unto my servant Calves Wilson, that they also shall take their journey unto the western countries, and proclaim my gospel, even as I have commanded them.

16 And he who is faithful shall overcome all things, and shall be [alifted up](#note16a) at the last day.

17 And again, I say unto my servant Major N. Ashley, and my servant Burr Riggs, let them take their journey also into the south country.

18 Yea, let all those take their journey, as I have commanded them, going from [ahouse to house](#note18a), and from village to village, and from city to city.

19 And in whatsoever house ye enter, and they receive you, leave your blessing upon that house.

20 And in whatsoever house ye enter, and they receive you not, ye shall depart speedily from that house, and [ashake](#note20a) off the dust of your feet as a testimony against them.

21 And you shall be filled with [ajoy](#note21a) and gladness; and know this, that in the day of judgment you shall be [bjudges](#note21b) of that house, and condemn them;

22 And it shall be more [atolerable](#note22a) for the [bheathen](#note22b) in the day of judgment, than for that house; therefore, [cgird](#note22c) up your loins and be faithful, and ye shall overcome all things, and be [dlifted up](#note22d) at the last day. Even so. Amen.

23 And again, thus saith the Lord unto you, O ye elders of my church, who have [agiven](#note23a) your names that you might know his will concerning you—

24 Behold, I say unto you, that it is the [aduty](#note24a) of the church to assist in [bsupporting](#note24b) the families of those, and also to support the families of those who are called and must needs be sent unto the world to proclaim the gospel unto the world.

25 Wherefore, I, the Lord, give unto you this commandment, that ye obtain places for your [afamilies](#note25a), inasmuch as your brethren are willing to open their hearts.

26 And let all such as can obtain places for their families, and support of the church for them, not fail to go into the world, whether to the east or to the west, or to the north, or to the south.

27 Let them [aask](#note27a) and they shall receive, knock and it shall be opened unto them, and be made known from on high, even by the [bComforter](#note27b), whither they shall go.

28 And again, verily I say unto you, that every [aman](#note28a) who is obliged to [bprovide](#note28b) for his own [cfamily](#note28c), let him provide, and he shall in nowise lose his crown; and let him labor in the church.

29 Let every man be [adiligent](#note29a) in all things. And the [bidler](#note29b) shall not have place in the church, except he repent and mend his ways.

30 Wherefore, let my servant Simeon Carter and my servant Emer Harris be united in the ministry;

31 And also my servant Ezra Thayre and my servant [aThomas B. Marsh](#note31a);

32 Also my servant Hyrum Smith and my servant Reynolds Cahoon;

33 And also my servant Daniel Stanton and my servant Seymour Brunson;

34 And also my servant Sylvester Smith and my servant Gideon Carter;

35 And also my servant Ruggles Eames and my servant [aStephen Burnett](#note35a);

36 And also my servant Micah B. Welton and also my servant [aEden Smith](#note36a). Even so. Amen.
