Section 76

A vision given to Joseph Smith the Prophet and Sidney Rigdon, at Hiram, Ohio, February 16, 1832. Prefacing the record of this vision, Joseph Smith’s history states: “Upon my return from Amherst conference, I resumed the translation of the Scriptures. From sundry revelations which had been received, it was apparent that many important points touching the salvation of man had been taken from the Bible, or lost before it was compiled. It appeared self-evident from what truths were left, that if God rewarded every one according to the deeds done in the body the term ‘Heaven,’ as intended for the Saints’ eternal home, must include more kingdoms than one. Accordingly, … while translating St. John’s Gospel, myself and Elder Rigdon saw the following vision.” At the time this vision was given, the Prophet was translating John 5:29.

[1–4](76.xhtml#p1), The Lord is God; [5–10](76.xhtml#p5), The mysteries of the kingdom will be revealed to all the faithful; [11–17](76.xhtml#p11), All will come forth in the resurrection of the just or the unjust; [18–24](76.xhtml#p18), The inhabitants of many worlds are begotten sons and daughters unto God through the Atonement of Jesus Christ; [25–29](76.xhtml#p25), An angel of God fell and became the devil; [30–49](76.xhtml#p30), Sons of perdition suffer eternal damnation; all others gain some degree of salvation; [50–70](76.xhtml#p50), The glory and reward of exalted beings in the celestial kingdom is described; [71–80](76.xhtml#p71), Those who will inherit the terrestrial kingdom are described; [81–113](76.xhtml#p81), The status of those in the telestial, terrestrial, and celestial glories is explained; [114–19](76.xhtml#p114), The faithful may see and understand the mysteries of God’s kingdom by the power of the Holy Spirit.

1 [aHear](#note1a), O ye heavens, and give ear, O earth, and rejoice ye inhabitants thereof, for the Lord is [bGod](#note1b), and beside him there is [cno](#note1c) [dSavior](#note1d).

2 [aGreat](#note2a) is his wisdom, [bmarvelous](#note2b) are his ways, and the extent of his doings none can find out.

3 His [apurposes](#note3a) fail not, neither are there any who can stay his hand.

4 From eternity to eternity he is the [asame](#note4a), and his years never [bfail](#note4b).

5 For thus saith the Lord—I, the Lord, am [amerciful](#note5a) and gracious unto those who [bfear](#note5b) me, and delight to honor those who [cserve](#note5c) me in righteousness and in truth unto the end.

6 Great shall be their reward and eternal shall be their [aglory](#note6a).

7 And to them will I [areveal](#note7a) all [bmysteries](#note7b), yea, all the hidden mysteries of my kingdom from days of old, and for ages to come, will I make known unto them the good pleasure of my will concerning all things pertaining to my kingdom.

8 Yea, even the wonders of [aeternity](#note8a) shall they know, and things to come will I show them, even the things of many generations.

9 And their [awisdom](#note9a) shall be great, and their [bunderstanding](#note9b) reach to heaven; and before them the wisdom of the wise shall [cperish](#note9c), and the understanding of the [dprudent](#note9d) shall come to naught.

10 For by my [aSpirit](#note10a) will I [benlighten](#note10b) them, and by my [cpower](#note10c) will I make known unto them the [dsecrets](#note10d) of my [ewill](#note10e)—yea, even those things which [feye](#note10f) has not seen, nor ear heard, nor yet entered into the heart of man.

11 We, Joseph Smith, Jun., and Sidney Rigdon, being [ain](#note11a) the Spirit on the sixteenth day of February, in the year of our Lord one thousand eight hundred and thirty-two—

12 By the power of the [aSpirit](#note12a) our [beyes](#note12b) were opened and our understandings were enlightened, so as to see and understand the things of God—

13 Even those things which were from the beginning before the world was, which were ordained of the Father, through his Only Begotten Son, who was in the bosom of the Father, even from the [abeginning](#note13a);

14 Of whom we bear record; and the record which we bear is the fulness of the gospel of Jesus Christ, who is the Son, whom we saw and with whom we [aconversed](#note14a) in the heavenly [bvision](#note14b).

15 For while we were doing the work of [atranslation](#note15a), which the Lord had appointed unto us, we came to the twenty-ninth verse of the fifth chapter of John, which was given unto us as follows—

16 Speaking of the resurrection of the dead, concerning those who shall [ahear](#note16a) the voice of the [bSon of Man](#note16b):

17 And shall come forth; [athey](#note17a) who have done [bgood](#note17b), in the [cresurrection](#note17c) of the [djust](#note17d); and they who have done evil, in the resurrection of the unjust.

18 Now this caused us to marvel, for it was given unto us of the Spirit.

19 And while we [ameditated](#note19a) upon these things, the Lord touched the eyes of our understandings and they were opened, and the [bglory](#note19b) of the Lord shone round about.

20 And we beheld the [aglory](#note20a) of the Son, on the [bright hand](#note20b) of the [cFather](#note20c), and received of his fulness;

21 And saw the holy [aangels](#note21a), and them who are [bsanctified](#note21b) before his throne, worshiping God, and the Lamb, who [cworship](#note21c) him forever and ever.

22 And now, after the many testimonies which have been given of him, this is the [atestimony](#note22a), last of all, which we give of him: That he [blives](#note22b)!

23 For we [asaw](#note23a) him, even on the [bright hand](#note23b) of [cGod](#note23c); and we heard the voice bearing record that he is the [dOnly Begotten](#note23d) of the Father—

24 That by [ahim](#note24a), and through him, and of him, the [bworlds](#note24b) are and were created, and the [cinhabitants](#note24c) thereof are begotten [dsons](#note24d) and daughters unto God.

25 And this we saw also, and bear record, that an [aangel](#note25a) of God who was in authority in the presence of God, who [brebelled](#note25b) against the Only Begotten [cSon](#note25c) whom the Father [dloved](#note25d) and who was in the bosom of the Father, was thrust down from the presence of God and the Son,

26 And was called [aPerdition](#note26a), for the heavens [bwept](#note26b) over him—he was [cLucifer](#note26c), a son of the morning.

27 And we beheld, and lo, he is [afallen](#note27a)! is fallen, even a son of the morning!

28 And while we were yet in the Spirit, the Lord commanded us that we should write the vision; for we beheld Satan, that old [aserpent](#note28a), even the [bdevil](#note28b), who rebelled against God, and sought to take the kingdom of our [cGod](#note28c) and his Christ—

29 Wherefore, he maketh [awar](#note29a) with the saints of God, and encompasseth them round about.

30 And we saw a vision of the [asufferings](#note30a) of those with whom he made war and overcame, for thus came the voice of the Lord unto us:

31 Thus saith the Lord concerning all those who know my power, and have been made partakers thereof, and [asuffered](#note31a) themselves through the power of the devil to be overcome, and to deny the truth and defy my power—

32 They are they who are the [asons](#note32a) of [bperdition](#note32b), of whom I say that it had been better for them never to have been born;

33 For they are [avessels](#note33a) of wrath, doomed to suffer the wrath of God, with the devil and his angels in eternity;

34 Concerning whom I have said there is [ano](#note34a) [bforgiveness](#note34b) in this world nor in the world to come—

35 Having [adenied](#note35a) the Holy Spirit after having received it, and having denied the Only Begotten Son of the Father, having [bcrucified](#note35b) him unto themselves and put him to an open [cshame](#note35c).

36 These are they who shall go away into the [alake](#note36a) of fire and brimstone, with the devil and his angels—

37 And the [aonly](#note37a) ones on whom the [bsecond](#note37b) [cdeath](#note37c) shall have any power;

38 Yea, verily, the only ones who shall [anot be redeemed](#note38a) in the due time of the Lord, after the sufferings of his wrath.

39 For all the rest shall be [abrought forth](#note39a) by the resurrection of the dead, through the [btriumph](#note39b) and the glory of the Lamb, who was slain, who was in the bosom of the Father [cbefore](#note39c) the worlds were made.

40 And this is the [agospel](#note40a), the glad [btidings](#note40b), which the voice out of the heavens bore record unto us—

41 That he [acame](#note41a) into the world, even Jesus, to be [bcrucified](#note41b) for the world, and to [cbear](#note41c) the sins of the [dworld](#note41d), and to [esanctify](#note41e) the world, and to [fcleanse](#note41f) it from all unrighteousness;

42 That through him all might be [asaved](#note42a) whom the Father had put into his [bpower](#note42b) and made by him;

43 Who [aglorifies](#note43a) the Father, and saves all the works of his hands, except those sons of [bperdition](#note43b) who deny the Son after the Father has revealed him.

44 Wherefore, he saves all [aexcept](#note44a) them—they shall go away into [beverlasting](#note44b) [cpunishment](#note44c), which is endless punishment, which is eternal punishment, to [dreign](#note44d) with the [edevil](#note44e) and his angels in eternity, where their [fworm](#note44f) dieth not, and the fire is not quenched, which is their torment—

45 And the [aend](#note45a) thereof, neither the place thereof, nor their torment, no man knows;

46 Neither was it revealed, neither is, neither will be revealed unto man, except to them who are made partakers thereof;

47 Nevertheless, I, the Lord, show it by [avision](#note47a) unto many, but straightway shut it up again;

48 Wherefore, the end, the width, the height, the [adepth](#note48a), and the misery thereof, they understand not, neither any man except those who are [bordained](#note48b) unto this [ccondemnation](#note48c).

49 And we heard the voice, saying: [aWrite](#note49a) the vision, for lo, this is the end of the vision of the sufferings of the ungodly.

50 And again we bear record—for we [asaw](#note50a) and heard, and this is the [btestimony](#note50b) of the [cgospel](#note50c) of Christ concerning them who shall come forth in the resurrection of the [djust](#note50d)—

51 They are they who received the [atestimony](#note51a) of Jesus, and [bbelieved](#note51b) on his name and were [cbaptized](#note51c) after the [dmanner](#note51d) of his burial, being [eburied](#note51e) in the water in his name, and this according to the commandment which he has given—

52 That by [akeeping](#note52a) the commandments they might be [bwashed](#note52b) and [ccleansed](#note52c) from all their sins, and receive the Holy Spirit by the laying on of the [dhands](#note52d) of him who is [eordained](#note52e) and sealed unto this power;

53 And who [aovercome](#note53a) by faith, and are [bsealed](#note53b) by the Holy Spirit of [cpromise](#note53c), which the Father [dsheds](#note53d) forth upon all those who are just and true.

54 They are they who are the [achurch](#note54a) of the [bFirstborn](#note54b).

55 They are they into whose hands the Father has given [aall](#note55a) things—

56 They are they who are [apriests](#note56a) and [bkings](#note56b), who have received of his fulness, and of his glory;

57 And are [apriests](#note57a) of the Most High, after the order of Melchizedek, which was after the order of [bEnoch](#note57b), which was after the [corder](#note57c) of the Only Begotten Son.

58 Wherefore, as it is written, they are [agods](#note58a), even the [bsons](#note58b) of [cGod](#note58c)—

59 Wherefore, [aall](#note59a) things are theirs, whether life or death, or things present, or things to come, all are theirs and they are Christ’s, and Christ is God’s.

60 And they shall [aovercome](#note60a) all things.

61 Wherefore, let no man [aglory](#note61a) in man, but rather let him [bglory](#note61b) in God, who shall [csubdue](#note61c) all enemies under his feet.

62 These shall [adwell](#note62a) in the [bpresence](#note62b) of God and his Christ forever and ever.

63 These are they whom he shall bring with him, when he shall [acome](#note63a) in the [bclouds](#note63b) of heaven to [creign](#note63c) on the earth over his people.

64 These are they who shall have part in the [afirst resurrection](#note64a).

65 These are they who shall come forth in the resurrection of the [ajust](#note65a).

66 These are they who are come unto [aMount](#note66a) [bZion](#note66b), and unto the city of the living God, the heavenly place, the holiest of all.

67 These are they who have come to an innumerable company of [aangels](#note67a), to the general assembly and church of [bEnoch](#note67b), and of the [cFirstborn](#note67c).

68 These are they whose names are [awritten](#note68a) in heaven, where God and Christ are the [bjudge](#note68b) of all.

69 These are they who are [ajust](#note69a) men made [bperfect](#note69b) through Jesus the mediator of the [cnew covenant](#note69c), who wrought out this perfect [datonement](#note69d) through the shedding of his own [eblood](#note69e).

70 These are they whose bodies are [acelestial](#note70a), whose [bglory](#note70b) is that of the [csun](#note70c), even the glory of God, the [dhighest](#note70d) of all, whose glory the sun of the firmament is written of as being typical.

71 And again, we saw the [aterrestrial](#note71a) world, and behold and lo, these are they who are of the terrestrial, whose glory differs from that of the church of the [bFirstborn](#note71b) who have received the fulness of the Father, even as that of the [cmoon](#note71c) differs from the sun in the firmament.

72 Behold, these are they who died [awithout](#note72a) [blaw](#note72b);

73 And also they who are the [aspirits](#note73a) of men kept in [bprison](#note73b), whom the Son visited, and [cpreached](#note73c) the [dgospel](#note73d) unto them, that they might be judged according to men in the flesh;

74 Who [areceived not](#note74a) the [btestimony](#note74b) of Jesus in the flesh, but afterwards received it.

75 These are they who are [ahonorable](#note75a) men of the earth, who were [bblinded](#note75b) by the craftiness of men.

76 These are they who receive of his glory, but not of his fulness.

77 These are they who receive of the [apresence](#note77a) of the Son, but not of the fulness of the Father.

78 Wherefore, they are [abodies terrestrial](#note78a), and not bodies celestial, and differ in glory as the moon differs from the sun.

79 These are they who are not [avaliant](#note79a) in the [btestimony](#note79b) of Jesus; wherefore, they obtain not the crown over the kingdom of our God.

80 And now this is the end of the [avision](#note80a) which we saw of the terrestrial, that the Lord commanded us to [bwrite](#note80b) while we were yet in the Spirit.

81 And again, we [asaw](#note81a) the glory of the [btelestial](#note81b), which glory is that of the lesser, even as the [cglory](#note81c) of the stars differs from that of the glory of the moon in the firmament.

82 These are they who received not the gospel of Christ, neither the [atestimony](#note82a) of Jesus.

83 These are they who [adeny](#note83a) not the Holy Spirit.

84 These are they who are thrust down to [ahell](#note84a).

85 These are they who shall not be redeemed from the [adevil](#note85a) until the [blast resurrection](#note85b), until the Lord, even Christ the [cLamb](#note85c), shall have finished his work.

86 These are they who receive not of his fulness in the eternal world, but of the Holy Spirit through the ministration of the terrestrial;

87 And the terrestrial through the [aministration](#note87a) of the celestial.

88 And also the telestial receive it of the administering of angels who are appointed to minister for them, or who are appointed to be [aministering spirits](#note88a) for them; for they shall be [bheirs](#note88b) of salvation.

89 And thus we saw, in the heavenly vision, the glory of the [atelestial](#note89a), which surpasses all understanding;

90 And no man knows it except him to whom God has revealed it.

91 And thus we saw the glory of the [aterrestrial](#note91a) which excels in all things the glory of the telestial, even in glory, and in power, and in might, and in dominion.

92 And thus we saw the [aglory](#note92a) of the celestial, which [bexcels](#note92b) in all things—where God, even the Father, reigns upon his [cthrone](#note92c) forever and ever;

93 Before whose throne all things bow in humble [areverence](#note93a), and give him glory forever and ever.

94 They who dwell in his [apresence](#note94a) are the church of the [bFirstborn](#note94b); and they see as they are seen, and [cknow](#note94c) as they are known, having received of his fulness and of his [dgrace](#note94d);

95 And he makes them [aequal](#note95a) in power, and in might, and in dominion.

96 And the glory of the celestial is one, even as the glory of the [asun](#note96a) is one.

97 And the glory of the terrestrial is one, even as the glory of the moon is one.

98 And the glory of the telestial is one, even as the glory of the stars is one; for as one star differs from another star in glory, even so differs one from another in glory in the telestial world;

99 For these are they who are of [aPaul](#note99a), and of Apollos, and of Cephas.

100 These are they who say they are some of one and some of another—some of Christ and some of John, and some of Moses, and some of Elias, and some of [aEsaias](#note100a), and some of Isaiah, and some of Enoch;

101 But [areceived not](#note101a) the gospel, neither the testimony of Jesus, neither the prophets, neither the [beverlasting covenant](#note101b).

102 Last of all, these all are they who will not be [agathered](#note102a) with the saints, to be [bcaught up](#note102b) unto the [cchurch](#note102c) of the Firstborn, and received into the cloud.

103 These are [athey](#note103a) who are [bliars](#note103b), and [csorcerers](#note103c), and [dadulterers](#note103d), and [ewhoremongers](#note103e), and whosoever loves and makes a lie.

104 These are they who suffer the [awrath](#note104a) of God on earth.

105 These are they who suffer the [avengeance](#note105a) of eternal fire.

106 These are they who are cast down to [ahell](#note106a) and [bsuffer](#note106b) the wrath of [cAlmighty God](#note106c), until the [dfulness](#note106d) of times, when Christ shall have [esubdued](#note106e) all enemies under his [ffeet](#note106f), and shall have [gperfected](#note106g) his work;

107 When he shall [adeliver](#note107a) up the [bkingdom](#note107b), and present it unto the Father, spotless, saying: I have [covercome](#note107c) and have [dtrodden](#note107d) the [ewine-press](#note107e) [falone](#note107f), even the wine-press of the fierceness of the wrath of Almighty God.

108 Then shall he be [acrowned](#note108a) with the crown of his glory, to sit on the [bthrone](#note108b) of his power to reign forever and ever.

109 But behold, and lo, we saw the glory and the inhabitants of the telestial world, that they were as [ainnumerable](#note109a) as the stars in the firmament of heaven, or as the sand upon the seashore;

110 And heard the voice of the Lord saying: These all shall bow the knee, and every tongue shall [aconfess](#note110a) to him who sits upon the throne forever and ever;

111 For they shall be judged according to their [aworks](#note111a), and every man shall receive according to his own [bworks](#note111b), his own [cdominion](#note111c), in the [dmansions](#note111d) which are prepared;

112 And they shall be [aservants](#note112a) of the Most High; but [bwhere](#note112b) God and Christ [cdwell](#note112c) they [dcannot](#note112d) come, [eworlds](#note112e) without end.

113 This is the end of the vision which we saw, which we were commanded to write while we were yet in the Spirit.

114 But [agreat](#note114a) and marvelous are the works of the Lord, and the [bmysteries](#note114b) of his kingdom which he showed unto us, which surpass all understanding in glory, and in might, and in dominion;

115 Which he commanded us we should not write while we were yet in the Spirit, and are not [alawful](#note115a) for man to utter;

116 Neither is man [acapable](#note116a) to make them known, for they are only to be [bseen](#note116b) and [cunderstood](#note116c) by the power of the Holy Spirit, which God bestows on those who [dlove](#note116d) him, and purify themselves before him;

117 To whom he grants this privilege of [aseeing](#note117a) and knowing for themselves;

118 That through the power and manifestation of the Spirit, while in the flesh, they may be able to [abear](#note118a) his [bpresence](#note118b) in the world of glory.

119 And to God and the Lamb be [aglory](#note119a), and honor, and dominion forever and ever. Amen.

Section 77

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, about March 1832. Joseph Smith’s history states, “In connection with the translation of the Scriptures, I received the following explanation of the Revelation of St. John.”

[1–4](77.xhtml#p1), Beasts have spirits and will dwell in eternal felicity; [5–7](77.xhtml#p5), This earth has a temporal existence of 7,000 years; [8–10](77.xhtml#p8), Various angels restore the gospel and minister on earth; [11](77.xhtml#p11), The sealing of the 144,000; [12–14](77.xhtml#p12), Christ will come in the beginning of the seventh thousand years; [15](77.xhtml#p15), Two prophets will be raised up to the Jewish nation.

1 Q. What is the [asea](#note1a) of glass spoken of by John, 4th chapter, and 6th verse of the Revelation?A. It is the [bearth](#note1b), in its [csanctified](#note1c), [dimmortal](#note1d), and [eeternal](#note1e) state.

2 Q. What are we to understand by the four beasts, spoken of in the same verse?A. They are [afigurative](#note2a) expressions, used by the Revelator, John, in describing [bheaven](#note2b), the [cparadise](#note2c) of God, the [dhappiness](#note2d) of man, and of beasts, and of creeping things, and of the fowls of the air; that which is spiritual being in the likeness of that which is temporal; and that which is temporal in the likeness of that which is spiritual; the [espirit](#note2e) of man in the likeness of his person, as also the spirit of the [fbeast](#note2f), and every other creature which God has created.

3 Q. Are the four beasts limited to individual beasts, or do they represent classes or [aorders](#note3a)?A. They are limited to four individual beasts, which were shown to John, to represent the glory of the classes of beings in their destined [border](#note3b) or [csphere](#note3c) of creation, in the enjoyment of their [deternal](#note3d) [efelicity](#note3e).

4 Q. What are we to understand by the [aeyes](#note4a) and [bwings](#note4b), which the beasts had?A. Their eyes are a representation of light and knowledge, that is, they are full of [cknowledge](#note4c); and their wings are a [drepresentation](#note4d) of [epower](#note4e), to move, to act, etc.

5 Q. What are we to understand by the four and twenty [aelders](#note5a), spoken of by John?A. We are to understand that these elders whom John saw, were elders who had been [bfaithful](#note5b) in the work of the ministry and were dead; who belonged to the [cseven](#note5c) churches, and were then in the paradise of God.

6 Q. What are we to understand by the book which John saw, which was [asealed](#note6a) on the back with seven seals?A. We are to understand that it contains the revealed will, [bmysteries](#note6b), and the works of God; the hidden things of his economy concerning this [cearth](#note6c) during the seven thousand years of its continuance, or its temporal existence.

7 Q. What are we to understand by the seven [aseals](#note7a) with which it was sealed?A. We are to understand that the first seal contains the things of the [bfirst thousand](#note7b) years, and the [csecond](#note7c) also of the second thousand years, and so on until the seventh.

8 Q. What are we to understand by the four [aangels](#note8a), spoken of in the 7th chapter and 1st verse of Revelation?A. We are to understand that they are four angels sent forth from God, to whom is given power over the four parts of the earth, to save life and to destroy; these are they who have the [beverlasting gospel](#note8b) to commit to every nation, kindred, tongue, and people; having power to [cshut up](#note8c) the heavens, to seal up unto life, or to cast down to the [dregions](#note8d) of darkness.

9 Q. What are we to understand by the angel [aascending](#note9a) from the east, Revelation 7th chapter and 2nd verse?A. We are to understand that the angel ascending from the east is he to whom is given the seal of the living God over the twelve tribes of [bIsrael](#note9b); wherefore, he crieth unto the four angels having the everlasting gospel, saying: Hurt not the earth, neither the sea, nor the trees, till we have sealed the servants of our God in their [cforeheads](#note9c). And, if you will receive it, this is [dElias](#note9d) which was to come to gather together the tribes of Israel and [erestore](#note9e) all things.

10 Q. What time are the things spoken of in this chapter to be accomplished?A. They are to be accomplished in the [asixth thousand](#note10a) years, or the opening of the sixth seal.

11 Q. What are we to understand by sealing the [aone](#note11a) hundred and forty-four thousand, out of all the tribes of Israel—twelve thousand out of every tribe?A. We are to understand that those who are sealed are [bhigh priests](#note11b), ordained unto the holy order of God, to administer the everlasting gospel; for they are they who are ordained out of every nation, kindred, tongue, and people, by the angels to whom is given power over the nations of the earth, to bring as many as will come to the church of the [cFirstborn](#note11c).

12 Q. What are we to understand by the sounding of the [atrumpets](#note12a), mentioned in the 8th chapter of Revelation?A. We are to understand that as God [bmade](#note12b) the world in six days, and on the seventh day he finished his work, and [csanctified](#note12c) it, and also formed man out of the [ddust](#note12d) of the earth, even so, in the beginning of the seventh thousand years will the Lord God [esanctify](#note12e) the earth, and complete the salvation of man, and [fjudge](#note12f) all things, and shall [gredeem](#note12g) all things, except that which he hath not put into his power, when he shall have sealed all things, unto the end of all things; and the sounding of the trumpets of the seven angels are the preparing and finishing of his work, in the beginning of the seventh thousand years—the [hpreparing](#note12h) of the way before the time of his coming.

13 Q. When are the things to be accomplished, which are written in the 9th chapter of Revelation?A. They are to be accomplished after the [aopening](#note13a) of the seventh seal, [bbefore](#note13b) the coming of Christ.

14 Q. What are we to understand by the little book which was [aeaten](#note14a) by John, as mentioned in the 10th chapter of Revelation?A. We are to understand that it was a mission, and an ordinance, for him to [bgather](#note14b) the tribes of Israel; behold, this is Elias, who, as it is written, must come and [crestore](#note14c) all things.

15 Q. What is to be understood by the two [awitnesses](#note15a), in the eleventh chapter of Revelation?A. They are two prophets that are to be raised up to the [bJewish nation](#note15b) in the last days, at the time of the [crestoration](#note15c), and to prophesy to the Jews after they are gathered and have built the city of Jerusalem in the [dland](#note15d) of their fathers.

Section 78

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, March 1, 1832. On that day, the Prophet and other leaders had assembled to discuss Church business. This revelation originally instructed the Prophet, Sidney Rigdon, and Newel K. Whitney to travel to Missouri and organize the Church’s mercantile and publishing endeavors by creating a “firm” that would oversee these efforts, generating funds for the establishment of Zion and for the benefit of the poor. This firm, known as the United Firm, was organized in April 1832 and disbanded in 1834 (see [section 82](82.xhtml)). Sometime after its dissolution, under the direction of Joseph Smith, the phrase “the affairs of the storehouse for the poor” replaced “mercantile and publishing establishments” in the revelation, and the word “order” replaced the word “firm.”

[1–4](78.xhtml#p1), The Saints should organize and establish a storehouse; [5–12](78.xhtml#p5), Wise use of their properties will lead to salvation; [13–14](78.xhtml#p13), The Church should be independent of earthly powers; [15–16](78.xhtml#p15), Michael (Adam) serves under the direction of the Holy One (Christ); [17–22](78.xhtml#p17), Blessed are the faithful, for they will inherit all things.

1 The Lord spake unto Joseph Smith, Jun., saying: Hearken unto me, saith the Lord your God, who are ordained unto the [ahigh priesthood](#note1a) of my church, who have assembled yourselves together;

2 And listen to the [acounsel](#note2a) of him who has [bordained](#note2b) you from on high, who shall speak in your ears the words of [cwisdom](#note2c), that salvation may be unto you in that thing which you have presented before me, saith the Lord God.

3 For verily I say unto you, the time has come, and is now at hand; and behold, and lo, it must needs be that there be an [aorganization](#note3a) of my people, in regulating and establishing the affairs of the [bstorehouse](#note3b) for the [cpoor](#note3c) of my people, both in this place and in the land of [dZion](#note3d)—

4 For a permanent and everlasting establishment and order unto my church, to advance the cause, which ye have espoused, to the salvation of man, and to the glory of your Father who is in heaven;

5 That you may be [aequal](#note5a) in the bonds of heavenly things, yea, and earthly things also, for the obtaining of heavenly things.

6 For if ye are not equal in earthly things ye cannot be [aequal](#note6a) in obtaining heavenly things;

7 For if you will that I give unto you a place in the [acelestial](#note7a) world, you must [bprepare](#note7b) yourselves by [cdoing](#note7c) the things which I have commanded you and required of you.

8 And now, verily thus saith the Lord, it is expedient that all things be done unto my [aglory](#note8a), by you who are joined together in this [border](#note8b);

9 Or, in other words, let my servant Newel K. Whitney and my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., and my servant Sidney Rigdon sit in council with the saints which are in [aZion](#note9a);

10 Otherwise [aSatan](#note10a) seeketh to turn their [bhearts](#note10b) away from the truth, that they become [cblinded](#note10c) and understand not the things which are prepared for them.

11 Wherefore, a commandment I give unto you, to prepare and organize yourselves by a [abond](#note11a) or everlasting [bcovenant](#note11b) that cannot be broken.

12 And he who breaketh it shall lose his office and standing in the church, and shall be [adelivered](#note12a) over to the [bbuffetings](#note12b) of Satan until the day of redemption.

13 Behold, this is the preparation wherewith I prepare you, and the foundation, and the [aensample](#note13a) which I give unto you, whereby you may accomplish the commandments which are given you;

14 That through my providence, notwithstanding the [atribulation](#note14a) which shall descend upon you, that the church may stand independent above all other creatures beneath the celestial world;

15 That you may come up unto the [acrown](#note15a) prepared for you, and be made [brulers](#note15b) over many kingdoms, saith the Lord God, the Holy One of Zion, who hath established the foundations of [cAdam-ondi-Ahman](#note15c);

16 Who hath appointed [aMichael](#note16a) your prince, and established his feet, and set him upon high, and given unto him the keys of salvation under the counsel and direction of the [bHoly One](#note16b), who is without beginning of days or end of life.

17 Verily, verily, I say unto you, ye are [alittle children](#note17a), and ye have not as yet understood how great blessings the Father hath in his own hands and prepared for you;

18 And ye cannot [abear](#note18a) all things now; nevertheless, be of good [bcheer](#note18b), for I will [clead](#note18c) you along. The kingdom is yours and the blessings thereof are yours, and the [driches](#note18d) of [eeternity](#note18e) are yours.

19 And he who receiveth all things with [athankfulness](#note19a) shall be made glorious; and the things of this earth shall be added unto him, even an [bhundred fold](#note19b), yea, more.

20 Wherefore, do the things which I have commanded you, saith your Redeemer, even the Son [aAhman](#note20a), who prepareth all things before he [btaketh](#note20b) you;

21 For ye are the [achurch](#note21a) of the [bFirstborn](#note21b), and he will take you up in a [ccloud](#note21c), and appoint every man his portion.

22 And he that is a faithful and [awise](#note22a) [bsteward](#note22b) shall inherit [call](#note22c) things. Amen.

Section 79

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, March 12, 1832.

[1–4](79.xhtml#p1), Jared Carter is called to preach the gospel by the Comforter.

1 Verily I say unto you, that it is my will that my servant Jared Carter should go again into the eastern countries, from place to place, and from city to city, in the power of the [aordination](#note1a) wherewith he has been ordained, proclaiming glad tidings of great joy, even the [beverlasting gospel](#note1b).

2 And I will send upon him the [aComforter](#note2a), which shall teach him the truth and the [bway](#note2b) whither he shall go;

3 And inasmuch as he is faithful, I will crown him again with [asheaves](#note3a).

4 Wherefore, let your heart be glad, my servant Jared Carter, and [afear not](#note4a), saith your Lord, even Jesus Christ. Amen.

Section 80

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Stephen Burnett, at Hiram, Ohio, March 7, 1832.

[1–5](80.xhtml#p1), Stephen Burnett and Eden Smith are called to preach in whatever place they choose.

1 Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you my servant [aStephen Burnett](#note1a): Go ye, go ye into the world and preach the gospel to every [bcreature](#note1b) that cometh under the sound of your voice.

2 And inasmuch as you desire a companion, I will give unto you my servant [aEden Smith](#note2a).

3 Wherefore, go ye and preach my gospel, whether to the north or to the south, to the east or to the west, it mattereth not, for ye cannot go amiss.

4 Therefore, declare the things which ye have heard, and verily believe, and [aknow](#note4a) to be true.

5 Behold, this is the will of him who hath [acalled](#note5a) you, your Redeemer, even Jesus Christ. Amen.

Section 81

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, March 15, 1832. Frederick G. Williams is called to be a high priest and a counselor in the Presidency of the High Priesthood. The historical records show that when this revelation was received in March 1832, it called Jesse Gause to the office of counselor to Joseph Smith in the Presidency. However, when he failed to continue in a manner consistent with this appointment, the call was subsequently transferred to Frederick G. Williams. The revelation (dated March 1832) should be regarded as a step toward the formal organization of the First Presidency, specifically calling for the office of counselor in that body and explaining the dignity of the appointment. Brother Gause served for a time but was excommunicated from the Church in December 1832. Brother Williams was ordained to the specified office on March 18, 1833.

[1–2](81.xhtml#p1), The keys of the kingdom are always held by the First Presidency; [3–7](81.xhtml#p3), If Frederick G. Williams is faithful in his ministry, he will have eternal life.

1 Verily, verily, I say unto you my servant Frederick G. Williams: Listen to the voice of him who speaketh, to the word of the Lord your God, and hearken to the calling wherewith you are called, even to be a [ahigh priest](#note1a) in my church, and a [bcounselor](#note1b) unto my servant Joseph Smith, Jun.;

2 Unto whom I have given the [akeys](#note2a) of the kingdom, which belong always unto the [bPresidency](#note2b) of the High Priesthood:

3 Therefore, verily I acknowledge him and will bless him, and also thee, inasmuch as thou art faithful in counsel, in the office which I have appointed unto you, in prayer always, vocally and in thy heart, in public and in private, also in thy [aministry](#note3a) in proclaiming the gospel in the [bland](#note3b) of the living, and among thy brethren.

4 And in doing these things thou wilt do the greatest [agood](#note4a) unto thy fellow beings, and wilt promote the [bglory](#note4b) of him who is your Lord.

5 Wherefore, be faithful; stand in the office which I have appointed unto you; [asuccor](#note5a) the [bweak](#note5b), lift up the hands which hang down, and [cstrengthen](#note5c) the [dfeeble](#note5d) knees.

6 And if thou art [afaithful](#note6a) unto the end thou shalt have a [bcrown](#note6b) of [cimmortality](#note6c), and eternal life in the [dmansions](#note6d) which I have prepared in the house of my Father.

7 Behold, and lo, these are the words of Alpha and Omega, even Jesus Christ. Amen.

Section 82

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, in Independence, Jackson County, Missouri, April 26, 1832. The occasion was a council of high priests and elders of the Church. At the council, Joseph Smith was sustained as the President of the High Priesthood, to which office he had previously been ordained at a conference of high priests, elders, and members, at Amherst, Ohio, January 25, 1832 (see the [heading to section 75](75.xhtml)). This revelation reiterates instructions given in an earlier revelation ([section 78](78.xhtml)) to establish a firm—known as the United Firm (under Joseph Smith’s direction, the term “order” later replaced “firm”)—to govern the Church’s mercantile and publishing endeavors.

[1–4](82.xhtml#p1), Where much is given, much is required; [5–7](82.xhtml#p5), Darkness reigns in the world; [8–13](82.xhtml#p8), The Lord is bound when we do what He says; [14–18](82.xhtml#p14), Zion must increase in beauty and holiness; [19–24](82.xhtml#p19), Every man should seek the interest of his neighbor.

1 Verily, verily, I say unto you, my servants, that inasmuch as you have [aforgiven](#note1a) one another your trespasses, even so I, the Lord, forgive you.

2 Nevertheless, there are those among you who have sinned exceedingly; yea, even [aall](#note2a) of you have sinned; but verily I say unto you, beware from henceforth, and [brefrain](#note2b) from sin, lest sore judgments fall upon your heads.

3 For of him unto whom [amuch](#note3a) is [bgiven](#note3b) much is [crequired](#note3c); and he who [dsins](#note3d) against the greater [elight](#note3e) shall [freceive](#note3f) the greater [gcondemnation](#note3g).

4 Ye call upon my name for [arevelations](#note4a), and I give them unto you; and inasmuch as ye keep not my sayings, which I give unto you, ye become transgressors; and [bjustice](#note4b) and judgment are the penalty which is affixed unto my law.

5 Therefore, what I say unto one I say unto all: [aWatch](#note5a), for the [badversary](#note5b) [cspreadeth](#note5c) his dominions, and [ddarkness](#note5d) reigneth;

6 And the anger of God kindleth against the inhabitants of the earth; and [anone](#note6a) doeth good, for all have gone out of the [bway](#note6b).

7 And now, verily I say unto you, I, the Lord, will not lay any [asin](#note7a) to your charge; go your ways and sin no more; but unto that soul who sinneth shall the [bformer](#note7b) sins return, saith the Lord your God.

8 And again, I say unto you, I give unto you a [anew](#note8a) commandment, that you may understand my will concerning you;

9 Or, in other words, I give unto you directions how you may [aact](#note9a) before me, that it may [bturn](#note9b) to you for your salvation.

10 I, the Lord, am [abound](#note10a) when ye do what I say; but when ye do not what I say, ye have no [bpromise](#note10b).

11 Therefore, verily I say unto you, that it is expedient for my servants Edward Partridge and Newel K. Whitney, A. Sidney Gilbert and Sidney Rigdon, and my servant Joseph Smith, and John Whitmer and Oliver Cowdery, and W. W. Phelps and Martin Harris to be bound [atogether](#note11a) by a bond and covenant that cannot be [bbroken](#note11b) by transgression, except judgment shall immediately follow, in your several [cstewardships](#note11c)—

12 To manage the affairs of the poor, and all things pertaining to the bishopric [aboth](#note12a) in the land of Zion and in the land of Kirtland;

13 For I have consecrated the land of Kirtland in mine own due time for the benefit of the saints of the Most High, and for a [astake](#note13a) to Zion.

14 For [aZion](#note14a) must increase in [bbeauty](#note14b), and in [choliness](#note14c); her borders must be enlarged; her [dstakes](#note14d) must be strengthened; yea, verily I say unto you, Zion must [earise](#note14e) and put on her [fbeautiful garments](#note14f).

15 Therefore, I give unto you this commandment, that ye bind yourselves by this covenant, and it shall be done according to the laws of the Lord.

16 Behold, here is [awisdom](#note16a) also in me for your good.

17 And you are to be [aequal](#note17a), or in other words, you are to have equal [bclaims](#note17b) on the [cproperties](#note17c), for the benefit of [dmanaging](#note17d) the concerns of your stewardships, every man according to his wants and his needs, inasmuch as his wants are just—

18 And all this for the benefit of the church of the living God, that every man may [aimprove](#note18a) upon his [btalent](#note18b), that every man may [cgain](#note18c) other [dtalents](#note18d), yea, even an hundred fold, to be cast into the Lord’s [estorehouse](#note18e), to become the common [fproperty](#note18f) of the whole church—

19 [aEvery](#note19a) man seeking the interest of his [bneighbor](#note19b), and doing all things with an [ceye](#note19c) single to the glory of God.

20 This order I have appointed to be an [aeverlasting](#note20a) [border](#note20b) unto you, and unto your successors, inasmuch as you sin not.

21 And the soul that sins against this covenant, and [ahardeneth](#note21a) his heart against it, shall be dealt with according to the laws of my church, and shall be delivered over to the [bbuffetings](#note21b) of Satan until the day of redemption.

22 And now, verily I say unto you, and this is wisdom, make unto yourselves friends with the [amammon](#note22a) of unrighteousness, and they will not destroy you.

23 Leave judgment alone with me, for it is mine and I will [arepay](#note23a). Peace be with you; my blessings continue with you.

24 For even yet the [akingdom](#note24a) is yours, and shall be forever, if you fall not from your [bsteadfastness](#note24b). Even so. Amen.

Section 83

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Independence, Missouri, April 30, 1832. This revelation was received as the Prophet sat in council with his brethren.

[1–4](83.xhtml#p1), Women and children have claim upon their husbands and fathers for their support; [5–6](83.xhtml#p5), Widows and orphans have claim upon the Church for their support.

1 Verily, thus saith the Lord, in addition to the [alaws](#note1a) of the church concerning women and children, those who belong to the church, who have [blost](#note1b) their husbands or fathers:

2 [aWomen](#note2a) have [bclaim](#note2b) on their husbands for their maintenance, until their [chusbands](#note2c) are taken; and if they are not found transgressors they shall have fellowship in the church.

3 And if they are not faithful they shall not have fellowship in the church; yet they may remain upon their inheritances according to the laws of the land.

4 All [achildren](#note4a) have claim upon their [bparents](#note4b) for their [cmaintenance](#note4c) until they are of age.

5 And after that, they have [aclaim](#note5a) upon the church, or in other words upon the Lord’s [bstorehouse](#note5b), if their parents have not wherewith to give them inheritances.

6 And the storehouse shall be kept by the consecrations of the church; and [awidows](#note6a) and orphans shall be provided for, as also the [bpoor](#note6b). Amen.

Section 84

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, September 22 and 23, 1832. During the month of September, elders had begun to return from their missions in the eastern states and to make reports of their labors. It was while they were together in this season of joy that the following communication was received. The Prophet designated it a revelation on priesthood.

[1–5](84.xhtml#p1), The New Jerusalem and the temple will be built in Missouri; [6–17](84.xhtml#p6), The line of priesthood from Moses to Adam is given; [18–25](84.xhtml#p18), The greater priesthood holds the key of the knowledge of God; [26–32](84.xhtml#p26), The lesser priesthood holds the key of the ministering of angels and of the preparatory gospel; [33–44](84.xhtml#p33), Men gain eternal life through the oath and covenant of the priesthood; [45–53](84.xhtml#p45), The Spirit of Christ enlightens men, and the world lies in sin; [54–61](84.xhtml#p54), The Saints must testify of those things they have received; [62–76](84.xhtml#p62), They are to preach the gospel, and signs will follow; [77–91](84.xhtml#p77), Elders are to go forth without purse or scrip, and the Lord will care for their needs; [92–97](84.xhtml#p92), Plagues and cursings await those who reject the gospel; [98–102](84.xhtml#p98), The new song of the redemption of Zion is given; [103–10](84.xhtml#p103), Let every man stand in his own office and labor in his own calling; [111–20](84.xhtml#p111), The Lord’s servants are to proclaim the abomination of desolation of the last days.

1 A [arevelation](#note1a) of Jesus Christ unto his servant Joseph Smith, Jun., and six elders, as they [bunited](#note1b) their hearts and [clifted](#note1c) their voices on high.

2 Yea, the word of the Lord concerning his church, established in the last days for the [arestoration](#note2a) of his people, as he has spoken by the mouth of his [bprophets](#note2b), and for the [cgathering](#note2c) of his [dsaints](#note2d) to stand upon [eMount Zion](#note2e), which shall be the city of [fNew Jerusalem](#note2f).

3 Which city shall be [abuilt](#note3a), beginning at the [btemple](#note3b) lot, which is appointed by the finger of the Lord, in the western boundaries of the State of Missouri, and [cdedicated](#note3c) by the hand of Joseph Smith, Jun., and others with whom the Lord was well pleased.

4 Verily this is the word of the Lord, that the city [aNew Jerusalem](#note4a) shall be built by the gathering of the saints, beginning at this place, even the place of the temple, which [btemple](#note4b) shall be [creared](#note4c) in this [dgeneration](#note4d).

5 For verily this generation shall not all [apass away](#note5a) until an [bhouse](#note5b) shall be built unto the Lord, and a [ccloud](#note5c) shall rest upon it, which cloud shall be even the [dglory](#note5d) of the Lord, which shall fill the house.

6 [aAnd](#note6a) the [bsons of Moses](#note6b), according to the Holy Priesthood which he received under the [chand](#note6c) of his father-in-law, [dJethro](#note6d);

7 And Jethro received it under the hand of Caleb;

8 And Caleb received it under the hand of Elihu;

9 And Elihu under the hand of Jeremy;

10 And Jeremy under the hand of Gad;

11 And Gad under the hand of Esaias;

12 And Esaias received it under the hand of God.

13 [aEsaias](#note13a) also lived in the days of Abraham, and was blessed of him—

14 Which [aAbraham](#note14a) received the priesthood from [bMelchizedek](#note14b), who received it through the lineage of his fathers, even till [cNoah](#note14c);

15 And from Noah till [aEnoch](#note15a), through the lineage of their fathers;

16 And from Enoch to [aAbel](#note16a), who was slain by the [bconspiracy](#note16b) of his brother, who [creceived](#note16c) the priesthood by the commandments of God, by the hand of his father [dAdam](#note16d), who was the first man—

17 Which [apriesthood](#note17a) [bcontinueth](#note17b) in the church of God in all generations, and is without [cbeginning](#note17c) of days or end of years.

18 And the Lord confirmed a [apriesthood](#note18a) also upon [bAaron](#note18b) and his [cseed](#note18c), throughout all their generations, which priesthood also continueth and [dabideth](#note18d) forever with the priesthood which is after the holiest order of God.

19 And this greater [apriesthood](#note19a) administereth the gospel and holdeth the [bkey](#note19b) of the [cmysteries](#note19c) of the kingdom, even the key of the [dknowledge](#note19d) of God.

20 Therefore, in the [aordinances](#note20a) thereof, the power of [bgodliness](#note20b) is manifest.

21 And without the ordinances thereof, and the [aauthority](#note21a) of the priesthood, the power of godliness is [bnot](#note21b) manifest unto men in the flesh;

22 For without this no [aman](#note22a) can see the face of God, even the Father, and live.

23 Now this [aMoses](#note23a) plainly taught to the children of Israel in the wilderness, and sought diligently to [bsanctify](#note23b) his people that they might [cbehold](#note23c) the face of God;

24 But they [ahardened](#note24a) their hearts and could not endure his [bpresence](#note24b); therefore, the Lord in his [cwrath](#note24c), for his [danger](#note24d) was kindled against them, swore that they should not [eenter](#note24e) into his rest while in the wilderness, which rest is the fulness of his glory.

25 Therefore, he took [aMoses](#note25a) out of their midst, and the Holy [bPriesthood](#note25b) also;

26 And the lesser [apriesthood](#note26a) continued, which priesthood holdeth the [bkey](#note26b) of the [cministering](#note26c) of angels and the [dpreparatory gospel](#note26d);

27 Which [agospel](#note27a) is the gospel of [brepentance](#note27b) and of [cbaptism](#note27c), and the [dremission](#note27d) of sins, and the [elaw](#note27e) of [fcarnal](#note27f) commandments, which the Lord in his wrath caused to continue with the house of Aaron among the children of Israel until [gJohn](#note27g), whom God raised up, being [hfilled](#note27h) with the Holy Ghost from his mother’s womb.

28 For he was baptized while he was yet in his childhood, and was [aordained](#note28a) by the angel of God at the time he was [beight](#note28b) days old unto this power, to overthrow the kingdom of the Jews, and to [cmake](#note28c) straight the way of the Lord before the face of his people, to prepare them for the [dcoming](#note28d) of the Lord, in whose hand is given [eall power](#note28e).

29 And again, the [aoffices](#note29a) of elder and bishop are necessary [bappendages](#note29b) belonging unto the high priesthood.

30 And again, the offices of [ateacher](#note30a) and deacon are necessary appendages belonging to the lesser priesthood, which priesthood was confirmed upon [bAaron](#note30b) and his sons.

31 Therefore, as I said [aconcerning](#note31a) the sons of Moses—for the sons of Moses and also the sons of Aaron shall offer an acceptable [boffering](#note31b) and sacrifice in the house of the Lord, which house shall be built unto the Lord in this generation, upon the consecrated [cspot](#note31c) as I have appointed—

32 And the sons of Moses and of Aaron shall be filled with the [aglory](#note32a) of the Lord, upon [bMount Zion](#note32b) in the Lord’s house, whose sons are ye; and also many whom I have called and sent forth to build up my [cchurch](#note32c).

33 For whoso is [afaithful](#note33a) unto the obtaining these two [bpriesthoods](#note33b) of which I have spoken, and the [cmagnifying](#note33c) their calling, are [dsanctified](#note33d) by the Spirit unto the [erenewing](#note33e) of their bodies.

34 They become the [asons of Moses](#note34a) and of Aaron and the [bseed](#note34b) of [cAbraham](#note34c), and the church and kingdom, and the [delect](#note34d) of God.

35 And also all they who receive this priesthood [areceive](#note35a) me, saith the Lord;

36 For he that receiveth my servants [areceiveth](#note36a) me;

37 And he that [areceiveth](#note37a) me receiveth my Father;

38 And he that receiveth my Father receiveth my Father’s [akingdom](#note38a); therefore [ball](#note38b) that my Father hath shall be given unto him.

39 And this is according to the [aoath and covenant](#note39a) which belongeth to the priesthood.

40 Therefore, all those who receive the [apriesthood](#note40a), receive this [boath and covenant](#note40b) of my Father, which he cannot break, neither can it be moved.

41 But whoso breaketh this [acovenant](#note41a) after he hath received it, and altogether turneth therefrom, shall [bnot](#note41b) have forgiveness of sins in this world nor in the world to come.

42 And wo unto all those who come not unto this priesthood which ye have received, which I now confirm upon you who are present this day, by mine own voice out of the heavens; and even I have given the heavenly hosts and mine angels [acharge](#note42a) concerning you.

43 And I now give unto you a commandment to beware concerning yourselves, to give [adiligent](#note43a) [bheed](#note43b) to the words of eternal life.

44 For you shall [alive](#note44a) by every word that proceedeth forth from the mouth of God.

45 For the [aword](#note45a) of the Lord is truth, and whatsoever is truth is [blight](#note45b), and whatsoever is light is [cSpirit](#note45c), even the Spirit of Jesus Christ.

46 And the [aSpirit](#note46a) giveth [blight](#note46b) to [cevery man](#note46c) that cometh into the world; and the Spirit enlighteneth every man through the world, that hearkeneth to the voice of the Spirit.

47 And every one that hearkeneth to the voice of the Spirit [acometh](#note47a) unto God, even the Father.

48 And the Father [ateacheth](#note48a) him of the covenant which he has [brenewed](#note48b) and confirmed upon you, which is confirmed upon you for your sakes, and not for your sakes only, but for the sake of the [cwhole world](#note48c).

49 And the whole [aworld](#note49a) lieth in sin, and groaneth under [bdarkness](#note49b) and under the [cbondage](#note49c) of sin.

50 And by this you may know they are under the [abondage](#note50a) of sin, because they come not unto me.

51 For whoso cometh not unto me is under the [abondage](#note51a) of sin.

52 And whoso receiveth not my voice is not acquainted with [amy voice](#note52a), and is not of me.

53 And by this you may know the righteous from the wicked, and that the whole [aworld](#note53a) [bgroaneth](#note53b) under sin and darkness even now.

54 And your [aminds](#note54a) in times past have been [bdarkened](#note54b) because of [cunbelief](#note54c), and because you have treated [dlightly](#note54d) the things you have received—

55 Which [avanity](#note55a) and unbelief have brought the whole church under condemnation.

56 And this condemnation resteth upon the children of [aZion](#note56a), even all.

57 And they shall remain under this condemnation until they repent and remember the new [acovenant](#note57a), even the [bBook of Mormon](#note57b) and the [cformer](#note57c) commandments which I have given them, not only to say, but to [ddo](#note57d) according to that which I have written—

58 That they may bring forth [afruit](#note58a) meet for their Father’s kingdom; otherwise there remaineth a [bscourge](#note58b) and judgment to be poured out upon the children of Zion.

59 For shall the children of the kingdom [apollute](#note59a) my holy land? Verily, I say unto you, Nay.

60 Verily, verily, I say unto you who now hear my [awords](#note60a), which are my voice, blessed are ye inasmuch as you receive these things;

61 For I will [aforgive](#note61a) you of your sins with this commandment—that you remain [bsteadfast](#note61b) in your minds in [csolemnity](#note61c) and the spirit of prayer, in bearing [dtestimony](#note61d) to all the world of those things which are communicated unto you.

62 Therefore, [ago](#note62a) ye into all the world; and unto whatsoever place ye cannot go ye shall send, that the testimony may go from you into all the world unto every creature.

63 And as I said unto mine apostles, even so I say unto you, for you are mine [aapostles](#note63a), even God’s high priests; ye are they whom my Father hath [bgiven](#note63b) me; ye are my [cfriends](#note63c);

64 Therefore, as I said unto mine apostles I say unto you again, that every [asoul](#note64a) who [bbelieveth](#note64b) on your words, and is baptized by water for the [cremission](#note64c) of sins, shall [dreceive](#note64d) the Holy Ghost.

65 And these [asigns](#note65a) shall follow them that believe—

66 In my name they shall do many wonderful [aworks](#note66a);

67 In my [aname](#note67a) they shall cast out devils;

68 In my name they shall [aheal](#note68a) the sick;

69 In my name they shall [aopen](#note69a) the eyes of the blind, and unstop the ears of the deaf;

70 And the tongue of the dumb shall speak;

71 And if any man shall administer [apoison](#note71a) unto them it shall not hurt them;

72 And the [apoison](#note72a) of a serpent shall not have power to harm them.

73 But a commandment I give unto them, that they shall not [aboast](#note73a) themselves of these things, neither speak them before the world; for these things are given unto you for your profit and for salvation.

74 Verily, verily, I say unto you, they who believe not on your words, and are not [abaptized](#note74a) in water in my name, for the [bremission](#note74b) of their sins, that they may receive the Holy Ghost, shall be [cdamned](#note74c), and shall not come into my Father’s kingdom where my Father and I am.

75 And this revelation unto you, and commandment, is in force from this very hour upon all the [aworld](#note75a), and the gospel is unto all who have not received it.

76 But, verily I say unto all those to whom the kingdom has been given—from you it [amust](#note76a) be [bpreached](#note76b) unto them, that they shall repent of their former evil works; for they are to be upbraided for their evil [chearts](#note76c) of unbelief, and your brethren in Zion for their [drebellion](#note76d) against you at the time I sent you.

77 And again I say unto you, my friends, for from henceforth I shall call you [afriends](#note77a), it is expedient that I give unto you this commandment, that ye become even as my friends in days when I was with them, traveling to preach the gospel in my power;

78 For I suffered them not to have [apurse](#note78a) or scrip, neither two coats.

79 Behold, I [asend](#note79a) you out to [bprove](#note79b) the world, and the laborer is worthy of his [chire](#note79c).

80 And any man that shall go and preach this [agospel](#note80a) of the kingdom, and fail not to continue [bfaithful](#note80b) in all things, shall not be weary in mind, neither darkened, neither in body, limb, nor joint; and a [chair](#note80c) of his head shall not fall to the ground unnoticed. And they shall not go hungry, neither athirst.

81 Therefore, take ye no [athought](#note81a) for the morrow, for what ye shall eat, or what ye shall drink, or wherewithal ye shall be clothed.

82 For, [aconsider](#note82a) the [blilies](#note82b) of the field, how they grow, they toil not, neither do they spin; and the kingdoms of the world, in all their glory, are not arrayed like one of these.

83 For your [aFather](#note83a), who is in heaven, [bknoweth](#note83b) that you have need of all these things.

84 Therefore, let the morrow take [athought](#note84a) for the things of itself.

85 Neither take ye thought beforehand [awhat](#note85a) ye shall say; but [btreasure](#note85b) up in your minds continually the words of life, and it shall be [cgiven](#note85c) you in the very hour that portion that shall be meted unto every man.

86 Therefore, let no man among you, for this commandment is unto all the [afaithful](#note86a) who are called of God in the church unto the ministry, from this hour take purse or scrip, that goeth forth to proclaim this gospel of the kingdom.

87 Behold, I send you out to [areprove](#note87a) the world of all their unrighteous deeds, and to teach them of a judgment which is to come.

88 And whoso [areceiveth](#note88a) you, there I will be also, for I will go [bbefore](#note88b) your face. I will be on your right hand and on your left, and my [cSpirit](#note88c) shall be in your hearts, and mine [dangels](#note88d) round about you, to bear you up.

89 Whoso receiveth you receiveth me; and the same will feed you, and clothe you, and give you money.

90 And he who feeds you, or clothes you, or gives you money, shall in nowise [alose](#note90a) his reward.

91 And he that doeth not these things is not my disciple; by this you may know [amy disciples](#note91a).

92 He that receiveth you not, go away from him alone by yourselves, and [acleanse](#note92a) your feet even with water, pure water, whether in heat or in cold, and bear testimony of it unto your Father which is in heaven, and return not again unto that man.

93 And in whatsoever village or city ye enter, do likewise.

94 Nevertheless, search diligently and spare not; and wo unto that house, or that village or city that rejecteth you, or your words, or your testimony concerning me.

95 Wo, I say again, unto that house, or that village or city that rejecteth you, or your words, or your testimony of me;

96 For I, the [aAlmighty](#note96a), have laid my hands upon the nations, to [bscourge](#note96b) them for their [cwickedness](#note96c).

97 And [aplagues](#note97a) shall go forth, and they shall not be taken from the earth until I have completed my work, which shall be cut [bshort](#note97b) in righteousness—

98 Until all shall [aknow](#note98a) me, who remain, even from the least unto the greatest, and shall be filled with the knowledge of the Lord, and shall [bsee](#note98b) eye to eye, and shall lift up their voice, and with the voice together [csing](#note98c) this new song, saying:

99 The Lord hath brought again Zion;The Lord hath [aredeemed](#note99a) his people, [bIsrael](#note99b),According to the [celection](#note99c) of [dgrace](#note99d),Which was brought to pass by the faithAnd [ecovenant](#note99e) of their fathers.

100 The Lord hath redeemed his people;And Satan is [abound](#note100a) and [btime](#note100b) is no longer.The Lord hath gathered all things in [cone](#note100c).The Lord hath brought down [dZion](#note100d) from above.The Lord hath [ebrought up](#note100e) Zion from beneath.

101 The [aearth](#note101a) hath travailed and [bbrought forth](#note101b) her strength;And truth is established in her bowels;And the heavens have smiled upon her;And she is clothed with the [cglory](#note101c) of her God;For he [dstands](#note101d) in the midst of his [epeople](#note101e).

102 Glory, and honor, and power, and might,Be ascribed to our God; for he is full of [amercy](#note102a),Justice, grace and truth, and [bpeace](#note102b),Forever and ever, Amen.

103 And again, verily, verily, I say unto you, it is expedient that every man who goes forth to proclaim mine everlasting gospel, that inasmuch as they have [afamilies](#note103a), and receive [bmoney](#note103b) by gift, that they should send it unto them or make use of it for their benefit, as the Lord shall direct them, for thus it seemeth me good.

104 And let all those who have not families, who receive [amoney](#note104a), send it up unto the bishop in Zion, or unto the bishop in Ohio, that it may be consecrated for the bringing forth of the revelations and the printing thereof, and for establishing Zion.

105 And if any man shall give unto any of you a coat, or a suit, take the old and cast it unto the [apoor](#note105a), and go on your way rejoicing.

106 And if any man among you be [astrong](#note106a) in the Spirit, let him take with him him that is [bweak](#note106b), that he may be [cedified](#note106c) in all [dmeekness](#note106d), that he may become strong also.

107 Therefore, take with you those who are ordained unto the [alesser priesthood](#note107a), and send them [bbefore](#note107b) you to make appointments, and to prepare the way, and to fill appointments that you yourselves are not able to fill.

108 Behold, this is the way that mine apostles, in ancient days, built up my church unto me.

109 Therefore, let every man stand in his own [aoffice](#note109a), and [blabor](#note109b) in his own calling; and let not the [chead](#note109c) say unto the feet it hath no need of the feet; for without the feet how shall the body be able to stand?

110 Also the body hath need of every [amember](#note110a), that all may be [bedified](#note110b) together, that the system may be kept perfect.

111 And behold, the [ahigh priests](#note111a) should travel, and also the elders, and also the lesser [bpriests](#note111b); but the [cdeacons](#note111c) and [dteachers](#note111d) should be appointed to [ewatch](#note111e) over the church, to be standing ministers unto the church.

112 And the bishop, Newel K. Whitney, also should travel round about and among all the churches, searching after the poor to [aadminister](#note112a) to their wants by [bhumbling](#note112b) the rich and the proud.

113 He should also employ an [aagent](#note113a) to take charge and to do his secular business as he shall direct.

114 Nevertheless, let the bishop go unto the city of New York, also to the city of Albany, and also to the city of Boston, and warn the people of those [acities](#note114a) with the sound of the gospel, with a loud voice, of the [bdesolation](#note114b) and utter abolishment which await them if they do reject these things.

115 For if they do reject these things the hour of their judgment is nigh, and their house shall be left unto them [adesolate](#note115a).

116 Let him [atrust](#note116a) in me and he shall not be [bconfounded](#note116b); and a [chair](#note116c) of his head shall not fall to the ground unnoticed.

117 And verily I say unto you, the rest of my servants, go ye forth as your circumstances shall permit, in your several callings, unto the great and notable cities and villages, [areproving](#note117a) the world in righteousness of all their unrighteous and ungodly deeds, setting forth clearly and understandingly the desolation of [babomination](#note117b) in the last days.

118 For, with you saith the Lord [aAlmighty](#note118a), I will [brend](#note118b) their [ckingdoms](#note118c); I will not only [dshake](#note118d) the earth, but the [estarry](#note118e) heavens shall tremble.

119 For I, the Lord, have put forth my hand to exert the [apowers](#note119a) of heaven; ye cannot see it now, yet a [blittle while](#note119b) and ye shall see it, and know that I am, and that [cI](#note119c) will [dcome](#note119d) and reign with my people.

120 I am [aAlpha and Omega](#note120a), the beginning and the end. Amen.

Section 85

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, November 27, 1832. This section is an extract from a letter of the Prophet to William W. Phelps, who was living in Independence, Missouri. It answers questions about those Saints who had moved to Zion but who had not followed the commandment to consecrate their properties and had thus not received their inheritances according to the established order in the Church.

[1–5](85.xhtml#p1), Inheritances in Zion are to be received through consecration; [6–12](85.xhtml#p6), One mighty and strong will give the Saints their inheritance in Zion.

1 It is the duty of the Lord’s clerk, whom he has appointed, to keep a [ahistory](#note1a), and a general church [brecord](#note1b) of all things that transpire in Zion, and of all those who [cconsecrate](#note1c) properties, and receive [dinheritances](#note1d) legally from the bishop;

2 And also their manner of life, their faith, and works; and also of the [aapostates](#note2a) who apostatize after receiving their inheritances.

3 It is contrary to the will and commandment of God that those who receive not their [ainheritance](#note3a) by [bconsecration](#note3b), agreeable to his [claw](#note3c), which he has given, that he may [dtithe](#note3d) his people, to prepare them against the day of [evengeance](#note3e) and burning, should have their [fnames](#note3f) enrolled with the people of God.

4 Neither is their [agenealogy](#note4a) to be kept, or to be had where it may be found on any of the records or history of the church.

5 Their names shall not be found, neither the names of the fathers, nor the names of the children written in the [abook](#note5a) of the law of God, saith the Lord of Hosts.

6 Yea, thus saith the [astill small voice](#note6a), which whispereth through and [bpierceth](#note6b) all things, and often times it maketh my bones to quake while it maketh manifest, saying:

7 And it shall come to pass that I, the Lord God, will send one mighty and strong, holding the scepter of power in his hand, clothed with light for a covering, whose mouth shall utter words, eternal words; while his bowels shall be a fountain of truth, to set in [aorder](#note7a) the house of God, and to arrange by [blot](#note7b) the [cinheritances](#note7c) of the saints whose names are found, and the names of their fathers, and of their children, enrolled in the book of the law of God;

8 While that man, who was called of God and appointed, that putteth forth his hand to [asteady](#note8a) the [bark](#note8b) of God, shall fall by the shaft of death, like as a tree that is smitten by the vivid shaft of lightning.

9 And all they who are not found written in the [abook of remembrance](#note9a) shall find none inheritance in that day, but they shall be cut asunder, and their portion shall be appointed them among [bunbelievers](#note9b), where are [cwailing](#note9c) and gnashing of teeth.

10 These things I say not of [amyself](#note10a); therefore, as the Lord speaketh, he will also fulfil.

11 And they who are of the High Priesthood, whose names are not found written in the [abook](#note11a) of the law, or that are found to have [bapostatized](#note11b), or to have been [ccut off](#note11c) from the church, as well as the lesser priesthood, or the members, in that day shall [dnot](#note11d) find an inheritance among the saints of the Most High;

12 Therefore, it shall be done unto them as unto the [achildren](#note12a) of the [bpriest](#note12b), as will be found recorded in the second chapter and sixty-first and second verses of Ezra.

Section 86

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, December 6, 1832. This revelation was received while the Prophet was reviewing and editing the manuscript of the translation of the Bible.

[1–7](86.xhtml#p1), The Lord gives the meaning of the parable of the wheat and tares; [8–11](86.xhtml#p8), He explains priesthood blessings to those who are lawful heirs according to the flesh.

1 Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you my servants, concerning the [aparable](#note1a) of the [bwheat](#note1b) and of the tares:

2 Behold, verily I say, the field was the world, and the apostles were the [asowers](#note2a) of the seed;

3 And after they have fallen asleep the great persecutor of the church, the apostate, the [awhore](#note3a), even [bBabylon](#note3b), that maketh all nations to drink of her cup, in whose hearts the enemy, even Satan, sitteth to reign—behold he soweth the [ctares](#note3c); wherefore, the tares choke the wheat and drive the [dchurch](#note3d) into the wilderness.

4 But behold, in the [alast days](#note4a), even now while the Lord is beginning to bring forth the word, and the blade is springing up and is yet tender—

5 Behold, verily I say unto you, the [aangels](#note5a) are crying unto the Lord day and night, who are ready and waiting to be sent forth to [breap](#note5b) down the fields;

6 But the Lord saith unto them, pluck not up the tares while the blade is yet tender (for verily your faith is weak), lest you destroy the wheat also.

7 Therefore, let the wheat and the [atares](#note7a) grow together until the harvest is fully ripe; then ye shall first gather out the wheat from among the tares, and after the gathering of the wheat, behold and lo, the tares are bound in bundles, and the field remaineth to be [bburned](#note7b).

8 Therefore, thus saith the Lord unto you, with whom the [apriesthood](#note8a) hath continued through the lineage of your fathers—

9 For ye are lawful [aheirs](#note9a), according to the flesh, and have been [bhid](#note9b) from the world with Christ in God—

10 Therefore your life and the [apriesthood](#note10a) have remained, and must needs remain through you and your lineage until the [brestoration](#note10b) of all things spoken by the mouths of all the holy prophets since the world began.

11 Therefore, blessed are ye if ye continue in my [agoodness](#note11a), a [blight](#note11b) unto the Gentiles, and through this priesthood, a [csavior](#note11c) unto my people [dIsrael](#note11d). The Lord hath said it. Amen.

Section 87

Revelation and prophecy on war, given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at or near Kirtland, Ohio, December 25, 1832. At this time disputes in the United States over slavery and South Carolina’s nullification of federal tariffs were prevalent. Joseph Smith’s history states that “appearances of troubles among the nations” were becoming “more visible” to the Prophet “than they had previously been since the Church began her journey out of the wilderness.”

[1–4](87.xhtml#p1), War is foretold between the Northern States and the Southern States; [5–8](87.xhtml#p5), Great calamities will fall upon all the inhabitants of the earth.

1 Verily, thus saith the Lord concerning the [awars](#note1a) that will [bshortly](#note1b) come to pass, beginning at the rebellion of [cSouth Carolina](#note1c), which will eventually terminate in the death and misery of many souls;

2 And the [atime](#note2a) will come that [bwar](#note2b) will be poured out upon all nations, beginning at this place.

3 For behold, the Southern States shall be divided against the Northern States, and the Southern States will call on other nations, even the nation of Great Britain, as it is called, and they shall also call upon other nations, in order to defend themselves against other nations; and then [awar](#note3a) shall be poured out upon all nations.

4 And it shall come to pass, after many days, [aslaves](#note4a) shall rise up against their masters, who shall be marshaled and disciplined for war.

5 And it shall come to pass also that the [aremnants](#note5a) who are left of the land will marshal themselves, and shall become exceedingly angry, and shall vex the Gentiles with a sore vexation.

6 And thus, with the [asword](#note6a) and by bloodshed the inhabitants of the earth shall [bmourn](#note6b); and with [cfamine](#note6c), and plague, and earthquake, and the thunder of heaven, and the fierce and vivid lightning also, shall the inhabitants of the earth be made to feel the wrath, and indignation, and [dchastening](#note6d) [ehand](#note6e) of an Almighty God, until the consumption decreed hath made a full [fend](#note6f) of all [gnations](#note6g);

7 That the cry of the saints, and of the [ablood](#note7a) of the saints, shall cease to come up into the ears of the Lord of [bSabaoth](#note7b), from the earth, to be avenged of their enemies.

8 Wherefore, [astand](#note8a) ye in holy places, and be not moved, until the day of the Lord come; for behold, it cometh [bquickly](#note8b), saith the Lord. Amen.
