Section 88

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet at Kirtland, Ohio, December 27 and 28, 1832, and January 3, 1833. The Prophet designated it as the “‘olive leaf’ … plucked from the Tree of Paradise, the Lord’s message of peace to us.” The revelation was given after high priests at a conference prayed “separately and vocally to the Lord to reveal his will unto us concerning the upbuilding of Zion.”

[1–5](88.xhtml#p1), Faithful Saints receive that Comforter, which is the promise of eternal life; [6–13](88.xhtml#p6), All things are controlled and governed by the Light of Christ; [14–16](88.xhtml#p14), The Resurrection comes through the Redemption; [17–31](88.xhtml#p17), Obedience to celestial, terrestrial, or telestial law prepares men for those respective kingdoms and glories; [32–35](88.xhtml#p32), Those who will to abide in sin remain filthy still; [36–41](88.xhtml#p36), All kingdoms are governed by law; [42–45](88.xhtml#p42), God has given a law unto all things; [46–50](88.xhtml#p46), Man will comprehend even God; [51–61](88.xhtml#p51), The parable of the man sending his servants into the field and visiting them in turn; [62–73](88.xhtml#p62), Draw near unto the Lord, and ye will see His face; [74–80](88.xhtml#p74), Sanctify yourselves and teach one another the doctrines of the kingdom; [81–85](88.xhtml#p81), Every man who has been warned should warn his neighbor; [86–94](88.xhtml#p86), Signs, upheavals of the elements, and angels prepare the way for the coming of the Lord; [95–102](88.xhtml#p95), Angelic trumps call forth the dead in their order; [103–16](88.xhtml#p103), Angelic trumps proclaim the restoration of the gospel, the fall of Babylon, and the battle of the great God; [117–26](88.xhtml#p117), Seek learning, establish a house of God (a temple), and clothe yourselves with the bond of charity; [127–41](88.xhtml#p127), The order of the School of the Prophets is set forth, including the ordinance of washing of feet.

1 Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you who have assembled yourselves together to receive his will concerning you:

2 Behold, this is pleasing unto your Lord, and the angels [arejoice](#note2a) over you; the [balms](#note2b) of your prayers have come up into the ears of the Lord of [cSabaoth](#note2c), and are recorded in the [dbook](#note2d) of the names of the sanctified, even them of the celestial world.

3 Wherefore, I now send upon you another [aComforter](#note3a), even upon you my friends, that it may abide in your hearts, even the Holy Spirit of [bpromise](#note3b); which other Comforter is the same that I promised unto my disciples, as is recorded in the testimony of John.

4 This Comforter is the [apromise](#note4a) which I give unto you of [beternal life](#note4b), even the [cglory](#note4c) of the celestial kingdom;

5 Which glory is that of the church of the [aFirstborn](#note5a), even of God, the holiest of all, through Jesus Christ his Son—

6 He that [aascended](#note6a) up on high, as also he [bdescended](#note6b) below all things, in that he [ccomprehended](#note6c) all things, that he might be in all and through all things, the [dlight](#note6d) of truth;

7 Which truth shineth. This is the [alight of Christ](#note7a). As also he is in the sun, and the light of the sun, and the power thereof by which it was [bmade](#note7b).

8 As also he is in the moon, and is the light of the moon, and the power thereof by which it was made;

9 As also the light of the stars, and the power thereof by which they were made;

10 And the earth also, and the power thereof, even the earth upon which you [astand](#note10a).

11 And the light which shineth, which giveth you light, is through him who enlighteneth your eyes, which is the same light that quickeneth your [aunderstandings](#note11a);

12 Which [alight](#note12a) proceedeth forth from the presence of God to [bfill](#note12b) the immensity of space—

13 The [alight](#note13a) which is in all things, which giveth [blife](#note13b) to all things, which is the [claw](#note13c) by which all things are governed, even the [dpower](#note13d) of God who [esitteth](#note13e) upon his throne, who is in the bosom of eternity, who is in the midst of all things.

14 Now, verily I say unto you, that through the [aredemption](#note14a) which is made for you is brought to pass the resurrection from the dead.

15 And the [aspirit](#note15a) and the [bbody](#note15b) are the [csoul](#note15c) of man.

16 And the [aresurrection](#note16a) from the dead is the redemption of the soul.

17 And the redemption of the soul is through him that [aquickeneth](#note17a) all things, in whose bosom it is decreed that the [bpoor](#note17b) and the [cmeek](#note17c) of the [dearth](#note17d) shall inherit it.

18 Therefore, it must needs be [asanctified](#note18a) from all [bunrighteousness](#note18b), that it may be prepared for the [ccelestial](#note18c) glory;

19 For after it hath filled the measure of its creation, it shall be crowned with [aglory](#note19a), even with the presence of God the Father;

20 That bodies who are of the [acelestial](#note20a) kingdom may [bpossess](#note20b) it forever and ever; for, for this [cintent](#note20c) was it made and created, and for this intent are they [dsanctified](#note20d).

21 And they who are not [asanctified](#note21a) through the [blaw](#note21b) which I have given unto you, even the law of Christ, must inherit [canother](#note21c) kingdom, even that of a terrestrial kingdom, or that of a telestial kingdom.

22 For he who is not able to abide the [alaw](#note22a) of a celestial kingdom cannot [babide](#note22b) a [ccelestial](#note22c) glory.

23 And he who cannot abide the law of a [aterrestrial](#note23a) kingdom cannot abide a terrestrial glory.

24 And he who cannot abide the law of a [atelestial](#note24a) [bkingdom](#note24b) cannot abide a telestial [cglory](#note24c); therefore he is not meet for a kingdom of glory. Therefore he must abide a kingdom which is not a kingdom of glory.

25 And again, verily I say unto you, the [aearth](#note25a) abideth the law of a celestial kingdom, for it filleth the [bmeasure](#note25b) of its creation, and transgresseth not the law—

26 Wherefore, it shall be [asanctified](#note26a); yea, notwithstanding it shall [bdie](#note26b), it shall be [cquickened](#note26c) again, and shall abide the power by which it is quickened, and the [drighteous](#note26d) shall [einherit](#note26e) it.

27 For notwithstanding they die, they also shall [arise](#note27a) again, a [bspiritual body](#note27b).

28 They who are of a celestial [aspirit](#note28a) shall receive the same [bbody](#note28b) which was a natural body; even ye shall receive your bodies, and your [cglory](#note28c) shall be that glory by which your bodies are [dquickened](#note28d).

29 Ye who are [aquickened](#note29a) by a portion of the [bcelestial](#note29b) glory shall then receive of the same, even a fulness.

30 And they who are quickened by a portion of the [aterrestrial](#note30a) glory shall then receive of the same, even a fulness.

31 And also they who are quickened by a portion of the [atelestial](#note31a) glory shall then receive of the same, even a fulness.

32 And they who remain shall also be [aquickened](#note32a); nevertheless, they shall return again to their own place, to enjoy that which they are [bwilling](#note32b) to receive, because they were not willing to enjoy that which they might have received.

33 For what doth it profit a man if a gift is bestowed upon him, and he receive not the gift? Behold, he rejoices not in that which is given unto him, neither rejoices in him who is the giver of the gift.

34 And again, verily I say unto you, that which is [agoverned](#note34a) by law is also preserved by law and perfected and [bsanctified](#note34b) by the same.

35 That which [abreaketh](#note35a) a law, and [babideth not](#note35b) by [claw](#note35c), but seeketh to become a law unto itself, and willeth to abide in sin, and altogether abideth in sin, cannot be sanctified by law, neither by mercy, [djustice](#note35d), nor [ejudgment](#note35e). Therefore, they must remain [ffilthy](#note35f) still.

36 All kingdoms have a law given;

37 And there are many [akingdoms](#note37a); for there is no [bspace](#note37b) in the which there is no [ckingdom](#note37c); and there is no kingdom in which there is no space, either a greater or a lesser kingdom.

38 And unto every kingdom is given a [alaw](#note38a); and unto every law there are certain bounds also and conditions.

39 All beings who abide not in those [aconditions](#note39a) are not [bjustified](#note39b).

40 For [aintelligence](#note40a) cleaveth unto intelligence; [bwisdom](#note40b) receiveth wisdom; [ctruth](#note40c) embraceth truth; [dvirtue](#note40d) loveth virtue; [elight](#note40e) cleaveth unto light; [fmercy](#note40f) hath [gcompassion](#note40g) on mercy and claimeth her own; [hjustice](#note40h) continueth its course and claimeth its own; judgment goeth before the face of him who sitteth upon the throne and governeth and executeth all things.

41 He [acomprehendeth](#note41a) all things, and all things are before him, and all things are round about him; and he is above all things, and in all things, and is through all things, and is round about all things; and all things are by him, and of him, even God, forever and ever.

42 And again, verily I say unto you, he hath given a [alaw](#note42a) unto all things, by which they move in their [btimes](#note42b) and their seasons;

43 And their courses are fixed, even the courses of the heavens and the earth, which comprehend the earth and all the planets.

44 And they give [alight](#note44a) to each other in their times and in their seasons, in their minutes, in their hours, in their days, in their weeks, in their months, in their years—all these are [bone](#note44b) year with God, but not with man.

45 The earth [arolls](#note45a) upon her wings, and the [bsun](#note45b) giveth his light by day, and the moon giveth her light by night, and the stars also give their light, as they roll upon their wings in their glory, in the midst of the [cpower](#note45c) of God.

46 Unto what shall I liken these kingdoms, that ye may understand?

47 Behold, all these are [akingdoms](#note47a), and any man who hath [bseen](#note47b) any or the least of these hath [cseen](#note47c) God [dmoving](#note47d) in his majesty and power.

48 I say unto you, he hath seen him; nevertheless, he who came unto his [aown](#note48a) was not comprehended.

49 The [alight](#note49a) shineth in darkness, and the darkness comprehendeth it not; nevertheless, the day shall come when you shall [bcomprehend](#note49b) even God, being quickened in him and by him.

50 Then shall ye know that ye have [aseen](#note50a) me, that I am, and that I am the true [blight](#note50b) that is in you, and that you are in me; otherwise ye could not abound.

51 Behold, I will liken these kingdoms unto a man having a field, and he sent forth his servants into the field to dig in the field.

52 And he said unto the first: Go ye and labor in the field, and in the first hour I will come unto you, and ye shall behold the joy of my countenance.

53 And he said unto the second: Go ye also into the field, and in the second hour I will visit you with the joy of my countenance.

54 And also unto the third, saying: I will visit you;

55 And unto the fourth, and so on unto the twelfth.

56 And the lord of the field went unto the first in the first hour, and tarried with him all that hour, and he was made glad with the light of the countenance of his lord.

57 And then he withdrew from the first that he might visit the second also, and the third, and the fourth, and so on unto the twelfth.

58 And thus they all received the light of the countenance of their lord, every man in his hour, and in his time, and in his season—

59 Beginning at the first, and so on unto the [alast](#note59a), and from the last unto the first, and from the first unto the last;

60 Every man in his own [aorder](#note60a), until his hour was finished, even according as his lord had commanded him, that his lord might be glorified in him, and he in his lord, that they all might be glorified.

61 Therefore, unto this parable I will liken all these [akingdoms](#note61a), and the [binhabitants](#note61b) thereof—every kingdom in its hour, and in its time, and in its season, even according to the decree which God hath made.

62 And again, verily I say unto you, my [afriends](#note62a), I leave these [bsayings](#note62b) with you to [cponder](#note62c) in your hearts, with this commandment which I give unto you, that ye shall [dcall](#note62d) upon me while I am near—

63 [aDraw](#note63a) [bnear](#note63b) unto me and I will draw near unto you; [cseek](#note63c) me diligently and ye shall [dfind](#note63d) me; ask, and ye shall receive; knock, and it shall be opened unto you.

64 Whatsoever ye [aask](#note64a) the Father in my name it shall be given unto you, that is [bexpedient](#note64b) for you;

65 And if ye ask anything that is not [aexpedient](#note65a) for you, it shall turn unto your [bcondemnation](#note65b).

66 Behold, that which you hear is as the [avoice](#note66a) of one crying in the wilderness—in the wilderness, because you cannot see him—my voice, because my voice is [bSpirit](#note66b); my Spirit is truth; [ctruth](#note66c) abideth and hath no end; and if it be in you it shall abound.

67 And if your eye be [asingle](#note67a) to my [bglory](#note67b), your whole bodies shall be filled with light, and there shall be no darkness in you; and that body which is filled with light [ccomprehendeth](#note67c) all things.

68 Therefore, [asanctify](#note68a) yourselves that your [bminds](#note68b) become [csingle](#note68c) to God, and the days will come that you shall [dsee](#note68d) him; for he will unveil his face unto you, and it shall be in his own time, and in his own way, and according to his own will.

69 Remember the great and last promise which I have made unto you; cast away your [aidle](#note69a) thoughts and your [bexcess](#note69b) of [claughter](#note69c) far from you.

70 Tarry ye, tarry ye in this place, and call a [asolemn assembly](#note70a), even of those who are the first [blaborers](#note70b) in this last kingdom.

71 And let those whom they have warned in their traveling call on the Lord, and [aponder](#note71a) the [bwarning](#note71b) in their hearts which they have received, for a little season.

72 Behold, and lo, I will take care of your [aflocks](#note72a), and will raise up elders and send unto them.

73 Behold, I will [ahasten](#note73a) my work in its time.

74 And I give unto you, who are the first [alaborers](#note74a) in this last kingdom, a commandment that you assemble yourselves together, and organize yourselves, and prepare yourselves, and [bsanctify](#note74b) yourselves; yea, purify your hearts, and [ccleanse](#note74c) your hands and your feet before me, that I may make you [dclean](#note74d);

75 That I may testify unto your [aFather](#note75a), and your God, and my [bGod](#note75b), that you are clean from the [cblood](#note75c) of this wicked generation; that I may fulfil this promise, this great and last [dpromise](#note75d), which I have made unto you, when I will.

76 Also, I give unto you a commandment that ye shall continue in [aprayer](#note76a) and fasting from this time forth.

77 And I give unto you a commandment that you shall [ateach](#note77a) one another the [bdoctrine](#note77b) of the kingdom.

78 Teach ye diligently and my [agrace](#note78a) shall attend you, that you may be [binstructed](#note78b) more perfectly in theory, in principle, in doctrine, in the law of the gospel, in all things that pertain unto the kingdom of God, that are expedient for you to understand;

79 Of things both in [aheaven](#note79a) and in the earth, and under the earth; things which have been, things which are, things which must [bshortly](#note79b) come to pass; things which are at home, things which are abroad; the wars and the perplexities of the [cnations](#note79c), and the judgments which are on the land; and a [dknowledge](#note79d) also of countries and of kingdoms—

80 That ye may be prepared in all things when I shall send you again to [amagnify](#note80a) the calling whereunto I have called you, and the [bmission](#note80b) with which I have commissioned you.

81 Behold, I sent you out to [atestify](#note81a) and warn the people, and it becometh every man who hath been warned to [bwarn](#note81b) his neighbor.

82 Therefore, they are left [awithout](#note82a) excuse, and their sins are upon their [bown heads](#note82b).

83 He that [aseeketh](#note83a) me [bearly](#note83b) shall find me, and shall not be forsaken.

84 Therefore, tarry ye, and labor diligently, that you may be perfected in your ministry to go forth among the [aGentiles](#note84a) for the last time, as many as the mouth of the Lord shall name, to [bbind](#note84b) up the law and [cseal](#note84c) up the testimony, and to prepare the saints for the hour of judgment which is to come;

85 That their souls may escape the wrath of God, the [adesolation of abomination](#note85a) which awaits the wicked, both in this world and in the world to come. Verily, I say unto you, let those who are not the [bfirst](#note85b) elders continue in the vineyard until the mouth of the Lord shall [ccall](#note85c) them, for their time is not yet come; their garments are not [dclean](#note85d) from the blood of this generation.

86 Abide ye in the [aliberty](#note86a) wherewith ye are made [bfree](#note86b); [centangle](#note86c) not yourselves in [dsin](#note86d), but let your hands be [eclean](#note86e), until the Lord comes.

87 For not many days hence and the [aearth](#note87a) shall [btremble](#note87b) and reel to and fro as a drunken man; and the [csun](#note87c) shall [dhide](#note87d) his face, and shall refuse to give light; and the moon shall be bathed in [eblood](#note87e); and the stars shall become exceedingly angry, and shall [fcast](#note87f) themselves down as a fig that falleth from off a fig tree.

88 And after your [atestimony](#note88a) cometh wrath and indignation upon the people.

89 For after your testimony cometh the testimony of [aearthquakes](#note89a), that shall cause groanings in the midst of her, and men shall fall upon the ground and shall not be able to stand.

90 And also cometh the testimony of the [avoice](#note90a) of thunderings, and the voice of lightnings, and the voice of tempests, and the voice of the waves of the sea heaving themselves beyond their bounds.

91 And all things shall be in [acommotion](#note91a); and surely, men’s [bhearts](#note91b) shall fail them; for fear shall come upon all people.

92 And [aangels](#note92a) shall fly through the midst of heaven, crying with a loud voice, sounding the trump of God, saying: Prepare ye, prepare ye, O inhabitants of the earth; for the [bjudgment](#note92b) of our God is come. Behold, and lo, the [cBridegroom](#note92c) cometh; go ye out to meet him.

93 And immediately there shall appear a [agreat sign](#note93a) in heaven, and all people shall see it together.

94 And another angel shall sound his trump, saying: That [agreat](#note94a) [bchurch](#note94b), the [cmother](#note94c) of abominations, that made all nations drink of the wine of the wrath of her [dfornication](#note94d), that [epersecuteth](#note94e) the saints of God, that shed their blood—she who sitteth upon many waters, and upon the islands of the sea—behold, she is the [ftares](#note94f) of the earth; she is bound in bundles; her bands are made strong, no man can loose them; therefore, she is ready to be burned. And he shall sound his trump both long and loud, and all nations shall hear it.

95 And there shall be [asilence](#note95a) in [bheaven](#note95b) for the space of half an hour; and immediately after shall the curtain of heaven be unfolded, as a [cscroll](#note95c) is unfolded after it is rolled up, and the [dface](#note95d) of the Lord shall be unveiled;

96 And the saints that are upon the earth, who are alive, shall be quickened and be [acaught up](#note96a) to meet him.

97 And they who have slept in their graves shall [acome](#note97a) forth, for their graves shall be opened; and they also shall be caught up to meet him in the midst of the [bpillar](#note97b) of heaven—

98 They are Christ’s, the [afirst fruits](#note98a), they who shall descend with him first, and they who are on the earth and in their graves, who are first caught up to meet him; and all this by the voice of the sounding of the trump of the angel of God.

99 And after this another angel shall sound, which is the second trump; and then cometh the redemption of those who are Christ’s at his [acoming](#note99a); who have received their part in that [bprison](#note99b) which is prepared for them, that they might receive the gospel, and be [cjudged](#note99c) according to men in the flesh.

100 And again, another trump shall sound, which is the third trump; and then come the [aspirits](#note100a) of men who are to be judged, and are found under [bcondemnation](#note100b);

101 And these are the rest of the [adead](#note101a); and they live not again until the [bthousand](#note101b) years are ended, neither again, until the end of the earth.

102 And another trump shall sound, which is the fourth trump, saying: There are found among those who are to remain until that great and last day, even the end, who shall [aremain](#note102a) [bfilthy](#note102b) still.

103 And another trump shall sound, which is the fifth trump, which is the fifth angel who committeth the [aeverlasting gospel](#note103a)—flying through the midst of heaven, unto all nations, kindreds, tongues, and people;

104 And this shall be the sound of his trump, saying to all people, both in heaven and in earth, and that are under the earth—for [aevery](#note104a) ear shall hear it, and every knee shall [bbow](#note104b), and every tongue shall confess, while they hear the sound of the trump, saying: [cFear](#note104c) God, and give glory to him who sitteth upon the throne, [dforever](#note104d) and ever; for the hour of his judgment is come.

105 And again, another angel shall sound his trump, which is the sixth angel, saying: She is [afallen](#note105a) who made all nations drink of the wine of the wrath of her fornication; she is fallen, is fallen!

106 And again, another angel shall sound his trump, which is the seventh angel, saying: It is finished; it is finished! The [aLamb](#note106a) of God hath [bovercome](#note106b) and [ctrodden](#note106c) the wine-press alone, even the wine-press of the fierceness of the wrath of Almighty God.

107 And then shall the angels be crowned with the glory of his might, and the [asaints](#note107a) shall be filled with his [bglory](#note107b), and receive their [cinheritance](#note107c) and be made [dequal](#note107d) with him.

108 And then shall the first angel again sound his trump in the ears of all living, and [areveal](#note108a) the secret acts of men, and the mighty works of God in the [bfirst thousand](#note108b) years.

109 And then shall the second angel sound his trump, and reveal the secret acts of men, and the thoughts and intents of their hearts, and the mighty [aworks](#note109a) of God in the second thousand years—

110 And so on, until the seventh angel shall sound his trump; and he shall [astand](#note110a) forth upon the land and upon the sea, and [bswear](#note110b) in the name of him who sitteth upon the throne, that there shall be [ctime](#note110c) no longer; and [dSatan](#note110d) shall be bound, that old serpent, who is called the devil, and shall not be loosed for the space of a [ethousand](#note110e) years.

111 And then he shall be [aloosed](#note111a) for a little season, that he may gather together his armies.

112 And [aMichael](#note112a), the seventh angel, even the archangel, shall gather together his armies, even the hosts of heaven.

113 And the devil shall gather together his [aarmies](#note113a); even the hosts of hell, and shall come up to battle against Michael and his armies.

114 And then cometh the [abattle](#note114a) of the great God; and the devil and his armies shall be [bcast away](#note114b) into their own place, that they shall not have power over the saints any more at all.

115 For Michael shall fight their battles, and shall overcome him who [aseeketh](#note115a) the throne of him who sitteth upon the throne, even the Lamb.

116 This is the glory of God, and the [asanctified](#note116a); and they shall not any more see [bdeath](#note116b).

117 Therefore, verily I say unto you, my [afriends](#note117a), call your solemn assembly, as I have [bcommanded](#note117b) you.

118 And as all have not [afaith](#note118a), seek ye diligently and [bteach](#note118b) one another words of [cwisdom](#note118c); yea, seek ye out of the best [dbooks](#note118d) words of wisdom; seek learning, even by study and also by faith.

119 [aOrganize](#note119a) yourselves; prepare every needful thing; and establish a [bhouse](#note119b), even a house of prayer, a house of fasting, a house of faith, a house of learning, a house of glory, a house of order, a house of God;

120 That your [aincomings](#note120a) may be in the name of the Lord; that your outgoings may be in the name of the Lord; that all your salutations may be in the name of the Lord, with [buplifted](#note120b) hands unto the Most High.

121 Therefore, [acease](#note121a) from all your light speeches, from all [blaughter](#note121b), from all your [clustful](#note121c) desires, from all your [dpride](#note121d) and light-mindedness, and from all your wicked doings.

122 Appoint among yourselves a teacher, and let [anot](#note122a) all be spokesmen at once; but let one speak at a time and let all listen unto his sayings, that when all have spoken that all may be [bedified](#note122b) of all, and that every man may have an equal privilege.

123 See that ye [alove](#note123a) one another; cease to be [bcovetous](#note123b); learn to impart one to another as the gospel requires.

124 Cease to be [aidle](#note124a); cease to be [bunclean](#note124b); cease to [cfind fault](#note124c) one with another; cease to [dsleep](#note124d) longer than is needful; retire to thy bed early, that ye may not be weary; arise early, that your bodies and your minds may be [einvigorated](#note124e).

125 And above all things, clothe yourselves with the bond of [acharity](#note125a), as with a mantle, which is the bond of perfectness and [bpeace](#note125b).

126 [aPray](#note126a) always, that ye may not faint, until I [bcome](#note126b). Behold, and lo, I will come quickly, and receive you unto myself. Amen.

127 And again, the order of the house prepared for the [apresidency](#note127a) of the [bschool](#note127b) of the [cprophets](#note127c), established for their instruction in all things that are expedient for them, even for all the [dofficers](#note127d) of the church, or in other words, those who are called to the ministry in the church, beginning at the high priests, even down to the deacons—

128 And this shall be the order of the house of the presidency of the school: He that is appointed to be president, or teacher, shall be found standing in his place, in the house which shall be prepared for him.

129 Therefore, he shall be first in the house of God, in a place that the congregation in the house may hear his words carefully and distinctly, not with loud speech.

130 And when he cometh into the house of God, for he should be first in the house—behold, this is [abeautiful](#note130a), that he may be an [bexample](#note130b)—

131 Let him offer himself in prayer upon his knees before God, in [atoken](#note131a) or remembrance of the everlasting covenant.

132 And when any shall come in after him, let the teacher arise, and, with [auplifted](#note132a) hands to heaven, yea, even directly, salute his brother or brethren with these words:

133 Art thou a brother or brethren? I salute you in the name of the Lord Jesus Christ, in token or remembrance of the everlasting covenant, in which covenant I receive you to [afellowship](#note133a), in a determination that is fixed, immovable, and unchangeable, to be your [bfriend](#note133b) and [cbrother](#note133c) through the grace of God in the bonds of love, to walk in all the commandments of God blameless, in thanksgiving, forever and ever. Amen.

134 And he that is found [aunworthy](#note134a) of this salutation shall not have place among you; for ye shall not suffer that mine house shall be [bpolluted](#note134b) by him.

135 And he that cometh in and is faithful before me, and is a brother, or if they be brethren, they shall salute the president or teacher with uplifted hands to heaven, with this same prayer and covenant, or by saying Amen, in token of the same.

136 Behold, verily, I say unto you, this is an ensample unto you for a salutation to one another in the house of God, in the school of the prophets.

137 And ye are called to do this by prayer and thanksgiving, as the Spirit shall give utterance in all your doings in the house of the Lord, in the school of the prophets, that it may become a sanctuary, a tabernacle of the Holy Spirit to your [aedification](#note137a).

138 And ye shall not receive any among you into this school save he is clean from the [ablood](#note138a) of this generation;

139 And he shall be received by the ordinance of the [awashing](#note139a) of feet, for unto this end was the ordinance of the washing of feet instituted.

140 And again, the ordinance of washing feet is to be administered by the president, or presiding elder of the church.

141 It is to be commenced with prayer; and after partaking of [abread and wine](#note141a), he is to gird himself according to the [bpattern](#note141b) given in the thirteenth chapter of John’s testimony concerning me. Amen.

Section 89

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, February 27, 1833. As a consequence of the early brethren using tobacco in their meetings, the Prophet was led to ponder upon the matter; consequently, he inquired of the Lord concerning it. This revelation, known as the Word of Wisdom, was the result.

[1–9](89.xhtml#p1), The use of wine, strong drinks, tobacco, and hot drinks is proscribed; [10–17](89.xhtml#p10), Herbs, fruits, flesh, and grain are ordained for the use of man and of animals; [18–21](89.xhtml#p18), Obedience to gospel law, including the Word of Wisdom, brings temporal and spiritual blessings.

1 A [aWord of Wisdom](#note1a), for the benefit of the council of high priests, assembled in Kirtland, and the church, and also the saints in Zion—

2 To be sent greeting; not by commandment or constraint, but by revelation and the [aword of wisdom](#note2a), showing forth the order and [bwill](#note2b) of God in the temporal salvation of all saints in the last days—

3 Given for a principle with [apromise](#note3a), adapted to the capacity of the [bweak](#note3b) and the weakest of all [csaints](#note3c), who are or can be called saints.

4 Behold, verily, thus saith the Lord unto you: In consequence of [aevils](#note4a) and designs which do and will exist in the hearts of [bconspiring](#note4b) men in the last days, I have [cwarned](#note4c) you, and forewarn you, by giving unto you this word of wisdom by revelation—

5 That inasmuch as any man [adrinketh](#note5a) [bwine](#note5b) or strong drink among you, behold it is not good, neither meet in the sight of your Father, only in assembling yourselves together to offer up your sacraments before him.

6 And, behold, this should be wine, yea, [apure](#note6a) wine of the grape of the vine, of your own make.

7 And, again, [astrong drinks](#note7a) are not for the belly, but for the washing of your bodies.

8 And again, tobacco is not for the [abody](#note8a), neither for the belly, and is not good for man, but is an herb for bruises and all sick cattle, to be used with judgment and skill.

9 And again, hot drinks are not for the body or belly.

10 And again, verily I say unto you, all wholesome [aherbs](#note10a) God hath ordained for the constitution, nature, and use of man—

11 Every herb in the season thereof, and every fruit in the season thereof; all these to be used with [aprudence](#note11a) and [bthanksgiving](#note11b).

12 Yea, [aflesh](#note12a) also of [bbeasts](#note12b) and of the fowls of the air, I, the Lord, have ordained for the use of man with thanksgiving; nevertheless they are to be used [csparingly](#note12c);

13 And it is pleasing unto me that they should not be [aused](#note13a), only in times of winter, or of cold, or [bfamine](#note13b).

14 All [agrain](#note14a) is ordained for the use of man and of beasts, to be the staff of life, not only for man but for the beasts of the field, and the fowls of heaven, and all wild animals that run or creep on the earth;

15 And [athese](#note15a) hath God made for the use of man only in times of famine and excess of hunger.

16 All grain is good for the [afood](#note16a) of man; as also the [bfruit](#note16b) of the vine; that which yieldeth fruit, whether in the ground or above the ground—

17 Nevertheless, wheat for man, and corn for the ox, and oats for the horse, and rye for the fowls and for swine, and for all beasts of the field, and barley for all useful animals, and for mild drinks, as also other grain.

18 And all saints who remember to keep and do these sayings, walking in obedience to the commandments, [ashall](#note18a) receive [bhealth](#note18b) in their navel and marrow to their bones;

19 And shall [afind](#note19a) [bwisdom](#note19b) and great [ctreasures](#note19c) of [dknowledge](#note19d), even hidden treasures;

20 And shall [arun](#note20a) and not be [bweary](#note20b), and shall walk and not faint.

21 And I, the Lord, give unto them a promise, that the [adestroying](#note21a) angel shall [bpass](#note21b) by them, as the children of Israel, and not slay them. Amen.

Section 90

Revelation to Joseph Smith the Prophet, given at Kirtland, Ohio, March 8, 1833. This revelation is a continuing step in the establishment of the First Presidency (see the [heading to section 81](81.xhtml)); as a consequence thereof, the counselors mentioned were ordained on March 18, 1833.

[1–5](90.xhtml#p1), The keys of the kingdom are committed to Joseph Smith and through him to the Church; [6–7](90.xhtml#p6), Sidney Rigdon and Frederick G. Williams are to serve in the First Presidency; [8–11](90.xhtml#p8), The gospel is to be preached to the nations of Israel, to the Gentiles, and to the Jews, every man hearing in his own tongue; [12–18](90.xhtml#p12), Joseph Smith and his counselors are to set the Church in order; [19–37](90.xhtml#p19), Various individuals are counseled by the Lord to walk uprightly and serve in His kingdom.

1 Thus saith the Lord, verily, verily I say unto you my son, thy sins are [aforgiven](#note1a) thee, according to thy petition, for thy prayers and the prayers of thy brethren have come up into my ears.

2 Therefore, thou art blessed from henceforth that bear the [akeys](#note2a) of the kingdom given unto you; which [bkingdom](#note2b) is coming forth for the last time.

3 Verily I say unto you, the keys of this [akingdom](#note3a) shall [bnever](#note3b) be taken from you, while thou art in the world, neither in the world to come;

4 Nevertheless, through you shall the [aoracles](#note4a) be given to another, yea, even unto the church.

5 And all they who receive the [aoracles](#note5a) of God, let them beware how they hold them lest they are accounted as a light thing, and are brought under condemnation thereby, and stumble and fall when the storms descend, and the winds blow, and the [brains](#note5b) descend, and beat upon their house.

6 And again, verily I say unto thy brethren, Sidney Rigdon and [aFrederick G. Williams](#note6a), their sins are forgiven them also, and they are accounted as [bequal](#note6b) with thee in holding the keys of this last kingdom;

7 As also through your administration the keys of the [aschool](#note7a) of the prophets, which I have commanded to be organized;

8 That thereby they may be [aperfected](#note8a) in their ministry for the salvation of Zion, and of the nations of Israel, and of the Gentiles, as many as will believe;

9 That through your administration they may receive the word, and through their administration the word may go forth unto the ends of the earth, unto the [aGentiles](#note9a) [bfirst](#note9b), and then, behold, and lo, they shall turn unto the Jews.

10 And then cometh the day when the arm of the Lord shall be [arevealed](#note10a) in power in convincing the nations, the [bheathen](#note10b) nations, the house of [cJoseph](#note10c), of the gospel of their salvation.

11 For it shall come to pass in that day, that every man shall [ahear](#note11a) the fulness of the gospel in his own tongue, and in his own [blanguage](#note11b), through those who are [cordained](#note11c) unto this [dpower](#note11d), by the administration of the [eComforter](#note11e), shed forth upon them for the [frevelation](#note11f) of Jesus Christ.

12 And now, verily I say unto you, I give unto you a commandment that you continue in the [aministry](#note12a) and presidency.

13 And when you have finished the [atranslation](#note13a) of the prophets, you shall from thenceforth [bpreside](#note13b) over the affairs of the church and the school;

14 And from time to time, as shall be manifested by the Comforter, receive [arevelations](#note14a) to unfold the [bmysteries](#note14b) of the kingdom;

15 And set in order the churches, and [astudy](#note15a) and [blearn](#note15b), and become acquainted with all good books, and with [clanguages](#note15c), tongues, and people.

16 And this shall be your business and mission in all your lives, to preside in council, and set in [aorder](#note16a) all the affairs of this church and kingdom.

17 Be not [aashamed](#note17a), neither confounded; but be admonished in all your high-mindedness and [bpride](#note17b), for it bringeth a snare upon your souls.

18 Set in [aorder](#note18a) your houses; keep [bslothfulness](#note18b) and [cuncleanness](#note18c) far from you.

19 Now, verily I say unto you, let there be a [aplace](#note19a) provided, as soon as it is possible, for the family of thy counselor and scribe, even Frederick G. Williams.

20 And let mine aged servant, [aJoseph Smith, Sen.](#note20a), continue with his family upon the place where he now lives; and let it not be sold until the mouth of the Lord shall name.

21 And let my counselor, even [aSidney Rigdon](#note21a), remain where he now resides until the mouth of the Lord shall name.

22 And let the bishop search diligently to obtain an [aagent](#note22a), and let him be a man who has got [briches](#note22b) in store—a man of God, and of strong faith—

23 That thereby he may be enabled to discharge every debt; that the storehouse of the Lord may not be brought into disrepute before the eyes of the people.

24 Search [adiligently](#note24a), [bpray](#note24b) always, and be believing, and [call](#note24c) things shall work together for your good, if ye walk uprightly and remember the [dcovenant](#note24d) wherewith ye have covenanted one with another.

25 Let your families be [asmall](#note25a), especially mine aged servant Joseph Smith’s, Sen., as pertaining to those who do not belong to your families;

26 That those things that are provided for you, to bring to pass my work, be not taken from you and given to those that are not worthy—

27 And thereby you be hindered in accomplishing those things which I have commanded you.

28 And again, verily I say unto you, it is my will that my handmaid Vienna Jaques should receive [amoney](#note28a) to bear her expenses, and go up unto the land of Zion;

29 And the residue of the money may be consecrated unto me, and she be rewarded in mine own due time.

30 Verily I say unto you, that it is meet in mine eyes that she should go up unto the land of Zion, and receive an [ainheritance](#note30a) from the hand of the bishop;

31 That she may settle down in peace inasmuch as she is faithful, and not be idle in her days from thenceforth.

32 And behold, verily I say unto you, that ye shall [awrite](#note32a) this commandment, and say unto your brethren in Zion, in love greeting, that I have called you also to [bpreside](#note32b) over Zion in mine own due time.

33 Therefore, let them cease wearying me concerning this matter.

34 Behold, I say unto you that your brethren in Zion begin to repent, and the angels rejoice over them.

35 Nevertheless, I am not well pleased with many things; and I am not well pleased with my servant [aWilliam E. McLellin](#note35a), neither with my servant Sidney Gilbert; and the bishop also, and others have many things to repent of.

36 But verily I say unto you, that I, the Lord, will contend with [aZion](#note36a), and plead with her strong ones, and [bchasten](#note36b) her until she overcomes and is [cclean](#note36c) before me.

37 For she shall not be removed out of her place. I, the Lord, have spoken it. Amen.

Section 91

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, March 9, 1833. The Prophet was at this time engaged in the translation of the Old Testament. Having come to that portion of the ancient writings called the Apocrypha, he inquired of the Lord and received this instruction.

[1–3](91.xhtml#p1), The Apocrypha is mostly translated correctly but contains many interpolations by the hands of men that are not true; [4–6](91.xhtml#p4), It benefits those enlightened by the Spirit.

1 Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you concerning the [aApocrypha](#note1a)—There are many things contained therein that are true, and it is mostly translated correctly;

2 There are many things contained therein that are not true, which are [ainterpolations](#note2a) by the hands of men.

3 Verily, I say unto you, that it is not needful that the Apocrypha should be [atranslated](#note3a).

4 Therefore, whoso readeth it, let him [aunderstand](#note4a), for the Spirit manifesteth truth;

5 And whoso is enlightened by the [aSpirit](#note5a) shall obtain benefit therefrom;

6 And whoso receiveth not by the Spirit, cannot be benefited. Therefore it is not needful that it should be translated. Amen.

* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1a)

* [◀](#ldsBACK_note2a)

Section 92

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, March 15, 1833. The revelation instructs Frederick G. Williams, who had recently been appointed a counselor to Joseph Smith, on his duties in the United Firm (see the [headings to sections 78](78.xhtml) and [82](82.xhtml)).

[1–2](92.xhtml#p1), The Lord gives a commandment relative to admission to the united order.

1 Verily, thus saith the Lord, I give unto the [aunited order](#note1a), organized agreeable to the commandment previously given, a revelation and commandment concerning my servant [bFrederick G. Williams](#note1b), that ye shall receive him into the order. What I say unto one I say unto all.

2 And again, I say unto you my servant Frederick G. Williams, you shall be a lively member in this order; and inasmuch as you are faithful in keeping all former commandments you shall be blessed forever. Amen.

* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1a)

  1a

  [D&C 82:11 (11, 15–21)](82.xhtml#p11); [96:8 (6–9)](96.xhtml#p8).
* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1b)

  b

  [D&C 90:6](90.xhtml#p6).

Section 93

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, May 6, 1833.

[1–5](93.xhtml#p1), All who are faithful will see the Lord; [6–18](93.xhtml#p6), John bore record that the Son of God went from grace to grace until He received a fulness of the glory of the Father; [19–20](93.xhtml#p19), Faithful men, going from grace to grace, will also receive of His fulness; [21–22](93.xhtml#p21), Those who are begotten through Christ are the Church of the Firstborn; [23–28](93.xhtml#p23), Christ received a fulness of all truth, and man by obedience may do likewise; [29–32](93.xhtml#p29), Man was in the beginning with God; [33–35](93.xhtml#p33), The elements are eternal, and man may receive a fulness of joy in the Resurrection; [36–37](93.xhtml#p36), The glory of God is intelligence; [38–40](93.xhtml#p38), Children are innocent before God because of the redemption of Christ; [41–53](93.xhtml#p41), The leading brethren are commanded to set their families in order.

1 Verily, thus saith the Lord: It shall come to pass that every soul who [aforsaketh](#note1a) his [bsins](#note1b) and cometh unto me, and [ccalleth](#note1c) on my name, and [dobeyeth](#note1d) my voice, and keepeth my commandments, shall [esee](#note1e) my [fface](#note1f) and [gknow](#note1g) that I am;

2 And that I am the true [alight](#note2a) that lighteth every man that cometh into the world;

3 And that I am [ain](#note3a) the Father, and the Father in me, and the Father and I are one—

4 The Father [abecause](#note4a) he [bgave](#note4b) me of his fulness, and the Son because I was in the world and made [cflesh](#note4c) my [dtabernacle](#note4d), and dwelt among the sons of men.

5 I was in the world and received of my Father, and the [aworks](#note5a) of him were plainly manifest.

6 And [aJohn](#note6a) saw and bore record of the fulness of my [bglory](#note6b), and the fulness of [cJohn’s](#note6c) record is hereafter to be revealed.

7 And he bore record, saying: I saw his glory, that he was in the [abeginning](#note7a), before the world was;

8 Therefore, in the beginning the [aWord](#note8a) was, for he was the Word, even the messenger of salvation—

9 The [alight](#note9a) and the Redeemer of the world; the Spirit of truth, who came into the world, because the world was made by him, and in him was the life of men and the light of men.

10 The worlds were [amade](#note10a) by him; men were made by him; all things were made by him, and through him, and of him.

11 And I, John, [abear record](#note11a) that I beheld his [bglory](#note11b), as the glory of the Only Begotten of the Father, full of grace and truth, even the Spirit of truth, which came and dwelt in the flesh, and dwelt among us.

12 And I, John, saw that he received not of the [afulness](#note12a) at the first, but received [bgrace](#note12b) for grace;

13 And he received not of the fulness at first, but continued from [agrace](#note13a) to grace, until he received a fulness;

14 And thus he was called the [aSon of God](#note14a), because he received not of the fulness at the first.

15 And I, [aJohn](#note15a), bear record, and lo, the heavens were opened, and the Holy Ghost descended upon him in the form of a dove, and sat upon him, and there came a voice out of heaven saying: This is my [bbeloved Son](#note15b).

16 And I, John, bear record that he received a fulness of the glory of the Father;

17 And he received [aall](#note17a) [bpower](#note17b), both in heaven and on earth, and the glory of the Father was with him, for he dwelt in him.

18 And it shall come to pass, that if you are faithful you shall receive the [afulness](#note18a) of the record of John.

19 I give unto you these sayings that you may understand and know how to worship, and [aknow](#note19a) what you worship, that you may come unto the Father in my name, and in due time receive of his fulness.

20 For if you keep my [acommandments](#note20a) you shall receive of his [bfulness](#note20b), and be [cglorified](#note20c) in me as I am in the Father; therefore, I say unto you, you shall receive [dgrace](#note20d) for grace.

21 And now, verily I say unto you, I was in the [abeginning](#note21a) with the Father, and am the [bFirstborn](#note21b);

22 And all those who are begotten through me are [apartakers](#note22a) of the [bglory](#note22b) of the same, and are the [cchurch](#note22c) of the Firstborn.

23 Ye were also in the beginning with the Father; that which is [aSpirit](#note23a), even the Spirit of truth;

24 And [atruth](#note24a) is [bknowledge](#note24b) of things as they are, and as they were, and as they are to come;

25 And whatsoever is [amore](#note25a) or less than this is the spirit of that wicked one who was a [bliar](#note25b) from the beginning.

26 The Spirit of [atruth](#note26a) is of God. I am the Spirit of truth, and John bore record of me, saying: He [breceived](#note26b) a fulness of truth, yea, even of all truth;

27 And no man receiveth a [afulness](#note27a) unless he keepeth his commandments.

28 He that [akeepeth](#note28a) his commandments receiveth [btruth](#note28b) and [clight](#note28c), until he is glorified in truth and [dknoweth](#note28d) all things.

29 Man was also in the [abeginning](#note29a) with God. [bIntelligence](#note29b), or the [clight](#note29c) of [dtruth](#note29d), was not [ecreated](#note29e) or made, neither indeed can be.

30 All truth is independent in that [asphere](#note30a) in which God has placed it, to [bact](#note30b) for itself, as all intelligence also; otherwise there is no existence.

31 Behold, here is the [aagency](#note31a) of man, and here is the condemnation of man; because that which was from the beginning is [bplainly manifest](#note31b) unto them, and they receive not the light.

32 And every man whose spirit receiveth not the [alight](#note32a) is under condemnation.

33 For man is [aspirit](#note33a). The elements are [beternal](#note33b), and [cspirit](#note33c) and element, inseparably connected, receive a fulness of joy;

34 And when [aseparated](#note34a), man cannot receive a fulness of joy.

35 The [aelements](#note35a) are the [btabernacle](#note35b) of God; yea, man is the tabernacle of God, even [ctemples](#note35c); and whatsoever temple is [ddefiled](#note35d), God shall destroy that temple.

36 The [aglory](#note36a) of God is [bintelligence](#note36b), or, in other words, [clight](#note36c) and truth.

37 Light and truth forsake that [aevil one](#note37a).

38 Every [aspirit](#note38a) of man was [binnocent](#note38b) in the beginning; and God having [credeemed](#note38c) man from the [dfall](#note38d), men became again, in their infant state, [einnocent](#note38e) before God.

39 And that [awicked one](#note39a) cometh and [btaketh](#note39b) away light and truth, through [cdisobedience](#note39c), from the children of men, and because of the [dtradition](#note39d) of their fathers.

40 But I have commanded you to bring up your [achildren](#note40a) in [blight](#note40b) and truth.

41 But verily I say unto you, my servant Frederick G. Williams, you have continued under this condemnation;

42 You have not [ataught](#note42a) your children light and truth, according to the commandments; and that wicked one hath power, as yet, over you, and this is the cause of your [baffliction](#note42b).

43 And now a commandment I give unto you—if you will be delivered you shall set in [aorder](#note43a) your own house, for there are many things that are not right in your house.

44 Verily, I say unto my servant Sidney Rigdon, that in some things he hath not kept the commandments concerning his children; therefore, first set in order thy house.

45 Verily, I say unto my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., or in other words, I will call you [afriends](#note45a), for you are my friends, and ye shall have an inheritance with me—

46 I called you [aservants](#note46a) for the world’s sake, and ye are their servants for my sake—

47 And now, verily I say unto Joseph Smith, Jun.—You have not kept the commandments, and must needs stand [arebuked](#note47a) before the Lord;

48 Your [afamily](#note48a) must needs repent and forsake some things, and give more earnest heed unto your sayings, or be removed out of their place.

49 What I say unto one I say unto all; [apray](#note49a) always lest that wicked one have power in you, and remove you out of your place.

50 My servant Newel K. Whitney also, a bishop of my church, hath need to be [achastened](#note50a), and set in [border](#note50b) his family, and see that they are more [cdiligent](#note50c) and concerned at home, and pray always, or they shall be removed out of their [dplace](#note50d).

51 Now, I say unto you, my friends, let my servant Sidney Rigdon go on his journey, and make haste, and also proclaim the [aacceptable](#note51a) year of the Lord, and the [bgospel](#note51b) of salvation, as I shall give him utterance; and by your prayer of faith with one consent I will uphold him.

52 And let my servants Joseph Smith, Jun., and Frederick G. Williams make haste also, and it shall be given them even according to the prayer of faith; and inasmuch as you keep my sayings you shall not be confounded in this world, nor in the world to come.

53 And, verily I say unto you, that it is my will that you should [ahasten](#note53a) to [btranslate my scriptures](#note53b), and to [cobtain](#note53c) a [dknowledge](#note53d) of history, and of countries, and of kingdoms, of [elaws](#note53e) of God and man, and all this for the salvation of Zion. Amen.

Section 94

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, August 2, 1833. Hyrum Smith, Reynolds Cahoon, and Jared Carter are appointed as a Church building committee.

[1–9](94.xhtml#p1), The Lord gives a commandment relative to the erection of a house for the work of the Presidency; [10–12](94.xhtml#p10), A printing house is to be built; [13–17](94.xhtml#p13), Certain inheritances are assigned.

1 And again, verily I say unto you, my [afriends](#note1a), a commandment I give unto you, that ye shall commence a work of laying out and preparing a beginning and foundation of the city of the [bstake](#note1b) of Zion, here in the land of Kirtland, beginning at my house.

2 And behold, it must be done according to the [apattern](#note2a) which I have given unto you.

3 And let the first lot on the south be consecrated unto me for the building of a house for the presidency, for the work of the presidency, in obtaining revelations; and for the work of the ministry of the [apresidency](#note3a), in all things pertaining to the church and kingdom.

4 Verily I say unto you, that it shall be built fifty-five by sixty-five feet in the width thereof and in the length thereof, in the inner court.

5 And there shall be a lower court and a higher court, according to the pattern which shall be given unto you hereafter.

6 And it shall be [adedicated](#note6a) unto the Lord from the foundation thereof, according to the [border](#note6b) of the priesthood, according to the pattern which shall be given unto you hereafter.

7 And it shall be wholly dedicated unto the Lord for the work of the [apresidency](#note7a).

8 And ye shall not suffer any [aunclean thing](#note8a) to come in unto it; and my [bglory](#note8b) shall be there, and my [cpresence](#note8c) shall be there.

9 But if there shall come into it any [aunclean thing](#note9a), my glory shall not be there; and my presence shall not come into it.

10 And again, verily I say unto you, the second lot on the south shall be dedicated unto me for the building of a house unto me, for the work of the [aprinting](#note10a) of the [btranslation of my scriptures](#note10b), and all things whatsoever I shall command you.

11 And it shall be fifty-five by sixty-five feet in the width thereof and the length thereof, in the inner court; and there shall be a lower and a higher court.

12 And this house shall be wholly dedicated unto the Lord from the foundation thereof, for the work of the printing, in all things whatsoever I shall command you, to be holy, undefiled, according to the pattern in all things as it shall be given unto you.

13 And on the third lot shall my servant Hyrum Smith receive his [ainheritance](#note13a).

14 And on the first and second lots on the north shall my servants Reynolds Cahoon and Jared Carter receive their inheritances—

15 That they may do the work which I have appointed unto them, to be a committee to build mine houses, according to the commandment, which I, the Lord God, have given unto you.

16 These two houses are not to be built until I give unto you a commandment concerning them.

17 And now I give unto you no more at this time. Amen.

Section 95

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, June 1, 1833. This revelation is a continuation of divine directions to build a house for worship and instruction, the house of the Lord (see [section 88:119–36](88.xhtml#p119)).

[1–6](95.xhtml#p1), The Saints are chastened for their failure to build the house of the Lord; [7–10](95.xhtml#p7), The Lord desires to use His house to endow His people with power from on high; [11–17](95.xhtml#p11), The house is to be dedicated as a place of worship and for the school of the Apostles.

1 Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you whom I love, and whom I [alove](#note1a) I also chasten that their sins may be [bforgiven](#note1b), for with the [cchastisement](#note1c) I prepare a way for their [ddeliverance](#note1d) in all things out of [etemptation](#note1e), and I have loved you—

2 Wherefore, ye must needs be chastened and stand rebuked before my face;

3 For ye have sinned against me a very grievous sin, in that ye have not considered the great commandment in all things, that I have given unto you concerning the building of mine [ahouse](#note3a);

4 For the preparation wherewith I design to prepare mine apostles to [aprune](#note4a) my vineyard for the last time, that I may bring to pass my [bstrange act](#note4b), that I may [cpour out](#note4c) my Spirit upon all flesh—

5 But behold, verily I say unto you, that there are many who have been ordained among you, whom I have called but few of them are [achosen](#note5a).

6 They who are not chosen have sinned a very grievous sin, in that they are [awalking](#note6a) in [bdarkness](#note6b) at noon-day.

7 And for this cause I gave unto you a commandment that you should call your [asolemn assembly](#note7a), that your [bfastings](#note7b) and your [cmourning](#note7c) might come up into the ears of the Lord of [dSabaoth](#note7d), which is by interpretation, the [ecreator](#note7e) of the first day, the beginning and the end.

8 Yea, verily I say unto you, I gave unto you a commandment that you should [abuild](#note8a) a house, in the which house I design to [bendow](#note8b) those whom I have [cchosen](#note8c) with power from on high;

9 For this is the [apromise](#note9a) of the Father unto you; therefore I command you to tarry, even as mine apostles at Jerusalem.

10 Nevertheless, my servants sinned a very grievous sin; and [acontentions](#note10a) arose in the [bschool](#note10b) of the prophets; which was very grievous unto me, saith your Lord; therefore I sent them forth to be chastened.

11 Verily I say unto you, it is my will that you should build a house. [aIf](#note11a) you keep my commandments you shall have power to build it.

12 If you [akeep](#note12a) not my commandments, the [blove](#note12b) of the Father shall not continue with you, therefore you shall [cwalk](#note12c) in darkness.

13 Now here is wisdom, and the [amind](#note13a) of the Lord—let the house be built, not after the manner of the world, for I give not unto you that ye shall live after the manner of the world;

14 Therefore, let it be built after the [amanner](#note14a) which I shall show unto three of you, whom ye shall appoint and ordain unto this power.

15 And the size thereof shall be fifty and five feet in width, and let it be sixty-five feet in length, in the inner court thereof.

16 And let the lower part of the inner court be dedicated unto me for your sacrament offering, and for your preaching, and your fasting, and your praying, and the [aoffering](#note16a) up of your most holy desires unto me, saith your Lord.

17 And let the higher part of the inner court be dedicated unto me for the [aschool](#note17a) of mine apostles, saith Son [bAhman](#note17b); or, in other words, Alphus; or, in other words, Omegus; even Jesus Christ your [cLord](#note17c). Amen.

Section 96

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, showing the order of the city or stake of Zion at Kirtland, Ohio, June 4, 1833, as an example to the Saints in Kirtland. The occasion was a conference of high priests, and the chief subject of consideration was the disposal of certain lands, known as the French farm, possessed by the Church near Kirtland. Since the conference could not agree who should take charge of the farm, all agreed to inquire of the Lord concerning the matter.

[1](96.xhtml#p1), The Kirtland Stake of Zion is to be made strong; [2–5](96.xhtml#p2), The bishop is to divide the inheritances for the Saints; [6–9](96.xhtml#p6), John Johnson is to be a member of the united order.

1 Behold, I say unto you, here is wisdom, whereby ye may know how to act concerning this matter, for it is expedient in me that this [astake](#note1a) that I have set for the strength of Zion should be made strong.

2 Therefore, let my servant [aNewel K. Whitney](#note2a) take charge of the place which is named among you, upon which I design to build mine [bholy house](#note2b).

3 And again, let it be divided into lots, according to wisdom, for the benefit of those who seek [ainheritances](#note3a), as it shall be determined in council among you.

4 Therefore, take heed that ye see to this matter, and that portion that is necessary to benefit mine [aorder](#note4a), for the purpose of bringing forth my word to the children of men.

5 For behold, verily I say unto you, this is the most expedient in me, that my word should go forth unto the children of men, for the purpose of subduing the hearts of the children of men for your good. Even so. Amen.

6 And again, verily I say unto you, it is wisdom and expedient in me, that my servant [aJohn Johnson](#note6a) whose offering I have accepted, and whose prayers I have heard, unto whom I give a promise of eternal life inasmuch as he keepeth my commandments from henceforth—

7 For he is a descendant of [aJoseph](#note7a) and a partaker of the blessings of the promise made unto his fathers—

8 Verily I say unto you, it is expedient in me that he should become a member of the [aorder](#note8a), that he may assist in bringing forth my word unto the children of men.

9 Therefore ye shall ordain him unto this blessing, and he shall seek diligently to take away [aincumbrances](#note9a) that are upon the house named among you, that he may dwell therein. Even so. Amen.

Section 97

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, August 2, 1833. This revelation deals particularly with the affairs of the Saints in Zion, Jackson County, Missouri, in response to the Prophet’s inquiry of the Lord for information. Members of the Church in Missouri were at this time subjected to severe persecution and, on July 23, 1833, had been forced to sign an agreement to leave Jackson County.

[1–2](97.xhtml#p1), Many of the Saints in Zion (Jackson County, Missouri) are blessed for their faithfulness; [3–5](97.xhtml#p3), Parley P. Pratt is commended for his labors in the school in Zion; [6–9](97.xhtml#p6), Those who observe their covenants are accepted by the Lord; [10–17](97.xhtml#p10), A house is to be built in Zion in which the pure in heart will see God; [18–21](97.xhtml#p18), Zion is the pure in heart; [22–28](97.xhtml#p22), Zion will escape the Lord’s scourge if she is faithful.

1 Verily I say unto you my friends, I speak unto you with my [avoice](#note1a), even the voice of my Spirit, that I may show unto you my will concerning your brethren in the land of [bZion](#note1b), many of whom are truly humble and are seeking diligently to learn wisdom and to find truth.

2 Verily, verily I say unto you, blessed are such, for they shall obtain; for I, the Lord, show mercy unto all the [ameek](#note2a), and upon all whomsoever I will, that I may be [bjustified](#note2b) when I shall bring them unto judgment.

3 Behold, I say unto you, concerning the school in Zion, I, the Lord, am well pleased that there should be a [aschool](#note3a) in Zion, and also with my servant [bParley P. Pratt](#note3b), for he abideth in me.

4 And inasmuch as he continueth to abide in me he shall continue to preside over the school in the land of Zion until I shall give unto him other commandments.

5 And I will bless him with a multiplicity of blessings, in expounding all scriptures and mysteries to the edification of the [aschool](#note5a), and of the church in Zion.

6 And to the residue of the school, I, the Lord, am willing to show mercy; nevertheless, there are those that must needs be [achastened](#note6a), and their works shall be made known.

7 The [aax](#note7a) is laid at the root of the trees; and every tree that bringeth not forth good fruit shall be hewn down and cast into the fire. I, the Lord, have spoken it.

8 Verily I say unto you, all among them who know their hearts are [ahonest](#note8a), and are broken, and their spirits contrite, and are [bwilling](#note8b) to observe their covenants by [csacrifice](#note8c)—yea, every sacrifice which I, the Lord, shall command—they are [daccepted](#note8d) of me.

9 For I, the Lord, will cause them to bring forth as a very fruitful [atree](#note9a) which is planted in a goodly land, by a pure stream, that yieldeth much precious fruit.

10 Verily I say unto you, that it is my will that a [ahouse](#note10a) should be built unto me in the land of Zion, like unto the [bpattern](#note10b) which I have given you.

11 Yea, let it be built speedily, by the tithing of my people.

12 Behold, this is the [atithing](#note12a) and the [bsacrifice](#note12b) which I, the Lord, require at their hands, that there may be a [chouse](#note12c) built unto me for the salvation of Zion—

13 For a place of [athanksgiving](#note13a) for all saints, and for a place of instruction for all those who are called to the work of the ministry in all their several callings and offices;

14 That they may be perfected in the [aunderstanding](#note14a) of their ministry, in theory, in principle, and in doctrine, in all things pertaining to the [bkingdom](#note14b) of God on the earth, the [ckeys](#note14c) of which kingdom have been [dconferred](#note14d) upon you.

15 And inasmuch as my people [abuild](#note15a) a [bhouse](#note15b) unto me in the [cname](#note15c) of the Lord, and do not suffer any [dunclean thing](#note15d) to come into it, that it be not defiled, my [eglory](#note15e) shall rest upon it;

16 Yea, and my [apresence](#note16a) shall be there, for I will come into it, and all the [bpure](#note16b) in heart that shall come into it shall see God.

17 But if it be defiled I will not come into it, and my glory shall not be there; for I will not come into [aunholy](#note17a) temples.

18 And, now, behold, if Zion do these things she shall [aprosper](#note18a), and spread herself and become very glorious, very great, and very terrible.

19 And the [anations](#note19a) of the earth shall honor her, and shall say: Surely [bZion](#note19b) is the city of our God, and surely Zion cannot fall, neither be moved out of her place, for God is there, and the hand of the Lord is there;

20 And he hath sworn by the power of his might to be her salvation and her high [atower](#note20a).

21 Therefore, verily, thus saith the Lord, let Zion rejoice, for this is [aZion](#note21a)—the pure in heart; therefore, let Zion rejoice, while all the wicked shall mourn.

22 For behold, and lo, [avengeance](#note22a) cometh speedily upon the ungodly as the whirlwind; and who shall escape it?

23 The Lord’s [ascourge](#note23a) shall pass over by night and by day, and the report thereof shall vex all people; yea, it shall not be stayed until the Lord come;

24 For the [aindignation](#note24a) of the Lord is kindled against their abominations and all their wicked works.

25 Nevertheless, Zion shall [aescape](#note25a) if she observe to do all things whatsoever I have commanded her.

26 But if she [aobserve](#note26a) not to do whatsoever I have commanded her, I will [bvisit](#note26b) her [caccording](#note26c) to all her works, with sore affliction, with [dpestilence](#note26d), with [eplague](#note26e), with sword, with [fvengeance](#note26f), with [gdevouring fire](#note26g).

27 Nevertheless, let it be read this once to her ears, that I, the Lord, have accepted of her offering; and if she sin no more [anone](#note27a) of these things shall come upon her;

28 And I will bless her with [ablessings](#note28a), and multiply a multiplicity of blessings upon her, and upon her generations forever and ever, saith the Lord your God. Amen.

Section 98

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, August 6, 1833. This revelation came in consequence of the persecution upon the Saints in Missouri. Increased settlement of Church members in Missouri troubled some other settlers, who felt threatened by the Saints’ numbers, political and economic influence, and cultural and religious differences. In July 1833, a mob destroyed Church property, tarred and feathered two Church members, and demanded that the Saints leave Jackson County. Although some news of the problems in Missouri had no doubt reached the Prophet in Kirtland (nine hundred miles away), the seriousness of the situation could have been known to him at this date only by revelation.

[1–3](98.xhtml#p1), The afflictions of the Saints will be for their good; [4–8](98.xhtml#p4), The Saints are to befriend the constitutional law of the land; [9–10](98.xhtml#p9), Honest, wise, and good men should be supported for secular government; [11–15](98.xhtml#p11), Those who lay down their lives in the Lord’s cause will have eternal life; [16–18](98.xhtml#p16), Renounce war and proclaim peace; [19–22](98.xhtml#p19), The Saints in Kirtland are reproved and commanded to repent; [23–32](98.xhtml#p23), The Lord reveals His laws governing the persecutions and afflictions imposed on His people; [33–38](98.xhtml#p33), War is justified only when the Lord commands it; [39–48](98.xhtml#p39), The Saints are to forgive their enemies, who, if they repent, will also escape the Lord’s vengeance.

1 Verily I say unto you my friends, [afear not](#note1a), let your hearts be comforted; yea, rejoice evermore, and in everything give [bthanks](#note1b);

2 [aWaiting](#note2a) patiently on the Lord, for your prayers have entered into the ears of the Lord of Sabaoth, and are recorded with this seal and testament—the Lord hath sworn and decreed that they shall be granted.

3 Therefore, he giveth this promise unto you, with an immutable covenant that they shall be fulfilled; and all things wherewith you have been [aafflicted](#note3a) shall work together for your [bgood](#note3b), and to my name’s glory, saith the Lord.

4 And now, verily I say unto you concerning the [alaws](#note4a) of the land, it is my will that my people should observe to do all things whatsoever I command them.

5 And that [alaw](#note5a) of the land which is [bconstitutional](#note5b), supporting that principle of freedom in maintaining rights and privileges, belongs to all mankind, and is justifiable before me.

6 Therefore, I, the Lord, justify you, and your brethren of my church, in befriending that law which is the [aconstitutional](#note6a) law of the land;

7 And as pertaining to law of man, whatsoever is more or less than this, cometh of evil.

8 I, the Lord God, make you [afree](#note8a), therefore ye are free indeed; and the law also maketh you free.

9 Nevertheless, when the [awicked](#note9a) [brule](#note9b) the people mourn.

10 Wherefore, [ahonest](#note10a) men and wise men should be sought for diligently, and good men and wise men ye should observe to uphold; otherwise whatsoever is less than these cometh of evil.

11 And I give unto you a commandment, that ye shall forsake all evil and cleave unto all [agood](#note11a), that ye shall live by every [bword](#note11b) which proceedeth forth out of the mouth of God.

12 For he will [agive](#note12a) unto the faithful line upon line, precept upon precept; and I will [btry](#note12b) you and prove you herewith.

13 And whoso [alayeth](#note13a) down his life in my cause, for my name’s sake, shall find it again, even life eternal.

14 Therefore, be not [aafraid](#note14a) of your enemies, for I have decreed in my heart, saith the Lord, that I will [bprove](#note14b) you in all things, whether you will abide in my covenant, [ceven](#note14c) unto death, that you may be found worthy.

15 For if ye will not abide in my covenant ye are not worthy of me.

16 Therefore, [arenounce](#note16a) war and proclaim peace, and seek diligently to [bturn](#note16b) the hearts of the children to their fathers, and the hearts of the fathers to the children;

17 And again, the hearts of the [aJews](#note17a) unto the prophets, and the prophets unto the Jews; lest I come and smite the whole earth with a curse, and all flesh be consumed before me.

18 Let not your hearts be troubled; for in my Father’s house are [amany mansions](#note18a), and I have prepared a place for you; and where my Father and I am, there ye shall be also.

19 Behold, I, the Lord, am not well [apleased](#note19a) with many who are in the church at Kirtland;

20 For they do not [aforsake](#note20a) their sins, and their wicked ways, the pride of their hearts, and their covetousness, and all their detestable things, and observe the words of wisdom and eternal life which I have given unto them.

21 Verily I say unto you, that I, the Lord, will [achasten](#note21a) them and will do whatsoever I list, if they do not repent and observe all things whatsoever I have said unto them.

22 And again I say unto you, if ye observe to [ado](#note22a) whatsoever I command you, I, the Lord, will turn away all [bwrath](#note22b) and indignation from you, and the [cgates](#note22c) of hell shall not prevail against you.

23 Now, I speak unto you concerning your families—if men will [asmite](#note23a) you, or your families, once, and ye [bbear](#note23b) it patiently and [crevile](#note23c) not against them, neither seek [drevenge](#note23d), ye shall be [erewarded](#note23e);

24 But if ye bear it not patiently, it shall be accounted unto you as being [ameted](#note24a) out as a just measure unto you.

25 And again, if your enemy shall smite you the second time, and you revile not against your enemy, and bear it patiently, your reward shall be an [ahundred-fold](#note25a).

26 And again, if he shall smite you the third time, and ye bear it [apatiently](#note26a), your reward shall be doubled unto you four-fold;

27 And these three [atestimonies](#note27a) shall stand against your enemy if he repent not, and shall not be blotted out.

28 And now, verily I say unto you, if that enemy shall escape my vengeance, that he be not brought into judgment before me, then ye shall see to it that ye [awarn](#note28a) him in my name, that he come no more upon you, neither upon your family, even your children’s children unto the third and fourth generation.

29 And then, if he shall come upon you or your children, or your children’s children unto the third and fourth generation, I have delivered thine [aenemy](#note29a) into thine hands;

30 And then if thou wilt spare him, thou shalt be rewarded for thy [arighteousness](#note30a); and also thy children and thy children’s children unto the third and fourth generation.

31 Nevertheless, thine enemy is in thine hands; and if thou rewardest him according to his works thou art justified; if he has sought thy life, and thy life is endangered by him, thine enemy is in thine hands and thou art justified.

32 Behold, this is the law I gave unto my servant Nephi, and thy [afathers](#note32a), Joseph, and Jacob, and Isaac, and Abraham, and all mine ancient prophets and apostles.

33 And again, this is the [alaw](#note33a) that I gave unto mine ancients, that they should not go out unto battle against any nation, kindred, tongue, or people, save I, the Lord, commanded them.

34 And if any nation, tongue, or people should proclaim war against them, they should first lift a standard of [apeace](#note34a) unto that people, nation, or tongue;

35 And if that people did not accept the offering of peace, neither the second nor the third time, they should bring these testimonies before the Lord;

36 Then I, the Lord, would give unto them a commandment, and justify them in going out to battle against that nation, tongue, or people.

37 And I, the Lord, would [afight](#note37a) their battles, and their children’s battles, and their children’s children’s, until they had avenged themselves on all their enemies, to the third and fourth generation.

38 Behold, this is an [aensample](#note38a) unto all people, saith the Lord your God, for justification before me.

39 And again, verily I say unto you, if after thine [aenemy](#note39a) has come upon thee the first time, he repent and come unto thee praying thy forgiveness, thou shalt forgive him, and shalt hold it no more as a testimony against thine enemy—

40 And so on unto the second and third time; and as oft as thine enemy repenteth of the trespass wherewith he has trespassed against thee, thou shalt [aforgive](#note40a) him, until seventy times seven.

41 And if he trespass against thee and repent not the first time, nevertheless thou shalt forgive him.

42 And if he trespass against thee the second time, and repent not, nevertheless thou shalt forgive him.

43 And if he trespass against thee the third time, and repent not, thou shalt also forgive him.

44 But if he trespass against thee the fourth time thou shalt not forgive him, but shalt bring these testimonies before the Lord; and they shall not be blotted out until he repent and [areward](#note44a) thee four-fold in all things wherewith he has trespassed against thee.

45 And if he do this, thou shalt forgive him with all thine heart; and if he do not this, I, the Lord, will [aavenge](#note45a) thee of thine enemy an hundred-fold;

46 And upon his children, and upon his children’s [achildren](#note46a) of all them that [bhate](#note46b) me, unto the [cthird](#note46c) and fourth generation.

47 But if the [achildren](#note47a) shall repent, or the children’s children, and [bturn](#note47b) to the Lord their God, with all their hearts and with all their might, mind, and strength, and [crestore](#note47c) four-fold for all their trespasses wherewith they have trespassed, or wherewith their fathers have trespassed, or their fathers’ fathers, then thine indignation shall be turned away;

48 And vengeance shall [ano more](#note48a) come upon them, saith the Lord thy God, and their trespasses shall never be brought any more as a testimony before the Lord against them. Amen.
