  John 14:2; Ether 12:32 (32–37); [D&C 59:2](59.xhtml#p2); [76:111](76.xhtml#p111); [81:6](81.xhtml#p6); [98:18](98.xhtml#p18).

Section 107

Revelation on the priesthood, given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, about April 1835. Although this section was recorded in 1835, the historical records affirm that most of [verses 60 through 100](107.xhtml#p60) incorporate a revelation given through Joseph Smith on November 11, 1831. This section was associated with the organization of the Quorum of the Twelve in February and March 1835. The Prophet likely delivered it in the presence of those who were preparing to depart May 3, 1835, on their first quorum mission.

[1–6](107.xhtml#p1), There are two priesthoods: the Melchizedek and the Aaronic; [7–12](107.xhtml#p7), Those who hold the Melchizedek Priesthood have power to officiate in all offices in the Church; [13–17](107.xhtml#p13), The bishopric presides over the Aaronic Priesthood, which administers in outward ordinances; [18–20](107.xhtml#p18), The Melchizedek Priesthood holds the keys of all spiritual blessings; the Aaronic Priesthood holds the keys of the ministering of angels; [21–38](107.xhtml#p21), The First Presidency, the Twelve, and the Seventy constitute the presiding quorums, whose decisions are to be made in unity and righteousness; [39–52](107.xhtml#p39), The patriarchal order is established from Adam to Noah; [53–57](107.xhtml#p53), Ancient Saints assembled at Adam-ondi-Ahman, and the Lord appeared to them; [58–67](107.xhtml#p58), The Twelve are to set the officers of the Church in order; [68–76](107.xhtml#p68), Bishops serve as common judges in Israel; [77–84](107.xhtml#p77), The First Presidency and the Twelve constitute the highest court in the Church; [85–100](107.xhtml#p85), Priesthood presidents govern their respective quorums.

1 There are, in the church, two [apriesthoods](#note1a), namely, the Melchizedek and [bAaronic](#note1b), including the Levitical Priesthood.

2 Why the first is called the [aMelchizedek Priesthood](#note2a) is because [bMelchizedek](#note2b) was such a great high priest.

3 Before his day it was called *the Holy [aPriesthood](#note3a), after the [bOrder](#note3b) of the Son of God.*

4 But out of [arespect](#note4a) or [breverence](#note4b) to the name of the Supreme Being, to avoid the too frequent repetition of his name, they, the church, in ancient days, called that priesthood after Melchizedek, or the Melchizedek Priesthood.

5 All other authorities or offices in the church are [aappendages](#note5a) to this priesthood.

6 But there are two divisions or grand heads—one is the Melchizedek Priesthood, and the other is the Aaronic or [aLevitical Priesthood](#note6a).

7 The office of an [aelder](#note7a) comes under the priesthood of Melchizedek.

8 The [aMelchizedek Priesthood](#note8a) holds the right of presidency, and has power and [bauthority](#note8b) over all the offices in the church in all ages of the world, to administer in spiritual things.

9 The [aPresidency](#note9a) of the High Priesthood, after the order of Melchizedek, have a right to officiate in all the offices in the church.

10 [aHigh priests](#note10a) after the order of the Melchizedek Priesthood have a [bright](#note10b) to officiate in their own [cstanding](#note10c), under the direction of the presidency, in administering spiritual things, and also in the office of an elder, [dpriest](#note10d) (of the Levitical order), teacher, deacon, and member.

11 An elder has a right to officiate in his stead when the high priest is not present.

12 The high priest and [aelder](#note12a) are to administer in spiritual things, agreeable to the covenants and commandments of the church; and they have a right to officiate in all these offices of the church when there are no higher authorities present.

13 The second priesthood is called the [aPriesthood of Aaron](#note13a), because it was conferred upon Aaron and his seed, throughout all their generations.

14 Why it is called the lesser priesthood is because it is an [aappendage](#note14a) to the greater, or the Melchizedek Priesthood, and has power in administering outward ordinances.

15 The [abishopric](#note15a) is the presidency of this priesthood, and holds the [bkeys](#note15b) or authority of the same.

16 No man has a legal right to this office, to hold the keys of this priesthood, except he be a [aliteral descendant](#note16a) of [bAaron](#note16b).

17 But as a high priest of the [aMelchizedek Priesthood](#note17a) has authority to officiate in all the lesser offices, he may officiate in the office of [bbishop](#note17b) when no literal descendant of Aaron can be found, provided he is called and [cset apart](#note17c) and ordained unto this power by the hands of the [dPresidency](#note17d) of the Melchizedek Priesthood.

18 The power and authority of the higher, or Melchizedek Priesthood, is to hold the [akeys](#note18a) of all the spiritual blessings of the church—

19 To have the privilege of receiving the [amysteries](#note19a) of the kingdom of heaven, to have the [bheavens](#note19b) opened unto them, to commune with the [cgeneral](#note19c) assembly and church of the [dFirstborn](#note19d), and to enjoy the communion and [epresence](#note19e) of God the Father, and Jesus the [fmediator](#note19f) of the new covenant.

20 The [apower](#note20a) and authority of the lesser, or [bAaronic Priesthood](#note20b), is to hold the [ckeys](#note20c) of the ministering of angels, and to [dadminister](#note20d) in outward [eordinances](#note20e), the letter of the gospel, the baptism of repentance for the [fremission](#note20f) of sins, agreeable to the covenants and commandments.

21 Of necessity there are presidents, or presiding [aofficers](#note21a) growing out of, or appointed of or from among those who are ordained to the several offices in these two priesthoods.

22 Of the [aMelchizedek Priesthood](#note22a), three [bPresiding](#note22b) High Priests, chosen by the body, appointed and ordained to that office, and [cupheld](#note22c) by the confidence, faith, and prayer of the church, form a quorum of the Presidency of the Church.

23 The [atwelve](#note23a) traveling councilors are called to be the Twelve [bApostles](#note23b), or special [cwitnesses](#note23c) of the name of Christ in all the world—thus differing from other officers in the church in the duties of their calling.

24 And they form a quorum, [aequal](#note24a) in authority and power to the three presidents previously mentioned.

25 The [aSeventy](#note25a) are also called to [bpreach](#note25b) the gospel, and to be especial witnesses unto the Gentiles and in all the world—thus differing from other officers in the church in the duties of their calling.

26 And they form a quorum, equal in [aauthority](#note26a) to that of the Twelve special witnesses or Apostles just named.

27 And every decision made by either of these quorums must be by the [aunanimous](#note27a) voice of the same; that is, every member in each quorum must be agreed to its decisions, in order to make their decisions of the same power or validity one with the other—

28 A majority may form a quorum when circumstances render it impossible to be otherwise—

29 Unless this is the case, their decisions are not entitled to the same blessings which the decisions of a quorum of three presidents were anciently, who were ordained after the order of Melchizedek, and were [arighteous](#note29a) and holy men.

30 The decisions of these quorums, or either of them, are to be made in all [arighteousness](#note30a), in holiness, and lowliness of heart, meekness and [blong-suffering](#note30b), and in [cfaith](#note30c), and [dvirtue](#note30d), and knowledge, temperance, patience, godliness, brotherly kindness and charity;

31 Because the promise is, if these things abound in them they shall not be [aunfruitful](#note31a) in the knowledge of the Lord.

32 And in case that any decision of these quorums is made in unrighteousness, it may be brought before a general assembly of the several quorums, which constitute the spiritual authorities of the church; otherwise there can be no [aappeal](#note32a) from their decision.

33 The [aTwelve](#note33a) are a [bTraveling](#note33b) Presiding High Council, to officiate in the name of the Lord, under the direction of the Presidency of the Church, agreeable to the institution of heaven; to build up the church, and regulate all the affairs of the same in all nations, first unto the [cGentiles](#note33c) and secondly unto the Jews.

34 The [aSeventy](#note34a) are to act in the name of the Lord, under the direction of the [bTwelve](#note34b) or the traveling high council, in building up the church and regulating all the affairs of the same in all nations, first unto the Gentiles and then to the Jews—

35 The Twelve being [asent](#note35a) out, holding the keys, to open the door by the proclamation of the gospel of Jesus Christ, and first unto the Gentiles and then unto the Jews.

36 The standing [ahigh councils](#note36a), at the stakes of Zion, form a quorum equal in authority in the affairs of the church, in all their decisions, to the quorum of the presidency, or to the traveling high council.

37 The [ahigh council](#note37a) in Zion form a quorum equal in authority in the affairs of the church, in all their decisions, to the councils of the Twelve at the stakes of Zion.

38 It is the duty of the traveling high council to call upon the [aSeventy](#note38a), when they need assistance, to fill the several calls for preaching and administering the gospel, instead of any others.

39 It is the duty of the [aTwelve](#note39a), in all large branches of the church, to ordain [bevangelical](#note39b) ministers, as they shall be designated unto them by revelation—

40 The order of this priesthood was confirmed to be handed down from father to son, and rightly belongs to the literal descendants of the chosen seed, to whom the promises were made.

41 This [aorder](#note41a) was instituted in the days of [bAdam](#note41b), and came down by [clineage](#note41c) in the following manner:

42 From Adam to [aSeth](#note42a), who was [bordained](#note42b) by Adam at the age of sixty-nine years, and was blessed by him three years previous to his (Adam’s) death, and received the promise of God by his father, that his posterity should be the chosen of the Lord, and that they should be [cpreserved](#note42c) unto the end of the earth;

43 Because he (Seth) was a [aperfect](#note43a) man, and his [blikeness](#note43b) was the express likeness of his father, insomuch that he seemed to be like unto his father in all things, and could be distinguished from him only by his age.

44 Enos was ordained at the age of one hundred and thirty-four years and four months, by the hand of Adam.

45 God called upon Cainan in the wilderness in the fortieth year of his age; and he met Adam in journeying to the place Shedolamak. He was eighty-seven years old when he received his ordination.

46 Mahalaleel was four hundred and ninety-six years and seven days old when he was ordained by the hand of Adam, who also blessed him.

47 Jared was two hundred years old when he was ordained under the hand of Adam, who also blessed him.

48 [aEnoch](#note48a) was twenty-five years old when he was ordained under the hand of Adam; and he was sixty-five and Adam blessed him.

49 And he [asaw](#note49a) the Lord, and he walked with him, and was before his face continually; and he [bwalked](#note49b) with God three hundred and sixty-five years, making him four hundred and thirty years old when he was translated.

50 Methuselah was one hundred years old when he was ordained under the hand of Adam.

51 Lamech was thirty-two years old when he was ordained under the hand of Seth.

52 Noah was ten years old when he was [aordained](#note52a) under the hand of Methuselah.

53 Three years previous to the death of Adam, he called Seth, Enos, Cainan, Mahalaleel, Jared, Enoch, and Methuselah, who were all [ahigh priests](#note53a), with the residue of his posterity who were righteous, into the valley of [bAdam-ondi-Ahman](#note53b), and there bestowed upon them his last blessing.

54 And the Lord appeared unto them, and they rose up and blessed [aAdam](#note54a), and called him Michael, the prince, the archangel.

55 And the Lord administered comfort unto Adam, and said unto him: I have set thee to be at the head; a multitude of nations shall come of thee, and thou art a [aprince](#note55a) over them forever.

56 And Adam stood up in the midst of the congregation; and, notwithstanding he was bowed down with age, being full of the Holy Ghost, [apredicted](#note56a) whatsoever should befall his posterity unto the latest generation.

57 These things were all written in the book of [aEnoch](#note57a), and are to be testified of in due time.

58 It is the duty of the [aTwelve](#note58a), also, to [bordain](#note58b) and set in order all the other officers of the church, agreeable to the revelation which says:

59 To the church of Christ in the land of Zion, in addition to the church [alaws](#note59a) respecting church business—

60 Verily, I say unto you, saith the Lord of Hosts, there must needs be [apresiding](#note60a) elders to preside over those who are of the office of an elder;

61 And also [apriests](#note61a) to preside over those who are of the office of a priest;

62 And also teachers to preside over those who are of the office of a teacher, in like manner, and also the [adeacons](#note62a)—

63 Wherefore, from deacon to teacher, and from teacher to priest, and from priest to elder, severally as they are appointed, according to the covenants and commandments of the church.

64 Then comes the High Priesthood, which is the greatest of all.

65 Wherefore, it must needs be that one be appointed of the [aHigh Priesthood](#note65a) to preside over the priesthood, and he shall be called President of the High Priesthood of the Church;

66 Or, in other words, the [aPresiding](#note66a) High Priest over the High Priesthood of the Church.

67 From the same comes the administering of ordinances and blessings upon the church, by the [alaying](#note67a) on of the hands.

68 Wherefore, the office of a bishop is not equal unto it; for the office of a [abishop](#note68a) is in administering all [btemporal](#note68b) things;

69 Nevertheless a [abishop](#note69a) must be chosen from the [bHigh Priesthood](#note69b), unless he is a [cliteral descendant](#note69c) of Aaron;

70 For unless he is a [aliteral descendant](#note70a) of Aaron he cannot hold the keys of that priesthood.

71 Nevertheless, a high priest, that is, after the order of Melchizedek, may be set apart unto the ministering of temporal things, having a [aknowledge](#note71a) of them by the Spirit of truth;

72 And also to be a [ajudge](#note72a) in Israel, to do the business of the church, to sit in [bjudgment](#note72b) upon transgressors upon testimony as it shall be laid before him according to the laws, by the assistance of his [ccounselors](#note72c), whom he has chosen or will choose among the elders of the church.

73 This is the duty of a bishop who is not a literal descendant of Aaron, but has been ordained to the High Priesthood after the order of Melchizedek.

74 Thus shall he be a [ajudge](#note74a), even a common judge among the inhabitants of Zion, or in a stake of Zion, or in any branch of the church where he shall be [bset apart](#note74b) unto this ministry, until the borders of Zion are enlarged and it becomes necessary to have other bishops or judges in Zion or elsewhere.

75 And inasmuch as there are other bishops appointed they shall act in the same office.

76 But a literal descendant of Aaron has a legal right to the presidency of this priesthood, to the [akeys](#note76a) of this ministry, to act in the office of bishop independently, without counselors, except in a case where a President of the High Priesthood, after the order of Melchizedek, is tried, to sit as a judge in Israel.

77 And the decision of either of these councils, agreeable to the commandment which says:

78 Again, verily, I say unto you, the most important business of the church, and the most [adifficult](#note78a) cases of the church, inasmuch as there is not satisfaction upon the decision of the bishop or judges, it shall be handed over and carried up unto the council of the church, before the [bPresidency](#note78b) of the High Priesthood.

79 And the Presidency of the council of the High Priesthood shall have power to call other high priests, even twelve, to assist as counselors; and thus the Presidency of the High Priesthood and its counselors shall have power to decide upon testimony according to the laws of the church.

80 And after this decision it shall be had in remembrance no more before the Lord; for this is the highest council of the church of God, and a final decision upon controversies in spiritual matters.

81 There is not any person belonging to the church who is exempt from this council of the church.

82 And inasmuch as a President of the High Priesthood shall transgress, he shall be had in remembrance before the [acommon](#note82a) council of the church, who shall be assisted by twelve counselors of the High Priesthood;

83 And their decision upon his head shall be an end of controversy concerning him.

84 Thus, none shall be exempted from the [ajustice](#note84a) and the [blaws](#note84b) of God, that all things may be done in [corder](#note84c) and in solemnity before him, according to truth and righteousness.

85 And again, verily I say unto you, the duty of a president over the office of a [adeacon](#note85a) is to preside over twelve deacons, to sit in council with them, and to [bteach](#note85b) them their duty, [cedifying](#note85c) one another, as it is given according to the covenants.

86 And also the duty of the president over the office of the [ateachers](#note86a) is to preside over twenty-four of the teachers, and to sit in council with them, teaching them the duties of their office, as given in the covenants.

87 Also the duty of the president over the Priesthood of Aaron is to preside over forty-eight [apriests](#note87a), and sit in council with them, to teach them the duties of their office, as is given in the covenants—

88 This president is to be a [abishop](#note88a); for this is one of the duties of this priesthood.

89 Again, the duty of the president over the office of [aelders](#note89a) is to preside over ninety-six elders, and to sit in council with them, and to teach them according to the covenants.

90 This presidency is a distinct one from that of the seventy, and is designed for those who do not [atravel](#note90a) into all the world.

91 And again, the duty of the President of the office of the High Priesthood is to [apreside](#note91a) over the whole church, and to be like unto [bMoses](#note91b)—

92 Behold, here is wisdom; yea, to be a [aseer](#note92a), a [brevelator](#note92b), a translator, and a [cprophet](#note92c), having all the [dgifts](#note92d) of God which he bestows upon the head of the church.

93 And it is according to the vision showing the order of the [aSeventy](#note93a), that they should have seven presidents to preside over them, chosen out of the number of the seventy;

94 And the seventh president of these presidents is to preside over the six;

95 And these seven presidents are to choose other seventy besides the first seventy to whom they belong, and are to preside over them;

96 And also other seventy, until seven times seventy, if the labor in the vineyard of necessity requires it.

97 And these [aseventy](#note97a) are to be [btraveling](#note97b) ministers, unto the Gentiles first and also unto the Jews.

98 Whereas other officers of the church, who belong not unto the Twelve, neither to the Seventy, are not under the responsibility to travel among all nations, but are to travel as their circumstances shall allow, notwithstanding they may hold as high and responsible offices in the church.

99 Wherefore, now let every man learn his [aduty](#note99a), and to act in the office in which he is appointed, in all [bdiligence](#note99b).

100 He that is [aslothful](#note100a) shall not be counted [bworthy](#note100b) to stand, and he that learns not his duty and shows himself not approved shall not be counted worthy to stand. Even so. Amen.

Section 108

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Kirtland, Ohio, December 26, 1835. This section was received at the request of Lyman Sherman, who had previously been ordained a seventy and who had come to the Prophet with a request for a revelation to make known his duty.

[1–3](108.xhtml#p1), Lyman Sherman forgiven of his sins; [4–5](108.xhtml#p4), He is to be numbered with the leading elders of the Church; [6–8](108.xhtml#p6), He is called to preach the gospel and strengthen his brethren.

1 Verily thus saith the Lord unto you, my servant Lyman: Your sins are forgiven you, because you have obeyed my [avoice](#note1a) in coming up hither this morning to receive counsel of him whom I have appointed.

2 Therefore, let your soul be at [arest](#note2a) concerning your spiritual standing, and resist no more my voice.

3 And arise up and be more careful henceforth in observing your [avows](#note3a), which you have made and do make, and you shall be blessed with exceeding great blessings.

4 Wait patiently until the [asolemn assembly](#note4a) shall be called of my servants, then you shall be remembered with the [bfirst](#note4b) of mine elders, and receive right by ordination with the rest of mine elders whom I have chosen.

5 Behold, this is the [apromise](#note5a) of the Father unto you if you continue faithful.

6 And it shall be fulfilled upon you in that day that you shall have right to [apreach](#note6a) my gospel wheresoever I shall send you, from henceforth from that time.

7 Therefore, [astrengthen](#note7a) your brethren in all your conversation, in all your prayers, in all your exhortations, and in all your doings.

8 And behold, and lo, I am with you to bless you and [adeliver](#note8a) you forever. Amen.

* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1a)

  1a

* [◀](#ldsBACK_note2a)

  2a

  TG Peace of God.
* [◀](#ldsBACK_note3a)

  3a

  TG Integrity; Vow.

Section 109

Prayer offered at the dedication of the temple at Kirtland, Ohio, March 27, 1836. According to the Prophet’s written statement, this prayer was given to him by revelation.

[1–5](109.xhtml#p1), The Kirtland Temple was built as a place for the Son of Man to visit; [6–21](109.xhtml#p6), It is to be a house of prayer, fasting, faith, learning, glory, and order, and a house of God; [22–33](109.xhtml#p22), May the unrepentant who oppose the Lord’s people be confounded; [34–42](109.xhtml#p34), May the Saints go forth in power to gather the righteous to Zion; [43–53](109.xhtml#p43), May the Saints be delivered from the terrible things to be poured out upon the wicked in the last days; [54–58](109.xhtml#p54), May nations and peoples and churches be prepared for the gospel; [59–67](109.xhtml#p59), May the Jews, the Lamanites, and all Israel be redeemed; [68–80](109.xhtml#p68), May the Saints be crowned with glory and honor and gain eternal salvation.

1 [aThanks](#note1a) be to thy name, O Lord God of Israel, who keepest [bcovenant](#note1b) and showest mercy unto thy servants who walk uprightly before thee, with all their hearts—

2 Thou who hast commanded thy servants to [abuild](#note2a) a house to thy name in this place [Kirtland].

3 And now thou beholdest, O Lord, that thy servants have done according to thy commandment.

4 And now we ask thee, Holy Father, in the name of Jesus Christ, the Son of thy bosom, in whose name alone salvation can be administered to the children of men, we ask thee, O Lord, to accept of this [ahouse](#note4a), the [bworkmanship](#note4b) of the hands of us, thy servants, which thou didst command us to build.

5 For thou knowest that we have done this work through great tribulation; and out of our poverty we have [agiven](#note5a) of our substance to build a [bhouse](#note5b) to thy name, that the Son of Man might have a place to [cmanifest](#note5c) himself to his people.

6 And as thou hast said in a [arevelation](#note6a), given to us, calling us thy friends, saying—Call your solemn assembly, as I have commanded you;

7 And as all have not faith, seek ye diligently and teach one another words of wisdom; yea, seek ye out of the best [abooks](#note7a) words of wisdom, seek learning even by study and also by faith;

8 Organize yourselves; [aprepare](#note8a) every needful thing, and establish a house, even a [bhouse](#note8b) of prayer, a house of fasting, a house of faith, a house of learning, a house of glory, a house of [corder](#note8c), a [dhouse](#note8d) of God;

9 That your [aincomings](#note9a) may be in the name of the Lord, that your outgoings may be in the name of the Lord, that all your salutations may be in the name of the Lord, with uplifted hands unto the Most High—

10 And now, Holy Father, we ask thee to assist us, thy people, with thy grace, in calling our [asolemn assembly](#note10a), that it may be done to thine honor and to thy divine acceptance;

11 And in a manner that we may be found worthy, in thy sight, to secure a fulfilment of the [apromises](#note11a) which thou hast made unto us, thy people, in the revelations given unto us;

12 That thy [aglory](#note12a) may rest down upon thy people, and upon this thy house, which we now dedicate to thee, that it may be sanctified and consecrated to be holy, and that thy holy presence may be continually in this house;

13 And that all people who shall enter upon the threshold of the Lord’s house may feel thy power, and feel constrained to acknowledge that thou hast sanctified it, and that it is thy house, a [aplace](#note13a) of thy holiness.

14 And do thou grant, Holy Father, that all those who shall worship in this house may be taught words of wisdom out of the best [abooks](#note14a), and that they may seek learning even by study, and also by faith, as thou hast said;

15 And that they may grow up in thee, and receive a fulness of the Holy Ghost, and be organized according to thy laws, and be prepared to obtain every needful thing;

16 And that this house may be a house of prayer, a house of fasting, a house of faith, a house of glory and of God, even thy house;

17 That all the incomings of thy people, into this house, may be in the name of the Lord;

18 That all their outgoings from this house may be in the name of the Lord;

19 And that all their salutations may be in the name of the Lord, with holy hands, uplifted to the Most High;

20 And that no [aunclean thing](#note20a) shall be permitted to come into thy house to [bpollute](#note20b) it;

21 And when thy people [atransgress](#note21a), any of them, they may speedily repent and return unto thee, and find favor in thy sight, and be restored to the blessings which thou hast ordained to be poured out upon those who shall [breverence](#note21b) thee in thy house.

22 And we ask thee, Holy Father, that thy servants may go forth from this house armed with thy power, and that thy [aname](#note22a) may be upon them, and thy glory be round about them, and thine [bangels](#note22b) have charge over them;

23 And from this place they may bear exceedingly great and glorious tidings, in truth, unto the [aends](#note23a) of the earth, that they may know that this is thy work, and that thou hast put forth thy hand, to fulfil that which thou hast spoken by the mouths of the prophets, concerning the last days.

24 We ask thee, Holy Father, to establish the people that shall worship, and honorably hold a name and standing in this thy house, to all generations and for eternity;

25 That no weapon [aformed](#note25a) against them shall prosper; that he who diggeth a [bpit](#note25b) for them shall fall into the same himself;

26 That no combination of wickedness shall have power to rise up and [aprevail](#note26a) over thy people upon whom thy [bname](#note26b) shall be put in this house;

27 And if any people shall rise against this people, that thine anger be kindled against them;

28 And if they shall smite this people thou wilt smite them; thou wilt [afight](#note28a) for thy people as thou didst in the day of battle, that they may be delivered from the hands of all their enemies.

29 We ask thee, Holy Father, to confound, and astonish, and to bring to [ashame](#note29a) and confusion, all those who have spread [blying](#note29b) reports abroad, over the world, against thy servant or servants, if they will not repent, when the everlasting gospel shall be proclaimed in their ears;

30 And that all their works may be brought to naught, and be swept away by the [ahail](#note30a), and by the judgments which thou wilt send upon them in thine anger, that there may be an end to [blyings](#note30b) and slanders against thy people.

31 For thou knowest, O Lord, that thy servants have been innocent before thee in [abearing](#note31a) record of thy name, for which they have suffered these things.

32 Therefore we plead before thee for a full and complete [adeliverance](#note32a) from under this [byoke](#note32b);

33 Break it off, O Lord; break it off from the necks of thy servants, by thy power, that we may rise up in the midst of this generation and do thy work.

34 O Jehovah, have mercy upon this people, and as all men [asin](#note34a), forgive the transgressions of thy people, and let them be blotted out forever.

35 Let the [aanointing](#note35a) of thy ministers be sealed upon them with power from on high.

36 Let it be fulfilled upon them, as upon those on the day of Pentecost; let the gift of [atongues](#note36a) be poured out upon thy people, even [bcloven](#note36b) tongues as of fire, and the interpretation thereof.

37 And let thy house be filled, as with a rushing mighty [awind](#note37a), with thy [bglory](#note37b).

38 Put upon thy servants the [atestimony](#note38a) of the covenant, that when they go out and proclaim thy word they may [bseal](#note38b) up the law, and prepare the hearts of thy saints for all those judgments thou art about to send, in thy wrath, upon the inhabitants of the [cearth](#note38c), because of their transgressions, that thy people may not faint in the day of trouble.

39 And whatsoever city thy servants shall enter, and the people of that city [areceive](#note39a) their testimony, let thy peace and thy salvation be upon that city; that they may gather out of that city the righteous, that they may come forth to [bZion](#note39b), or to her stakes, the places of thine appointment, with songs of everlasting joy;

40 And until this be accomplished, let not thy judgments fall upon that city.

41 And whatsoever city thy servants shall enter, and the people of that city receive not the testimony of thy servants, and thy servants warn them to save themselves from this untoward generation, let it be upon that city according to that which thou hast spoken by the mouths of thy prophets.

42 But deliver thou, O Jehovah, we beseech thee, thy servants from their hands, and [acleanse](#note42a) them from their blood.

43 O Lord, we delight not in the destruction of our fellow men; their [asouls](#note43a) are precious before thee;

44 But thy word must be fulfilled. Help thy servants to say, with thy [agrace](#note44a) assisting them: Thy will be done, O Lord, and not ours.

45 We know that thou hast spoken by the mouth of thy prophets terrible things concerning the [awicked](#note45a), in the last days—that thou wilt pour out thy judgments, without measure;

46 Therefore, O Lord, deliver thy people from the calamity of the wicked; enable thy servants to seal up the law, and [abind](#note46a) up the testimony, that they may be prepared against the day of burning.

47 We ask thee, Holy Father, to remember those who have been [adriven](#note47a) by the inhabitants of Jackson county, Missouri, from the lands of their inheritance, and break off, O Lord, this [byoke](#note47b) of affliction that has been put upon them.

48 Thou knowest, O Lord, that they have been greatly [aoppressed](#note48a) and afflicted by wicked men; and our [bhearts](#note48b) flow out with sorrow because of their grievous [cburdens](#note48c).

49 O Lord, [ahow](#note49a) long wilt thou suffer this people to bear this affliction, and the [bcries](#note49b) of their innocent ones to ascend up in thine ears, and their [cblood](#note49c) come up in testimony before thee, and not make a display of thy testimony in their behalf?

50 Have [amercy](#note50a), O Lord, upon the wicked mob, who have driven thy people, that they may cease to spoil, that they may repent of their sins if repentance is to be found;

51 But if they will not, make bare thine arm, O Lord, and [aredeem](#note51a) that which thou didst appoint a Zion unto thy people.

52 And if it cannot be otherwise, that the cause of thy people may not fail before thee may thine anger be kindled, and thine [aindignation](#note52a) fall upon them, that they may be wasted away, both root and branch, from under heaven;

53 But inasmuch as they will repent, thou art [agracious](#note53a) and merciful, and wilt turn away thy wrath when thou lookest upon the face of thine Anointed.

54 Have mercy, O Lord, upon all the [anations](#note54a) of the earth; have mercy upon the rulers of our land; may those principles, which were so honorably and nobly defended, namely, the [bConstitution](#note54b) of our land, by our fathers, be established forever.

55 [aRemember](#note55a) the kings, the princes, the nobles, and the great ones of the earth, and all people, and the churches, all the poor, the needy, and afflicted ones of the earth;

56 That their hearts may be softened when thy servants shall go out from thy house, O Jehovah, to bear testimony of thy name; that their prejudices may give way before the [atruth](#note56a), and thy people may obtain favor in the sight of all;

57 That all the ends of the earth may know that we, thy servants, have [aheard](#note57a) thy voice, and that thou hast sent us;

58 That from among all these, thy servants, the sons of Jacob, may gather out the righteous to build a holy [acity](#note58a) to thy name, as thou hast commanded them.

59 We ask thee to appoint unto Zion other [astakes](#note59a) besides this one which thou hast appointed, that the gathering of thy [bpeople](#note59b) may roll on in great power and majesty, that thy work may be cut [cshort](#note59c) in righteousness.

60 Now these words, O Lord, we have spoken before thee, concerning the revelations and commandments which thou hast given unto us, who are identified with the [aGentiles](#note60a).

61 But thou knowest that thou hast a great love for the children of Jacob, who have been [ascattered](#note61a) upon the [bmountains](#note61b) for a long time, in a [ccloudy](#note61c) and dark day.

62 We therefore ask thee to have mercy upon the children of Jacob, that [aJerusalem](#note62a), from this hour, may begin to be redeemed;

63 And the yoke of bondage may begin to be broken off from the house of [aDavid](#note63a);

64 And the children of [aJudah](#note64a) may begin to return to the [blands](#note64b) which thou didst give to Abraham, their father.

65 And cause that the [aremnants](#note65a) of Jacob, who have been cursed and smitten because of their transgression, be [bconverted](#note65b) from their wild and savage condition to the fulness of the everlasting gospel;

66 That they may lay down their weapons of bloodshed, and cease their rebellions.

67 And may all the scattered remnants of [aIsrael](#note67a), who have been driven to the ends of the earth, come to a knowledge of the truth, believe in the Messiah, and be redeemed from [boppression](#note67b), and rejoice before thee.

68 O Lord, remember thy servant, Joseph Smith, Jun., and all his afflictions and persecutions—how he has [acovenanted](#note68a) with [bJehovah](#note68b), and vowed to thee, O Mighty God of Jacob—and the commandments which thou hast given unto him, and that he hath sincerely striven to do thy will.

69 Have mercy, O Lord, upon his [awife](#note69a) and children, that they may be exalted in thy presence, and preserved by thy fostering hand.

70 Have mercy upon all their [aimmediate connections](#note70a), that their prejudices may be broken up and swept away as with a flood; that they may be [bconverted](#note70b) and redeemed with Israel, and know that thou art God.

71 Remember, O Lord, the presidents, even all the presidents of thy church, that thy right hand may exalt them, with all their families, and their immediate connections, that their names may be perpetuated and had in everlasting remembrance from generation to generation.

72 Remember all thy church, O Lord, with all their families, and all their immediate connections, with all their sick and afflicted ones, with all the poor and meek of the earth; that the [akingdom](#note72a), which thou hast set up without hands, may become a great mountain and fill the whole earth;

73 That thy [achurch](#note73a) may come forth out of the wilderness of darkness, and shine forth fair as the [bmoon](#note73b), clear as the sun, and terrible as an army with banners;

74 And be adorned as a bride for that day when thou shalt unveil the heavens, and cause the mountains to [aflow](#note74a) down at thy presence, and the [bvalleys](#note74b) to be exalted, the rough places made smooth; that thy glory may fill the earth;

75 That when the trump shall sound for the dead, we shall be [acaught up](#note75a) in the cloud to meet thee, that we may ever be with the Lord;

76 That our garments may be pure, that we may be clothed upon with [arobes](#note76a) of [brighteousness](#note76b), with palms in our hands, and [ccrowns](#note76c) of glory upon our heads, and reap eternal [djoy](#note76d) for all our [esufferings](#note76e).

77 O Lord God Almighty, hear us in these our petitions, and answer us from heaven, thy holy habitation, where thou sittest enthroned, with [aglory](#note77a), honor, power, majesty, might, dominion, truth, justice, judgment, mercy, and an infinity of fulness, from everlasting to everlasting.

78 O hear, O hear, O hear us, O Lord! And answer these petitions, and accept the [adedication](#note78a) of this house unto thee, the [bwork](#note78b) of our hands, which we have built unto thy name;

79 And also this church, to put upon it thy [aname](#note79a). And help us by the power of thy Spirit, that we may [bmingle](#note79b) our voices with those bright, shining [cseraphs](#note79c) around thy throne, with acclamations of [dpraise](#note79d), singing Hosanna to God and the [eLamb](#note79e)!

80 And let these, thine [aanointed](#note80a) ones, be clothed with salvation, and thy saints [bshout](#note80b) aloud for joy. Amen, and Amen.

Section 110

Visions manifested to Joseph Smith the Prophet and Oliver Cowdery in the temple at Kirtland, Ohio, April 3, 1836. The occasion was that of a Sabbath day meeting. Joseph Smith’s history states: “In the afternoon, I assisted the other Presidents in distributing the Lord’s Supper to the Church, receiving it from the Twelve, whose privilege it was to officiate at the sacred desk this day. After having performed this service to my brethren, I retired to the pulpit, the veils being dropped, and bowed myself, with Oliver Cowdery, in solemn and silent prayer. After rising from prayer, the following vision was opened to both of us.”

[1–10](110.xhtml#p1), The Lord Jehovah appears in glory and accepts the Kirtland Temple as His house; [11–12](110.xhtml#p11), Moses and Elias each appear and commit their keys and dispensations; [13–16](110.xhtml#p13), Elijah returns and commits the keys of his dispensation as promised by Malachi.

1 The [aveil](#note1a) was taken from our minds, and the [beyes](#note1b) of our [cunderstanding](#note1c) were opened.

2 We [asaw](#note2a) the Lord [bstanding](#note2b) upon the breastwork of the pulpit, before us; and under his feet was a paved work of pure [cgold](#note2c), in color like amber.

3 His [aeyes](#note3a) were as a flame of fire; the hair of his head was white like the pure snow; his [bcountenance](#note3b) shone above the brightness of the sun; and his [cvoice](#note3c) was as the sound of the rushing of great waters, even the voice of [dJehovah](#note3d), saying:

4 I am the [afirst](#note4a) and the last; I am he who [bliveth](#note4b), I am he who was slain; I am your [cadvocate](#note4c) with the Father.

5 Behold, your sins are [aforgiven](#note5a) you; you are clean before me; therefore, lift up your heads and [brejoice](#note5b).

6 Let the hearts of your brethren rejoice, and let the hearts of all my people rejoice, who have, with their might, [abuilt](#note6a) this house to my name.

7 For behold, I have [aaccepted](#note7a) this [bhouse](#note7b), and my name shall be here; and I will [cmanifest](#note7c) myself to my people in mercy in this house.

8 Yea, I will [aappear](#note8a) unto my servants, and speak unto them with mine own voice, if my people will keep my commandments, and do not [bpollute](#note8b) this [choly house](#note8c).

9 Yea the hearts of thousands and tens of thousands shall greatly rejoice in consequence of the [ablessings](#note9a) which shall be poured out, and the [bendowment](#note9b) with which my servants have been endowed in this house.

10 And the fame of this house shall spread to foreign lands; and this is the beginning of the blessing which shall be [apoured](#note10a) out upon the heads of my people. Even so. Amen.

11 After this [avision](#note11a) closed, the heavens were again [bopened](#note11b) unto us; and [cMoses](#note11c) appeared before us, and committed unto us the [dkeys](#note11d) of the [egathering](#note11e) of Israel from the four parts of the earth, and the leading of the ten tribes from the land of the [fnorth](#note11f).

12 After this, [aElias](#note12a) appeared, and committed the [bdispensation](#note12b) of the [cgospel](#note12c) of Abraham, saying that in us and our seed all [dgenerations](#note12d) after us should be [eblessed](#note12e).

13 After this vision had closed, another great and glorious [avision](#note13a) burst upon us; for [bElijah](#note13b) the prophet, who was taken to heaven without tasting death, stood before us, and said:

14 Behold, the time has fully come, which was spoken of by the mouth of Malachi—testifying that he [Elijah] should be sent, before the great and dreadful day of the Lord come—

15 To [aturn](#note15a) the [bhearts](#note15b) of the fathers to the children, and the children to the fathers, lest the whole earth be smitten with a curse—

16 Therefore, the [akeys](#note16a) of this [bdispensation](#note16b) are committed into your hands; and by this ye may know that the great and dreadful [cday](#note16c) of the Lord is near, even at the doors.

Section 111

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Salem, Massachusetts, August 6, 1836. At this time the leaders of the Church were heavily in debt due to their labors in the ministry. Hearing that a large amount of money would be available to them in Salem, the Prophet, Sidney Rigdon, Hyrum Smith, and Oliver Cowdery traveled there from Kirtland, Ohio, to investigate this claim, along with preaching the gospel. The brethren transacted several items of Church business and did some preaching. When it became apparent that no money was to be forthcoming, they returned to Kirtland. Several of the factors prominent in the background are reflected in the wording of this revelation.

[1–5](111.xhtml#p1), The Lord looks to the temporal needs of His servants; [6–11](111.xhtml#p6), He will deal mercifully with Zion and arrange all things for the good of His servants.

1 I, the Lord your God, am [anot](#note1a) displeased with your coming this journey, notwithstanding your follies.

2 I have much [atreasure](#note2a) in this city for you, for the benefit of Zion, and many people in this city, whom I will gather out in due time for the benefit of Zion, through your instrumentality.

3 Therefore, it is expedient that you should form [aacquaintance](#note3a) with men in this city, as you shall be led, and as it shall be given you.

4 And it shall come to pass in due time that I will [agive](#note4a) this city into your hands, that you shall have power over it, insomuch that they shall not [bdiscover](#note4b) your secret parts; and its wealth pertaining to gold and silver shall be yours.

5 Concern not yourselves about your [adebts](#note5a), for I will give you power to pay them.

6 Concern not yourselves about Zion, for I will deal mercifully with her.

7 Tarry in this place, and in the regions round about;

8 And the place where it is my will that you should tarry, for the main, shall be signalized unto you by the [apeace](#note8a) and power of my [bSpirit](#note8b), that shall flow unto you.

9 This place you may obtain by hire. And inquire diligently concerning the more ancient inhabitants and founders of this city;

10 For there are more treasures than one for you in this city.

11 Therefore, be ye as [awise](#note11a) as serpents and yet without [bsin](#note11b); and I will order all things for your [cgood](#note11c), as fast as ye are able to receive them. Amen.

Section 112

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet to Thomas B. Marsh, at Kirtland, Ohio, July 23, 1837, concerning the Twelve Apostles of the Lamb. This revelation was received on the day Elders Heber C. Kimball and Orson Hyde first preached the gospel in England. Thomas B. Marsh was at this time President of the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles.

[1–10](112.xhtml#p1), The Twelve are to send the gospel and raise the warning voice to all nations and people; [11–15](112.xhtml#p11), They are to take up their cross, follow Jesus, and feed His sheep; [16–20](112.xhtml#p16), Those who receive the First Presidency receive the Lord; [21–29](112.xhtml#p21), Darkness covers the earth, and only those who believe and are baptized will be saved; [30–34](112.xhtml#p30), The First Presidency and the Twelve hold the keys of the dispensation of the fulness of times.

1 Verily thus saith the Lord unto you my servant Thomas: I have heard thy prayers; and thine [aalms](#note1a) have come up as a [bmemorial](#note1b) before me, in behalf of those, thy brethren, who were chosen to bear testimony of my name and to [csend](#note1c) it abroad among all nations, kindreds, tongues, and people, and ordained through the instrumentality of my servants.

2 Verily I say unto you, there have been some few things in thine heart and with thee with which I, the Lord, was not well pleased.

3 Nevertheless, inasmuch as thou hast [aabased](#note3a) thyself thou shalt be exalted; therefore, all thy sins are forgiven thee.

4 Let thy heart be of good [acheer](#note4a) before my face; and thou shalt bear record of my name, not only unto the [bGentiles](#note4b), but also unto the Jews; and thou shalt send forth my word unto the ends of the earth.

5 [aContend](#note5a) thou, therefore, morning by morning; and day after day let thy [bwarning](#note5b) voice go forth; and when the night cometh let not the inhabitants of the earth slumber, because of thy [cspeech](#note5c).

6 Let thy habitation be known in Zion, and [aremove](#note6a) not thy house; for I, the Lord, have a great work for thee to do, in publishing my name among the children of men.

7 Therefore, [agird](#note7a) up thy loins for the work. Let thy feet be shod also, for thou art chosen, and thy path lieth among the mountains, and among many nations.

8 And by thy word many high ones shall be brought low, and by thy word many low ones shall be [aexalted](#note8a).

9 Thy voice shall be a rebuke unto the transgressor; and at thy [arebuke](#note9a) let the tongue of the slanderer cease its perverseness.

10 Be thou [ahumble](#note10a); and the Lord thy God shall [blead](#note10b) thee by the hand, and give thee answer to thy prayers.

11 I know thy heart, and have heard thy prayers concerning thy brethren. Be not partial towards them in love above many others, but let thy [alove](#note11a) be for them as for thyself; and let thy love abound unto all men, and unto all who love my name.

12 And pray for thy brethren of the Twelve. [aAdmonish](#note12a) them sharply for my name’s sake, and let them be admonished for all their sins, and be ye faithful before me unto my [bname](#note12b).

13 And after their [atemptations](#note13a), and much [btribulation](#note13b), behold, I, the Lord, will feel after them, and if they harden not their hearts, and [cstiffen](#note13c) not their necks against me, they shall be [dconverted](#note13d), and I will heal them.

14 Now, I say unto you, and what I say unto you, I say unto all the Twelve: Arise and gird up your loins, take up your [across](#note14a), follow me, and [bfeed](#note14b) my sheep.

15 Exalt not yourselves; [arebel](#note15a) not against my servant Joseph; for verily I say unto you, I am with him, and my hand shall be over him; and the [bkeys](#note15b) which I have given unto him, and also to youward, shall not be taken from him till I come.

16 Verily I say unto you, my servant Thomas, thou art the man whom I have chosen to hold the [akeys](#note16a) of my kingdom, as pertaining to the Twelve, abroad among all nations—

17 That thou mayest be my servant to unlock the door of the kingdom in all places where my servant Joseph, and my servant Sidney, and my servant Hyrum, [acannot](#note17a) come;

18 For on them have I laid the burden of all the churches for a little season.

19 Wherefore, whithersoever they shall send you, go ye, and I will be with you; and in whatsoever place ye shall proclaim my name an [aeffectual](#note19a) door shall be opened unto you, that they may receive my word.

20 Whosoever [areceiveth](#note20a) my word receiveth me, and whosoever receiveth me, receiveth those, the First Presidency, whom I have sent, whom I have made counselors for my name’s sake unto you.

21 And again, I say unto you, that whosoever ye shall send in my name, by the voice of your brethren, the [aTwelve](#note21a), duly recommended and [bauthorized](#note21b) by you, shall have power to open the door of my kingdom unto any nation whithersoever ye shall send them—

22 Inasmuch as they shall humble themselves before me, and abide in my word, and [ahearken](#note22a) to the voice of my Spirit.

23 Verily, verily, I say unto you, [adarkness](#note23a) covereth the earth, and gross darkness the minds of the people, and all flesh has become [bcorrupt](#note23b) before my face.

24 Behold, [avengeance](#note24a) cometh speedily upon the inhabitants of the earth, a day of wrath, a day of burning, a day of [bdesolation](#note24b), of [cweeping](#note24c), of mourning, and of lamentation; and as a whirlwind it shall come upon all the face of the earth, saith the Lord.

25 And upon my [ahouse](#note25a) shall it [bbegin](#note25b), and from my house shall it go forth, saith the Lord;

26 First among those among you, saith the Lord, who have [aprofessed](#note26a) to know my [bname](#note26b) and have not [cknown](#note26c) me, and have [dblasphemed](#note26d) against me in the midst of my house, saith the Lord.

27 Therefore, see to it that ye trouble not yourselves concerning the affairs of my church [ain](#note27a) this place, saith the Lord.

28 But [apurify](#note28a) your hearts before me; and then [bgo](#note28b) ye into all the world, and preach my gospel unto every creature who has not received it;

29 And he that [abelieveth](#note29a) and is [bbaptized](#note29b) shall be saved, and he that believeth not, and is not baptized, shall be [cdamned](#note29c).

30 For unto you, the [aTwelve](#note30a), and those, the First Presidency, who are appointed with you to be your [bcounselors](#note30b) and your leaders, is the [cpower](#note30c) of this priesthood given, for the last days and for the last time, in the which is the dispensation of the [dfulness](#note30d) of times,

31 Which power you hold, in connection with all those who have received a [adispensation](#note31a) at any time from the beginning of the creation;

32 For verily I say unto you, the [akeys](#note32a) of the dispensation, which ye have received, have [bcome](#note32b) down from the fathers, and last of all, being sent down from heaven unto you.

33 Verily I say unto you, behold how great is your calling. [aCleanse](#note33a) your hearts and your garments, lest the blood of this generation be [brequired](#note33b) at your hands.

34 Be faithful until I come, for I [acome](#note34a) quickly; and my reward is with me to recompense every man according as his [bwork](#note34b) shall be. I am Alpha and Omega. Amen.

Section 113

Answers to certain questions on the writings of Isaiah, given by Joseph Smith the Prophet, at or near Far West, Missouri, March 1838.

[1–6](113.xhtml#p1), The Stem of Jesse, the rod coming therefrom, and the root of Jesse are identified; [7–10](113.xhtml#p7), The scattered remnants of Zion have a right to the priesthood and are called to return to the Lord.

1 Who is the [aStem of Jesse](#note1a) spoken of in the 1st, 2d, 3d, 4th, and 5th verses of the 11th chapter of Isaiah?

2 Verily thus saith the Lord: It is Christ.

3 What is the [arod](#note3a) spoken of in the first verse of the 11th chapter of Isaiah, that should come of the Stem of Jesse?

4 Behold, thus saith the Lord: It is a servant in the hands of Christ, who is partly a descendant of Jesse as well as of [aEphraim](#note4a), or of the house of Joseph, on whom there is laid much [bpower](#note4b).

5 What is the [aroot of Jesse](#note5a) spoken of in the 10th verse of the 11th chapter?

6 Behold, thus saith the Lord, it is a [adescendant](#note6a) of Jesse, as well as of Joseph, unto whom rightly belongs the [bpriesthood](#note6b), and the [ckeys](#note6c) of the kingdom, for an [densign](#note6d), and for the gathering of my people in the [elast days](#note6e).

7 Questions by Elias Higbee: What is meant by the command in Isaiah, 52d chapter, 1st verse, which saith: Put on thy strength, O Zion—and what people had Isaiah reference to?

8 He had reference to those whom God should call in the last days, who should hold the [apower](#note8a) of [bpriesthood](#note8b) to bring again [cZion](#note8c), and the redemption of Israel; and to put on her [dstrength](#note8d) is to put on the [eauthority](#note8e) of the [fpriesthood](#note8f), which she, Zion, has a [gright](#note8g) to by lineage; also to return to that power which she had lost.

9 What are we to understand by Zion loosing herself from the bands of her neck; 2d verse?

10 We are to understand that the [ascattered](#note10a) [bremnants](#note10b) are exhorted to [creturn](#note10c) to the Lord from whence they have fallen; which if they do, the promise of the Lord is that he will speak to them, or give them revelation. See the 6th, 7th, and 8th verses. The [dbands](#note10d) of her neck are the curses of God upon her, or the remnants of Israel in their scattered condition among the Gentiles.

Section 114

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Far West, Missouri, April 11, 1838.

[1–2](114.xhtml#p1), Church positions held by those who are not faithful will be given to others.

1 Verily thus saith the Lord: It is wisdom in my servant David W. Patten, that he settle up all his business as soon as he possibly can, and make a disposition of his merchandise, that he may [aperform](#note1a) a mission unto me next spring, in company with others, even twelve including himself, to testify of my name and bear glad tidings unto all the world.

2 For verily thus saith the Lord, that inasmuch as there are those among you who [adeny](#note2a) my name, others shall be [bplanted](#note2b) in their [cstead](#note2c) and receive their [dbishopric](#note2d). Amen.

  [D&C 118:5](118.xhtml#p5).

Section 115

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Far West, Missouri, April 26, 1838, making known the will of God concerning the building up of that place and of the Lord’s house. This revelation is addressed to the presiding officers and the members of the Church.

[1–4](115.xhtml#p1), The Lord names His church The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints; [5–6](115.xhtml#p5), Zion and her stakes are places of defense and refuge for the Saints; [7–16](115.xhtml#p7), The Saints are commanded to build a house of the Lord at Far West; [17–19](115.xhtml#p17), Joseph Smith holds the keys of the kingdom of God on earth.

1 Verily thus saith the Lord unto you, my servant Joseph Smith, Jun., and also my servant Sidney Rigdon, and also my servant Hyrum Smith, and your [acounselors](#note1a) who are and shall be appointed hereafter;

2 And also unto you, my servant [aEdward Partridge](#note2a), and his counselors;

3 And also unto my faithful servants who are of the high council of my [achurch](#note3a) in Zion, for thus it shall be called, and unto all the elders and people of my Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, scattered abroad in all the world;

4 For thus shall [amy](#note4a) [bchurch](#note4b) be called in the last days, even The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day [cSaints](#note4c).

5 Verily I say unto you all: [aArise](#note5a) and shine forth, that thy [blight](#note5b) may be a [cstandard](#note5c) for the [dnations](#note5d);

6 And that the [agathering](#note6a) together upon the land of [bZion](#note6b), and upon her [cstakes](#note6c), may be for a defense, and for a [drefuge](#note6d) from the storm, and from wrath when it shall be [epoured](#note6e) out without mixture upon the whole earth.

7 Let the city, Far West, be a holy and consecrated land unto me; and it shall be called most holy, for the ground upon which thou standest is [aholy](#note7a).

8 Therefore, I command you to [abuild](#note8a) a house unto me, for the gathering together of my saints, that they may [bworship](#note8b) me.

9 And let there be a beginning of this work, and a foundation, and a preparatory work, this following summer;

10 And let the beginning be made on the fourth day of July next; and from that time forth let my people [alabor](#note10a) diligently to build a house unto my name;

11 And in [aone](#note11a) year from this day let them re-commence laying the foundation of my [bhouse](#note11b).

12 Thus let them from that time forth labor diligently until it shall be finished, from the cornerstone thereof unto the top thereof, until there shall not anything remain that is not finished.

13 Verily I say unto you, let not my servant Joseph, neither my servant Sidney, neither my servant Hyrum, get in [adebt](#note13a) any more for the building of a house unto my name;

14 But let a house be built unto my name according to the [apattern](#note14a) which I will show unto them.

15 And if my people build it not according to the pattern which I shall show unto their presidency, I will not accept it at their hands.

16 But if my people do build it according to the pattern which I shall show unto their presidency, even my servant Joseph and his counselors, then I will accept it at the hands of my people.

17 And again, verily I say unto you, it is my will that the city of Far West should be built up speedily by the gathering of my saints;

18 And also that other places should be appointed for [astakes](#note18a) in the regions round about, as they shall be manifested unto my servant Joseph, from time to time.

19 For behold, I will be with him, and I will sanctify him before the people; for unto him have I given the [akeys](#note19a) of this kingdom and ministry. Even so. Amen.

Section 116

Revelation given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, near Wight’s Ferry, at a place called Spring Hill, Daviess County, Missouri, May 19, 1838.

1 Spring Hill is named by the Lord [aAdam-ondi-Ahman](#note1a), because, said he, it is the place where [bAdam](#note1b) shall come to visit his people, or the Ancient of Days shall sit, as spoken of by Daniel the prophet.

* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1a)

  1a

  Dan. 7:13 (13–14, 22); [D&C 78:15 (15, 20)](78.xhtml#p15); [95:17](95.xhtml#p17); [107:53](107.xhtml#p53); [117:8 (8, 11)](117.xhtml#p8). Note that a song titled “Adam-ondi-Ahman,” written by William W. Phelps, was sung at the Kirtland Temple dedication.
* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1b)

  b

  TG Adam.

Section 117

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Far West, Missouri, July 8, 1838, concerning the immediate duties of William Marks, Newel K. Whitney, and Oliver Granger.

[1–9](117.xhtml#p1), The Lord’s servants should not covet temporal things, for “what is property unto the Lord?”; [10–16](117.xhtml#p10), They are to forsake littleness of soul, and their sacrifices will be sacred unto the Lord.

1 Verily thus saith the Lord unto my servant [aWilliam Marks](#note1a), and also unto my servant Newel K. Whitney, let them settle up their business speedily and journey from the land of Kirtland, before I, the Lord, [bsend](#note1b) again the snows upon the earth.

2 Let them awake, and arise, and [acome](#note2a) forth, and not tarry, for I, the Lord, command it.

3 Therefore, if they [atarry](#note3a) it shall not be well with them.

4 Let them repent of all their sins, and of all their covetous desires, before me, saith the Lord; for what is [aproperty](#note4a) unto me? saith the Lord.

5 Let the properties of Kirtland be turned out for [adebts](#note5a), saith the Lord. Let them go, saith the Lord, and whatsoever remaineth, let it remain in your hands, saith the Lord.

6 For have I not the fowls of heaven, and also the fish of the sea, and the beasts of the mountains? Have I not [amade](#note6a) the earth? Do I not hold the [bdestinies](#note6b) of all the armies of the nations of the earth?

7 Therefore, will I not make [asolitary](#note7a) places to bud and to [bblossom](#note7b), and to bring forth in abundance? saith the Lord.

8 Is there not room enough on the mountains of [aAdam-ondi-Ahman](#note8a), and on the plains of Olaha [bShinehah](#note8b), or the land where [cAdam](#note8c) dwelt, that you should covet that which is but the drop, and neglect the more weighty matters?

9 Therefore, come up hither unto the land of my people, even Zion.

10 Let my servant William Marks be [afaithful](#note10a) over a few things, and he shall be a ruler over many. Let him preside in the midst of my people in the city of Far West, and let him be blessed with the blessings of my people.

11 Let my servant Newel K. Whitney be ashamed of the [aNicolaitane](#note11a) band and of all their [bsecret abominations](#note11b), and of all his littleness of soul before me, saith the Lord, and come up to the land of Adam-ondi-Ahman, and be a [cbishop](#note11c) unto my people, saith the Lord, not in name but in deed, saith the Lord.

12 And again, I say unto you, I remember my servant [aOliver Granger](#note12a); behold, verily I say unto him that his name shall be had in sacred remembrance from generation to generation, forever and ever, saith the Lord.

13 Therefore, let him contend earnestly for the redemption of the First Presidency of my Church, saith the Lord; and when he falls he shall rise again, for his [asacrifice](#note13a) shall be more sacred unto me than his increase, saith the Lord.

14 Therefore, let him come up hither speedily, unto the land of Zion; and in the due time he shall be made a merchant unto my name, saith the Lord, for the benefit of my people.

15 Therefore let no man despise my servant Oliver Granger, but let the blessings of my people be on him forever and ever.

16 And again, verily I say unto you, let all my servants in the land of Kirtland remember the Lord their God, and mine house also, to keep and preserve it holy, and to overthrow the moneychangers in mine own due time, saith the Lord. Even so. Amen.

Section 118

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Far West, Missouri, July 8, 1838, in response to the supplication, “Show us thy will, O Lord, concerning the Twelve.”

[1–3](118.xhtml#p1), The Lord will provide for the families of the Twelve; [4–6](118.xhtml#p4), Vacancies in the Twelve are filled.

1 Verily, thus saith the Lord: Let a conference be held immediately; let the Twelve be organized; and let men be appointed to [asupply](#note1a) the place of those who are fallen.

2 Let my servant [aThomas](#note2a) remain for a season in the land of Zion, to publish my word.

3 Let the residue continue to preach from that hour, and if they will do this in all [alowliness](#note3a) of heart, in meekness and humility, and [blong-suffering](#note3b), I, the Lord, give unto them a [cpromise](#note3c) that I will provide for their families; and an effectual door shall be opened for them, from henceforth.

4 And next spring let them depart to go over the great waters, and there promulgate my gospel, the fulness thereof, and bear record of my name.

5 Let them [atake](#note5a) leave of my saints in the city of Far West, on the [btwenty-sixth](#note5b) day of April next, on the building-spot of my house, saith the Lord.

6 Let my servant John Taylor, and also my servant John E. Page, and also my servant Wilford Woodruff, and also my servant Willard Richards, be appointed to fill the places of those who have [afallen](#note6a), and be officially notified of their appointment.

Section 119

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Far West, Missouri, July 8, 1838, in answer to his supplication: “O Lord! Show unto thy servants how much thou requirest of the properties of thy people for a tithing.” The law of tithing, as understood today, had not been given to the Church previous to this revelation. The term *tithing* in the prayer just quoted and in previous revelations ([64:23](64.xhtml#p23); [85:3](85.xhtml#p3); [97:11](97.xhtml#p11)) had meant not just one-tenth, but all free-will offerings, or contributions, to the Church funds. The Lord had previously given to the Church the law of consecration and stewardship of property, which members (chiefly the leading elders) entered into by a covenant that was to be everlasting. Because of failure on the part of many to abide by this covenant, the Lord withdrew it for a time and gave instead the law of tithing to the whole Church. The Prophet asked the Lord how much of their property He required for sacred purposes. The answer was this revelation.

[1–5](119.xhtml#p1), The Saints are to pay their surplus property and then give, as tithing, one-tenth of their interest annually; [6–7](119.xhtml#p6), Such a course will sanctify the land of Zion.

1 Verily, thus saith the Lord, I require all their [asurplus](#note1a) property to be put into the hands of the bishop of my church in Zion,

2 For the building of mine [ahouse](#note2a), and for the laying of the foundation of Zion and for the priesthood, and for the debts of the Presidency of my Church.

3 And this shall be the beginning of the [atithing](#note3a) of my people.

4 And after that, those who have thus been [atithed](#note4a) shall pay one-tenth of all their interest annually; and this shall be a standing law unto them forever, for my holy priesthood, saith the Lord.

5 Verily I say unto you, it shall come to pass that all those who gather unto the land of [aZion](#note5a) shall be tithed of their surplus properties, and shall observe this law, or they shall not be found worthy to abide among you.

6 And I say unto you, if my people observe not this law, to keep it holy, and by this law sanctify the land of Zion unto me, that my statutes and my judgments may be kept thereon, that it may be most holy, behold, verily I say unto you, it shall not be a land of [aZion](#note6a) unto you.

7 And this shall be an ensample unto all the [astakes](#note7a) of Zion. Even so. Amen.

Section 120

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Far West, Missouri, July 8, 1838, making known the disposition of the properties tithed as named in the preceding revelation, [section 119](119.xhtml).

1 Verily, thus saith the Lord, the time is now come, that [ait](#note1a) shall be [bdisposed](#note1b) of by a council, composed of the First Presidency of my Church, and of the bishop and his council, and by my high council; and by mine own voice unto them, saith the Lord. Even so. Amen.

* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1a)

  1a

  [D&C 119:1 (1–4)](119.xhtml#p1).
* [◀](#ldsBACK_note1b)

  b

  TG Accountability; Church Organization.

Section 121

Prayer and prophecies written by Joseph Smith the Prophet in an epistle to the Church while he was a prisoner in the jail at Liberty, Missouri, dated March 20, 1839. The Prophet and several companions had been months in prison. Their petitions and appeals directed to the executive officers and the judiciary had failed to bring them relief.

[1–6](121.xhtml#p1), The Prophet pleads with the Lord for the suffering Saints; [7–10](121.xhtml#p7), The Lord speaks peace to him; [11–17](121.xhtml#p11), Cursed are all those who raise false cries of transgression against the Lord’s people; [18–25](121.xhtml#p18), They will not have right to the priesthood and will be damned; [26–32](121.xhtml#p26), Glorious revelations promised those who endure valiantly; [33–40](121.xhtml#p33), Why many are called and few are chosen; [41–46](121.xhtml#p41), The priesthood should be used only in righteousness.

1 O God, [awhere](#note1a) art thou? And where is the pavilion that covereth thy [bhiding place](#note1b)?

2 [aHow](#note2a) long shall thy hand be stayed, and thine eye, yea thy pure eye, behold from the eternal heavens the wrongs of thy people and of thy servants, and thine ear be penetrated with their cries?

3 Yea, O Lord, [ahow](#note3a) long shall they suffer these wrongs and unlawful [boppressions](#note3b), before thine heart shall be softened toward them, and thy bowels be moved with [ccompassion](#note3c) toward them?

4 O Lord God [aAlmighty](#note4a), maker of [bheaven](#note4b), earth, and seas, and of all things that in them are, and who controllest and subjectest the devil, and the dark and benighted dominion of Sheol—stretch forth thy hand; let thine eye pierce; let thy [cpavilion](#note4c) be taken up; let thy [dhiding place](#note4d) no longer be covered; let thine ear be inclined; let thine [eheart](#note4e) be softened, and thy bowels moved with compassion toward us.

5 Let thine [aanger](#note5a) be kindled against our enemies; and, in the fury of thine heart, with thy [bsword](#note5b) [cavenge](#note5c) us of our wrongs.

6 Remember thy [asuffering](#note6a) saints, O our God; and thy servants will rejoice in thy name forever.

7 My son, [apeace](#note7a) be unto thy soul; thine [badversity](#note7b) and thine afflictions shall be but a [csmall](#note7c) moment;

8 And then, if thou [aendure](#note8a) it well, God shall exalt thee on high; thou shalt triumph over all thy [bfoes](#note8b).

9 Thy [afriends](#note9a) do stand by thee, and they shall hail thee again with warm hearts and friendly hands.

10 Thou art not yet as Job; thy [afriends](#note10a) do not contend against thee, neither charge thee with transgression, as they did Job.

11 And they who do charge thee with transgression, their hope shall be blasted, and their prospects shall [amelt](#note11a) away as the hoar frost melteth before the burning rays of the rising sun;

12 And also that God hath set his hand and seal to change the [atimes](#note12a) and seasons, and to blind their [bminds](#note12b), that they may not understand his [cmarvelous workings](#note12c); that he may [dprove](#note12d) them also and take them in their own craftiness;

13 Also because their hearts are corrupted, and the things which they are willing to bring upon others, and love to have others suffer, may come upon [athemselves](#note13a) to the very uttermost;

14 That they may be [adisappointed](#note14a) also, and their hopes may be cut off;

15 And not many years hence, that they and their [aposterity](#note15a) shall be [bswept](#note15b) from under heaven, saith God, that not one of them is left to stand by the wall.

16 [aCursed](#note16a) are all those that shall lift up the [bheel](#note16b) against mine [canointed](#note16c), saith the Lord, and cry they have [dsinned](#note16d) when they have not sinned before me, saith the Lord, but have done that which was meet in mine eyes, and which I commanded them.

17 But [athose](#note17a) who cry transgression do it because they are the servants of sin, and are the [bchildren](#note17b) of disobedience themselves.

18 And those who [aswear](#note18a) [bfalsely](#note18b) against my servants, that they might bring them into bondage and death—

19 Wo unto them; because they have [aoffended](#note19a) my little ones they shall be severed from the [bordinances](#note19b) of mine house.

20 Their [abasket](#note20a) shall not be full, their houses and their barns shall perish, and they themselves shall be [bdespised](#note20b) by those that flattered them.

21 They shall not have right to the [apriesthood](#note21a), nor their posterity after them from generation to generation.

22 It had been [abetter](#note22a) for them that a millstone had been hanged about their necks, and they drowned in the depth of the sea.

23 Wo unto all those that [adiscomfort](#note23a) my people, and drive, and [bmurder](#note23b), and testify against them, saith the Lord of Hosts; a [cgeneration](#note23c) of vipers shall not escape the damnation of hell.

24 Behold, mine eyes [asee](#note24a) and know all their works, and I have in reserve a swift [bjudgment](#note24b) in the season thereof, for them all;

25 For there is a [atime](#note25a) [bappointed](#note25b) for every man, according as his [cworks](#note25c) shall be.

26 God shall give unto you [aknowledge](#note26a) by his [bHoly Spirit](#note26b), yea, by the unspeakable [cgift](#note26c) of the Holy Ghost, that has not been [drevealed](#note26d) since the world was until now;

27 Which our forefathers have awaited with [aanxious](#note27a) expectation to be revealed in the last times, which their minds were pointed to by the angels, as held in reserve for the fulness of their glory;

28 A time to come in the which [anothing](#note28a) shall be withheld, whether there be [bone God](#note28b) or many [cgods](#note28c), they shall be manifest.

29 All thrones and dominions, principalities and powers, shall be [arevealed](#note29a) and set forth upon all who have endured [bvaliantly](#note29b) for the gospel of Jesus Christ.

30 And also, if there be [abounds](#note30a) set to the heavens or to the seas, or to the dry land, or to the sun, moon, or stars—

31 All the times of their revolutions, all the appointed days, months, and years, and all the days of their days, months, and years, and all their [aglories](#note31a), laws, and set times, shall be revealed in the days of the [bdispensation](#note31b) of the fulness of times—

32 According to that which was [aordained](#note32a) in the midst of the [bCouncil](#note32b) of the Eternal [cGod](#note32c) of all other gods before this [dworld](#note32d) was, that should be reserved unto the finishing and the end thereof, when every man shall enter into his eternal [epresence](#note32e) and into his immortal [frest](#note32f).

33 How long can rolling waters remain impure? What [apower](#note33a) shall stay the heavens? As well might man stretch forth his puny arm to stop the Missouri river in its decreed course, or to turn it up stream, as to [bhinder](#note33b) the [cAlmighty](#note33c) from pouring down [dknowledge](#note33d) from heaven upon the heads of the Latter-day Saints.

34 Behold, there are many [acalled](#note34a), but few are chosen. And why are they not chosen?

35 Because their [ahearts](#note35a) are set so much upon the things of this [bworld](#note35b), and [caspire](#note35c) to the [dhonors](#note35d) of men, that they do not learn this one lesson—

36 That the [arights](#note36a) of the priesthood are inseparably connected with the powers of heaven, and that the powers of heaven cannot be [bcontrolled](#note36b) nor handled only upon the [cprinciples](#note36c) of righteousness.

37 That they may be conferred upon us, it is true; but when we undertake to [acover](#note37a) our [bsins](#note37b), or to gratify our [cpride](#note37c), our vain ambition, or to exercise control or [ddominion](#note37d) or compulsion upon the souls of the children of men, in any degree of unrighteousness, behold, the heavens [ewithdraw](#note37e) themselves; the Spirit of the Lord is grieved; and when it is withdrawn, Amen to the priesthood or the authority of that man.

38 Behold, ere he is aware, he is left unto himself, to [akick](#note38a) against the pricks, to [bpersecute](#note38b) the saints, and to [cfight](#note38c) against God.

39 We have learned by sad experience that it is the [anature](#note39a) and disposition of almost all men, as soon as they get a little [bauthority](#note39b), as they suppose, they will immediately begin to exercise [cunrighteous](#note39c) dominion.

40 Hence many are called, but [afew](#note40a) are chosen.

41 No [apower](#note41a) or influence can or ought to be maintained by virtue of the [bpriesthood](#note41b), only by [cpersuasion](#note41c), by [dlong-suffering](#note41d), by gentleness and meekness, and by love unfeigned;

42 By [akindness](#note42a), and pure [bknowledge](#note42b), which shall greatly enlarge the [csoul](#note42c) without [dhypocrisy](#note42d), and without [eguile](#note42e)—

43 [aReproving](#note43a) betimes with [bsharpness](#note43b), when [cmoved](#note43c) upon by the Holy Ghost; and then showing forth afterwards an increase of [dlove](#note43d) toward him whom thou hast reproved, lest he esteem thee to be his enemy;

44 That he may know that thy faithfulness is stronger than the cords of [adeath](#note44a).

45 Let thy [abowels](#note45a) also be full of charity towards all men, and to the household of faith, and let [bvirtue](#note45b) garnish thy thoughts unceasingly; then shall thy [cconfidence](#note45c) wax strong in the [dpresence](#note45d) of God; and the doctrine of the priesthood shall distil upon thy soul as the [edews](#note45e) from heaven.

46 The Holy Ghost shall be thy constant [acompanion](#note46a), and thy scepter an unchanging scepter of [brighteousness](#note46b) and truth; and thy [cdominion](#note46c) shall be an everlasting dominion, and without compulsory means it shall flow unto thee forever and ever.
