Section 132

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Nauvoo, Illinois, recorded July 12, 1843, relating to the new and everlasting covenant, including the eternity of the marriage covenant and the principle of plural marriage. Although the revelation was recorded in 1843, evidence indicates that some of the principles involved in this revelation were known by the Prophet as early as 1831. See Official Declaration 1.

[1–6](132.xhtml#p1), Exaltation is gained through the new and everlasting covenant; [7–14](132.xhtml#p7), The terms and conditions of that covenant are set forth; [15–20](132.xhtml#p15), Celestial marriage and a continuation of the family unit enable men to become gods; [21–25](132.xhtml#p21), The strait and narrow way leads to eternal lives; [26–27](132.xhtml#p26), The law is given relative to blasphemy against the Holy Ghost; [28–39](132.xhtml#p28), Promises of eternal increase and exaltation are made to prophets and Saints in all ages; [40–47](132.xhtml#p40), Joseph Smith is given the power to bind and seal on earth and in heaven; [48–50](132.xhtml#p48), The Lord seals upon him his exaltation; [51–57](132.xhtml#p51), Emma Smith is counseled to be faithful and true; [58–66](132.xhtml#p58), Laws governing plural marriage are set forth.

1 Verily, thus saith the Lord unto you my servant Joseph, that inasmuch as you have inquired of my hand to know and understand wherein I, the Lord, justified my servants Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, as also Moses, David and Solomon, my servants, as touching the principle and doctrine of their having many [awives](#note1a) and [bconcubines](#note1b)—

2 Behold, and lo, I am the Lord thy God, and will answer thee as touching this matter.

3 Therefore, [aprepare](#note3a) thy heart to receive and [bobey](#note3b) the instructions which I am about to give unto you; for all those who have this law revealed unto them must obey the same.

4 For behold, I reveal unto you a new and an everlasting [acovenant](#note4a); and if ye abide not that covenant, then are ye [bdamned](#note4b); for no one can [creject](#note4c) this covenant and be permitted to enter into my glory.

5 For all who will have a [ablessing](#note5a) at my hands shall abide the [blaw](#note5b) which was appointed for that blessing, and the conditions thereof, as were instituted from before the foundation of the world.

6 And as pertaining to the [anew and everlasting covenant](#note6a), it was instituted for the fulness of my [bglory](#note6b); and he that receiveth a fulness thereof must and shall abide the law, or he shall be damned, saith the Lord God.

7 And verily I say unto you, that the [aconditions](#note7a) of this law are these: All covenants, contracts, bonds, obligations, [boaths](#note7b), [cvows](#note7c), performances, connections, associations, or expectations, that are not made and entered into and [dsealed](#note7d) by the Holy Spirit of promise, of him who is [eanointed](#note7e), both as well for time and for all eternity, and that too most holy, by [frevelation](#note7f) and commandment through the medium of mine anointed, whom I have appointed on the earth to hold this [gpower](#note7g) (and I have appointed unto my servant Joseph to hold this [hpower](#note7h) in the last days, and there is never but one on the earth at a time on whom this power and the [ikeys](#note7i) of this priesthood are conferred), are of no efficacy, virtue, or force in and after the resurrection from the dead; for all contracts that are not made unto this end have an end when men are dead.

8 Behold, mine house is a house of [aorder](#note8a), saith the Lord God, and not a house of confusion.

9 Will I [aaccept](#note9a) of an offering, saith the Lord, that is not made in my name?

10 Or will I receive at your hands that which I have not [aappointed](#note10a)?

11 And will I appoint unto you, saith the Lord, except it be by law, even as I and my Father [aordained](#note11a) unto you, before the world was?

12 I am the Lord thy God; and I give unto you this commandment—that no man shall [acome](#note12a) unto the Father but by me or by my word, which is my law, saith the Lord.

13 And everything that is in the world, whether it be ordained of men, by [athrones](#note13a), or principalities, or powers, or things of name, whatsoever they may be, that are not by me or by my word, saith the Lord, shall be thrown down, and shall [bnot remain](#note13b) after men are dead, neither in nor after the resurrection, saith the Lord your God.

14 For whatsoever things remain are by me; and whatsoever things are not by me shall be shaken and destroyed.

15 Therefore, if a [aman](#note15a) marry him a wife in the world, and he marry her not by me nor by my word, and he covenant with her so long as he is in the world and she with him, their covenant and marriage are not of force when they are dead, and when they are out of the world; therefore, they are not bound by any law when they are out of the world.

16 Therefore, when they are out of the world they neither marry nor are given in [amarriage](#note16a); but are appointed angels in [bheaven](#note16b), which angels are ministering [cservants](#note16c), to minister for those who are worthy of a far more, and an exceeding, and an eternal weight of glory.

17 For these angels did not abide my law; therefore, they cannot be enlarged, but remain separately and singly, without exaltation, in their saved condition, to all eternity; and from henceforth are not gods, but are [aangels](#note17a) of God forever and ever.

18 And again, verily I say unto you, if a man marry a wife, and make a covenant with her for time and for all eternity, if that [acovenant](#note18a) is not by me or by my word, which is my law, and is not sealed by the Holy Spirit of promise, through him whom I have anointed and appointed unto this power, then it is not valid neither of force when they are out of the world, because they are not joined by me, saith the Lord, neither by my word; when they are out of the world it cannot be received there, because the angels and the gods are appointed there, by whom they cannot pass; they cannot, therefore, inherit my glory; for my house is a house of order, saith the Lord God.

19 And again, verily I say unto you, if a man [amarry](#note19a) a wife by my word, which is my law, and by the [bnew and everlasting covenant](#note19b), and it is [csealed](#note19c) unto them by the Holy Spirit of [dpromise](#note19d), by him who is anointed, unto whom I have appointed this power and the [ekeys](#note19e) of this priesthood; and it shall be said unto them—Ye shall come forth in the first resurrection; and if it be after the first resurrection, in the next resurrection; and shall inherit [fthrones](#note19f), kingdoms, principalities, and powers, dominions, all heights and depths—then shall it be written in the Lamb’s [gBook of Life](#note19g), that he shall commit no [hmurder](#note19h) whereby to shed innocent [iblood](#note19i), and if ye abide in my covenant, and commit no murder whereby to shed innocent blood, it shall be done unto them in all things whatsoever my servant hath put upon them, in time, and through all eternity; and shall be of full force when they are out of the world; and they shall pass by the angels, and the gods, which are set there, to their [jexaltation](#note19j) and glory in all things, as hath been sealed upon their heads, which glory shall be a fulness and a continuation of the [kseeds](#note19k) forever and ever.

20 Then shall they be gods, because they have no end; therefore shall they be from [aeverlasting](#note20a) to everlasting, because they continue; then shall they be above all, because all things are subject unto them. Then shall they be [bgods](#note20b), because they have [call power](#note20c), and the angels are subject unto them.

21 Verily, verily, I say unto you, except ye abide my [alaw](#note21a) ye cannot attain to this glory.

22 For [astrait](#note22a) is the gate, and narrow the [bway](#note22b) that leadeth unto the exaltation and continuation of the [clives](#note22c), and few there be that find it, because ye receive me not in the world neither do ye know me.

23 But if ye receive me in the world, then shall ye know me, and shall receive your exaltation; that [awhere](#note23a) I am ye shall be also.

24 This is [aeternal lives](#note24a)—to [bknow](#note24b) the only wise and true God, and Jesus Christ, whom he hath [csent](#note24c). I am he. Receive ye, therefore, my law.

25 [aBroad](#note25a) is the gate, and wide the way that leadeth to the [bdeaths](#note25b); and many there are that go in thereat, because they [creceive](#note25c) me not, neither do they abide in my law.

26 Verily, verily, I say unto you, if a man marry a wife according to my word, and they are sealed by the [aHoly Spirit of promise](#note26a), according to mine appointment, and he or she shall commit any sin or transgression of the new and everlasting covenant whatever, and all manner of blasphemies, and if they [bcommit](#note26b) no murder wherein they shed innocent blood, yet they shall come forth in the first resurrection, and enter into their exaltation; but they shall be destroyed in the flesh, and shall be [cdelivered](#note26c) unto the buffetings of [dSatan](#note26d) unto the day of [eredemption](#note26e), saith the Lord God.

27 The [ablasphemy](#note27a) against the Holy Ghost, which shall [bnot](#note27b) be [cforgiven](#note27c) in the world nor out of the world, is in that ye commit [dmurder](#note27d) wherein ye shed innocent blood, and assent unto my death, after ye have received my new and everlasting covenant, saith the Lord God; and he that abideth not this law can in nowise enter into my glory, but shall be [edamned](#note27e), saith the Lord.

28 I am the Lord thy God, and will give unto thee the [alaw](#note28a) of my Holy Priesthood, as was ordained by me and my Father before the world was.

29 [aAbraham](#note29a) received all things, whatsoever he received, by revelation and commandment, by my word, saith the Lord, and hath entered into his exaltation and sitteth upon his throne.

30 [aAbraham](#note30a) received promises concerning his seed, and of the fruit of his loins—from whose [bloins](#note30b) ye are, namely, my servant Joseph—which were to continue so long as they were in the world; and as touching Abraham and his seed, out of the world they should continue; both in the world and out of the world should they continue as innumerable as the [cstars](#note30c); or, if ye were to count the sand upon the seashore ye could not number them.

31 This promise is yours also, because ye are of [aAbraham](#note31a), and the promise was made unto Abraham; and by this law is the continuation of the works of my Father, wherein he glorifieth himself.

32 Go ye, therefore, and do the [aworks](#note32a) of Abraham; enter ye into my law and ye shall be saved.

33 But if ye enter not into my law ye cannot receive the promise of my Father, which he made unto Abraham.

34 God [acommanded](#note34a) Abraham, and Sarah gave [bHagar](#note34b) to Abraham to wife. And why did she do it? Because this was the law; and from Hagar sprang many people. This, therefore, was fulfilling, among other things, the promises.

35 Was Abraham, therefore, under condemnation? Verily I say unto you, Nay; for I, the Lord, [acommanded](#note35a) it.

36 Abraham was [acommanded](#note36a) to offer his son Isaac; nevertheless, it was written: Thou shalt not [bkill](#note36b). Abraham, however, did not refuse, and it was accounted unto him for [crighteousness](#note36c).

37 Abraham received [aconcubines](#note37a), and they bore him children; and it was accounted unto him for righteousness, because they were given unto him, and he abode in my law; as Isaac also and [bJacob](#note37b) did none other things than that which they were commanded; and because they did none other things than that which they were commanded, they have entered into their [cexaltation](#note37c), according to the promises, and sit upon thrones, and are not angels but are gods.

38 David also received [amany wives](#note38a) and concubines, and also Solomon and Moses my servants, as also many others of my servants, from the beginning of creation until this time; and in nothing did they sin save in those things which they received not of me.

39 [aDavid’s](#note39a) wives and concubines were [bgiven](#note39b) unto him of me, by the hand of Nathan, my servant, and others of the prophets who had the [ckeys](#note39c) of this power; and in none of these things did he [dsin](#note39d) against me save in the case of [eUriah](#note39e) and his wife; and, therefore he hath [ffallen](#note39f) from his exaltation, and received his portion; and he shall not inherit them out of the world, for I [ggave](#note39g) them unto another, saith the Lord.

40 I am the Lord thy God, and I gave unto thee, my servant Joseph, an [aappointment](#note40a), and restore all things. Ask what ye will, and it shall be given unto you according to my word.

41 And as ye have asked concerning adultery, verily, verily, I say unto you, if a man [areceiveth](#note41a) a wife in the new and everlasting covenant, and if she be with another man, and I have not appointed unto her by the holy [banointing](#note41b), she hath committed [cadultery](#note41c) and shall be destroyed.

42 If she be not in the new and everlasting covenant, and she be with another man, she has [acommitted](#note42a) adultery.

43 And if her husband be with another woman, and he was under a [avow](#note43a), he hath broken his vow and hath committed adultery.

44 And if she hath not committed adultery, but is innocent and hath not broken her vow, and she knoweth it, and I reveal it unto you, my servant Joseph, then shall you have power, by the power of my Holy Priesthood, to take her and [agive](#note44a) her unto him that hath not committed [badultery](#note44b) but hath been [cfaithful](#note44c); for he shall be made ruler over many.

45 For I have conferred upon you the [akeys](#note45a) and power of the priesthood, wherein I [brestore](#note45b) all things, and make known unto you all things in due time.

46 And verily, verily, I say unto you, that whatsoever you [aseal](#note46a) on earth shall be sealed in heaven; and whatsoever you [bbind](#note46b) on earth, in my name and by my word, saith the Lord, it shall be eternally bound in the heavens; and whosesoever sins you [cremit](#note46c) on earth shall be remitted eternally in the heavens; and whosesoever sins you retain on earth shall be retained in heaven.

47 And again, verily I say, whomsoever you bless I will bless, and whomsoever you curse I will [acurse](#note47a), saith the Lord; for I, the Lord, am thy God.

48 And again, verily I say unto you, my servant Joseph, that whatsoever you give on earth, and to whomsoever you [agive](#note48a) any one on earth, by my word and according to my law, it shall be visited with blessings and not cursings, and with my power, saith the Lord, and shall be without condemnation on earth and in heaven.

49 For I am the Lord thy God, and will be [awith thee](#note49a) even unto the [bend](#note49b) of the world, and through all eternity; for verily I [cseal](#note49c) upon you your [dexaltation](#note49d), and prepare a throne for you in the kingdom of my Father, with Abraham your [efather](#note49e).

50 Behold, I have seen your [asacrifices](#note50a), and will forgive all your sins; I have seen your [bsacrifices](#note50b) in obedience to that which I have told you. Go, therefore, and I make a way for your escape, as I [caccepted](#note50c) the offering of Abraham of his son Isaac.

51 Verily, I say unto you: A commandment I give unto mine handmaid, Emma Smith, your wife, whom I have given unto you, that she stay herself and partake not of that which I commanded you to offer unto her; for I did it, saith the Lord, to [aprove](#note51a) you all, as I did Abraham, and that I might require an offering at your hand, by covenant and sacrifice.

52 And let mine handmaid, Emma Smith, [areceive](#note52a) all those that have been given unto my servant Joseph, and who are virtuous and pure before me; and those who are not pure, and have said they were pure, shall be destroyed, saith the Lord God.

53 For I am the Lord thy God, and ye shall obey my voice; and I give unto my servant Joseph that he shall be made ruler over many things; for he hath been [afaithful](#note53a) over a few things, and from henceforth I will strengthen him.

54 And I command mine handmaid, Emma Smith, to abide and [acleave](#note54a) unto my servant Joseph, and to none else. But if she will not abide this commandment she shall be [bdestroyed](#note54b), saith the Lord; for I am the Lord thy God, and will destroy her if she abide not in my law.

55 But if she will not abide this commandment, then shall my servant Joseph do all things for her, even as he hath said; and I will bless him and multiply him and give unto him an [ahundred-fold](#note55a) in this world, of fathers and mothers, brothers and sisters, houses and lands, wives and children, and crowns of [beternal lives](#note55b) in the eternal worlds.

56 And again, verily I say, let mine handmaid [aforgive](#note56a) my servant Joseph his trespasses; and then shall she be forgiven her trespasses, wherein she has trespassed against me; and I, the Lord thy God, will bless her, and multiply her, and make her heart to [brejoice](#note56b).

57 And again, I say, let not my servant Joseph put his property out of his hands, lest an enemy come and destroy him; for [aSatan](#note57a) [bseeketh](#note57b) to destroy; for I am the Lord thy God, and he is my servant; and behold, and lo, I am with him, as I was with Abraham, thy father, even unto his [cexaltation](#note57c) and glory.

58 Now, as touching the law of the [apriesthood](#note58a), there are many things pertaining thereunto.

59 Verily, if a man be called of my Father, as was [aAaron](#note59a), by mine own voice, and by the voice of him that [bsent](#note59b) me, and I have endowed him with the [ckeys](#note59c) of the power of this priesthood, if he do anything in my name, and according to my law and by my word, he will not commit [dsin](#note59d), and I will justify him.

60 Let no one, therefore, set on my servant Joseph; for I will justify him; for he shall do the sacrifice which I require at his hands for his transgressions, saith the Lord your God.

61 And again, as pertaining to the law of the priesthood—if any man espouse a virgin, and desire to espouse [aanother](#note61a), and the first give her consent, and if he espouse the second, and they are virgins, and have vowed to no other man, then is he justified; he cannot commit adultery for they are given unto him; for he cannot commit adultery with that that belongeth unto him and to no one else.

62 And if he have [aten](#note62a) virgins given unto him by this law, he cannot commit adultery, for they belong to him, and they are given unto him; therefore is he justified.

63 But if one or either of the ten virgins, after she is espoused, shall be with another man, she has committed adultery, and shall be destroyed; for they are given unto him to [amultiply](#note63a) and replenish the earth, according to my commandment, and to fulfil the promise which was given by my Father before the foundation of the world, and for their exaltation in the eternal worlds, that they may bear the souls of men; for herein is the work of my Father continued, that he may be [bglorified](#note63b).

64 And again, verily, verily, I say unto you, if any man have a wife, who holds the keys of this power, and he teaches unto her the law of my priesthood, as pertaining to these things, then shall she believe and administer unto him, or she shall be destroyed, saith the Lord your God; for I will destroy her; for I will magnify my name upon all those who receive and abide in my law.

65 Therefore, it shall be lawful in me, if she receive not this law, for him to receive all things whatsoever I, the Lord his God, will give unto him, because she did not believe and administer unto him according to my word; and she then becomes the transgressor; and he is exempt from the law of Sarah, who administered unto Abraham according to the law when I commanded Abraham to take [aHagar](#note65a) to wife.

66 And now, as pertaining to this law, verily, verily, I say unto you, I will reveal more unto you, hereafter; therefore, let this suffice for the present. Behold, I am Alpha and Omega. Amen.

Section 133

Revelation given through Joseph Smith the Prophet, at Hiram, Ohio, November 3, 1831. Prefacing this revelation, Joseph Smith’s history states, “At this time there were many things which the Elders desired to know relative to preaching the Gospel to the inhabitants of the earth, and concerning the gathering; and in order to walk by the true light, and be instructed from on high, on the 3rd of November, 1831, I inquired of the Lord and received the following important revelation.” This section was first added to the book of Doctrine and Covenants as an appendix and was subsequently assigned a section number.

[1–6](133.xhtml#p1), The Saints are commanded to prepare for the Second Coming; [7–16](133.xhtml#p7), All men are commanded to flee from Babylon, come to Zion, and prepare for the great day of the Lord; [17–35](133.xhtml#p17), He will stand on Mount Zion, the continents will become one land, and the lost tribes of Israel will return; [36–40](133.xhtml#p36), The gospel was restored through Joseph Smith to be preached in all the world; [41–51](133.xhtml#p41), The Lord will come down in vengeance upon the wicked; [52–56](133.xhtml#p52), It will be the year of His redeemed; [57–74](133.xhtml#p57), The gospel is to be sent forth to save the Saints and for the destruction of the wicked.

1 Hearken, O ye people of my church, saith the Lord your God, and hear the word of the Lord concerning you—

2 The Lord who shall suddenly [acome](#note2a) to his temple; the Lord who shall come down upon the world with a curse to [bjudgment](#note2b); yea, upon all the nations that [cforget](#note2c) God, and upon all the ungodly among you.

3 For he shall make [abare](#note3a) his holy arm in the eyes of all the nations, and all the ends of the earth shall see the [bsalvation](#note3b) of their God.

4 Wherefore, prepare ye, prepare ye, O my people; sanctify yourselves; gather ye together, O ye people of my church, upon the land of Zion, all you that have not been commanded to [atarry](#note4a).

5 Go ye out from [aBabylon](#note5a). Be ye [bclean](#note5b) that bear the vessels of the Lord.

6 Call your [asolemn assemblies](#note6a), and [bspeak](#note6b) often one to another. And let every man call upon the name of the Lord.

7 Yea, verily I say unto you again, the time has come when the voice of the Lord is unto you: Go ye out of Babylon; [agather](#note7a) ye out from among the nations, from the [bfour](#note7b) winds, from one end of heaven to the other.

8 Send forth the elders of my church unto the [anations](#note8a) which are afar off; unto the [bislands](#note8b) of the sea; send forth unto foreign lands; call upon all nations, first upon the [cGentiles](#note8c), and then upon the Jews.

9 And behold, and lo, this shall be their cry, and the voice of the Lord unto all people: Go ye forth unto the land of Zion, that the borders of my people may be enlarged, and that her [astakes](#note9a) may be strengthened, and that [bZion](#note9b) may go forth unto the regions round about.

10 Yea, let the cry go forth among all people: Awake and arise and go forth to meet the [aBridegroom](#note10a); behold and lo, the Bridegroom cometh; go ye out to meet him. Prepare yourselves for the [bgreat day](#note10b) of the Lord.

11 [aWatch](#note11a), therefore, for ye [bknow](#note11b) neither the day nor the hour.

12 Let them, therefore, who are [aamong](#note12a) the Gentiles flee unto [bZion](#note12b).

13 And let them who be of [aJudah](#note13a) flee unto [bJerusalem](#note13b), unto the [cmountains](#note13c) of the Lord’s [dhouse](#note13d).

14 Go ye [aout](#note14a) from among the nations, even from [bBabylon](#note14b), from the midst of [cwickedness](#note14c), which is spiritual Babylon.

15 But verily, thus saith the Lord, let not your flight be in [ahaste](#note15a), but let all things be prepared before you; and he that goeth, let him [bnot](#note15b) look back lest sudden destruction shall come upon him.

16 Hearken and hear, O ye inhabitants of the earth. [aListen](#note16a), ye elders of my church together, and hear the voice of the Lord; for he calleth upon all men, and he commandeth all men everywhere to [brepent](#note16b).

17 For behold, the Lord God hath [asent](#note17a) forth the angel crying through the midst of heaven, saying: Prepare ye the way of the Lord, and make his paths straight, for the hour of his [bcoming](#note17b) is nigh—

18 When the [aLamb](#note18a) shall stand upon [bMount Zion](#note18b), and with him a [chundred](#note18c) and forty-four thousand, having his Father’s name written on their foreheads.

19 Wherefore, prepare ye for the [acoming](#note19a) of the Bridegroom; go ye, go ye out to meet him.

20 For behold, he shall [astand](#note20a) upon the mount of Olivet, and upon the mighty ocean, even the great deep, and upon the islands of the sea, and upon the land of Zion.

21 And he shall [autter](#note21a) his voice out of [bZion](#note21b), and he shall speak from Jerusalem, and his [cvoice](#note21c) shall be heard among all people;

22 And it shall be a voice as the [avoice](#note22a) of many waters, and as the voice of a great [bthunder](#note22b), which shall [cbreak](#note22c) down the mountains, and the valleys shall not be found.

23 He shall command the great deep, and it shall be driven back into the north countries, and the [aislands](#note23a) shall become one land;

24 And the [aland of Jerusalem](#note24a) and the land of [bZion](#note24b) shall be turned back into their own place, and the [cearth](#note24c) shall be like as it was in the days before it was [ddivided](#note24d).

25 And the Lord, even the Savior, shall [astand](#note25a) in the midst of his people, and shall [breign](#note25b) over all flesh.

26 And they who are in the [anorth](#note26a) countries shall come in remembrance before the Lord; and their prophets shall hear his voice, and shall no longer stay themselves; and they shall [bsmite](#note26b) the rocks, and the ice shall flow down at their presence.

27 And an [ahighway](#note27a) shall be cast up in the midst of the great deep.

28 Their enemies shall become a prey unto them,

29 And in the [abarren](#note29a) deserts there shall come forth pools of [bliving water](#note29b); and the parched ground shall no longer be a thirsty land.

30 And they shall bring forth their rich [atreasures](#note30a) unto the children of Ephraim, my servants.

31 And the boundaries of the everlasting [ahills](#note31a) shall tremble at their presence.

32 And there shall they fall down and be [acrowned](#note32a) with glory, even in Zion, by the hands of the servants of the Lord, even the children of [bEphraim](#note32b).

33 And they shall be filled with [asongs](#note33a) of everlasting joy.

34 Behold, this is the [ablessing](#note34a) of the [beverlasting](#note34b) God upon the [ctribes](#note34c) of [dIsrael](#note34d), and the richer blessing upon the head of [eEphraim](#note34e) and his fellows.

35 And they also of the tribe of [aJudah](#note35a), after their pain, shall be [bsanctified](#note35b) in [choliness](#note35c) before the Lord, to dwell in his [dpresence](#note35d) day and night, forever and ever.

36 And now, verily saith the Lord, that these things might be known among you, O inhabitants of the earth, I have sent forth mine [aangel](#note36a) flying through the midst of heaven, having the everlasting [bgospel](#note36b), who hath appeared unto some and hath committed it unto man, who shall appear unto [cmany](#note36c) that dwell on the earth.

37 And this [agospel](#note37a) shall be [bpreached](#note37b) unto [cevery](#note37c) nation, and kindred, and tongue, and people.

38 And the [aservants](#note38a) of God shall go forth, saying with a loud voice: Fear God and give glory to him, for the hour of his judgment is come;

39 And [aworship](#note39a) him that made heaven, and earth, and the sea, and the [bfountains](#note39b) of waters—

40 Calling upon the name of the Lord day and night, saying: O that thou wouldst [arend](#note40a) the heavens, that thou wouldst come down, that the mountains might flow down at thy presence.

41 And it shall be answered upon their heads; for the presence of the Lord shall be [aas](#note41a) the melting fire that burneth, and as the fire which causeth the waters to boil.

42 O Lord, thou shalt come down to make thy name known to thine adversaries, and all nations shall tremble at thy presence—

43 When thou doest [aterrible](#note43a) things, things they look not for;

44 Yea, when thou comest down, and the mountains flow down at thy presence, thou shalt [ameet](#note44a) him who rejoiceth and worketh righteousness, who remembereth thee in thy ways.

45 For since the beginning of the world have not men heard nor perceived by the ear, neither hath any eye seen, O God, besides thee, how great things thou hast [aprepared](#note45a) for him that [bwaiteth](#note45b) for thee.

46 And it shall be said: [aWho](#note46a) is this that cometh down from God in heaven with dyed [bgarments](#note46b); yea, from the regions which are not known, clothed in his glorious apparel, traveling in the greatness of his strength?

47 And he shall say: [aI](#note47a) am he who spake in [brighteousness](#note47b), mighty to save.

48 And the Lord shall be [ared](#note48a) in his apparel, and his garments like him that treadeth in the wine-vat.

49 And so great shall be the glory of his presence that the [asun](#note49a) shall hide his face in shame, and the moon shall withhold its light, and the stars shall be hurled from their places.

50 And his [avoice](#note50a) shall be heard: I have [btrodden](#note50b) the wine-press alone, and have brought judgment upon all people; and none were with me;

51 And I have [atrampled](#note51a) them in my fury, and I did tread upon them in mine anger, and their blood have I [bsprinkled](#note51b) upon my garments, and stained all my raiment; for this was the [cday](#note51c) of vengeance which was in my heart.

52 And now the year of my [aredeemed](#note52a) is come; and they shall mention the loving kindness of their Lord, and all that he has bestowed upon them according to his [bgoodness](#note52b), and according to his loving kindness, forever and ever.

53 In all their [aafflictions](#note53a) he was afflicted. And the angel of his presence saved them; and in his [blove](#note53b), and in his pity, he [credeemed](#note53c) them, and bore them, and carried them all the days of old;

54 Yea, and [aEnoch](#note54a) also, and they who were with him; the prophets who were before him; and [bNoah](#note54b) also, and they who were before him; and [cMoses](#note54c) also, and they who were before him;

55 And from [aMoses](#note55a) to Elijah, and from Elijah to John, who were with Christ in his [bresurrection](#note55b), and the holy apostles, with Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob, shall be in the presence of the Lamb.

56 And the [agraves](#note56a) of the [bsaints](#note56b) shall be [copened](#note56c); and they shall come forth and stand on the [dright hand](#note56d) of the Lamb, when he shall stand upon [eMount Zion](#note56e), and upon the holy city, the New Jerusalem; and they shall [fsing](#note56f) the [gsong](#note56g) of the [hLamb](#note56h), day and night forever and ever.

57 And for this cause, that men might be made [apartakers](#note57a) of the [bglories](#note57b) which were to be revealed, the Lord sent forth the fulness of his [cgospel](#note57c), his everlasting covenant, reasoning in plainness and simplicity—

58 To [aprepare](#note58a) the [bweak](#note58b) for those things which are coming on the earth, and for the Lord’s errand in the day when the weak shall [cconfound](#note58c) the [dwise](#note58d), and the little one become a [estrong](#note58e) [fnation](#note58f), and two shall put their tens of thousands to [gflight](#note58g).

59 And by the weak things of the earth the Lord shall [athresh](#note59a) the nations by the power of his Spirit.

60 And for this cause these commandments were given; they were commanded to be kept from the world in the day that they were given, but now are to [ago forth](#note60a) unto [ball](#note60b) flesh—

61 And this according to the mind and will of the Lord, who ruleth over all flesh.

62 And unto him that repenteth and [asanctifieth](#note62a) himself before the Lord shall be given eternal life.

63 And upon them that [ahearken not](#note63a) to the voice of the Lord shall be fulfilled that which was written by the prophet Moses, that they should be [bcut off](#note63b) from among the people.

64 And also that which was written by the prophet [aMalachi](#note64a): For, behold, the [bday](#note64b) cometh that shall [cburn](#note64c) as an oven, and all the proud, yea, and all that do [dwickedly](#note64d), shall be stubble; and the day that cometh shall burn them up, saith the Lord of hosts, that it shall leave them neither root nor branch.

65 Wherefore, this shall be the answer of the Lord unto them:

66 In that day when I came unto mine own, no man among you [areceived](#note66a) me, and you were driven out.

67 When I called again there was none of you to answer; yet my [aarm](#note67a) was not shortened at all that I could not redeem, neither my [bpower](#note67b) to deliver.

68 Behold, at my rebuke I [adry up](#note68a) the sea. I make the rivers a wilderness; their fish stink, and die for thirst.

69 I clothe the heavens with blackness, and make sackcloth their covering.

70 And [athis](#note70a) shall ye have of my hand—ye shall lie down in sorrow.

71 Behold, and lo, there are none to deliver you; for ye [aobeyed not](#note71a) my voice when I called to you out of the heavens; ye [bbelieved not](#note71b) my servants, and when they were [csent](#note71c) unto you ye received them not.

72 Wherefore, they sealed up the testimony and bound up the law, and ye were delivered over unto [adarkness](#note72a).

73 These shall go away into outer darkness, where there is [aweeping](#note73a), and wailing, and gnashing of teeth.

74 Behold the Lord your God hath spoken it. Amen.

Section 134

A declaration of belief regarding governments and laws in general, adopted by unanimous vote at a general assembly of the Church held at Kirtland, Ohio, August 17, 1835. Many Saints gathered together to consider the proposed contents of the first edition of the Doctrine and Covenants. At that time, this declaration was given the following preamble: “That our belief with regard to earthly governments and laws in general may not be misinterpreted nor misunderstood, we have thought proper to present, at the close of this volume, our opinion concerning the same.”

[1–4](134.xhtml#p1), Governments should preserve freedom of conscience and worship; [5–8](134.xhtml#p5), All men should uphold their governments and owe respect and deference to the law; [9–10](134.xhtml#p9), Religious societies should not exercise civil powers; [11–12](134.xhtml#p11), Men are justified in defending themselves and their property.

1 We believe that [agovernments](#note1a) were instituted of God for the benefit of man; and that he holds men [baccountable](#note1b) for their acts in relation to them, both in making laws and administering them, for the good and safety of society.

2 We believe that no government can exist in [apeace](#note2a), except such laws are framed and held inviolate as will secure to each individual the [bfree](#note2b) exercise of [cconscience](#note2c), the right and control of property, and the [dprotection](#note2d) of life.

3 We believe that all governments necessarily require [acivil](#note3a) [bofficers](#note3b) and magistrates to enforce the laws of the same; and that such as will administer the law in equity and justice should be sought for and upheld by the voice of the people if a republic, or the will of the sovereign.

4 We believe that religion is instituted of God; and that men are amenable to him, and to him only, for the exercise of it, unless their religious opinions prompt them to infringe upon the rights and liberties of others; but we do not believe that human law has a right to interfere in prescribing rules of [aworship](#note4a) to bind the consciences of men, nor dictate forms for public or private devotion; that the civil magistrate should restrain crime, but never control conscience; should punish [bguilt](#note4b), but never suppress the freedom of the soul.

5 We believe that all men are bound to [asustain](#note5a) and uphold the respective [bgovernments](#note5b) in which they reside, while protected in their inherent and inalienable rights by the laws of such governments; and that sedition and [crebellion](#note5c) are unbecoming every citizen thus protected, and should be punished accordingly; and that all governments have a right to enact such laws as in their own judgments are best calculated to secure the public interest; at the same time, however, holding sacred the freedom of conscience.

6 We believe that every man should be [ahonored](#note6a) in his station, rulers and magistrates as such, being placed for the protection of the innocent and the punishment of the guilty; and that to the [blaws](#note6b) all men owe [crespect](#note6c) and deference, as without them peace and harmony would be supplanted by anarchy and terror; human laws being instituted for the express purpose of regulating our interests as individuals and nations, between man and man; and divine laws given of heaven, prescribing rules on spiritual concerns, for faith and worship, both to be answered by man to his Maker.

7 We believe that rulers, states, and governments have a right, and are bound to enact laws for the protection of all [acitizens](#note7a) in the free exercise of their religious [bbelief](#note7b); but we do not believe that they have a right in justice to deprive citizens of this privilege, or proscribe them in their opinions, so long as a regard and reverence are shown to the laws and such religious opinions do not justify sedition nor conspiracy.

8 We believe that the commission of crime should be [apunished](#note8a) according to the nature of the offense; that murder, treason, robbery, theft, and the breach of the general peace, in all respects, should be punished according to their criminality and their tendency to evil among men, by the laws of that government in which the offense is committed; and for the public [bpeace](#note8b) and tranquility all men should step forward and use their ability in bringing [coffenders](#note8c) against good laws to punishment.

9 We do not believe it just to [amingle](#note9a) religious influence with civil government, whereby one religious society is fostered and another proscribed in its spiritual privileges, and the individual rights of its members, as citizens, denied.

10 We believe that all religious societies have a right to deal with their members for disorderly conduct, [aaccording](#note10a) to the rules and regulations of such societies; provided that such dealings be for fellowship and good standing; but we do not believe that any religious society has [bauthority](#note10b) to try men on the right of property or life, to take from them this world’s goods, or to put them in jeopardy of either life or limb, or to inflict any physical punishment upon them. They can only excommunicate them from their society, and withdraw from them their fellowship.

11 We believe that men should appeal to the civil law for redress of all [awrongs](#note11a) and grievances, where personal abuse is inflicted or the right of property or character infringed, where such laws exist as will protect the same; but we believe that all men are justified in [bdefending](#note11b) themselves, their friends, and property, and the government, from the unlawful assaults and encroachments of all persons in times of exigency, where immediate appeal cannot be made to the laws, and relief afforded.

12 We believe it just to [apreach](#note12a) the gospel to the nations of the earth, and warn the righteous to save themselves from the corruption of the world; but we do not believe it right to interfere with [bbond-servants](#note12b), neither preach the gospel to, nor baptize them contrary to the will and wish of their masters, nor to meddle with or influence them in the least to cause them to be dissatisfied with their situations in this life, thereby jeopardizing the lives of men; such interference we believe to be unlawful and unjust, and dangerous to the peace of every government allowing human beings to be held in [cservitude](#note12c).

Section 135

Announcement of the martyrdom of Joseph Smith the Prophet and his brother, Hyrum Smith the Patriarch, at Carthage, Illinois, June 27, 1844. This document was included at the end of the 1844 edition of the Doctrine and Covenants, which was nearly ready for publication when Joseph and Hyrum Smith were murdered.

[1–2](135.xhtml#p1), Joseph and Hyrum martyred in Carthage Jail; [3](135.xhtml#p3), The preeminent position of the Prophet is acclaimed; [4–7](135.xhtml#p4), Their innocent blood testifies of the truth and divinity of the work.

1 To seal the testimony of this book and the Book of Mormon, we announce the [amartyrdom](#note1a) of Joseph Smith the Prophet, and Hyrum Smith the Patriarch. They were shot in Carthage jail, on the 27th of June, 1844, about five o’clock p.m., by an armed mob—painted black—of from 150 to 200 persons. [bHyrum](#note1b) was shot first and fell calmly, exclaiming: *I am a [cdead](#note1c) man!* Joseph leaped from the window, and was shot dead in the attempt, exclaiming: *[dO](#note1d) Lord my God!* They were both shot after they were dead, in a brutal manner, and both received four balls.

2 John Taylor and Willard Richards, two of the Twelve, were the only persons in the room at the time; the former was wounded in a savage manner with four balls, but has since recovered; the latter, through the providence of God, escaped, without even a hole in his robe.

3 Joseph Smith, the [aProphet](#note3a) and [bSeer](#note3b) of the Lord, has done more, [csave](#note3c) Jesus only, for the salvation of men in this world, than any other man that ever lived in it. In the short space of twenty years, he has brought forth the Book of Mormon, which he translated by the gift and power of God, and has been the means of publishing it on two continents; has sent the [dfulness](#note3d) of the everlasting gospel, which it contained, to the four quarters of the earth; has brought forth the revelations and commandments which compose this book of Doctrine and Covenants, and many other wise documents and instructions for the benefit of the children of men; gathered many thousands of the Latter-day Saints, founded a great city, and left a fame and name that cannot be slain. He lived great, and he died great in the eyes of God and his people; and like most of the Lord’s anointed in ancient times, has sealed his mission and his works with his own [eblood](#note3e); and so has his brother Hyrum. In life they were not divided, and in death they were not [fseparated](#note3f)!

4 When Joseph went to Carthage to deliver himself up to the pretended requirements of the law, two or three days previous to his assassination, he said: “I am going like a [alamb](#note4a) to the slaughter; but I am calm as a summer’s morning; I have a [bconscience](#note4b) [cvoid](#note4c) of offense towards God, and towards all men. I shall die innocent, and it shall yet be said of me—he was murdered in cold blood.”—The same morning, after Hyrum had made ready to go—shall it be said to the slaughter? yes, for so it was—he read the following paragraph, near the close of the twelfth chapter of Ether, in the Book of Mormon, and turned down the leaf upon it:

5 *And it came to pass that I prayed unto the Lord that he would give unto the Gentiles grace, that they might have charity. And it came to pass that the Lord said unto me: If they have not charity it mattereth not unto thee, thou hast been [afaithful](#note5a); wherefore thy garments shall be made [bclean](#note5b). And because thou hast seen thy weakness, thou shalt be made strong, even unto the sitting down in the place which I have prepared in the mansions of my Father. And now I … bid farewell unto the Gentiles; yea, and also unto my brethren whom I love, until we shall meet before the [cjudgment-seat](#note5c) of Christ, where all men shall know that my garments are not spotted with your blood.* The [dtestators](#note5d) are now dead, and their [etestament](#note5e) is in force.

6 Hyrum Smith was forty-four years old in February, 1844, and Joseph Smith was thirty-eight in December, 1843; and henceforward their names will be classed among the [amartyrs](#note6a) of religion; and the reader in every nation will be reminded that the Book of Mormon, and this book of Doctrine and Covenants of the church, cost the best blood of the nineteenth century to bring them forth for the salvation of a ruined world; and that if the fire can scathe a [bgreen tree](#note6b) for the glory of God, how easy it will burn up the dry trees to purify the vineyard of corruption. They lived for glory; they died for glory; and glory is their eternal [creward](#note6c). From age to age shall their names go down to posterity as gems for the sanctified.

7 They were innocent of any crime, as they had often been proved before, and were only confined in jail by the conspiracy of traitors and wicked men; and their *innocent blood* on the floor of Carthage jail is a broad seal affixed to “Mormonism” that cannot be rejected by any court on earth, and their *innocent blood* on the [aescutcheon](#note7a) of the State of Illinois, with the broken faith of the State as pledged by the governor, is a witness to the truth of the everlasting gospel that all the world cannot impeach; and their *innocent blood* on the banner of liberty, and on the *magna charta* of the United States, is an ambassador for the religion of Jesus Christ, that will touch the hearts of honest men among all nations; and their *innocent blood,* with the innocent blood of all the martyrs under the [baltar](#note7b) that John saw, will cry unto the Lord of Hosts till he avenges that blood on the earth. Amen.

Section 136

The word and will of the Lord, given through President Brigham Young at Winter Quarters, the camp of Israel, Omaha Nation, on the west bank of the Missouri River, near Council Bluffs, Iowa.

[1–16](136.xhtml#p1), How the camp of Israel is to be organized for the westward journey is explained; [17–27](136.xhtml#p17), The Saints are commanded to live by numerous gospel standards; [28–33](136.xhtml#p28), The Saints should sing, dance, pray, and learn wisdom; [34–42](136.xhtml#p34), Prophets are slain so that they might be honored and the wicked condemned.

1 The Word and [aWill](#note1a) of the Lord concerning the Camp of [bIsrael](#note1b) in their journeyings to the West:

2 Let all the people of [aThe](#note2a) Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, and those who journey with them, be organized into companies, with a covenant and promise to [bkeep](#note2b) all the commandments and statutes of the Lord our God.

3 Let the companies be organized with captains of [ahundreds](#note3a), captains of fifties, and captains of tens, with a president and his two counselors at their head, under the direction of the Twelve [bApostles](#note3b).

4 And this shall be our [acovenant](#note4a)—that we will [bwalk](#note4b) in all the [cordinances](#note4c) of the Lord.

5 Let each company provide themselves with all the teams, wagons, provisions, clothing, and other necessaries for the journey, that they can.

6 When the companies are organized let them go to with their [amight](#note6a), to prepare for those who are to tarry.

7 Let each company, with their captains and presidents, decide how many can go next spring; then choose out a sufficient number of able-bodied and expert men, to take teams, seeds, and farming utensils, to go as pioneers to prepare for putting in spring crops.

8 Let each company [abear](#note8a) an equal proportion, according to the dividend of their property, in taking the poor, the [bwidows](#note8b), the [cfatherless](#note8c), and the families of those who have gone into the army, that the cries of the widow and the [dfatherless](#note8d) come not up into the ears of the Lord against this people.

9 Let each company prepare houses, and fields for raising [agrain](#note9a), for those who are to remain behind this season; and this is the will of the Lord concerning his people.

10 Let every man use all his influence and property to remove this people to the place where the Lord shall locate a [astake](#note10a) of Zion.

11 And if ye do this with a pure heart, in all faithfulness, ye shall be blessed; you shall be [ablessed](#note11a) in your flocks, and in your herds, and in your fields, and in your houses, and in your families.

12 Let my servants Ezra T. Benson and Erastus Snow organize a company.

13 And let my servants Orson Pratt and Wilford Woodruff organize a company.

14 Also, let my servants Amasa Lyman and George A. Smith organize a company.

15 And appoint presidents, and [acaptains](#note15a) of hundreds, and of fifties, and of tens.

16 And let my servants that have been appointed go and [ateach](#note16a) this, my will, to the saints, that they may be ready to go to a land of peace.

17 Go thy way and do as I have told you, and [afear not](#note17a) thine enemies; for they shall not have power to stop my work.

18 Zion shall be [aredeemed](#note18a) in mine own due time.

19 And if any man shall seek to build up himself, and seeketh not my [acounsel](#note19a), he shall have no power, and his [bfolly](#note19b) shall be made manifest.

20 [aSeek ye](#note20a); and keep all your [bpledges](#note20b) one with another; and [ccovet](#note20c) not that which is thy brother’s.

21 [aKeep](#note21a) yourselves from evil to take the name of the Lord in vain, for I am the Lord your God, even the [bGod](#note21b) of your fathers, the God of Abraham and of Isaac and of Jacob.

22 I am he who [aled](#note22a) the children of Israel out of the land of Egypt; and my arm is stretched out in the last days, to [bsave](#note22b) my people Israel.

23 Cease to [acontend](#note23a) one with another; cease to speak [bevil](#note23b) one of another.

24 Cease [adrunkenness](#note24a); and let your words tend to [bedifying](#note24b) one another.

25 If thou [aborrowest](#note25a) of thy [bneighbor](#note25b), thou shalt [crestore](#note25c) that which thou hast borrowed; and if thou canst not repay then go straightway and tell thy neighbor, lest he condemn thee.

26 If thou shalt find that which thy neighbor has [alost](#note26a), thou shalt make diligent search till thou shalt [bdeliver](#note26b) it to him again.

27 Thou shalt be [adiligent](#note27a) in [bpreserving](#note27b) what thou hast, that thou mayest be a wise [csteward](#note27c); for it is the free gift of the Lord thy God, and thou art his steward.

28 If thou art [amerry](#note28a), [bpraise](#note28b) the Lord with singing, with music, with [cdancing](#note28c), and with a [dprayer](#note28d) of praise and [ethanksgiving](#note28e).

29 If thou art [asorrowful](#note29a), call on the Lord thy God with supplication, that your souls may be [bjoyful](#note29b).

30 Fear not thine [aenemies](#note30a), for they are in mine hands and I will do my pleasure with them.

31 My people must be [atried](#note31a) in all things, that they may be prepared to receive the [bglory](#note31b) that I have for them, even the glory of Zion; and he that will not [cbear](#note31c) chastisement is not worthy of my kingdom.

32 Let him that is [aignorant](#note32a) [blearn](#note32b) [cwisdom](#note32c) by [dhumbling](#note32d) himself and calling upon the Lord his God, that his [eeyes](#note32e) may be opened that he may see, and his ears opened that he may hear;

33 For my [aSpirit](#note33a) is sent forth into the world to enlighten the [bhumble](#note33b) and contrite, and to the [ccondemnation](#note33c) of the ungodly.

34 Thy brethren have rejected you and your testimony, even the nation that has [adriven](#note34a) you out;

35 And now cometh the day of their calamity, even the days of sorrow, like a woman that is taken in travail; and their [asorrow](#note35a) shall be great unless they speedily repent, yea, very speedily.

36 For they [akilled](#note36a) the prophets, and them that were sent unto them; and they have [bshed](#note36b) innocent blood, which crieth from the ground against them.

37 Therefore, marvel not at these things, for ye are not yet [apure](#note37a); ye can not yet bear my glory; but ye shall behold it if ye are faithful in keeping all my words that I have [bgiven](#note37b) you, from the days of Adam to Abraham, from Abraham to Moses, from Moses to Jesus and his apostles, and from Jesus and his apostles to Joseph Smith, whom I did call upon by mine [cangels](#note37c), my ministering servants, and by mine own voice out of the heavens, to bring forth my work;

38 Which [afoundation](#note38a) he did lay, and was faithful; and I took him to myself.

39 Many have marveled because of his death; but it was needful that he should [aseal](#note39a) his [btestimony](#note39b) with his [cblood](#note39c), that he might be [dhonored](#note39d) and the wicked might be condemned.

40 Have I not delivered you from your [aenemies](#note40a), only in that I have left a witness of my name?

41 Now, therefore, hearken, O ye people of my [achurch](#note41a); and ye elders listen together; you have received my [bkingdom](#note41b).

42 Be [adiligent](#note42a) in keeping all my commandments, lest judgments come upon you, and your faith fail you, and your enemies triumph over you. So no more at present. Amen and Amen.

Section 137

A vision given to Joseph Smith the Prophet, in the temple at Kirtland, Ohio, January 21, 1836. The occasion was the administration of ordinances in preparation for the dedication of the temple.

[1–6](137.xhtml#p1), The Prophet sees his brother Alvin in the celestial kingdom; [7–9](137.xhtml#p7), The doctrine of salvation for the dead is revealed; [10](137.xhtml#p10), All children are saved in the celestial kingdom.

1 The [aheavens](#note1a) were [bopened](#note1b) upon us, and I beheld the [ccelestial](#note1c) kingdom of God, and the glory thereof, whether in the [dbody](#note1d) or out I cannot tell.

2 I saw the transcendent [abeauty](#note2a) of the [bgate](#note2b) through which the heirs of that kingdom will enter, which was [clike](#note2c) unto [dcircling](#note2d) flames of fire;

3 Also the [ablazing](#note3a) [bthrone](#note3b) of God, whereon was seated the [cFather](#note3c) and the [dSon](#note3d).

4 I saw the beautiful streets of that kingdom, which had the appearance of being paved with [agold](#note4a).

5 I saw Father [aAdam](#note5a) and [bAbraham](#note5b); and my [cfather](#note5c) and my mother; my brother [dAlvin](#note5d), that has long since [eslept](#note5e);

6 And [amarveled](#note6a) how it was that he had obtained an [binheritance](#note6b) in that kingdom, seeing that he had departed this life before the Lord had set his hand to [cgather](#note6c) Israel the second time, and had not been [dbaptized](#note6d) for the remission of sins.

7 Thus came the [avoice](#note7a) of the Lord unto me, saying: All who have died [bwithout](#note7b) a knowledge of this gospel, who would have received it if they had been permitted to tarry, shall be [cheirs](#note7c) of the celestial kingdom of God;

8 Also all that shall die henceforth without a knowledge of it, who [awould](#note8a) have received it with all their hearts, shall be heirs of that kingdom;

9 For I, the Lord, will [ajudge](#note9a) all men according to their [bworks](#note9b), according to the [cdesire](#note9c) of their hearts.

10 And I also beheld that all [achildren](#note10a) who die before they arrive at the [byears](#note10b) of accountability are [csaved](#note10c) in the celestial kingdom of heaven.

Section 138

A vision given to President Joseph F. Smith in Salt Lake City, Utah, on October 3, 1918. In his opening address at the 89th Semiannual General Conference of the Church, on October 4, 1918, President Smith declared that he had received several divine communications during the previous months. One of these, concerning the Savior’s visit to the spirits of the dead while His body was in the tomb, President Smith had received the previous day. It was written immediately following the close of the conference. On October 31, 1918, it was submitted to the counselors in the First Presidency, the Council of the Twelve, and the Patriarch, and it was unanimously accepted by them.

[1–10](138.xhtml#p1), President Joseph F. Smith ponders upon the writings of Peter and our Lord’s visit to the spirit world; [11–24](138.xhtml#p11), President Smith sees the righteous dead assembled in paradise and Christ’s ministry among them; [25–37](138.xhtml#p25), He sees how the preaching of the gospel was organized among the spirits; [38–52](138.xhtml#p38), He sees Adam, Eve, and many of the holy prophets in the spirit world who considered their spirit state before their resurrection as a bondage; [53–60](138.xhtml#p53), The righteous dead of this day continue their labors in the world of spirits.

1 On the third of October, in the year nineteen hundred and eighteen, I sat in my room [apondering](#note1a) over the scriptures;

2 And [areflecting](#note2a) upon the great [batoning](#note2b) [csacrifice](#note2c) that was made by the Son of God, for the [dredemption](#note2d) of the world;

3 And the great and wonderful [alove](#note3a) made manifest by the Father and the Son in the coming of the [bRedeemer](#note3b) into the world;

4 That through his [aatonement](#note4a), and by [bobedience](#note4b) to the principles of the gospel, mankind might be saved.

5 While I was thus engaged, my mind reverted to the writings of the apostle Peter, to the [aprimitive saints](#note5a) scattered abroad throughout [bPontus](#note5b), Galatia, Cappadocia, and other parts of Asia, where the gospel had been [cpreached](#note5c) after the crucifixion of the Lord.

6 I opened the Bible and read the [athird](#note6a) and fourth chapters of the first epistle of Peter, and as I read I was greatly [bimpressed](#note6b), more than I had ever been before, with the following passages:

7 “For Christ also hath once suffered for sins, the just for the unjust, that he might bring us to God, being put to death in the flesh, but quickened by the Spirit:

8 “By which also he went and preached unto the spirits in [aprison](#note8a);

9 “Which sometime were disobedient, when once the longsuffering of God waited in the days of Noah, while the ark was a preparing, wherein few, that is, eight souls were saved by water.” (1 Peter 3:18–20.)

10 “For for this cause was the gospel preached also to them that are dead, that they might be judged according to men in the flesh, but live according to God in the spirit.” (1 Peter 4:6.)

11 As I [apondered](#note11a) over these things which are [bwritten](#note11b), the [ceyes](#note11c) of my [dunderstanding](#note11d) were opened, and the Spirit of the Lord [erested](#note11e) upon me, and I saw the hosts of the [fdead](#note11f), both small and great.

12 And there were gathered together in one place an innumerable company of the spirits of the [ajust](#note12a), who had been [bfaithful](#note12b) in the [ctestimony](#note12c) of Jesus while they lived in mortality;

13 And who had offered [asacrifice](#note13a) in the [bsimilitude](#note13b) of the great sacrifice of the Son of God, and had suffered [ctribulation](#note13c) in their Redeemer’s [dname](#note13d).

14 All these had departed the mortal life, firm in the [ahope](#note14a) of a glorious [bresurrection](#note14b), through the [cgrace](#note14c) of God the [dFather](#note14d) and his [eOnly Begotten Son](#note14e), Jesus Christ.

15 I beheld that they were filled with [ajoy](#note15a) and gladness, and were rejoicing together because the day of their [bdeliverance](#note15b) was at hand.

16 They were assembled awaiting the advent of the Son of God into the [aspirit world](#note16a), to declare their [bredemption](#note16b) from the [cbands](#note16c) of death.

17 Their sleeping [adust](#note17a) was to be [brestored](#note17b) unto its [cperfect](#note17c) frame, [dbone](#note17d) to his bone, and the sinews and the flesh upon them, the [espirit](#note17e) and the body to be united never again to be divided, that they might receive a fulness of [fjoy](#note17f).

18 While this vast multitude waited and conversed, rejoicing in the hour of their [adeliverance](#note18a) from the chains of death, the Son of God appeared, declaring [bliberty](#note18b) to the [ccaptives](#note18c) who had been faithful;

19 And there he [apreached](#note19a) to them the everlasting [bgospel](#note19b), the doctrine of the [cresurrection](#note19c) and the redemption of mankind from the [dfall](#note19d), and from individual sins on conditions of [erepentance](#note19e).

20 But unto the [awicked](#note20a) he did not go, and among the ungodly and the unrepentant who had [bdefiled](#note20b) themselves while in the flesh, his voice was not raised;

21 Neither did the [arebellious](#note21a) who rejected the [btestimonies](#note21b) and the warnings of the ancient [cprophets](#note21c) behold his [dpresence](#note21d), nor look upon his face.

22 Where these were, [adarkness](#note22a) reigned, but among the righteous there was [bpeace](#note22b);

23 And the saints rejoiced in their [aredemption](#note23a), and bowed the [bknee](#note23b) and acknowledged the Son of God as their Redeemer and Deliverer from death and the [cchains](#note23c) of [dhell](#note23d).

24 Their countenances [ashone](#note24a), and the [bradiance](#note24b) from the presence of the Lord rested upon them, and they [csang](#note24c) praises unto his holy name.

25 I marveled, for I understood that the Savior spent about three years in his [aministry](#note25a) among the Jews and those of the house of Israel, endeavoring to [bteach](#note25b) them the everlasting gospel and call them unto repentance;

26 And yet, notwithstanding his mighty works, and miracles, and proclamation of the truth, in great [apower](#note26a) and authority, there were but [bfew](#note26b) who hearkened to his voice, and rejoiced in his presence, and received salvation at his hands.

27 But his ministry among those who were dead was limited to the [abrief](#note27a) time intervening between the crucifixion and his resurrection;

28 And I wondered at the words of Peter—wherein he said that the Son of God preached unto the [aspirits](#note28a) in prison, who sometime were disobedient, when once the long-suffering of God waited in the days of Noah—and how it was possible for him to preach to those spirits and perform the necessary labor among them in so short a time.

29 And as I wondered, my eyes were opened, and my understanding [aquickened](#note29a), and I perceived that the Lord went not in person among the [bwicked](#note29b) and the disobedient who had rejected the truth, to teach them;

30 But behold, from among the righteous, he [aorganized](#note30a) his forces and appointed [bmessengers](#note30b), [cclothed](#note30c) with power and authority, and [dcommissioned](#note30d) them to go forth and carry the light of the gospel to them that were in [edarkness](#note30e), even to [fall](#note30f) the spirits of men; and thus was the gospel preached to the dead.

31 And the chosen messengers went forth to declare the [aacceptable](#note31a) day of the Lord and proclaim [bliberty](#note31b) to the captives who were bound, even unto all who would [crepent](#note31c) of their sins and receive the gospel.

32 Thus was the gospel preached to those who had [adied](#note32a) in their sins, without a [bknowledge](#note32b) of the truth, or in [ctransgression](#note32c), having [drejected](#note32d) the prophets.

33 These were taught [afaith](#note33a) in God, repentance from sin, [bvicarious baptism](#note33b) for the [cremission](#note33c) of sins, the [dgift](#note33d) of the Holy Ghost by the laying on of hands,

34 And all other principles of the gospel that were necessary for them to know in order to qualify themselves that they might be [ajudged](#note34a) according to men in the flesh, but live according to God in the spirit.

35 And so it was made known among the dead, both small and great, the unrighteous as well as the faithful, that redemption had been wrought through the [asacrifice](#note35a) of the Son of God upon the [bcross](#note35b).

36 Thus was it made known that our Redeemer spent his time during his sojourn in the world of [aspirits](#note36a), instructing and preparing the faithful spirits of the [bprophets](#note36b) who had testified of him in the flesh;

37 That they might carry the message of redemption unto all the dead, unto whom he could not go personally, because of their [arebellion](#note37a) and transgression, that they through the ministration of his servants might also hear his words.

38 Among the great and [amighty](#note38a) ones who were assembled in this vast congregation of the righteous were Father [bAdam](#note38b), the [cAncient of Days](#note38c) and father of all,

39 And our glorious [aMother](#note39a) [bEve](#note39b), with many of her faithful [cdaughters](#note39c) who had lived through the ages and worshiped the true and living God.

40 [aAbel](#note40a), the first [bmartyr](#note40b), was there, and his brother [cSeth](#note40c), one of the mighty ones, who was in the express [dimage](#note40d) of his father, Adam.

41 [aNoah](#note41a), who gave warning of the flood; [bShem](#note41b), the great [chigh priest](#note41c); [dAbraham](#note41d), the father of the faithful; [eIsaac](#note41e), [fJacob](#note41f), and Moses, the great [glaw-giver](#note41g) of Israel;

42 And [aIsaiah](#note42a), who declared by prophecy that the Redeemer was anointed to bind up the broken-hearted, to proclaim liberty to the [bcaptives](#note42b), and the opening of the [cprison](#note42c) to them that were bound, were also there.

43 Moreover, Ezekiel, who was shown in vision the great valley of [adry bones](#note43a), which were to be [bclothed](#note43b) upon with flesh, to come forth again in the resurrection of the dead, living souls;

44 Daniel, who foresaw and foretold the establishment of the [akingdom](#note44a) of God in the latter days, never again to be destroyed nor given to other people;

45 [aElias](#note45a), who was with Moses on the Mount of Transfiguration;

46 And [aMalachi](#note46a), the prophet who testified of the coming of [bElijah](#note46b)—of whom also Moroni spake to the Prophet Joseph Smith, declaring that he should come before the ushering in of the great and dreadful [cday](#note46c) of the Lord—were also there.

47 The Prophet Elijah was to plant in the [ahearts](#note47a) of the children the promises made to their fathers,

48 Foreshadowing the great work to be done in the [atemples](#note48a) of the Lord in the [bdispensation](#note48b) of the fulness of times, for the redemption of the dead, and the [csealing](#note48c) of the children to their parents, lest the whole earth be smitten with a curse and utterly wasted at his coming.

49 All these and many more, even the [aprophets](#note49a) who dwelt among the Nephites and [btestified](#note49b) of the coming of the Son of God, mingled in the vast assembly and waited for their deliverance,

50 For the [adead](#note50a) had looked upon the long absence of their [bspirits](#note50b) from their bodies as a [cbondage](#note50c).

51 These the Lord taught, and gave them [apower](#note51a) to come forth, after his resurrection from the dead, to enter into his Father’s kingdom, there to be crowned with [bimmortality](#note51b) and eternal life,

52 And continue thenceforth their labor as had been promised by the Lord, and be partakers of all [ablessings](#note52a) which were held in reserve for them that love him.

53 The Prophet Joseph Smith, and my father, Hyrum Smith, Brigham Young, John Taylor, Wilford Woodruff, and other choice [aspirits](#note53a) who were [breserved](#note53b) to come forth in the [cfulness](#note53c) of times to take part in laying the [dfoundations](#note53d) of the great latter-day work,

54 Including the building of the [atemples](#note54a) and the performance of ordinances therein for the redemption of the [bdead](#note54b), were also in the spirit world.

55 I observed that they were also among the [anoble](#note55a) and great ones who were [bchosen](#note55b) in the beginning to be rulers in the Church of God.

56 Even before they were born, they, with many others, received their first [alessons](#note56a) in the world of spirits and were [bprepared](#note56b) to come forth in the due [ctime](#note56c) of the Lord to labor in his [dvineyard](#note56d) for the salvation of the souls of men.

57 I beheld that the faithful [aelders](#note57a) of this dispensation, when they depart from mortal life, continue their labors in the [bpreaching](#note57b) of the [cgospel](#note57c) of repentance and redemption, through the sacrifice of the Only Begotten Son of God, among those who are in darkness and under the bondage of sin in the great world of the [dspirits](#note57d) of the dead.

58 The dead who [arepent](#note58a) will be redeemed, through obedience to the [bordinances](#note58b) of the house of God,

59 And after they have paid the [apenalty](#note59a) of their transgressions, and are [bwashed](#note59b) clean, shall receive a [creward](#note59c) according to their [dworks](#note59d), for they are heirs of salvation.

60 Thus was the [avision](#note60a) of the redemption of the dead revealed to me, and I bear record, and I know that this [brecord](#note60b) is [ctrue](#note60c), through the blessing of our Lord and Savior, Jesus Christ, even so. Amen.
